books.title: After Rebirth, I Become The Emperor's White MoonLight
books.author: 多多亦杉
books.synopsis:
Jiang Mi is the Empress Dowager's niece — a woman who was forced upon the Emperor. Even knowing full well that her imperial cousin held no affection for her, she had stubbornly followed her heart into his inner palace. A great deal of scheming, and yet her end had not been a pleasant one.

After her rebirth, Jiang Mi came to her senses. Rather than waste a lifetime on an Emperor whose heart would never be hers, she would find herself someone who actually cherished her.

The young master of the Grand Councillor's family — refined and courteous. The young master of the Loyal Valor Marquis's household — gallant and handsome. The heir of the Duke Zhenguo's estate — gentle and agreeable. Jiang Mi counted her prospects on her fingers, quietly mapping out her plans.

When the Empress Dowager sought a boon from the new Emperor on her niece's behalf, everyone assumed the Jiang family would seize the opportunity to secure Jiang Mi a place in the imperial harem.

The new Emperor watched calmly as Jiang Mi, cheeks flushed, murmured in a small voice: "This subject's daughter wishes to… to ask Your Majesty to grant her a marriage decree."

The Emperor, ordinarily so temperate and gracious, let a flash of something dangerous cross his eyes. The jade archer's ring he had been turning idly between his fingers crumbled to dust.

chapters.title: Chapter 1
chapters.content:
Drifting clouds, a gentle breeze, and the silk music growing ever slower, it all made one drowsier by the moment.

Jiang Mi propped her cheek against her hand, eyes closed, her head bobbing in rhythm with the melody as she nearly drifted off to sleep.

Suddenly, a voice rang out beside her ear: "Miss?"

"Miss?"

"Miss, please wake up, the Empress Dowager is about to arrive!"

The voice grew louder and louder in her ear. She wanted to open her eyes, yet her eyelids felt as heavy as a thousand pounds, her consciousness hazy and muddled, like clouds shrouding the sky.

The Empress Dowager.

That was her aunt.

But, hadn't her aunt passed away three years ago?

Her lashes fluttered like the wings of a butterfly as she slowly opened her eyes. A pair of beautiful eyes, heavy with lingering drowsiness, gazed around in bewilderment.

Above her was a coffered ceiling, patterned in a grid.

To her left were diamond-lattice windows.

To her right hung a rosewood-and-gold landscape screen.

The familiarity of it all struck Jiang Mi with astonishment, was this not the warm pavilion of Ci'ning Palace? Whenever her aunt had been alive and she came to the palace, she would always stay in this very room.

Yet ever since her aunt's passing, Ci'ning Palace had been sealed off, no one permitted to enter. How had she come to be here?

And besides, had she not just breathed her last breath?

Qiu Yu, seeing her young mistress standing there in a daze, couldn't help but straighten up and wave her hand in front of Jiang Mi's face. "Miss, what's the matter?" she asked, puzzled.

At the sound of her voice, Jiang Mi turned to look at Qiu Yu. The girl stood before her with a healthy flush to her cheeks, she had not been punished and sent to the laundry, and had not accidentally fallen into a well.

Looking at everything before her, she suddenly had the strange feeling of falling into a dream, as if she had returned to her previous life, to the time before she entered the new Emperor's inner palace.

Jiang Mi pinched herself hard on the arm. The pain made her flinch!

Just then, the sound of court ladies and eunuchs offering their greetings drifted in from outside the door. Jiang Mi's scattered thoughts drew back together. She watched as her aunt, the Empress Dowager of Wei, leaning on Cui Mama's arm, slowly walked inside.

The moment she recognized the face before her, Jiang Mi's heart clenched. As she sank into a curtsy of greeting, her eyes instantly reddened.

After her aunt passed away, she had endured three long springs and autumns alone within the palace walls. The loneliness and desolation of those years was something perhaps no one could truly understand. Now a tangle of emotions swelled within her, wave after wave of warmth rising in her chest.

The Empress Dowager walked straight to her and bent down to help her up, revealing a tender smile. "Tangtang," she said gently, "are you feeling any better?"

Hearing her aunt call her childhood nickname, Jiang Mi felt a prickling at the tip of her nose. She fought back her tears and nodded, following her aunt's lead. "Thank you for your concern, Auntie. I'm much better."

The Empress Dowager patted Jiang Mi's hand. "Auntie knows, this has been an unfair burden on you. Once things are done, no matter how great the grievance, Auntie will see it set right for you."

Having said that, the Empress Dowager exchanged a glance with Cui Mama, who promptly carried over a food box.

The Empress Dowager smiled. "Inside is a sobering soup I had prepared. Tangtang, go in my place, won't you?"

Jiang Mi stared at the dragon pattern on the food box, and something seemed to explode in her mind with a resounding boom.

This sobering soup, it was the key to her entry through the palace gates.

"Don't be afraid. When the time comes, I will speak for you in all things."

The Empress Dowager held the food box out to her. Jiang Mi's face went pale as she accepted it.

Escorted by Cui Mama and the court ladies, Jiang Mi walked toward the door. A mild breeze swept past, and the osmanthus petals along the covered walkway drifted down in a soft cascade.

Her thoughts scattered as the memories of the past came flooding in, one after another.

The thirteenth day of the ninth month in the first year of Yuanxi, this very day in her previous life. She had come to the palace to celebrate her aunt's birthday. Having drunk a few more cups of wine while watching the opera, she'd grown lightheaded, and her aunt had sent her back to the warm pavilion to rest.

Not long after, her aunt had sent her to Wangyun Pavilion to deliver the sobering soup.

Who was in Wangyun Pavilion, she knew perfectly well.

The new Emperor had only recently ascended the throne, and it was the Empress Dowager's birthday. Even though he was not much of a drinker, he had to keep up appearances. The sight of the Emperor raising his cup to accompany the Empress Dowager had drawn praise from everyone who witnessed it, what a picture of filial devotion.

After several rounds of wine, the guests had dispersed. The Emperor dismissed his palanquin and walked back to Wangyun Pavilion to rest. Anyone with eyes could see that the Son of Heaven was somewhat drunk.

Her aunt had sent her at precisely this moment to deliver the sobering soup, naturally, it had been a deliberate arrangement.

She was the legitimate eldest daughter of the Jiang family. She understood all too well the careful cultivation her family had lavished on her over the years, it was so that she might become Empress and carry on the Jiang family's glory for generations to come.

And at the time, she had harbored tender feelings for the Emperor. She had wholeheartedly wished to enter his inner palace and remain by his side.

What a pity, for all their scheming, no one had been able to outwit that man who appeared gentle on the surface yet was, in truth, cold and ruthless at heart.

In her previous life, she had also come to deliver the sobering soup in the same way. Obediently following her aunt's arrangements, she had fed the Emperor a few spoonfuls of the medicine, then steeled herself, undone the clasps of her garment, let the outer robe fall, and stretched out her pale, slender arms to tremblingly encircle his waist.

Yet before the people her aunt had arranged could burst in to confirm the intimacy between them,

The Virtuous Consort had arrived first.

And behind the Virtuous Consort stood not only her own niece, Xie Mingshan, but half of the Imperial Academy of Medicine.

Outside, the Virtuous Consort was loudly scolding the court ladies guarding the door, clearly on the verge of pushing it open. Jiang Mi had been so frightened she nearly leapt to her feet, but at that very moment, the man beside her, who had been dead to the world in his drunken stupor, suddenly turned over.

An arm like iron tongs fell across her body, blocking any hope of escape.

The moment the Virtuous Consort stepped through the door, she saw Jiang Mi in a state of dishevelment.

Their eyes met. The Virtuous Consort feigned shock, then smiled with an expression that was neither quite a smile nor not. "It seems I've come at an inopportune moment," she said, "and disturbed Miss Jiang's affairs."

The imperial physicians all turned their backs at once. Before Jiang Mi could even speak, Xie Mingshan chimed in with a jeer: "An unmarried girl in such a hurry to shed her clothes, what fine upbringing the Jiang family has indeed."

All this commotion woke the man on the couch. He pressed a hand to his brow and sat up. Those long, narrow phoenix eyes swept over everyone present. When his gaze passed over her, Jiang Mi felt as though she were standing on a mountain of blades and a sea of fire.

The Virtuous Consort and Xie Mingshan hurried to pay their respects.

At the time, Jiang Mi had mustered her courage and stolen a glance at him from the corner of her eye, just in time to see his thin lips part slightly: "All of you, get out."

Jiang Mi clenched her fists. She could have died on the spot.

The chief eunuch, Li Fu, arrived in time and managed to usher the Virtuous Consort away. Jiang Mi, too, fled from Wangyun Pavilion, her face burning crimson.

Though the Empress Dowager wielded her authority to suppress the rumors, there is no fire in this world that can truly be contained.

Her reputation was ruined, in the end.

Later, her aunt employed every manner of scheme, and at last secured her entry into the new Emperor's harem. She had exhausted herself trying to win his favor, doing all manner of outrageous things, yet that man never once looked upon her any differently.

Without favor, how could there be status? The Jiang family's dream of producing another Empress was shattered beyond all hope.

As Jiang Mi recalled the events of her previous life, her steps grew slower and slower.

She no longer wanted to deliver the sobering soup. All she wanted was to flee the palace as quickly as possible.

......

Cui Mama, seeing the young miss walking more and more slowly, called out to remind her: "Third Miss, do not let the hour pass."

Jiang Mi knew time was pressing, it would be easier to succeed while the new Emperor had yet to sober up. But right now, she had no desire to succeed at anything.

Jiang Mi gave Cui Mama a perfunctory nod.

Before long, the great gate of Wangyun Pavilion came into view.

The court ladies standing guard at the door saw Jiang Mi and her party approaching and stepped forward in short, quick strides, lowering their voices. "Miss, please go inside quickly."

Jiang Mi smiled bitterly to herself. It seemed she could not escape after all.

Jiang Mi pushed open the door and entered. All was still and quiet. The air was thick with the scent of wine mingled with a faint trace of dragon's blood incense.

She rounded the screen and saw the man lying on the couch.

What met her eyes was a face of clear, refined beauty, identical to the one preserved in her memory.

The man's brow was furrowed now, his eyes shut tight, a faint flush coloring his cheeks. His collar had been pulled wide open, it seemed he had done it himself. Jiang Mi's gaze slipped past his prominent Adam's apple, and she looked away at once, as if scalded.

Forcing herself to appear calm, Jiang Mi set the food box on the table, opened the lid, and bowed her head as she stirred the medicinal soup, her hand trembling slightly with nerves.

Knowing him as she did from those three years together, she understood he would never drink a medicine of unknown origin. She only needed to stall for time and keep up appearances.

Jiang Mi cupped the warm jade bowl in both hands and walked toward the pot of orchids arranged on the windowsill. She poured a small portion of the sobering soup into the flower pot.

She cast a careful glance in the direction of the couch. The man was still asleep.

She let out a quiet breath of relief.

Yet in a room so filled with his presence, she felt deeply ill at ease, her whole body wound tight.

After placing the jade bowl back in the food box, she seated herself properly on the embroidered stool some distance away.

In her heart, Jiang Mi silently recited: This time, I will not take off my clothes, and I will not scheme against you. Just let me pass through this safely.

She sat, her heart anxious and tense, for nearly a quarter of an hour. Then hurried footsteps sounded from outside.

Jiang Mi pressed her lashes down.

......

The moment the Virtuous Consort learned the Emperor had gone to Wangyun Pavilion, she sent two quick-witted little eunuchs to scout out the situation.

The palace attendant reported back: there was no sign of the chief eunuch Li Fu before the gate of Wangyun Pavilion, only two young court ladies standing guard, their heads turning back and forth as though waiting for someone.

The Virtuous Consort heard this and was certain something was afoot. The Empress Dowager's intentions were known throughout the entire palace. Most likely, this was all orchestrated to pave the way for that niece of hers.

Using concern for His Majesty's wellbeing as her pretext, she led the physicians of the Imperial Academy of Medicine and Xie Mingshan straight toward Wangyun Pavilion.

Dark clouds swallowed the bright moon. The wind howled.

Before Wangyun Pavilion, the Virtuous Consort fixed Cui Mama, who stood blocking the entrance, with a sharp, cutting look, and said in a tone of controlled fury: "Outrageous! I came with the physicians to check on His Majesty in my concern for the imperial body, and you, a mere servant, dare to bar my way, do you intend some treachery?"

The Virtuous Consort laughed coldly within. She wanted to see for herself: if the legitimate daughter of the Jiang family were caught in the act before the very eyes of the court, how could she still hold her head up in the palace?

Cui Mama broke into a cold sweat, her face going white as paper. She opened her mouth to say something more but was seized by the attendants the Virtuous Consort had brought with her.

A mocking smile played on the Virtuous Consort's lips. She was just about to push the door open,

When, all of a sudden, that rosewood carved door was opened from within.

Out stepped a beautiful young woman dressed in a celadon moonlit skirt, a food box in hand.

She looked at the assembled crowd with a slight expression of surprise, then quickly composed herself and offered a curtsy to the Virtuous Consort. "This one pays her respects to the Noble Consort."

The Virtuous Consort narrowed her eyes and looked Jiang Mi up and down for a long moment.

Her clothing was intact. Her hair was unruffled.

"This late at night, what brings Miss Jiang here?"

Jiang Mi inclined her head with a smile, unhurried in her reply. "In answer to the Noble Consort, this one came by the Empress Dowager's orders to deliver sobering soup to His Majesty. How fortunate to have run into the Noble Consort. May I ask, is that also sobering soup that Miss Xie is carrying?"

Xie Mingshan, standing off to one side, flushed, pressed her lips together, and replied, "My aunt was concerned that His Majesty was unwell from the wine, and specially instructed the Imperial Academy of Medicine to prepare a medicinal brew."

Jiang Mi's smile did not waver. "What a remarkable coincidence, both the Empress Dowager and the Noble Consort are equally devoted in their maternal concern."

The Virtuous Consort had shown kindness to the new Emperor when he was young, and he held her in great respect. The Empress Dowager, however, though she occupied the position of official mother, had shared no close bond with him in the days when he was merely a prince. No one could have foreseen that after the Chaos of the Three Princes, it would be the unremarkable Sixth Prince who would ascend the throne.

The Virtuous Consort, trading on her old ties of affection with the Emperor, also hoped to place a niece of the Xie family in the position of consort.

The Virtuous Consort and her own aunt were no different, having spent so many years together in the palace, even their methods of pushing a niece forward were nearly identical.

The atmosphere had grown somewhat delicate. Jiang Mi had no desire to linger. "Since the Noble Consort and the physicians are all here, this one and Cui Mama shall take our leave."

"Wait!" The Virtuous Consort's tone shifted. "Let the physicians have a look at what's in that bowl."

At those words, Jiang Mi and Cui Mama both lifted their eyes.

The Virtuous Consort addressed the physician behind her: "His Majesty's imperial body concerns the welfare of the realm, I ask that Imperial Physician Li examine it carefully. Whatever enters His Majesty's mouth must be scrutinized with the utmost care."

The Virtuous Consort did not for a moment believe the Empress Dowager had simply sent Jiang Mi to deliver a bowl of sobering soup.

"Is the Noble Consort insinuating that the soup sent by the Empress Dowager is suspect?" Cui Mama stepped in front of Jiang Mi and challenged her, her face set in a stern expression.

The Virtuous Consort raised an eyebrow, and her own Mama spoke: "Hold your tongue, since when does a servant question her mistress? Cui Mama has been in the palace a long time; how can you know so little of propriety? That my lady has not punished you is already a concession made out of regard for the Empress Dowager."

Cui Mama's face flushed red with anger. Jiang Mi took hold of her hand, signaling her not to put herself at a disadvantage at this moment.

She wanted to see just what the physician the Virtuous Consort had brought would find. She was not afraid of waking the Emperor and drawing his wrath either.

The commotion now was no quieter than when the Virtuous Consort had come to catch them in the act in her previous life.

After a brief moment, Imperial Physician Li set down the sobering soup and shook his head in the Virtuous Consort's direction. "In reply to the Noble Consort, there is nothing amiss with this medicine."

The Virtuous Consort's composure slipped. Her cold gaze fell on Jiang Mi.

Her innocence proven, the great stone in Jiang Mi's heart settled steadily into place.

Drawing on her memories of the previous life, she knew this new Emperor, though gentle and composed on the surface, harbored the deepest aversion to the scheming and treachery of the inner palace. Though she had the upper hand for now, if one examined the situation carefully, her aunt's actions, dismissing the chief eunuch and sending her to intrude uninvited into the Emperor's chambers, were no small matters.

She had to leave quickly!

Jiang Mi offered another curtsy, her voice soft and unhurried: "Tonight is the Empress Dowager's birthday. His Majesty, in his filial devotion, drank more than a few cups of wine, and has only now managed to fall asleep. As the Noble Consort has business, perhaps it might wait until another day, let us not disturb His Majesty's peaceful rest."

With things put that way, the Virtuous Consort could only swallow her resentment and say nothing.

The crowd bowed and withdrew. The vermilion doors creaked shut.

On the couch, the man opened his eyes.

chapters.title: Chapter 2
chapters.content:
Ci'ning Palace was ablaze with lantern light, and the attendants within dared not make a sound.

Plum-blossom patterned glazed incense burners released the fragrance of aloeswood. The Empress Dowager Jiang reclined with her eyes closed against the soft divan, while her senior court lady, Qing Xue, gently massaged her temples.

Jiang Mi stepped forward slowly and sank to her knees. "Auntie, Tangtang has failed you."

The words of apology were on her lips, yet in her heart she felt a lightness she had never known before.

This time, she had not fled the palace in wretched, haggard disarray. She would not be too ashamed to go out because of those rumors, spending her days shut away in her room.

She only needed to endure this one night, and then she could go home.

As for her aunt's wish to have her enter the palace, she would find a way, gradually, to dissuade her.

For now, at least, she could breathe.

The Empress Dowager Jiang opened her eyes, sat up straight, and motioned for Qing Xue beside her to stop. "Come now, help the young miss up."

Jiang Mi's eyes reddened. She took Qing Xue's arm and rose to her feet.

Qing Xue supported Jiang Mi and walked a few steps toward the divan. The Empress Dowager took Jiang Mi's hand and drew her to sit beside her.

The Empress Dowager gazed at Jiang Mi's lovely face, delicate as a lotus blossom. Seeing the anxiety and unease in her expression, she patted her hand in comfort and let out a low sigh. "Dear child. This was not your fault. Auntie does not blame you."

The Empress Dowager suppressed her anger, grinding her teeth. "That woman Xie Ronghui has always set herself against me. She thinks she can lord it over me on the strength of that little bit of past kindness, she is simply dreaming..."

"Auntie, please calm yourself..." Jiang Mi gently rubbed the Empress Dowager's back, soothing her. Her aunt had never been in the best of health, and anger was apt to stir up her old ailments.

The Empress Dowager coughed several times. Once her breathing had steadied, she said, "It is fortunate that you acted quickly and were not caught in the act. What a pity, though, such a perfect opportunity lost."

Jiang Mi lowered her eyes, pressed her lips together, and swallowed the words she had wanted to say.

The Empress Dowager noticed that Jiang Mi's face was pale, fear written plainly on her expression.

Knowing the events of the day had frightened her, she said, "You must be tired as well. Go and rest!"

Just then, Cui Mama walked in with a strained expression on her face.

"Your Highness, word has come from Wangyun Pavilion. All the court ladies who attended there have been taken away by the Imperial Guard. Eunuch Li Fu has been flogged thirty times. The Director of the Imperial Academy of Medicine, Gu Hairong, and all the physicians with him have also been punished."

The Empress Dowager's expression shifted suddenly, and after several breaths her face took on a sunken, defeated cast.

She murmured softly, "He is not simply punishing people, he is slapping my face!"

The Empress Dowager swayed on her feet, a splitting headache striking her, and she nearly fell backward.

This gave everyone in the room a terrible fright.

Jiang Mi caught hold of the Empress Dowager in time. She told Qing Xue to fetch the medicine the Empress Dowager took each day, and instructed Cui Mama to summon an imperial physician.

By the time the Empress Dowager had settled and fallen asleep, the hour of midnight had long since passed.

Jiang Mi returned to the warm pavilion, exhausted to the bone, her clothes soaked through with cold sweat, the inner garment clinging chill and damp to her skin whenever the wind blew.

Qiu Yu had prepared hot water. Jiang Mi dismissed her from attending, and had just begun to undress when her hands went still.

She lowered her gaze to the strips of cloth bound around her chest, and a bitterness stole into her heart.

It was widely known that the new Emperor favored women of serene and virtuous character. As a result, many young ladies had not only dressed to suit his tastes, but also constrained their own behavior and bearing accordingly.

And she, she was somewhat fuller-figured than other girls her age, which made her figure appear less decorous.

So she had bound herself with strips of cloth to compress and flatten it. Though uncomfortable, she had willingly endured the discomfort for the sake of looking more proper.

Now, she could not care less what that man preferred. All she wanted was to be at ease. Unbecoming or not, so be it.

Jiang Mi unwound the cloth strips and let them fall to the floor. She lifted her foot and stepped into the bathing tub, its surface scattered with flower petals.

The warm water enveloped Jiang Mi's slender, pale body. She exhaled a long, slow breath.

Steam rose and curled around her. She closed her eyes and began to drift pleasantly toward sleep.

The water rippled gently, rose petals of deeper and lighter shades settling against her snow-white skin, making that lovely, water-beaded face look all the more enchanting.

In the midst of the misty warmth, it seemed as if the sound of a tiny bell drifted toward her.

Clear and melodious.

One peal, slow and lingering; another, unhurried and lilting; another, languid and floating...

Gradually the peals came faster, one after another, and faintly, there seemed to be a sound of suppressed crying, just barely escaping before it was swallowed back into silence, leaving only the bell sounds growing ever more tangled and frantic...

A flush crept across Jiang Mi's face from the heat. Her brow furrowed tightly. She suddenly flung out both arms and began slapping the surface of the water, as though struggling against some terrifying restraint.

All at once, her eyes flew open. Those wet, almond-shaped eyes held within them humiliation and grief.

Jiang Mi sniffled, stepped out of the tub, and changed into clean sleeping clothes. As she pulled on her silk stockings, her gaze fell on her small, pale ankles. There was no red imprint of fingers there yet, and no golden bell fastened there as punishment.

Jiang Mi curled herself into a ball and wrapped her arms tightly around herself. She could not bear to be treated that way again.

......

Dawn had barely broken when Jiang Mi threw on her outer robe and pushed open the window. A cool, refreshing breeze rushed in to meet her, carrying on it the scent of osmanthus blossoms, sweet and invigorating.

She had not slept well the night before, half-dreaming, half-awake, her whole being light and adrift, like a leaf floating on water, rocked by the wind with no place to settle.

Qiu Yu brought in hot water. Jiang Mi took the warm cloth and pressed it to her face, and when she saw some color returning to her complexion, she had Qiu Yu come over to help her dress her hair.

Jiang Mi stopped Qiu Yu from styling her hair into the flowing-cloud coiffure, asking instead for a simple hanging-loop bun, with a pearl tassel hairpin tucked in place.

When it came time to change her clothes, Qiu Yu held out a clean strip of binding cloth. Jiang Mi said, "Put it away. There's no need for it anymore."

Qiu Yu was puzzled, hadn't her young mistress always bound herself the moment she set foot in the palace? Why had she suddenly stopped?

Not daring to ask, Qiu Yu lowered her head and put the cloth away in the trunk.

......

Before leaving, Jiang Mi took along the jade-millet porridge that had been simmering over a low flame all night, and made her way to the Empress Dowager's bedchamber.

Qing Xue, standing guard at the door, greeted Jiang Mi with a curtsy. "Miss, you've come so early? The Empress Dowager hasn't woken yet."

Jiang Mi handed the porridge to Qing Xue. "Never mind. Have this kept warm. I'd like to go in and see my aunt."

Qing Xue opened a small panel of the door for Jiang Mi, and once she had stepped inside, quickly closed it again to keep the cold air from entering.

The moment Jiang Mi walked into the bedchamber, the air was heavy with the thick scent of medicinal herbs, just breathing it in made the tip of her tongue feel bitter.

She settled onto the embroidered stool beside the bed and tucked the covers more snugly in place.

Looking at her aunt's sickly face, her heart ached. In her previous life, at this time, she had not known her aunt's health had deteriorated to such a state, her aunt had not even managed to hold on for two years.

That was why her aunt had been so frantic, making one hasty misstep after another. Not only had things not gone as she wished, she had also drawn the new Emperor's displeasure.

And even if she had managed to install this useless niece of hers into the new Emperor's harem, what good had it done? In the end, even that had not saved the Jiang family.

Jiang Mi dabbed a warm cloth gently across the Empress Dowager's forehead.

Auntie, in this life, let's not grasp for things that were never meant to be ours. Let's try a different way, shall we?

......

In the flower hall of Ci'ning Palace, two or three consorts who had borne imperial children arrived one after another. All had come upon hearing that the Empress Dowager was unwell, to pay their respects and inquire after her health.

The Virtuous Consort arrived as well, accompanied by Princess Anyang and Xie Mingshan.

The consorts who had arrived earlier all rose to offer their greetings.

The Virtuous Consort gave a slight lift of her hand, and her own Mama helped her to the seat of honor on the left.

By the time Jiang Mi and Cui Mama came hurrying in, the court ladies had just served tea to the assembled consorts.

Jiang Mi dipped into a curtsy. "All the honored ladies and Your Highness, blessings to you. The Empress Dowager still requires rest and quiet. The ladies are most welcome to return another time."

The Virtuous Consort idly stirred her tea bowl's lid, letting out a soft laugh. "Oh? The Empress Dowager cannot come out to receive us, and now we're being hurried away even from sharing a cup of tea?"

Jiang Mi said simply, "I would not dare."

The Virtuous Consort raised her eyes with a half-smile. "I see Miss Jiang is quite bold indeed."

"The Noble Consort jests." Jiang Mi knew she had offended the Virtuous Consort yesterday, and the woman would not let it go easily.

One of the more easy-going consorts, seeing the awkward atmosphere, took the initiative to engage the Virtuous Consort in conversation. The others chimed in, and the flower hall became lively again.

Princess Anyang studied Jiang Mi for a long moment, then leaned over to murmur to Xie Mingshan, "Cousin, don't worry, Imperial Brother is certain to favor someone as serene and dignified as you."

Xie Mingshan blushed and pushed her away. "Princess, please don't say such things."

Scarcely had a cup of tea's time passed when a Mama from Changchun Palace came and whispered a few words in the Virtuous Consort's ear.

The Virtuous Consort rose, supported by her attendant. "Since the Empress Dowager is still resting, it would not do for us to disturb her further. Otherwise, someone might truly start grieving over the tea leaves."

Jiang Mi smiled. "The Noble Consort is too modest. If the consort enjoys the tea leaves, this one can certainly manage to send a few liang of them to the consort."

She had a court lady fetch the tea leaves at once.

The Virtuous Consort gave a dismissive sniff and swept past Jiang Mi, leading her party away.

......

The moment they were out of Ci'ning Palace, Princess Anyang asked, "Mother, why did you wait so long before leaving? What was so special about that tea?"

The Virtuous Consort had been on edge ever since the Emperor had punished the physicians she had brought to Wangyun Pavilion. She had come here today partly on account of hearing the Empress Dowager was ill.

She had just been stifled by that little Jiang girl, but thinking of the news she had just received, she felt a measure of satisfaction return.

"I wasn't there for the tea. I was specifically waiting to see if the Emperor would come to Ci'ning Palace to visit the sick! I've just learned that after the morning court session, His Majesty summoned several ministers and went directly to the Hall of Governance for deliberations. It seems our Empress Dowager falling ill wasn't enough to bring the Emperor here to pay his respects."

Princess Anyang brightened with understanding. "So Mother deliberately brought Cousin Ashan along, if Imperial Brother had come, Cousin Ashan could have had a chance to see him."

Xie Mingshan blushed with embarrassment at being teased.

The Virtuous Consort said, "Ashan, just set your heart at ease."

Xie Mingshan hesitated, wanting to speak. "But, but that Miss Jiang..."

The Virtuous Consort cut her off. "What does the Jiang family have to compete with you for the position of Empress? Your family is the Xie Ducal House, your father, your uncles, all hold important posts. The Jiang family is nothing but an empty shell. How can they compare to our Xie family? The Emperor has just quelled the rebellion of the traitorous princes; to purge the court, he needs the support of the great clans. The Jiang family holds only the hollow title of the Marquis of Gracious Favor. Her uncle has inherited the title but holds an idle post in the Ministry of Rites. Her second uncle is toiling away as a county magistrate in some dreary backwater. Her father even took a merchant's daughter from Jiangnan as his second wife, a thorough disgrace. Forget becoming Empress; in my view, the Emperor will never allow her into the inner palace at all!"

The Virtuous Consort patted Xie Mingshan's shoulder. "The Imperial Birthday Banquet is not far off. You must prepare well."

Xie Mingshan replied, "Yes, please set your heart at ease, Honored Aunt."

The Virtuous Consort gazed in the direction of Ci'ning Palace, pressing down the deep resentment within her.

In those early selection days, she had been the one most likely to become Empress! Yet the Grand Empress Dowager had believed that monk's reading of fate, that a woman of the Jiang family bore the phoenix destiny, and so a girl from a declined noble house had risen from a forgotten marquis's daughter to become Empress.

And she, daughter of a ducal house, had been suppressed all along by that upstart Jiang clan.

Even having smoothly ascended to the position of Empress Dowager, what had it mattered, the new Emperor on the throne had not come from her own womb.

Unlike herself, at the very least, she had cared for him for six months after his birth mother passed away.

More affection than that nominal Empress Dowager had ever had with him.

The blessing of old age, that ought to have been hers to enjoy.

chapters.title: Chapter 3
chapters.content:
"Both the Ministry of Rites and I have submitted lists to him, daughters of appropriate age from families of the third rank and above, all legitimate. But he rejected all of them. I've thought that perhaps the ones on those lists are not who he has in mind, he likely has his own ideas."

After the midday meal, the Empress Dowager's spirits had improved. She reclined against a soft bolster and spoke to Jiang Mi of the new Emperor's affairs.

"When he was sixteen, the late Emperor had arranged a betrothal for him. But the girl was ill-fated, the minor ceremony of betrothal had not yet been completed when she fell gravely ill and died. After that, he went to Yunzhou and stayed for seven years. The late Emperor recalled him to court a few times; when I did see him, we never had much to say to each other.

"Now that he has ascended the throne, the court ministers and great clans are all unsettled. After all, no one had expected a prince stationed at the far frontier to inherit the empire. They say that while he was at the border, he fought the foreign tribes, killing over twenty thousand enemy soldiers. The pacification of the rebel princes' uprising was also achieved by his own troops. The ministers at court worry about whether he has a harsh and brutal nature, whether he can tolerate others, and their unease naturally makes them waver.

"Yet he has exceeded everyone's expectations. His manner is gentle and scholarly, broad-minded in his dealings with these veteran officials. In matters of governance, he is remarkably fair and strict, those who ought to be investigated are investigated; he shows no favoritism to imperial relatives. He has steadied the hearts of the greater part of the court. This is a good thing for Wei, which has only just returned to stability."

The Empress Dowager finished speaking and rested for a moment. Jiang Mi took the opportunity to offer her water, listening quietly.

The tales of the new Emperor that her aunt told, she had heard them all the same way in her previous life and had believed them just as readily. More than that, she had cherished in her heart from girlhood how the then-Sixth Prince had once come to her aid. Yet in the three years she had spent in the palace after entering his court, the Emperor she had come to know, through what she had lived through, bore little resemblance to the gentle, tolerant sovereign her aunt described.

The Empress Dowager noticed Jiang Mi was drifting into thought, and took hold of her hand. "So many eyes are fixed on the position of his Empress. Our Jiang family may be a marquis's house, but in the eyes of the great clans and the imperial family, it is simply not enough. It is only because I stand here that we have any chance at all.

"Yesterday, had we seized that opportunity and seen things through, we could have used the Emperor's loss of propriety while drunk to claim he had taken liberties with you. Given his nature, he would have felt some measure of guilt. Had I urged him further, your position as Empress might well have been secured. What a pity... what a pity..." She coughed.

Jiang Mi hurriedly offered the jade bowl again. In her heart she thought: even if things had gone through, that man would never have felt any guilt toward her. He had never once considered her as a candidate for Empress. Her aunt's scheming had been in vain.

Jiang Mi said in comfort, "Auntie, please rest a while, you must take care of yourself."

The Empress Dowager sipped from the bowl and caught her breath. "The Imperial Birthday Banquet is in a few days. Is your gift ready?"

Jiang Mi started slightly, she had nearly forgotten about it. In her previous life, she had hidden at home during the Imperial Birthday Banquet, too ashamed to show her face, and had naturally missed it altogether.

The Empress Dowager saw her hesitation. "Tangtang?"

Jiang Mi came back to herself and said haltingly, "It, it is ready. I only don't know whether the Emperor will like it."

The Empress Dowager reassured her, "Don't worry. As long as the thought is there, it will be enough. Best of all if it goes to his heart. I estimate that after the Imperial Birthday Banquet, the ministers will again submit memorials urging the Emperor to fill the inner palace. If we can capture his attention at the banquet, even if you cannot become Empress, a consort's rank is certainly achievable."

Jiang Mi was surprised, hadn't her aunt been set on her becoming Empress?

The Empress Dowager smiled. "Naturally, becoming Empress would be best. But the mind of this Emperor of ours is truly beyond anyone's guessing. What happened at Wangyun Pavilion was already a risk, it cannot be repeated. We can only think of another way and proceed gradually. With me here, I will never let our Tangtang suffer."

Hearing her aunt's meaning, it was clear that no matter what, she was going to enter the palace.

Her aunt was ill at present; it was not the right time to tell her she had no wish to enter the palace. It might upset her and worsen her condition. She would wait until her aunt had recovered a little and then find an opportunity to make her feelings known.

Jiang Mi set aside the thoughts in her heart. She carried over the warmed medicine. "Auntie, please drink this while it's still hot, if your health improves, you can be my support, after all!"

The Empress Dowager laughed softly. "Very well, very well, Auntie will drink it."

After the Empress Dowager had finished the medicine, Jiang Mi remained by the bedside until her aunt fell asleep. Only then did she leave the bedchamber.

Standing in the courtyard, she caught again the scent of osmanthus drifting on the wind, as though it could chase away every trace of the medicinal smell that clung to her.

She thought of how her aunt had no appetite while ill, and wished to make some osmanthus cakes with her own hands.

Jiang Mi asked Qing Xue where the osmanthus tree was. Qing Xue said, "Miss, the sun is quite strong right now, why not let someone below pick some blossoms and bring them back?"

"Never mind, I'd like to go and have a look myself."

Qing Xue had no choice but to direct a court lady to lead Jiang Mi there.

The osmanthus tree grew on a small slope to the south of Ci'ning Palace. It looked to be several dozen years old, its trunk thick and sturdy, its branches dense with foliage.

Clusters of tiny golden blossoms lay hidden among the green leaves.

On the grass below, many petals blown down by the wind had gathered, carpeting the ground in gold.

Jiang Mi stood beneath the tree and rose on her tiptoes to reach for the fresher branches higher up.

The court lady who had led her here, seeing this, nimbly climbed the tree and pressed a branch down low so Jiang Mi could gather the blossoms more easily.

The autumn wind stirred at random, and the skirt billowed and fluttered like a butterfly's wings, pressed close against a graceful, willowy figure. The wind shook the osmanthus tree, sending petals raining softly down. The figure beneath was like a fairy from the Moon Palace, radiant, as if startled into flight.

Along the nearby path, a party of people was hurrying this way.

The man in front stopped, and cast a glance toward the small slope.

The attendants behind him halted one by one. Cheng Zhong, the godson of Chief Eunuch Li Fu, also looked up for a moment, then lowered his eyes, thinking privately: Gracious, which family's young miss is this? What a clever way to make an impression. Surely she has not been waiting here all along?

But if she had been waiting here, why was she not turning around?

Cheng Zhong, his godfather having just suffered thirty strokes and still unable to get up, followed close at his master's side, trying to gauge his master's thoughts. He hesitated, wondering whether to call out to the young miss to come and pay her respects.

But he saw that his master had already withdrawn his gaze and continued walking ahead.

......

Jiang Mi placed the flower branches she had gathered into the basket. Seeing she had enough, she prepared to leave.

She had only taken a few steps when she saw Qing Xue running toward her, breathless. "Miss, His Majesty has come! You must hurry back."

The smile vanished from Jiang Mi's face. Instinctively she stepped back.

Qing Xue took the flower basket from her hands and urged her on. "Miss, quickly now, the Empress Dowager has asked you to go serve tea."

The walk back to the bedchamber seemed both endless and all too brief.

Jiang Mi struggled with herself the whole way, knowing well she could not avoid seeing him.

The tea set was pressed into her hands by Qing Xue. With no other option, Jiang Mi stepped reluctantly into the bedchamber.

Through the screen, she could dimly make out a tall figure seated at the bedside, speaking.

"An urgent report came in from the south, a great rain fell for three days and three nights without stopping, and a dike has burst... The floodwaters swept through all of Yuzhou. I discussed the matter with the ministers of the Grand Secretariat, the Ministry of Works, and the Ministry of Revenue, we have settled on who to dispatch and how much silver to release for disaster relief. The delay kept me; I came to pay my respects to Mother Empress only now..."

The voice was clear and resonant as the sound of jade, the tone warm and gentle as a soft breeze.

"It is no matter at all, this is an old ailment of mine. The flood in Yuzhou is far more pressing. His Majesty's love for his people is a blessing to the realm." The Empress Dowager's voice sounded markedly more spirited than before.

Jiang Mi lightened her footsteps, carried the tea bowl around the screen, bowed her head, and sank into a curtsy. "Long life and peace to Your Majesty."

"No need for ceremony," Xiao Huaiyan said in a mild voice.

A voice like a spring rain, and yet it sent cold sweat prickling across Jiang Mi's back.

Once, that voice had given her commands in tones of cold indifference. "Take everything off."

No matter how she wept and begged him to spare her some dignity, he had been utterly unmoved.

Those unbearable nights, one after another, filling her with dread, with a racing heart...

"Tangtang, why are you standing there in a daze? Go and offer the tea to His Majesty." The Empress Dowager, seeing Jiang Mi motionless, called out to remind her.

Jiang Mi snapped back to herself. Holding her breath, she stepped forward, and with stiff, rigid movements, extended the tea bowl. "Your Majesty... please have some tea."

A voice like a songbird's trill, a face like a blooming lotus, a figure like a slender willow, and as she drew near, a faint trace of osmanthus fragrance drifted past.

Xiao Huaiyan leaned forward slightly and raised his hand to take the cup.

In the instant his fingers brushed against her slender ones, he felt the other person recoil sharply. Had he not already taken hold of the tea bowl, it would surely have shattered on the floor.

Xiao Huaiyan's expression did not change. A smile played at the corner of his lips. He turned the lid of the bowl idly and drank a slow sip. "Excellent tea," he remarked.

The Empress Dowager had noticed nothing amiss. She beckoned Jiang Mi to the bedside and smiled, saying, "I'm glad His Majesty likes it. This is a Da Hong Pao that came as tribute from Jiangnan last year. I have a little left, and I will have Tangtang find it for His Majesty to take back."

Xiao Huaiyan did not decline. "My thanks to Mother Empress."

The Empress Dowager was greatly heartened to see the Emperor accept her gift.

Jiang Mi received the Empress Dowager's instructions and felt as if she had been given a reprieve. She could have wished to step away and find the tea leaves at once.

She had barely moved her feet when she heard the man say, "Miss Jiang, a moment."

"What is Your Majesty's command?" Jiang Mi's voice went tight, her whole body tense.

chapters.title: Chapter 4
chapters.content:
"Was it under Mother Empress's order that Miss Jiang came to bring us the sobering soup last night?" Xiao Huaiyan's tone was even, giving no indication of whether he was pleased or displeased.

Jiang Mi did not know what he meant by this. Was it a reprimand? Or something else?

She bit her lip, held her breath, and answered, "It was. This one was presumptuous in disturbing Your Majesty, and begs forgiveness."

"It was I who was worried about His Majesty's health and lost my sense of propriety, that is why I sent this child barging in so thoughtlessly. I implore Your Majesty to pardon her." The Empress Dowager had assumed his display of punishment yesterday had closed the matter. Why was he bringing it up again of his own accord?

Xiao Huaiyan turned the jade thumb ring on his finger and smiled. "Mother Empress need not worry. How could I fault Miss Jiang? I ought to reward her."

This astonished both the Empress Dowager and Jiang Mi.

For the Empress Dowager, it was a welcome surprise.

For Jiang Mi, it was a shock that set her heart pounding.

What on earth did he intend?

Xiao Huaiyan continued, his tone unhurried, "Thanks to the timely medicine Miss Jiang delivered, it eased my symptoms and allowed me to attend the morning court session without disruption. As for rewarding Miss Jiang, ordinary gold and silver gifts might seem too commonplace for someone of her station."

He paused briefly, then added, "It so happens that recently I received a Emei Spring Orchid. I will bestow it upon Miss Jiang."

An Emei Spring Orchid?

In an instant, something flashed through Jiang Mi's mind, too quick for her to catch.

The Empress Dowager was delighted upon hearing this. "That is a rare treasure indeed! Tangtang, express your gratitude at once."

"Th, thank you for Your Majesty's grace." Jiang Mi's heart was in turmoil; even the hand gripping her handkerchief was trembling.

Xiao Huaiyan rose to his feet. "I have already asked the Director of the Imperial Academy of Medicine to come call again and take Mother Empress's pulse. Mother Empress, please rest and recover well. The matter of relocating the consorts still requires your attention."

The Empress Dowager, cheered by his visit and his kind words, felt her low spirits lift and her illness half-cured. She nodded in acceptance. "This is just my old ailment, a little medicine and rest and it will mend itself. There is no need to trouble the Director again. Earlier, Tangtang even complained that the medicine was too bitter and went to pick osmanthus blossoms specially to make me osmanthus cakes. Your Majesty need not worry, with this dear child looking after me so attentively, I will be well again before long."

Xiao Huaiyan glanced toward Jiang Mi. The Jiang Mi who in the past had always blushed and stolen glances at him now showed no delight at receiving the reward, and appeared somewhat distracted, head lowered, her mind seemingly elsewhere.

Xiao Huaiyan, who had been about to leave, paused. "Is that so? I did not know that Miss Jiang could also make osmanthus cakes."

The Empress Dowager had deliberately praised Jiang Mi in front of Xiao Huaiyan, and seeing him pick up on the remark, her smile deepened.

The Empress Dowager gave Jiang Mi a meaningful look, urging her to respond, but the child showed none of her usual quickness. The Empress Dowager had to answer on her behalf: "Tangtang's osmanthus cakes are soft and light, not cloying, with a faint floral fragrance. If His Majesty is not too busy..." She was hoping to seize the chance to keep the Emperor for the evening meal.

Xiao Huaiyan said with a note of regret, "It seems I am not so fortunate. There are still memorials piled up in the Imperial Study that I have yet to read through, I really must be going."

Jiang Mi, who had been sitting as if on pins and needles, finally heard the words that set her free. She raised her eyes ever so slightly, and her gaze landed on the man's long, elegant hands. She saw him turn the jade thumb ring on his finger once more.

That was his small habit when he was impatient, something she had only noticed after three years of living alongside him in the palace.

Jiang Mi's lips curved in a faint, sardonic smile. He found this tiresome? She was the one suffering.

The Empress Dowager could not press him to stay any further, and only said, "If His Majesty does not mind, tomorrow I will have Tangtang prepare a fresh batch of osmanthus cakes and send them over."

Xiao Huaiyan caught a glimpse from the corner of his eye of the young woman starting, those almond-shaped eyes suddenly went wide, rather like a startled little rabbit in the hills.

He lifted his lips in a smile. "Then I shall trouble Miss Jiang."

......

After the Emperor departed, it was not long before a young eunuch from Qianqing Palace arrived, delivering the gift.

Even though she had held onto a sliver of hope, Jiang Mi recognized it the moment she saw it, the very pot of orchids into which she had poured half a bowl of sobering soup that night at Wangyun Pavilion.

So this was the Emei Spring Orchid? She had thought at the time it was just an ordinary orchid.

The plant looked rather worse for wear, several leaves had turned yellow and listless, and the buds that had been about to bloom had shriveled up.

Whether it could survive at all was uncertain.

If an imperial gift withered in her hands, that would be a grave act of disrespect to the throne.

This was no gift, it was plainly a form of punishment.

What did he know? Or what was he testing?

Had he woken afterward and noticed something amiss? Or had he perhaps never truly been drunk at all last night?

Jiang Mi couldn't help but shudder. She dared not let her thoughts go further.

This gift, hot as a burning coal, had to be carried back to the warm pavilion for now.

Fortunately, during those few years in the palace she had tended flowers out of boredom, and knew something of what to do.

She had Qiu Yu smash open the pot and examined the soil, it was far too damp. When she turned over the roots of the orchid, they had gone black, clearly damaged from overwatering.

This orchid truly was as delicate as its reputation, half a bowl of medicinal soup had reduced it to this state.

It was said that the Emei Spring Orchid bloomed in March and April, yet this plant already had buds, a sign that whoever had offered it as tribute had put considerable thought and effort into its care.

Jiang Mi took a pair of scissors and trimmed away the blackened roots, then repotted it in fresh soil. All she could do now was leave it to fate, dead horse, living doctor.

......

By the time she had finished all of this, the evening was coming on. Jiang Mi washed her hands, changed into fresh clothes, and went to keep the Empress Dowager company for supper.

The Empress Dowager was in good spirits and ate nearly half a bowl more of the jade-millet porridge than usual.

As Jiang Mi was about to retire for the night, the Empress Dowager caught her hand and did not forget to remind her: "Tangtang, when you go to deliver the osmanthus cakes tomorrow, remember to find an opportunity to stay on in Qianqing Palace, spend a little more time in the Emperor's company."

Jiang Mi tossed and turned all that night, unable to sleep.

She opened her eyes, climbed down from the bed, draped a pale moon-white outer robe over her shoulders, pushed the window open, and stood before it, gazing up at the full moon.

In the still night, a bird called out now and then. The night wind carried a faint fragrance of flowers.

She did not know how long she stood there before Jiang Mi let out a sigh and closed the window again.

......

The following morning, Jiang Mi had barely stepped out when Cui Mama came over with a warm smile, telling her that all the ingredients for the osmanthus cakes had been laid out and she only needed to go over in person.

Jiang Mi followed Cui Mama to the small kitchen, where she found the ingredients neatly arranged and several kitchen maids and court ladies standing off to one side.

She turned to Cui Mama. "Mama, I don't like having too many people about when I make pastries. Please have them all step down for now."

Cui Mama hesitated. "Does the young miss truly not need them to help?" In her experience, many young ladies from distinguished families who claimed to "cook themselves" merely directed servants with their words, never lifting a finger. Did their Third Miss truly know how to make pastries? Yesterday, the Empress Dowager had woken and not found the Third Miss, and asked Qing Xue, who said the young miss had gone to pick osmanthus blossoms to make the Empress Dowager osmanthus cakes. The Empress Dowager had been pleased for quite a while, saying it was the Third Miss's filial care that was most precious.

Jiang Mi shook her head. She kept only Qiu Yu, and instructed her to bring a basin of clean water.

She rolled up her sleeves and submerged her slender, pale hands in the water.

Only after a quarter of an hour did she take them out, dry them carefully with a cloth, and then reach for the ingredients.

Qiu Yu stood watching as her young mistress's fingers shaped the dough into blossoms, her hands moving in the morning light like a dance, light and shadow intertwining, beautiful beyond description.

Jiang Mi was preparing two batches today: one to leave for the Empress Dowager, and one to send to Qianqing Palace.

When it came to adjusting the proportions of the ingredients, one batch was made just right. The other, well, there was a small slip of the hand. That one was just going through the motions; no one was expected to eat it, so a little carelessness was perfectly fine.

When the cakes were steamed, a clean, delicate osmanthus fragrance wafted forth. The cakes were translucent and lovely, just looking at them was enough to whet the appetite.

Jiang Mi packed the ones meant for Qianqing Palace into the food box. The other batch she had Cui Mama send to the Empress Dowager.

Unhurried, Jiang Mi followed a court lady assigned to show her the way to Qianqing Palace. The young attendants at the gate, seeing her party arrive, sent someone inside at once to announce her.

Before long, a round-faced little eunuch with a beaming smile came running out. He bowed to Jiang Mi. "This one pays his respects to Miss Jiang!"

Jiang Mi recognized him, he was Cheng Zhong, godson of the chief eunuch Li Fu, and in later years a favorite close attendant of the Emperor.

"Young Eunuch Cheng need not stand on ceremony," Jiang Mi said.

Cheng Zhong's face flushed even redder, and he stumbled over his words a little. "Miss Jiang is too kind, please, just call this one Little Chengzi."

Jiang Mi smiled and held out the food box. "By the Empress Dowager's commission, I've brought pastries for His Majesty. I ask that the young eunuch deliver them inside."

Cheng Zhong knew of this, he had been outside yesterday when he heard His Majesty ask Miss Jiang to send over some pastries.

So he accepted the box.

"I thank the young eunuch for the trouble," Jiang Mi said. "I will take my leave now."

And with that, she turned and walked away.

Cheng Zhong opened his mouth to call out to her, he had come out to say that His Majesty was not in, having gone to the training ground, and there was no knowing when he would return.

But Miss Jiang had not even asked whether His Majesty was in.

He had not managed to say a word before she walked away without a backward glance.

......

Half an hour passed. Footsteps sounded from outside the hall, and a bright, hearty laugh rang out: "There's nothing like a good match with His Majesty! I haven't moved my muscles like this in a long while. Since coming back from Yunzhou to the capital, if I kept up not doing anything, I'd have rusted through entirely."

"Young Master Gu had better let that injury of his heal properly before coming back," another voice teased. "Otherwise, you won't last even a few rounds against His Majesty."

Xiao Huaiyan stepped into the main hall first. He was dressed in a black robe embroidered with dragon patterns, his figure tall and straight. His features were handsome, and having just come from the training grounds, his brow carried a hint of keen, martial authority.

Behind him followed the Third Young Master of the Zhen Guo Duke's household, Xue Jingyuan, and the Second Young Master of the Loyal and Brave Marquis's household, Gu Chang.

Xue and Gu had both followed him from the days in Yunzhou, and shared a deep personal friendship with him.

Gu Chang was not bothered by Xue Jingyuan's mockery. He rubbed his shoulder and challenged back, "Xue San, don't just run your mouth. Come and try a few rounds yourself if you've got the nerve, or wait for the autumn hunt at the very least. We'll have a competition then!"

As he spoke, Gu Chang noticed a plate of exquisite, translucent cakes on the table. He picked one up and shoved it straight into his mouth.

Cheng Zhong, who was just leading the court ladies inside to serve tea, saw this and hesitated, casting a glance toward His Majesty. But His Majesty was talking to Lord Xue and paying no attention to Young General Gu's barbaric consumption of Miss Jiang's osmanthus cakes.

Just as Cheng Zhong was about to inform the Emperor of Miss Jiang's delivery, Young General Gu suddenly spat out a mouthful of cake with great force, snatched up the tea bowl beside him, and gulped it down in several frantic swallows.

"Sweet enough to choke a person to death! Did the imperial kitchen use straight sugar syrup? Greasy, cloying, sickening, how can anything taste this foul..." Gu Chang was beside himself with revulsion. He had come down from the training ground somewhat hungry, and seeing the pretty little cakes, had popped three into his mouth at once without thinking. He had eaten too fast, and very nearly choked.

Xiao Huaiyan's cool, clear gaze moved to Cheng Zhong. "Where did this come from?"

Cheng Zhong broke into a cold sweat. It turned out His Majesty had completely forgotten about the osmanthus cakes.

Cheng Zhong dropped to his knees. "In reply to Your Majesty, these were sent by Miss Jiang."

He quickly added, "Yesterday, did Your Majesty not say to have Miss Jiang send a batch of osmanthus cakes? This servant only dared to accept them on that account." These past days, no shortage of gifts had been sent to Qianqing Palace. Without His Majesty's prior approval, how could they have dared receive anything and bring it inside?

Xiao Huaiyan raised an eyebrow and told Cheng Zhong to bring the items over.

He picked up one of the flower-shaped cakes, small and translucent, with a faint trace of osmanthus fragrance. He asked, "How long did she wait outside?"

Cheng Zhong answered respectfully, "Miss Jiang conducted herself very properly. She handed the items to this servant and left."

Xiao Huaiyan tossed the little thing back onto the dish and smiled. "Oh? Is that so?"

chapters.title: Chapter 5
chapters.content:
In Changchun Palace, the sound of a qin drifted through the air, sometimes lilting, sometimes soaring. The player had put both skill and feeling into every note.

"Again!" The Virtuous Consort cut in.

Xie Mingshan, seated at the pavilion plucking the strings, startled with anxiety. "Yes, Honored Aunt."

Xie Mingshan closed her eyes, steadied her breath, and set her hands to the strings once more.

She had barely played a few lines when the Virtuous Consort set her teacup down on the table with a furrowed brow. Xie Mingshan, nerves fraying, struck a wrong note.

This time, without waiting for the Virtuous Consort to say a word, Xie Mingshan stopped herself.

Her face flushed red, she apologized in a small voice, "Honored, Honored Aunt, let me start over."

"There's no need. That's enough for today. Ashan, your heart is in disorder. No matter how many times you play, the music will be disordered as well." The Virtuous Consort's tone was neither harsh nor gentle, yet all who heard it could detect the disappointment beneath.

Xie Mingshan scrambled to her feet and knelt before the Virtuous Consort to beg forgiveness. "Noble Consort, please give Ashan another chance. Ashan will practice properly and will not disappoint Honored Aunt."

The Virtuous Consort looked at the frightened Xie Mingshan and asked, "Ashan, in the state of mind you're in now, do you think the music you play will make an impression at the Imperial Birthday Banquet?"

Xie Mingshan dared not answer. She herself had no confidence.

The Virtuous Consort shook her head. "The moment you hear the Emperor has given something to another, your composure falls apart. Even if you entered the palace and became Empress, you'd still have a long road ahead. A Empress must arrange the ladies' rotations to attend the Emperor, look after the favored consorts, and care for the children they bear. Can you contain yourself, can you endure all that? Right now, a small gift to another person is enough to unsettle you. The discomforts that lie ahead will only be greater still."

Xie Mingshan wept silently, saying nothing. She could not refute her aunt's words. She admitted it, she was jealous. She had spent the entire night wondering why the Emperor had given Jiang Mi that Emei Spring Orchid. Was it praise for her noble character? Had she caught his eye?

"When the late Emperor showed favor to the Imperial Noble Consort, the Honored Consort, and the Beautiful Consort in turn, even the Empress and I were forced to retreat a step and avoid their glory. When the sons of those three consorts competed for the throne, who could have imagined it would be today's Emperor who won? Look now at the fate of those consorts who once held such high positions, some died, some went mad. How many of them ended their days in peaceful enjoyment as a consort dowager? That's right, I want you to enter the palace and compete for favor. But what you are ultimately competing for is an heir. It is an heir that will let you have the last laugh. If a worthless flower pot can rob you of sleep and appetite, then Ashan, your temperament still needs a great deal of cultivating."

Princess Anyang came rushing in. Seeing her cousin's red-rimmed, tear-streaked face, she couldn't help but say, "Mother, stop scolding Cousin Ashan. I just heard that Jiang Mi dolled herself up and went to deliver some cakes to Imperial Brother, and they actually let her in. Those dog-servants in Qianqing Palace, when Cousin Ashan went to deliver soup, they kept her waiting outside for ages, and in the end she couldn't even get it through the door."

Xie Mingshan's face went even more crestfallen.

"Silence!" The Virtuous Consort pinched her brow, held herself in check, and said in a suppressed voice: "Anyang, take Ashan outside for a walk first."

Seeing her mother's displeased expression, Anyang did not dare say another word. She took Xie Mingshan by the arm and led her out.

The Mama attending beside them refilled the Virtuous Consort's tea and offered comfort: "Noble Consort, the Princess is still young, she can be taught in time. Miss Ashan is simply too eager. Once she thinks it through, she will understand your deeper intentions."

The Virtuous Consort said, "Anyang is already fifteen and still speaks without the least restraint, not knowing her place in the world, going around judging the Emperor's actions like that. I need to find her a husband soon, someone who can keep her in line. As for Ashan..."

The Virtuous Consort smiled. "She is still too young. What does it matter if Jiang Mi has her moment of glory for a while? There's no hurry..."

......

When Jiang Mi returned to Ci'ning Palace, Cui Mama hurried over to meet her, puzzled as to why the Third Miss had come back already.

"Mama, has Auntie risen yet?" Jiang Mi asked.

Cui Mama smiled and nodded. "Not even a quarter of an hour after you left, the Empress Dowager was up. Her color is good today, she drank nearly half a bowl of porridge and ate the osmanthus cakes you made, Young Miss. The Empress Dowager was very pleased; she praised your skill and couldn't stop after eating four pieces. The servants were worried that Her Highness is still too frail and might not digest well, so they persuaded her to stop. She's out in the courtyard now, taking in the sun. Go to her quickly, Young Miss."

Beneath the century-old ginkgo tree, beside the stone table, a nanmu wood divan had been set out. On the table sat clusters of grapes and melon slices. The Empress Dowager lay half-reclined against the divan, eyes closed in rest, a brocade coverlet draped over her, as several court ladies attended nearby.

As if she had heard a sound, the Empress Dowager opened her eyes and saw Jiang Mi.

She reached out a hand toward Jiang Mi, her eyes full of warmth. "Did you see the Emperor?"

Jiang Mi took that well-tended hand in hers and gave a gentle shake of her head. "His Majesty was occupied with affairs of state and did not call for me."

She produced the ready-made excuse, and without anyone going to Qianqing Palace to verify it, she offered it with a perfectly clear conscience.

The Empress Dowager had her sit beside her. "That's within expectation, after all. Still, you've left an impression on the Emperor now, and that is a good thing."

She patted Jiang Mi's hand. "I was too hasty."

A gentle breeze drifted past. The warm autumn sun was a pleasure to sit in.

Jiang Mi leaned against the Empress Dowager's shoulder the way she had done as a child. "What if he doesn't like me?" she said softly.

The Empress Dowager laughed and, seeing Jiang Mi's childlike manner, reached out and tapped her forehead gently. "Silly girl, then you find ways to make him like you."

"But affection that's been coaxed out isn't real affection..." The words came out barely above a breath, heard only by herself.

She had tried. In her previous life, she had worked herself ragged trying to win his favor, thrown caution to the wind to court him, but what she received in return was humiliation and mockery.

That was not love.

The Empress Dowager took her reaction for disappointment over not having seen the Emperor, and gave no weight to these little murmurings.

The Empress Dowager glanced at Qing Xue. "Go fetch that thing."

A moment later, Qing Xue came back carrying a bamboo basket, covered with a layer of soft velvet.

The Empress Dowager gave Jiang Mi a gentle push. "Go and see if you like it!"

Jiang Mi rose curiously and lifted the velvet, and there, nestled inside, lay a little cat, its fur thick and snowy white, perhaps two or three months old. Its eyes were deep as sapphires, its expression innocent and dazed. It retreated on its four tiny legs, lost its balance, and tumbled into a heap, letting out a timid little mew at Jiang Mi.

The sound went straight to Jiang Mi's heart.

She gathered the little cat tenderly from the basket and cradled it, stroking it gently.

Only when she heard the Empress Dowager's laughter did she come back to herself. "Many thanks to Auntie. I love it, I love it very much." The last two words she said with extra emphasis.

"I'm glad you like it. Tangtang has been such a good girl these past days. This little one is Auntie's reward for you."

......

As Jiang Mi carried the little cat back to the warm pavilion, her aunt's words echoed in her mind.

Obedience brought rewards, so what would happen if she stopped obeying?

She had lost her mother not long after she was born. She was brought to be raised at her grandmother's knee.

Four years after her mother's passing, her father had remarried, a daughter of a great merchant family from Jiangnan. Her grandmother had looked down on the woman for her merchant origins and had never given her a warm welcome.

That stepmother had tiptoed around everything, fearful of neglecting her, yet not daring to grow too close.

Her father, being a man, had little contact with her.

Her grandmother was growing old after all, and her care for Jiang Mi had its limits, mostly it had been the mamas and maidservants who brought her up.

The times that made her happiest were when her aunt would occasionally invite her into the palace.

Her aunt would give her all manner of rare trinkets, endless jewelry and fine clothes. She would read poems to her, and hold her for afternoon naps.

Though there were only a handful of such visits, they had given Jiang Mi a dependence on her aunt that was close to the bond with a mother.

She had always listened to her aunt obediently. To earn her aunt's praise, she had worked diligently at the qin, chess, calligraphy, and painting. Her aunt had even sent someone to teach her dancing, dances that were bold and daring, and though she had not fully understood them, she had mastered them to perfection out of shy determination.

All of it, to make her aunt happy.

Yet the thing she was planning to do would ultimately mean defying her aunt.

In the moment Jiang Mi's mind drifted, the little troublemaker she had brought back had already climbed up onto the windowsill and was stretching a tiny paw toward the Emei Spring Orchid.

"Oh no!" Jiang Mi quickly scooped it up. "You little rascal, don't touch that."

The orchid was barely clinging to life as it was. If this little creature took a few more swipes at it, it truly would be beyond saving.

Jiang Mi glanced over, still drooping, still listless, but at least still alive.

Jiang Mi grabbed the little creature's soft paws and gave them a poke. "Let's see if you dare go scratching at things again."

The little thing just stared blankly, making no move to dodge. It went limp in Jiang Mi's lap, rolled over, and began licking its own fur.

Seeing that the scolding had produced no effect whatsoever, Jiang Mi could only stroke its fluffy coat and squeeze its tiny paws. "You really are like a little cotton ball. I'll call you Mian Mian from now on."

......

After keeping the Empress Dowager company for the evening meal, Jiang Mi couldn't wait to get back to the warm pavilion to check on Mian Mian.

Before she even reached the door, she saw Qiu Yu come rushing out in a panic.

"What is it?" Jiang Mi asked.

Qiu Yu caught her breath and said anxiously, "Miss, the little cat is gone. I only went out for a moment to fetch water, and when I came back, I searched the whole room and couldn't find it anywhere. It must have slipped out through the window. I was just about to go look."

"Don't panic. Get a few court ladies to help search. Don't disturb Auntie's side for now. Mian Mian is still so small, it can't have gone far."

"Yes, Miss." Qiu Yu called over two young court ladies attending in the warm pavilion. Carrying lanterns, they went out to search.

Jiang Mi couldn't sit still. Thinking of how tiny Mian Mian was, she was full of worry and went out to search as well.

"Mian Mian... here, kitty kitty... come out..." Jiang Mi parted the dense grass with a branch and peered in. Nothing.

Jiang Mi changed direction and kept looking.

Without noticing it, she had wandered farther and farther from where she started. The palace grounds were complex, and taking one wrong turn could send you off in an entirely different direction.

The further Jiang Mi walked, the more uneasy she felt. The trees here were somehow denser than before. In the shifting shadows of branches swaying in the wind, a rustling unease crept into her heart. She was hesitating over whether to retrace her steps when, all at once, she heard a faint, tiny cat cry.

Jiang Mi looked intently ahead. Crouching in the undergrowth was a small white shape, shifting slightly before going still again.

Jiang Mi, forgetting her fear, lifted her skirts and ran toward it. It really was her Mian Mian!

She called out softly a couple of times, but the little cat didn't come to her.

She bent down to pick it up, and discovered its leg had become tangled in a vine, leaving it unable to free itself.

Jiang Mi abandoned all decorum, crouching right on the ground to untangle the vine from the small creature. She tapped its tiny nose in mild reproach: "Hurt now, aren't you? See if you go running off again."

The little cat stretched out its tongue and licked her finger.

Jiang Mi was just about to scoop it up and stand when a faint, thin sound of weeping suddenly reached her from across the way, giving her such a fright that her legs went weak and she stumbled back into the grass.

For a moment, her mind filled with some of the more frightening tales that circulated in the palace. She swallowed hard and told herself to stay calm.

In a few moments, alongside the crying, the sound of voices became audible, which brought a measure of warmth back to her.

It must be a court lady passing by, or something of the sort.

Curious, she cautiously peered out, and saw a woman dressed in court robes weeping softly, kneeling before a man. Jiang Mi's gaze moved upward to the man's face, and what she saw made her freeze, the breath stopping in her lungs, a chill racing up her spine.

"I beg Your Majesty to show mercy..."

The woman moved forward on her knees until she reached the man's legs. The collar of her court robes had fallen open, exposing snow-white shoulders. In a voice choked with grief, she said: "This concubine is not yet seventeen, and has not yet been granted favor. I do not wish to waste away my youth in the detached palace, I beg Your Majesty to show mercy..."

The man's voice was faintly cool. "Has the Consort called out to the wrong person?"

The woman startled.

"To whom does the Consort belong? Surely she has not grown confused? Although our dynasty has already abolished the practice of having consorts follow the deceased Emperor in death, if the Consort wishes so earnestly to be shown mercy by the late Emperor, then We shall grant the Consort's wish and let her be reunited with the late Emperor."

The man's voice was slow and gentle, yet the woman began to tremble as if caught in a sieve.

She shook her head frantically. "No, no, no, no, that is not what I meant. Your Majesty, Your Majesty..." Had they not all said the Emperor was of a gentle nature, that such a person was easily moved to pity? That she might even receive his grace and continue to hold her title as a consort, why, why was it turning out like this...

The woman was still trying to plead when attendants clapped a hand over her mouth and dragged her away.

Jiang Mi, too, pressed her own hand tightly over her mouth, terrified of being discovered.

What she could never have anticipated, though, at the very moment those people were about to leave, Mian Mian, cradled in her arms, let out a sudden, clear "Mew."

chapters.title: Six
chapters.content:
Xiao Huaiyan looked toward the shadow.

From that direction came another faint sound: "Meow."

Chengzhong bowed and said, "Your Majesty, it sounds like a cat. This time of year, there may be some strays that haven't been chased away yet."

Xiao Huaiyan fixed his gaze on the spot and said coldly, "Come out!"

The moment his words fell, the Embroidered Uniform Guards behind him drew their Xiuchun blades and advanced toward the shadow.

Jiang Mi saw the gleaming swords in the guards' hands and no longer dared to hide.

She hunched over and crawled out of the bushes, looking utterly disheveled.

Chengzhong stepped forward and raised his lantern to illuminate her. When he saw who it was, he exclaimed in surprise, "Miss Jiang, it's you!"

Jiang Mi desperately wished it weren't her! How could she be this unlucky?

She didn't dare look at the man, so she curtsied in the direction where he stood. "This subject's daughter pays her respects to His Majesty. Long live the Emperor."

Xiao Huaiyan clasped his hands behind his back and walked toward Jiang Mi.

With each step he took closer, Jiang Mi couldn't help but want to step back. She forced herself to endure, remaining rooted to the spot despite her trembling.

Xiao Huaiyan stopped, his gaze appraising the person before him.

Her face was strikingly beautiful, flushed with a trace of panic. Her almond-shaped eyes glistened with a watery shimmer, and her small, delicate lips were pressed together with unease. She clutched a snow-white cat against her slender chest, her posture swaying slightly without conscious thought. Bathed in the cold moonlight, she looked especially pitiful and lovely.

Xiao Huaiyan unconsciously rubbed his index finger together. He extended his hand.

Jiang Mi startled and instinctively tried to retreat—but she saw that those slender fingers were reaching toward Mianmian, the cat in her arms, and she stopped herself.

Xiao Huaiyan hooked the cat under the chin and played with its fluffy fur, while his long, slanted phoenix eyes looked down at Jiang Mi. "Make that sound again."

Jiang Mi froze. How did he know the meowing earlier was her imitating Mianmian?

His dismissive tone conjured the same dread she had felt in her past life when he punished her. She bit down hard on her lip, refusing to be manipulated by him.

"Meow~~"

Mianmian, who was being stroked quite pleasantly, let out a soft, languorous cry.

Jiang Mi went rigid.

Xiao Huaiyan laughed and patted Mianmian on the head. "What a well-behaved little creature."

Jiang Mi: ?

Had she misunderstood?

Jiang Mi looked up, and her gaze collided with those starlit eyes brimming with laughter.

She quickly looked away. Knowing his wicked nature, how could she have misunderstood? He had done that on purpose!

"Why is Miss Jiang here?" Xiao Huaiyan asked.
