"Do as I say. Be careful and don't let anyone find out — that's all." Qin Ruyin stretched lazily, but her tone was firm and left no room for argument.

"Zisu, you're my person. Of course I trust you — that's why I'm asking you to handle something this important. This is for our household's sake, and for my own sake."

"Of course, for those of you who serve me, it's for your benefit too. You should know — the Dowager and Second Uncle are using my marriage to further their own ambitions. That benefits the Qin family and the second branch, yes, but it is not good for me. We can't just obediently let them treat me like a lamb to the slaughter."

"Yes!" Zisu nodded hard. "I'll do whatever Miss says!"

Over these past few days, she had already seen that her young mistress was far from the passive, undemanding girl outsiders thought her to be. Inside that gentle manner was a person with a plan.

And she was genuinely glad of it!

Who wanted to serve a mistress who let the world walk over her without a word?

In the end, didn't the ones who suffered most tend to be the people serving at her side?

A mistress with her own strategy — that meant the people around her would have good days ahead.

"Don't worry, Miss. My brother is meticulous — no one will find out where you're going."

Zisu thought for a moment and added that much.

"Give your brother twenty taels of silver as well," Qin Ruyin said immediately. "Don't be stingy when there are arrangements to make outside."

"Whatever is left over is his — consider it a bonus. And once it's all done, there'll be more from me."

Zisu quickly protested: "Miss, that's not necessary — the wages you pay my brother are already very generous, and he's not greedy..."

She meant every word of it.

"When your master gives a reward, you take it." Qin Ruyin smiled.

No one in the world has ever felt silver was burning a hole in their pocket.

Zisu complied, her manner growing noticeably more respectful.

The Miss had always been generous. It was just that, before, the Miss was such a quiet and soft-spoken young lady that there had rarely been any occasion for her servants to earn a reward.

Zisu curtsied and withdrew.

Qin Ruyin watched her go, thoughtful.

This girl was clever and capable, and her mouth was sealed tight.

There was just one thing — she was perhaps a little too devoted to Lady Li.

The current Qin Ruyin was not the original, easygoing girl everyone had known. She acted differently.

Some things she did would probably feel like the sky was falling to her mother.

Lady Li had grown up as the pampered youngest daughter of a minor official family. Now past forty, she was famously protective of her children — but she didn't have the stomach for real storms.

She didn't want her mother worrying about her constantly.

Zisu would need some gentle reshaping.

If she truly intended to coast comfortably through her days, she needed as many capable people around her as possible.

She rubbed at her tired eyes and picked up the neatly folded paper on the table beside her.

It was what Lord Qin's long-time attendant Uncle Ning had brought to her that morning, sent by her father.

On it was a record of the dowry her father had put together for her.

When she finished reading it, Qin Ruyin was stunned.

By now she had a general sense of the basic costs of goods in the Great Ying Dynasty, and had also roughly estimated the value of the Qin family's collective assets.

The private dowry her father had prepared for her was extraordinarily generous.

There were two farmland estates totaling five hundred mu of good agricultural land.

Three shops in the county town on the outskirts of the capital.

Three thousand taels of silver for her trousseau chest.

And several antiques and paintings besides...

Lady Li had mentioned in passing that the first branch had never been wealthy. With so many children to raise and marry off, the savings had already been largely spent on weddings.

There had been some supplement from the family's public funds. Of course, to save face, the Dowager would grit her teeth and contribute something as well.

Qin Ruyin thought it over, then went to her father's study.

Today was one of his rest days.

Lord Qin had not expected his youngest daughter to come looking for him in the study.

Qin Ruyin got straight to the point and asked him about the dowry he had put together for her.

Lord Qin looked at her and smiled.

"Your two elder brothers and both elder sisters have the same — but you're the only one who came to ask your father about it."

"Why did you have to come and ask?"

Qin Ruyin called out "Father" in her most winning tone.

After all, she was just a teenage girl right now. A little bit of wheedling was perfectly reasonable.

"I know the first branch's circumstances, Father. The dowry you've put together for me is so generous — I was worried about you."

Her father was an official.

If the money hadn't come from proper channels...

She didn't dare think further down that path.

Lord Qin watched his youngest daughter with quiet amusement.

He looked at her for a long moment.

Such a little fool.

"Don't worry — everything here was earned through legitimate business dealings managed privately through Uncle Ning. There is nothing improper about any of it."

Before Qin Ruyin could react with surprise, Lord Qin said quietly, "Our first branch has been suppressed by your grandmother and her sons for years. We endured, yes — but you can't endure if you have no fallback position at all."

"Uncle Ning comes from a family of merchants, though fate scattered them. I helped him when he was young, and he has stayed at my side ever since, refusing to leave. Later, by a happy accident, I helped him find his family again — but he had already made an oath to repay me, and he wouldn't go. He has been my personal attendant all these years... but his instincts for business are extraordinary."

Qin Ruyin blinked.

So the family was not as precarious as she had imagined. That was a relief.

And Uncle Ning sounded like a man of rare character.

He also had a talent for business... and connections. She would have to find occasions to interact with Uncle Ning.

Seeing his youngest daughter go blank again, Lord Qin smiled to himself.

Qin Ruyin came back to herself. "Father, Uncle Ning has done so much for our family!"

Lord Qin said, moved, "He truly has. These years, I'm grateful we had him — or the first branch's life would have been even harder on the surface, and I would have truly done wrong by your mother and all of you."

"Does Mother know? And Elder Brother and the others?"

Lord Qin smiled and shook his head, then nodded.

"Your elder brother and sisters all know. I've kept it from your mother — and since you've never paid attention to these things, today is the first time you've come to ask."

"Oh..."

So even Little Brother, only thirteen years old, already knew there was a family business generating income.

The original Qin Ruyin truly had been the most carefree and unbothered member of the household.

"No matter — you take after your mother, the two of you, youngest daughters both. A little unworldly." Lord Qin sighed, though his eyes held a glimmer of guilt.

All these years, he had let his wife follow him into hardship. That was not right.

"Father?" Qin Ruyin was unsure what had caused the shift in his expression and looked at him with concern.

Lord Qin shook himself, smiled. "Now that you know to come and ask, your father is reassured."

He looked at her with warmth. "Our Ruyin has grown up."

"This is a private gift from Father to Ruyin — keep it close to your heart."

"There will be another portion in the formal dowry list as well..."

Qin Ruyin waved her hands frantically. "Father, this is already so much! Keep the rest for yourself — you need money for your own affairs at court!"

Lord Qin shook his head. "Father knows you are filial and sensible. But the Jiang family has been a great noble house for several hundred years — you cannot arrive with too modest a dowry!"

"A parent's gift is not to be declined. Listen to your father, Ruyin."

"Father — there's already a portion coming from the family funds, and Grandmother has promised a generous contribution, and the second branch owes a contribution as well..."

Lord Qin let out a cold laugh. "Ruyin, those are all empty words. Don't believe any of them."

Qin Ruyin thought to herself: those were very much not empty words. She intended to bleed the Dowager and the second branch thoroughly — at least once.

chapters.title: Her Fiancé, Jiang Jiuxiao
chapters.content:
The Jiang Residence.

Jiang Jiuxiao had a rest day the following morning, and so Dowager Lady Jiang had someone request his presence at her Rongan Hall for the evening meal.

In the course of casual conversation, the subject of his upcoming marriage naturally arose.

Ever since the second Qin son had come in person to the Jiang household to discuss the substitution, and even though the arrangements had been settled according to her son's wishes, Dowager Lady Jiang had been anything but pleased.

Dowager Lady Jiang was fifty-eight years of age.

Jiang Jiuxiao was her fourth son.

Below Jiang Jiuxiao, there was one more legitimate son, and one legitimate daughter — both already married and with children of their own.

It was this fourth son alone who had kept her up at night.

And yet he was the most accomplished of his generation in the family — the entire Jiang household's fate and standing rested on him alone.

As his mother, she had absolutely no power to control him.

Not long ago, her fourth son had returned from accompanying the Emperor on a visit to the Great Tianyin Temple and suddenly announced that he intended to remarry — and that the bride must come from the Qin family.

She had been so relieved at the time that she'd thought little of the details. She had immediately enlisted the highly respected Dowager Liu to call on the Qin family and make the proposal.

The first visit, the Qin household had been coy and noncommittal. She had then found another pretext to send Dowager Liu again to assess the Fifth Miss.

That visit had gone smoothly — the Qin family's Dowager had seemed genuinely pleased with the prospect. And then, without warning, a substitution was demanded.

It had caught her completely off guard.

But the substitution had been negotiated directly between the Qin Second Son and her fourth son, and there was nothing she could do about it.

What rankled was the sense that the Qin family had handled it all quite improperly.

"These past few days I've had people watching the Qin household — and do you know what I found? Those Qin people and their scheming! They've already gone and betrothed their Fifth Miss to the Wang family's second son!"

"What does that mean? The Qin second branch is too proud to have their daughter be your second wife, and yet they're reluctant to pass up a connection to the Jiang family?"

"They've worked out their calculations so neatly — but did they ever stop to consider whether the Jiang family deserves to be treated this way? Step back ten thousand steps — no family does business like that!"

...

Though Dowager Lady Jiang was nearly sixty and had always cultivated an air of composed and magnanimous ease, she was genuinely vexed today, and she couldn't stop herself from airing her grievances to her son.

"Jiuxiao — do you truly have to have a bride from the Qin family?"

Jiang Jiuxiao, who had been sitting quietly and drinking his tea while his mother vented, set his cup down and looked at her. His voice was low and unhurried.

"Mother — you know what Master Huiqing is capable of. The reading he risked his cultivation to make, just to find that my second wife must come from the Qin family — that alone was difficult enough."

"Even if the Qin family swapped who they sent, as long as she is a legitimate daughter of the Qin line, that is fate between her and me."

Dowager Lady Jiang fell silent, still unsatisfied.

She added, "The way the Qin second branch handled this — your mother is simply indignant on your behalf."

"It doesn't matter." Jiang Jiuxiao reassured her. "Perhaps having a different girl sent over is, in fact, a better outcome for our family. From what I can see, the first branch — though they keep a low profile — Lord Qin himself is a man of genuine integrity."

Dowager Lady Jiang sighed and shook her head. "You always have your own ideas — and you're very good at pacifying your mother. Very well. You have already agreed to it, and there is no going back now."

Then she asked, "But what about this Sixth Miss of the Qin family — have you looked into her at all?"

"I have not." Jiang Jiuxiao shook his head.

Entirely unconcerned.

When it came to women, it was always more or less the same to him.

Once the Qin Sixth Miss was brought into the household, he would give her the full dignity of a proper wife, take care of her for the rest of her life — that was all.

It did not occur to him how thoroughly he would be proven wrong in this particular indifference.

Dowager Lady Jiang considered his reaction, then nodded slowly.

"Fair enough. The Qin family has a Fifth Miss who is famously striking and remarkably talented — her younger sister behind her naturally doesn't stand out by comparison. It's reasonable that you wouldn't know much about her."

"But, Jiuxiao — your mother has borne and raised children. Now that you've agreed to marry Sixth Miss, you must treat her well. She is young compared to you, you know."

At those words, a rare and faint embarrassment crossed Jiang Jiuxiao's face — a face carved as if by blade and chisel.

A full twelve years younger. Yes — that was perhaps a bit much.

"Mm."

"Don't worry, Mother. If I hadn't wanted to marry, I wouldn't have agreed. But since I have — naturally I will not let her suffer."

He thought a moment, then added, "She is still young, and by all accounts she has been overlooked in the Qin household. When she comes here, Mother, please keep an eye out for her in the inner courtyard."

Jiang Jiuxiao said this not because he particularly cared about the Qin Sixth Miss — he simply didn't want trouble stirring up in his own household. His sisters-in-law were none of them easy women.

Seeing her son — who had always been utterly indifferent toward women — say such a thing, Dowager Lady Jiang felt genuinely cheered.

So he wasn't actually a reincarnated monk after all!

"You think I need reminding?"

She smiled even as she said it.

And inwardly she found herself warming to this Qin Sixth Miss she had never met.

The old woman was the sort who kept her accounts clearly. The Qin family's substitution was the second branch's doing — it had nothing to do with the first branch. And nothing to do with the young girl being sent over.

The more she thought about it, the sorrier she felt for the child.

A girl of sixteen, only just blooming — she had surely been hoping to marry someone like the Wang family's second son: talented, spirited, full of life and youth.

Of course, in Dowager Lady Jiang's own heart, her son was in every way superior — the Wang boy couldn't begin to compare.

But looking at her fourth son in his languid, aristocratic composure, she couldn't stop herself from asking, "Have you considered — what if the girl refuses? What if she doesn't want to be your second wife?"

Dowager Lady Jiang asked plainly.

Jiang Jiuxiao was accustomed to his mother's directness, and took no offense.

"A sixteen-year-old girl, the pampered youngest daughter of her family — a little willfulness is to be expected. I am generally easy-going, but this marriage is not something I will allow one young girl's whims to undo."

"You, easy-going?" Dowager Lady Jiang gave him a good-natured scolding, then sighed. "You, you..."

She trailed off, never quite landing on the reproach she had in mind. She just shook her head helplessly.

Jiang Jiuxiao had his own calculation.

Master Huiqing had damaged his own cultivation to read the divination for him — though the reading was vague, it had revealed this much: that only a daughter of the Qin family — a family whose ancestral home was in the great clans of the Jiangnan south — could avert the great catastrophe looming over the Jiang household.

He had sent people to trace every branch of every Qin family in the capital going back eighteen generations before confirming which Qin family it was.

Master Huiqing had instructed him to let things unfold naturally — to work with the current of events, not against it.

And that was exactly what he had done.

He simply hadn't expected the Qin family to demand a substitution at the last moment.

A substitution was fine.

As long as the girl was a legitimate daughter of the Qin bloodline, it made no difference to him which one he married.

The flow of fate — he would follow it.

The Jiang family had stood for centuries. Over three hundred lives depended on it. He was in no position to be petty about the Qin family's scheming.

...

"Master Huiqing's chosen auspicious date for the wedding is soon — we'll need to move through the betrothal rites quickly."

Dowager Lady Jiang and her son discussed the arrangements.

Jiang Jiuxiao nodded and clasped his hands in a bow. "The wedding arrangements — I'll leave it all to Mother's hands."

"If I'm not handling it for you, who is? Your mother is only grateful her health still holds, that she can still manage this for her son."

Dowager Lady Jiang spoke with all the devotion of a parent.

Jiang Jiuxiao listened, and something in his eyes softened.

"Since the Qin family substituted their girl — they did what they did, but Mother, I was thinking the bride-price should still be given generously, without any shortchanging of the Qin Sixth Miss."

Dowager Lady Jiang looked at her son. "How much do you think the bride-price should be?"

"Four thousand taels."

Dowager Lady Jiang was genuinely surprised.

The bride-price for his first wife had been only two thousand taels.

By custom, if the woman taking the role of second wife was considered to be of lower status than the first, the bride-price should not exceed what had been given before. Of course, there were cases where the groom's family held the second wife in especially high regard, in which case the amounts were made equal.

His first wife...

Let us not go into that.

Seeing his mother's expression of surprise, Jiang Jiuxiao said, "Mother — the Qin Sixth Miss is different from that one."

"Even if I feel nothing particular for Qin Sixth Miss — I do feel that I owe her something. I must at least give her face."

Dowager Lady Jiang understood.

She exhaled a quiet sigh, then nodded. "Very well then."

In the customs of the Great Ying Dynasty, four thousand taels as a bride-price was an extraordinarily generous sum — enough to seek the hand of a commandery princess or a county princess.

By convention, if the groom's family offered a large bride-price, the bride's family was expected to send a dowry worth at least twice that amount — often several times over. And it was also expected that the bride's family would return the bride-price to their daughter as part of her private trousseau — separate from, and not counted among, the formal dowry items.

...

The following day happened to be Jiang Jiuxiao's day off.

He was receiving guests at the Xilai Restaurant.

After bidding his friends farewell, he was stopped by a young attendant dressed as a page boy — one with an honest and simple-looking face.

His long-time personal attendant Li Yi stepped forward to intercept.

The page boy immediately raised both hands above his head holding out an invitation card, and dropped to his knees on the ground.

"Lord Jiang, if you would be so good as to hear me out — I have a card of invitation. Please, my lord, after reviewing the card, would you be so kind as to step into the private room just to the right, at the very end of the corridor — my master is there and awaits you..."

Li Yi turned to look at his master.

Jiang Jiuxiao had no intention of indulging this.

Then the page boy said urgently: "Lord Jiang — my master's surname is Qin!"

chapters.title: Meeting the Fiancé
chapters.content:
Qin.

Something shifted in the depths of Jiang Jiuxiao's dark eyes. "Bring it here."

Li Yi immediately took the invitation from the page boy's hands and turned to present it to his master with respectful deference.

Jiang Jiuxiao glanced at it. It bore the personal calling card of his future father-in-law.

And at this hour, calling on him like this...

Jiang Jiuxiao — it was his name, not Qin Jiuxiao — furrowed his handsome brow slightly.

"Lead the way."

The page boy had been holding his breath the entire time in the presence of this terrifyingly imposing lord.

The moment he heard the command to lead the way, the overwhelming relief caused him to go weak in the knees, and he nearly stumbled.

Seeing him lurch, Li Yi reached out a long arm and steadied him with a single practiced grab.

"Th... thank you..."

The page boy broke into a cold sweat, furious with himself for losing composure. He had let his mistress down.

If his sister ever found out, she was sure to scold him.

"Master, Lord Jiang has arrived."

When the page boy knocked and announced their arrival, his voice was still shaking.

Zisu heard her brother's voice and her heart seized.

She instinctively turned to look at her mistress — who was sitting with both hands propped under her chin, looking like she had been waiting long enough to run out of patience.

"Miss, he's here..."

Please compose yourself a little!

Qin Ruyin merely said "Oh" in a mild tone.

She didn't feel like performing. She didn't feel like adjusting herself.

This was a man who had made senior second-rank at twenty-eight years old.

Qin Ruyin knew she couldn't put on a convincing act in front of him.

Once Zisu opened the door, she didn't even manage to bow and greet Lord Jiang first — she stuck her head out like a thief and craned her neck in every direction.

The moment he stepped through, she grabbed Li Yi by the sleeve and yanked him in with them.

Then she slammed the door shut.

All in one seamless motion.

Jiang Jiuxiao: ...

Qin Ruyin looked over.

The man was tall, dressed in a dark ink-blue scholar's robe, cut in the style of a learned gentleman.

Then her eyes reached his face, and she nearly let out an audible gasp.

She had never seen a man so devastatingly handsome.

She couldn't find quite the right word for it — only that the beauty of his face carried an edge. It was the kind of handsomeness that felt like an assault.

But not her type. She preferred the refined and gracefully elegant kind.

When Jiang Jiuxiao looked over, he saw a young girl of fifteen or sixteen with both hands propped under her chin, an expression of impatience still fading from her lovely face — though her dark eyes had widened with a flicker of genuine shock.

He understood immediately who this was.

Quite the audacious little thing.

Jiang Jiuxiao's expression remained composed, but his eyes were dark and penetrating.

Li Yi, on the other hand, was startled. "My lord, this is..."

Jiang Jiuxiao raised his right hand and swept it back with a single measured gesture, stopping Li Yi short.

He's far too collected.

Faced with a young girl who had secretly arranged to meet him, he didn't show even a flicker of surprise.

And that gesture — dressed as a scholar though he was, the commanding presence in that single motion was formidable.

Qin Ruyin's heart beat a little faster despite herself.

"Miss Qin Sixth."

Qin Ruyin, in her nerves, had completely forgotten to stand and bow in greeting.

She sat there while Zisu made frantic eyes at her, blinking so desperately her face was practically twitching.

"Ah — yes! That's me, I'm Sixth Miss Qin." When her name was said aloud, Qin Ruyin startled like a student who had been called on in class, shot to her feet, and stood perfectly straight. "Lord Jiang, good day!"

She couldn't help it. The moment this man opened his presence up, she couldn't hold her ground.

Jiang Jiuxiao: ...

He had been about to observe that this girl's behavior was nothing like the gentle and sweet-tempered girl his mother had described.

But then — well. Perhaps it was close enough, after all.

He didn't know why, but he had a sudden, faint urge to laugh.

Even his attendant Li Yi, standing behind him, was struggling not to. He was holding his composure so hard his face had started to twitch.

"Lord Jiang, please be seated."

"Zisu — pour tea for Lord Jiang."

Once she saw that Lord Jiang's expression had eased by a degree or two, Qin Ruyin finally exhaled.

So they could actually have a conversation now?

Zisu's own heart, which had been rising and falling anxiously, finally settled. She heard her mistress's instruction, answered quickly, and stepped forward to pour Jiang Jiuxiao's tea.

"Lord Jiang, please have some tea." Qin Ruyin hastily extended her hand in a gesture of invitation.

Jiang Jiuxiao accepted the tea, though he had no intention of drinking it.

He glanced at her coolly and asked, "Miss Sixth — you used Lord Qin's calling card to arrange this meeting. What is it that you needed?"

Jiang Jiuxiao kept his expression neutral, but his mind was already working through possibilities.

He couldn't help it. What this Sixth Miss had done was far too unconventional.

The Qin first branch had spent years quietly suppressed by the second branch. The children of the first branch barely had a presence in the social circles of the capital's great families — they were practically invisible.

But she was still, at the end of the day, from a distinguished family.

She was, nominally at least, a young lady of quality...

Could it be — as his mother had speculated — that a girl her age, in the bloom of youth, didn't want to marry him, a widower nearly thirty years old?

Qin Ruyin had no idea what was going through Lord Jiang's mind. She was entirely occupied with her own thoughts.

This man prefers directness?

Then this couldn't be more perfect.

She had been planning to take the indirect route.

"I do have something to ask Lord Jiang!"

"How much of a bride-price are you planning to offer for me?"

Jiang Jiuxiao's eyes widened — just slightly.

He genuinely hadn't expected that.

He leaned forward slightly, compelled by some inexplicable curiosity: "Is that all Miss Sixth came to ask?"

A young girl had used her father's calling card to arrange a secret meeting with him, obviously behind her family's back and at considerable trouble to herself.

Just to ask this?

If anyone found out, this girl's reputation would be in shreds. Had she given that any thought at all?

"Yes." Qin Ruyin nodded with complete certainty.

What else?

Jiang Jiuxiao went quiet.

Qin Ruyin grew anxious.

"Lord Jiang, this is very important!"

"Is that really all you wanted to know?" Jiang Jiuxiao asked again.

Was it not because she didn't want to marry him — a widower of advanced years — and had come to beg him to let her out of the engagement?

Qin Ruyin patiently answered, "Of course it's not only this — but I need to see how much of a bride-price you're offering before we talk about anything else."

"Four thousand taels of silver."

Jiang Jiuxiao, satisfied that she was genuinely only concerned about this, felt his expression grow complicated.

This was a money-grubbing little creature.

"For that information, Miss Sixth need not have gone to all this trouble to come and ask me in person."

He paused, then continued, "The gifts ceremony will come soon enough."

The gifts ceremony — najing — was the formal occasion when the groom's family sent the betrothal gifts.

But the girl wasn't taking his hint at all.

"It's precisely because the gifts ceremony is so close that I was in such a hurry to see you in person!"

Jiang Jiuxiao reminded himself she was still very young.

He had no desire to argue with her about the logistics.

He simply asked, "And is this amount satisfactory to Miss Sixth?"

"It's not..."

Li Yi saw his master — upon hearing these three words from Miss Sixth — actually smile, and felt a jolt of alarm.

He had been at his master's side since boyhood. He knew his master better than anyone.

He quickly clasped his hands toward the presumably oblivious Sixth Miss. "Miss Sixth — the bride-price my master is offering you is already exceptionally generous! The late First Mistress's bride-price was only..."

"Li Yi!" Jiang Jiuxiao said his name.

"Stand back."

Li Yi's heart lurched. He quickly dropped his head with a small "yes" and stepped back behind Jiang Jiuxiao.

"That's — that's not what I meant."

Qin Ruyin already knew from Lady Li the general scale of bride-prices in the Great Ying Dynasty.

There were different standards for common people, for wealthy families, and for centuries-old noble houses like the Jiang family.

Four thousand taels as a bride-price was already an extraordinary amount — enough to seek the hand of royalty.

"Oh?" Jiang Jiuxiao drew out the sound, his curiosity genuinely piqued. "Then what did Miss Sixth mean?"

"Only this?" he asked once more.

Not because she was averse to marrying a widower past his prime, and had come to plead her case for breaking off the engagement?

Qin Ruyin kept her patience and replied: "Of course it's not only this — I need to see how much the bride-price is before we can discuss the rest."

chapters.title: She Doesn't Deserve Him!
chapters.content:
"What I mean is... Lord Jiang, I'd like to make a deal with you."

"A deal?"

Jiang Jiuxiao was briefly caught off guard.

Then amusement stirred within him.

This little girl wanted to make a deal with him?

Li Yi was astonished once again. His lord seemed to have smiled again?

He snuck another glance at this Sixth Miss Qin.

This girl kept saying "I, I, I" without using any humble forms of address — so rude — and here she was casually proposing some sort of deal to his lord. Could it be that his lord was laughing out of exasperation?

"What deal?" Jiang Jiuxiao considered for a moment, then fixed his gaze on Qin Ruyin.

Qin Ruyin's heart clenched, but the arrow was already nocked — there was no turning back now.

She closed her eyes briefly, then steeled herself.

"On the day of the betrothal gifts, I'd ask that Lord Jiang prepare ten thousand and one taels of silver as the bride price..."

Li Yi drew a sharp breath behind them.

This Sixth Miss Qin had truly audacious demands!

Ten thousand taels as a bride price would have been enough even for an emperor marrying an empress...

Even Zisu, upon hearing her own young mistress make such a breathtaking demand, felt the world go dark before her eyes.

The dynasty's founding emperor had come from poverty, and had once been forced to give up the woman he loved because her family demanded too high a bride price.

For this reason, the founding emperor harbored a deep disdain for extravagant bride prices and lavish betrothal gifts.

Though the matter had never been enshrined in law, the imperial family had taken the lead by providing brides with dowries several times the value of any bride price they received.

As a result, the princesses and imperial ladies of this dynasty were uniformly fabulously wealthy.

In time, this had the unintended consequence of making it difficult for some families to afford to marry off their daughters at all.

Qin Ruyin had come to understand all this later, and it had left her speechless with astonishment.

In the world she had come from in her previous life, there was a certain country that...

But she had not crossed over into this world to be some lofty, transcendent little immortal, untouched by worldly concerns.

As long as the customs of this world worked in her favor, she would use them.

Whether it would succeed or not was secondary — she had to at least try.

Seeing that Jiang Jiuxiao's thick, perfectly even brows had begun to furrow, Qin Ruyin quickly spoke up.

"Lord Jiang, please don't be in a rush to refuse. I..."

"Which ear of yours heard this official intending to refuse?" Jiang Jiuxiao's gaze was cool and clear.

"Good, so you won't refuse!" Qin Ruyin instinctively pressed her hand to her little heart.

Jiang Jiuxiao curved his lips slightly. "Only, what is Sixth Miss Qin's reason?"

He was not incapable of producing ten thousand taels as a bride price.

Bringing her home was itself a kind of transaction with her.

As long as she wanted silver, he was more than willing to give it.

So long as she did not become too greedy.

Qin Ruyin found that she rather liked his way of conducting himself.

At least it made communication smooth.

Clean and decisive, no dragging things out — he truly was someone destined for great things.

She straightened herself and her expression turned more solemn and earnest.

"Lord Jiang, I imagine you're already aware of my situation."

"Though my family holds the position of the main branch of the Qin household, the second branch manages everything inside and out of the residence."

"My father and brothers are all kept under my second uncle's thumb. While they hold official posts, you are at court and should be aware of the underhanded dealings involved."

"When all is said and done, my family background and standing are not worthy of you..."

"Why speak of such things?"

Seeing the self-deprecating expression on the little girl's face, Jiang Jiuxiao felt a flicker of discomfort.

The world said he was broad-minded and easy-going... but in truth, he was proud and cold.

The person he chose, even if he did not love her, was not something others were entitled to comment upon.

Not even that person herself.

"This official is already aware of all of that, and thought nothing of it — that is precisely why I agreed to the Qin family's request for a substitution marriage."

Qin Ruyin was taken aback. "You're serious?"

"This official is naturally serious." Jiang Jiuxiao seemed to recall something, and his expression cooled by two degrees. "But what about you, Sixth Miss? Does the fact that this official is so many years your senior not trouble you?"

"Ah?" Qin Ruyin's face was a picture of confusion.

In ancient times, wasn't an older husband and a younger wife one of the most common arrangements?

Besides, this was a family alliance — did she, a mere young woman, have any room to protest?

"You're not old. You haven't even reached thirty yet," said Qin Ruyin, shaking her head with a look of earnest sincerity.

She was not flattering him — she genuinely thought so.

Jiang Jiuxiao did not even realize it himself, but faint traces of amusement stirred within his dark eyes.

"If that is so, you may raise whatever reasons you like with this official." He looked at her, his expression still placid, but his tone considerably softer.

Qin Ruyin's eyes darted about.

A man of such rank would surely not deceive a little girl like her.

She steeled herself, and the words came tumbling out: "I'll be honest with Lord Jiang — I am very unhappy about being swapped out in this marriage by the Qin second branch's scheming!"

Jiang Jiuxiao fixed her with a heavy, intent gaze.

Was she still looking down on him?

Qin Ruyin felt her heart tremble under that gaze — his presence was overwhelming!

Instinctively she waved both hands. "Of course, I have no grievance against Lord Jiang!"

"Meeting you today, I feel Lord Jiang is quite easy to get along with. You think nothing of my background and standing, so marrying you would be a stroke of fortune for me — it is I who would be marrying above my station."

"But I cannot simply let the matter of the Qin second branch scheming against me go." Qin Ruyin's gaze became distant and pensive. "Previously, my mother already negotiated with them — they would compensate me with a somewhat larger dowry."

"But the size of the dowry also depends on how much the man's family contributes as a bride price. The more you give, the more the Qin family will have to provide as my dowry." Qin Ruyin watched Jiang Jiuxiao's impassive face.

"I'm not afraid of you laughing at me — everyone knows that our main branch has always been strapped for cash, both within the family and without. So the bulk of the dowry would have to come from the second branch, or be supplemented by our household's grand matriarch..."

By this point, how could Jiang Jiuxiao fail to understand?

This girl!

The corner of his mouth curved upward without him quite intending it.

Knowing how to fight for one's own interests was a virtue.

"The bride price will be set at the amount Sixth Miss Qin proposed."

"Ah?"

It had gone so smoothly?

Qin Ruyin was also a little stunned, her face forming a question mark.

But she quickly added: "You needn't worry though — I won't take advantage of you. Your original bride price was four thousand taels. Once I've married into the Jiang household, I'll return six thousand taels to you..."

"That will not be necessary!" Jiang Jiuxiao cut her off.

"Ten thousand and one."

So it would be ten thousand and one.

As long as this girl did not make a scene about dissolving the engagement, everything else was negotiable.

It was nothing more than helping her squeeze a larger dowry out of the Qin second branch.

And this little girl had no idea how much thought she had put into simply getting to see him today.

Come to think of it, that was rather endearing.

Seeing him agree so readily, Qin Ruyin let out a breath of relief, and felt genuinely pleased.

"Lord Jiang, you may think my way of doing this is beneath you, but think of it this way — I'm helping you vent your frustration too!"

With things going so unexpectedly smoothly, Qin Ruyin had relaxed, and her tongue loosened without her realizing it.

"The Qin second branch schemed against me — didn't they also scheme against you?"

"I know my own worth. Someone of your station, if you were to marry at all, should by rights have married my Fifth Sister — her status and standing would suit you far better..."

"She doesn't deserve him!"

Before she could finish her sigh, Jiang Jiuxiao had already cut her off.

Qin Ruyin stared at him in astonishment, momentarily speechless.

The little girl had stopped chattering — she looked as though she'd been startled by his manner.

Jiang Jiuxiao felt somewhat flustered.

A brief silence fell between them.

Fortunately, Li Yi was a quick-witted fellow.

He had already sensed that his lord was being remarkably patient with this outrageously bold Sixth Miss Qin.

After all, his lord, save for his feelings toward the Grand Matriarch, was cold as ice toward every other woman.

Under the circumstances, this was good news!

Seeing that the atmosphere had gone awkward, he quickly smiled at Qin Ruyin and said: "Sixth Miss, my lord bears you no ill will. The original betrothal to Fifth Miss Qin was never to my lord's liking!"

He even went so far as to add, with considerable emphasis: "My lord never took a liking to Fifth Miss Qin at all!"

Was that so?

Qin Ruyin glanced at Lord Jiang, whose expression had gone cold and stern.

chapters.title: As She Had Hoped
chapters.content:
As it turned out, Lord Jiang seemed to have misunderstood something.

Seeing her look over at him, he gave her a nod and let out a mild "Mm."

The more you know, the faster you die.

Qin Ruyin understood this principle, and had no desire to keep pressing for answers.

"Then... Lord Jiang, since the two of us have now spoken frankly with one another, shall we consider the matter of the bride price settled?"

"Mm." Jiang Jiuxiao made another sound of agreement, then paused and, worried the girl might overthink and misread his meaning, added a clear: "Agreed."

Having achieved her purpose, Qin Ruyin glanced at the sky, then rose and bid Jiang Jiuxiao farewell.

Jiang Jiuxiao also rose, watching the little girl perform her bow — perfunctory and sloppy — and the corner of his mouth twitched slightly.

Li Yi watched on in mild bewilderment.

Somehow, it felt like his lord's patience with this particular person was... not necessarily a good thing.

Tsk, tsk!

This Sixth Miss Qin... was truly unlike any pampered noble daughter he had ever encountered!

If this girl actually married into the upright and decorous Jiang household, would she embarrass herself — and by extension her lord — in the eyes of others?

He stood there with his head bowed, staring at his toes, lost in these rambling thoughts, when he heard his master call out to him.

"My lord, this servant is here!"

"Go tell the innkeeper to quickly prepare a serving of Chef Li's osmanthus water chestnut cake for Sixth Miss to eat on the way home."

Qin Ruyin immediately waved her hands in protest. "Thank you, Lord Jiang, but I'm not hungry, truly..."

"You haven't had your midday meal, and you say you're not hungry?" Jiang Jiuxiao paid no heed to her refusal and gave Li Yi a look.

Li Yi hastily acknowledged and went to carry out the order.

Qin Ruyin was genuinely puzzled.

How did Lord Jiang know that she had not eaten the midday meal?

Li Yi returned swiftly, carrying an elegantly crafted food box.

The osmanthus water chestnut cakes had just come out fresh from the kitchen — he had used his lord's name to "claim" them from one of his lord's subordinates who had ordered them.

"Miss Zisu, please hold this carefully. The osmanthus water chestnut cake is Xilai Restaurant's signature dish — it's best eaten while still warm."

Li Yi handed the food box to Zisu, adding this remark with particular care.

He was not normally a man of many words — had his lord not been so remarkably patient with this Sixth Miss Qin, he would never have dared speak up like this in his lord's presence.

He sneaked a glance at his lord. His lord showed no displeasure at his having spoken out of turn, and he felt quietly pleased with himself.

After Qin Ruyin thanked Jiang Jiuxiao, she departed from the restaurant with Zisu.

"Li Yi, have people follow Sixth Miss's carriage. Don't let her notice. If anyone troublesome crosses her path, deal with them."

Li Yi felt a jolt in his chest, but kept his expression perfectly respectful. "Yes, my lord!"

After returning to his residence, Jiang Jiuxiao went directly to the hall of his mother, Grand Matriarch Jiang, at Rong'an Hall.

"What brings you here at this hour? Has something important happened?"

This son of hers, outside of his two visits each month to dine with her in the evening, rarely came to her quarters at this time of day.

No wonder Grand Matriarch Jiang was somewhat braced for trouble.

Jiang Jiuxiao did not beat around the bush. He told her directly that the bride price for Qin Ruyin was to be raised to ten thousand and one taels.

"Raised to that amount? To what purpose?"

Grand Matriarch Jiang was even more astonished.

That number... was more than sufficient to cover the bride price the founding emperor's successors had offered when marrying their empresses!

It had only been in the last few reigns that the imperial family's bride prices for principal wives had gradually climbed higher.

And when the current emperor had taken his empress two years ago, his bride price had amounted to sixteen thousand six hundred taels...

"Mother, please prepare it at that amount. Beyond that, the grade of the betrothal gifts should also be raised by a factor of two."

Grand Matriarch Jiang could no longer restrain herself.

"Fourth child, it's not that your mother is reluctant, nor that I dislike Sixth Miss Qin — but what you're asking for is... a bit much!"

"Bear in mind that this is a second marriage for you. Even for a first marriage, the current bride prices don't go this high — much less when you also wish to double the standard of the betrothal gifts."

"Surely there is a reasonable explanation?"

"And besides, once the Yao family hears of this, they will certainly..."

Jiang Jiuxiao cut his mother off. "What about it if the Yao family hears? Do they have the face to come cause trouble?"

Grand Matriarch Jiang was momentarily at a loss for words.

Jiang Jiuxiao laughed coldly inside.

Even Sixth Miss Qin — a sixteen-year-old girl — knew that when you had been wronged, you fought to reclaim what was yours!

What of him?

In the past, the Yao family had leveraged the power of their patriarch, who served as the foremost of the Three Excellencies, to bully him and the Jiang family into swallowing a shameful and bitter humiliation.

"Oh! Your mother knows — you've never gotten over what happened with the Yao family. All these years, for the sake of the Jiang family, you have truly suffered."

"But look at you now — you've risen despite it all, and the family has produced some remarkable descendants. If the Yao family tries to suppress you or our Jiang household again, they'll have to think twice."

"On this matter, I defer to you!" Grand Matriarch Jiang made her decision without hesitation.

"As far as your mother is concerned, this time is no different from your marrying a first wife. For people of our standing, treating a principal wife with grand gestures is simply the right thing to do!"

Grand Matriarch Jiang did not deliberate long before resolving to support her son.

"Thank you, Mother!" Jiang Jiuxiao rose and bowed deeply before her.

"No need for formalities. That you are willing to do this... your mother is actually quite glad."

Grand Matriarch Jiang picked up her handkerchief and dabbed at her eyes.

Jiang Jiuxiao did not tell his mother about being summoned by that little girl to meet at Xilai Restaurant earlier that day.

On the way home in her carriage, Qin Ruyin promptly shared the osmanthus water chestnut cake that Jiang Jiuxiao had ordered for her with Zisu, and the two finished it off together while it was still warm.

Afterwards, she stopped at the capital's most popular pastry shop to buy several treats that her family members enjoyed and brought them home.

In addition to these, she bought a few small trinkets for her nephews and nieces.

Her eldest and second brothers each had a son and a daughter.

The oldest was six, the youngest still an infant in arms.

She also bought Grand Matriarch Qin an exquisitely crafted and embroidered forehead piece.

And some hair ornaments and small trinkets to placate the unmarried female cousins on the Qin family side.

That day's outing had been approved by Grand Matriarch Qin after Qin Ruyin's mother, Lady Li, had spoken to her on her behalf.

The stated reason was that, with her wedding approaching, Qin Ruyin wished to go out herself to purchase jewelry and clothing she liked.

Grand Matriarch Qin had been reluctant to agree at first.

But remembering that Qin Ruyin had always been obedient and well-behaved — even when her good match had been handed to another in this swap — the girl had neither cried nor thrown a tantrum, and so she relented.

There was also the consideration that if she refused and the girl did kick up a fuss, the situation would become difficult to manage.

She simply warned Lady Li to send a capable and discreet person along to accompany her.

Qin Ruyin, for her part, played the role thoroughly.

After parting from Jiang Jiuxiao, she spent the afternoon visiting several jewelry shops and clothing shops and making purchases before returning home.

What had transpired that day — that she had used her father's name card to invite Jiang Jiuxiao to meet — was known to no one except herself and the two siblings, Zisu and her brother.

Not even Grandma Sun, the trusted attendant Lady Li had assigned to accompany her, knew.

This was because she had had Zisu's brother play a small trick, causing poor Grandma Sun to make several urgent trips to the privy...

The coachman and Grandma Sun knew only that she had grown hungry and had, on the way, mentioned wanting to visit Xilai Restaurant to experience it — and so they assumed she had simply gone inside to dine with Zisu.

Grandma Sun had no idea that the girl had had no time to eat at all, having concluded her transaction with her future husband and then promptly departed from Xilai Restaurant.

Once back at the residence, Zisu was still lost in fond memories of the osmanthus water chestnut cake from Xilai Restaurant.

She even said to her young mistress, "Had I known the osmanthus water chestnut cake at Xilai Restaurant would be this delicious, we ought to have bought some to bring back for Madam to try."

Qin Ruyin, carefully admiring the gold ornaments she had purchased that day, smiled and replied, "Do you think they're that easy to come by? Why do you think I ate the whole portion on the spot — wasn't it precisely to avoid questions about how I'd gotten my hands on it when we got home?"

Zisu flushed slightly, thinking of how that portion had been ordered by Lord Jiang himself, with his personal attendant going directly to the innkeeper...

She then said with wholehearted sincerity: "Young Miss is so clever!"

Qin Ruyin shook her head with a smile and paid her no more mind.

Not long after, Lady Li hurried in, with a pale-faced Grandma Sun and a heavy-hearted senior maid named Chuncao trailing behind her.

chapters.title: So She Had This Much for a Dowry
chapters.content:
As it turned out, Grandma Sun had gone to report to Lady Li upon returning to the residence.

When Lady Li learned that Grandma Sun had eaten something bad and fallen ill early that morning, she was greatly alarmed. Even though Grandma Sun was the most trusted and relied-upon person by her side, Lady Li still scolded her soundly.

After all, she had arranged the day before for Grandma Sun to accompany her daughter Yin'er on today's outing.

She had trusted Grandma Sun enough to assign her to go along.

And yet Grandma Sun had carelessly eaten something that upset her stomach, and had spent the afternoon dashing off to public latrines several times, leaving Yin'er with only Zisu for company while she went to Xilai Restaurant...

Even though her little daughter had managed to buy what she'd wanted and return home safely, Lady Li was still not at ease.

And so she had come to check on her with her own eyes before she could settle her mind.

After Qin Ruyin welcomed her mother inside, Lady Li began complaining about Grandma Sun.

Qin Ruyin threw Grandma Sun an apologetic glance, saw that her complexion was ashen and that she was clearly still unwell — and that she had on top of that already endured a scolding from her mother — and quickly spoke up to put in a good word for her.

She then asked her mother to give Grandma Sun a two-day leave to rest and recuperate.

Grandma Sun was profoundly grateful.

After all, on the way home, Sixth Miss had deliberately taken a detour to bring her to see a physician at a medicine hall, and had had medicine prepared for her.

She had even given her a gift of ten taels of silver.

Grandma Sun had not dared to accept it at first, but Sixth Miss had talked her around until she had no choice but to take it.

In her heart, she felt anxious about how freely Sixth Miss spent money — this was not a sustainable habit...

Lady Li saw her little daughter speaking up so earnestly on Grandma Sun's behalf, and felt glad.

She thought: her little girl has truly grown up, and knows how to look after the people who serve her on her mother's behalf.

Seeing her mother's expression soften, Qin Ruyin tugged at her arm and coaxingly pulled her over to look at what she had bought.

It was a way of redirecting her mother's attention.

Lady Li had given her two hundred taels of silver for the day. That was not enough for any major jewelry pieces, but it was more than sufficient for a selection of delicate little ornaments.

Watching her little daughter pull her this way and that, exclaiming over everything with bright excitement, Lady Li's long-taut nerves finally eased a little.

Then she sat down with her daughter and began speaking of the matter of the dowry.

"In the Qin family, whether marrying off a daughter or arranging a son's marriage, the communal household funds set aside for those born of the principal wife amount to two thousand taels."

"For sons of the principal wife, one thousand taels goes to preparing betrothal gifts and one thousand taels constitutes the bride price sent to the other family. For daughters of the principal wife, one thousand taels is set aside as the bride's personal silver, and another thousand taels is spent preparing the trousseau..."

"When you had just come into the world, your mother began setting aside your dowry. These ten-odd years have passed, and it is neither too much nor too little."

"...There is one estate with fine farmland on the outskirts of the capital — over six hundred mu. There are two shops, both rented out, and they have been collecting rent for over ten years now. The rental income and the estate's earnings have all been converted into silver, set aside as your bedchamber treasure."

"Then there are silks and satins, gold and silver jewelry — altogether worth around seven or eight hundred taels... And lastly there is the sealed silver itself. With the rental income and the various earnings from the estate, your mother has rounded it to an even number: twelve hundred taels."

After listening to her mother's account, Qin Ruyin could not help but be taken aback.

This was truly a family of officials and scholars!

Even though the Qin main branch had long joked about their own poverty, and the household had many children, the dowry that her mother had accumulated for the original girl alone was already worth four or five thousand taels.

And then there was the portion her father had privately set aside for her — with all of that combined, she would be a woman of considerable means!

"Your Fifth Sister traded away your good match. The second branch can feel smug, they can feel pleased with themselves — but for the sake of appearances, they'll have to pay a price!"

"And that price will come in the form of the dowry."

"Under normal circumstances, the grand matriarch and the second branch together giving you half a trousseau's worth would have been generous enough."

"But this time, the grand matriarch is to prepare an additional full portion as a supplement to you, and your second uncle and aunt are also to prepare an additional full portion. Your father and I negotiated this with them."

After a brief pause, a sardonic smile curled at Lady Li's lips. "Only, how much they actually put in... will depend entirely on how much the Jiang family gives as the bride price and in betrothal gifts."

Qin Ruyin understood perfectly.

The second branch would tailor their generosity to the occasion.

She was not afraid.

"And then there's the portion from your father — he privately sets aside a share for each of his children, which your mother is aware of, but has never asked about and doesn't look into." Lady Li winked at Qin Ruyin.

Qin Ruyin was struck by sudden comprehension.

So her mother had known about this all along!

"Mother, you..."

"Is Yin'er wondering why your mother pretends not to know?"

Qin Ruyin nodded, still a little bewildered.

"What is there to ask about? If your father doesn't want your mother to know, then your mother doesn't know."

"As long as it all goes to you children, your mother couldn't be happier — why would she ask so many questions?"

"Your mother may seem confused at times, but she can't afford to be confused in her heart as well." Lady Li reached out and lovingly smoothed the hair from her daughter's forehead.

"Yin'er, among all the children your mother has borne, you are the most like your mother in her youth." Lady Li's eyes grew distant and her expression softened at the memory.

"Afterwards, I married your father, and then spent my days finding my footing under the hand of my stepmother-in-law. It's only through eating many bitter losses that a person gradually learns to fight and to contend for themselves."

"...Your brothers and sisters all take after your father — they have their own strategies and mettle. Your mother isn't worried about them."

"It's only you, Yin'er, that your mother worries about most! Once you're married into the Jiang household, you mustn't carry on as you do here at home. You must watch and learn — if you can fight for something, fight; if you can't, bide your time..."

"Grand Matriarch Jiang has a decent reputation among the noble ladies of the capital, but she is too strong-willed and her temper is blunt and quick..."

After a brief pause, Lady Li's expression became somewhat unreadable. "I've heard that old matriarch has no patience for foolishness. When you're in her presence, you'll need to keep your wits about you..."

Grand Matriarch Jiang had an intolerance for stupidity?

Qin Ruyin nearly burst out laughing.

What an interesting old woman.

In that case, once she was married into the Jiang household, all she needed to do was avoid getting on the old matriarch's bad side.

A person who cannot abide foolishness must, without exception, be exceedingly sharp and capable themselves.

Her mother had also told her that Lord Jiang had lost his father at a young age, and that it was Grand Matriarch Jiang who had held things together for him and managed everything after he first passed the imperial examinations.

Living under the hand of a sharp and capable mother-in-law was far easier than living under the hand of a foolish one who liked to stir up trouble.

Although, if she was the scheming type, that would be another matter entirely.

She thought back to Lord Jiang's bearing...

A woman who had raised a son like Lord Jiang couldn't be a bad person by nature.

Seeing the anxiety on her mother's face, Qin Ruyin spent a good while earnestly reassuring her.

Lady Li noticed that her little daughter had matured considerably these past few days, and at last felt somewhat comforted.

"I suppose this is what it looks like when a daughter is about to be married off. Just a few days ago you were still my little child, and now here you are, soothing your mother just like your elder sisters do."

Lady Li dabbed at her reddened eyes with a silk handkerchief, looking at her little daughter with a heart full of bittersweet pride.

The next day, the weather was fine.

Early cherry blossoms were in full bloom, and orioles chirped merrily.

Qin Ruyin was reviewing the dowry list that her mother had compiled and organized for her.

She saw her senior maid Zicao lift the door curtain and hurry inside.

"Miss, Miss — someone has come from the Grand Matriarch's courtyard asking you to go to Shouhe Hall!"

Qin Ruyin glanced at her.

Seeing the conflicted expression on the girl's face, Qin Ruyin set down the dowry list and asked evenly: "Do you know what the Grand Matriarch wants me for?"

chapters.title: Rooting Out the Spy
chapters.content:
"In reply to Miss — it is Grand Matriarch Jiang who has come."

Qin Ruyin's heart gave a small start.

Grand Matriarch Jiang had come over?

Could it be that after Jiang Jiuxiao told Grand Matriarch Jiang about the bride price, the old lady had refused to agree?

On reflection, that didn't quite add up. Knowing Grand Matriarch Jiang's character, even if she disapproved of the bride price arrangement, she would not personally come to the Qin residence over such a matter.

"Has our Grand Matriarch asked my mother to come over as well?"

Steadying her composure, Qin Ruyin asked Zicao.

Zicao shook her head. "Miss, the Grand Matriarch has only summoned you."

She paused, then added: "Second Madam is there as well."

A chill settled in Qin Ruyin's eyes.

Grand Matriarch Jiang was her future mother-in-law — if she had come to the Qin residence, propriety dictated that her mother be invited to welcome and entertain the guest alongside her.

Yet they had arranged for only her second aunt, Lady Tong, to appear alongside the grand matriarch.

Did Grand Matriarch Qin and Lady Tong truly not know that doing this would invite people to say the Qin family had no manners?

Of course they knew. It was simply that the two of them — that mother-in-law and daughter-in-law pair — did not want her mother and Grand Matriarch Jiang to have too much contact with one another.

They were also sending Grand Matriarch Jiang a signal: even if she and Jiang Jiuxiao were to marry, the Jiang family ought to cultivate closeness with the Qin second branch. Only the second branch had the power to make decisions in this household.

"Help me change my clothes."

Qin Ruyin rose, walked to her dressing table, looked at her reflection in the mirror, and instructed: "Take out the lotus-pink Shu-embroidered lined jacket that Mother had made for me last year."

Zicao softly acknowledged.

Her complexion leaned toward pale, and pink tones made her appear all the more delicate and gentle.

It was her first time meeting her future mother-in-law, and it was better to make a good first impression.

A poor first impression when living under the same roof would make friction far more likely in the long run.

Qin Ruyin was not the type who enjoyed winning over others. Doing her reasonable best to avoid problems that might arise was simply prudent.

Zicao stole a glance in the bronze mirror at her young mistress, who had changed into fresh clothes and looked even more vivid and lovely than usual, then bit her lip softly.

Qin Ruyin looked at herself in the mirror and was satisfied.

"Miss, in this servant's eyes, your hair ornaments are a little too plain. Why not set off the look with the jade hairpin the Grand Matriarch gifted you?"

Qin Ruyin had actually been thinking she'd like to add a hairpin as well.

She heard the girl's suggestion and gave her a thoughtful sidelong look.

She posed a question: "Is the emerald jade hairpin the Grand Matriarch gifted me a good match?"

"In reply to Miss — it is." Zicao nodded with certainty.

Qin Ruyin curved her lips slightly. "Very well then, find the jade hairpin the Grand Matriarch gifted me."

Zicao's brow lifted, and she acknowledged at once.

When she held the found jade hairpin out to Qin Ruyin with both hands, Qin Ruyin asked once more: "Zicao, do you truly think the jade hairpin matches what I'm wearing today?"

Zicao nodded immediately. "Miss has natural beauty and fair complexion — emerald green suits her best. No one could wear this jade hairpin as beautifully as Miss can!"

There was a distinct note of urgency in her words, as though she was desperate for Qin Ruyin to agree to wear the jade hairpin.

Qin Ruyin nodded. "All right then. Put it in for me."

She sat down before the dressing table.

Zicao went forward eagerly and pinned it in.

Looking at her reflection in the bronze mirror, Qin Ruyin saw that the moment the jade hairpin — whose color was not particularly luminous, running unevenly from lighter to deeper shades of green — was set into her hair, it immediately produced a gaudy, cheap effect.

It clashed horribly with the lotus-pink outfit she was wearing.

"Does it look nice?" Qin Ruyin asked, looking at Zicao's reflection in the mirror, a smile playing at the corners of her lips.

Zicao nodded repeatedly. "It looks wonderful, Miss!"

"Wonderful it may be, but this hairpin was, after all, a gift from the Grand Matriarch..." Qin Ruyin watched Zicao's reflection in the mirror with an expression of mild, knowing amusement.

"But your young mistress simply can't bear to wear it out. Let's put it away properly instead."

"Miss!"

Zicao called out urgently.

Qin Ruyin looked at her. "What is it?"

"Miss, this hairpin was a personal gift from the Grand Matriarch herself. Her old ladyship would certainly be pleased to see you wearing it often — especially when you're going to meet an important guest."

Such urgency.

A cold laugh settled in Qin Ruyin's heart.

Whatever Zicao's reasons for this, there was no keeping her now.

After all, the girl's greatest talent was in coordinating colors.

The seamstresses in the Qin family's needlework room had frequently plied her with snacks and treats in exchange for her color-matching advice.

Could she possibly not know that pink and green — especially a bold, saturated green like emerald — worn together would look cheap?

"Use the white magnolia hairpin instead." Qin Ruyin stood and reached up to remove the jade hairpin of mediocre quality.

She turned and saw Zicao's brow furrowed tight, her face unable to hide its anxious desperation.

She took the opportunity to hold the jade hairpin out toward Zicao's hand. "Put it away carefully."

Then she deliberately let go.

The tip of the hairpin faced the floor.

Crack!

A sharp, clean sound rang out. Zicao looked down at the floor in panic, her face draining to a deathly white.

"Zicao!"

"How could you be so careless! This was the Grand Matriarch's gift to me — and you've gone and smashed it?"

"Miss... Miss, it wasn't this servant — I didn't smash it..." Zicao waved her hands in flustered protest.

Qin Ruyin narrowed her eyes slightly. "Fine. You didn't smash it. Then tell me — if you couldn't even catch a single hairpin, is this how you serve your mistress?"

"Miss! Please spare this servant, I beg you, please!" Zicao was no fool. Regardless of how the hairpin had actually broken, it had broken because she had not caught it.

She was a servant — that responsibility fell to her, no matter what...

"These past few days, you've frequently seemed distracted and unfocused, often spacing out while you work. Word has already reached Mother."

"Mother was going to call you in for questioning, but I thought you were worried about your mother, who fell ill a few days ago, and so I told Mother not to summon you."

"And now, to go and break even the hairpin the Grand Matriarch gifted me... With someone as careless as you at my side, I don't know what day you'll bring trouble down on me."

"Miss! Miss, this servant was wrong. Please give this servant one more chance — I will work diligently from now on and never make another mistake..."

"I still have to go to the Grand Matriarch's courtyard, and I have no time to listen to you argue back. "

Qin Ruyin called out for Zisu.

Zisu promptly lifted the door curtain and came in from outside.

Seeing Zicao kneeling on the ground with her arms wrapped around her young mistress's legs, she immediately went cold in the face, stepped forward, and hauled Zicao to her feet.

"Zicao! Miss is still needed at the Grand Matriarch's courtyard — how can you be so ill-mannered!"

"Elder Sister Zisu, I beg you to help me plead with Miss..."

Zisu glared at her. "You may be a servant, but even if the sky were falling, you cannot hold Miss up from going to see the Grand Matriarch!"

After saying this, Zisu caught sight of the hairpin shattered in several pieces on the floor — and recognized it as the jade hairpin the Grand Matriarch had gifted their young mistress.

Her expression instantly hardened, and she snapped: "You couldn't even handle something as simple as that — you've gone and smashed the jade hairpin the Grand Matriarch gave Miss... and you still have the face to beg Miss for mercy?"

"I didn't — that wasn't me who smashed it..."

"Silence!" Seeing that Zicao still dared to argue back, Zisu was even more furious. "If you didn't smash it, who did? Just now, you were the only one attending to Miss!"

Zicao fell silent.

She naturally could not say that it was the mistress herself who had failed to hand it off properly, causing the jade hairpin to fall.

Even if the mistress had not handed it off well, the fault lay with her, the servant.

The mistress could make a mistake; a servant could not fail to catch it.

If she dared say such a thing, no one would take her side.

Not even the Grand Matriarch would...

At this thought, Zicao bit down on her lip, tears streaming down her face, fury simmering in her heart.

"Get up now, and come with me to the Grand Matriarch's courtyard."

Qin Ruyin had caught the hatred in the girl's eyes, and changed her mind.

chapters.title: Meeting Her Future Mother-in-Law
chapters.content:
Yet when Zicao heard that Qin Ruyin wanted her to come along to the Grand Matriarch's courtyard, she shook her head in repeated and frantic refusal.

She had a guilty conscience, and naturally did not dare to face Grand Matriarch Jiang.

If Grand Matriarch Jiang found out that her private motivations had sabotaged the Grand Matriarch's plans, she would be done for!

Not only would she herself be done for — her entire family would be dragged down with her!

Seeing her dare to defy their young mistress's instructions, Zisu grew even more furious.

She stepped forward and gave Zicao a kick.

"What's gotten into you? How dare you disobey Miss's words!"

"Or is it that you have a guilty conscience and don't dare go with Miss to face Grand Matriarch Jiang?"

Inadvertently, Zisu had struck right at the heart of the matter. Zicao's face went even paler.

"All right, Zisu — let her be. We need to go quickly."

Having tested what she needed to, Qin Ruyin had no desire to waste any more time.

As for Zicao — there was no way she could keep her.

And then there was the matter of taking advantage of Grand Matriarch Jiang's presence today to test Grand Matriarch Qin's intentions.

If the timing was right, this would be the perfect opportunity to remove Zicao from her side.

She could simultaneously rid herself of the spy Grand Matriarch Qin had planted beside her, and deal the old matriarch a pointed inconvenience.

Zisu thought it over, then quietly asked her young mistress, "Miss, has Zicao — that girl with ill intentions — finally shown her hand?"

Qin Ruyin curved the corner of her mouth.

"Yes. She insisted on getting me to wear the jade hairpin the Grand Matriarch had given me." Having said this, Qin Ruyin paused mid-step and tilted her head to ask Zisu.

"Zisu, tell me — does green go with what I'm wearing today?"

Zisu studied her young mistress carefully.

"The lotus-pink lined jacket with the pale grey mamianqun skirt that Miss is wearing today is a beautiful pairing!"

"It makes Miss look delicate and fresh, lovely and sweet... but if the hair bun were set with such a deeply saturated green jade hairpin, it would look... it would look somewhat off."

"Exactly how it would be off, this servant can't quite put into words — but it just feels wrong somehow."

Qin Ruyin smiled faintly. "Even you could tell it didn't suit — do you think Zicao, who is most skilled in color coordination, couldn't see it?"

Zisu understood immediately.

Her eyes widened and she said indignantly: "That little wretch! She did it on purpose! Knowing Miss was going to meet Grand Matriarch Jiang today, she deliberately led Miss into making a poor impression!"

"Leading me into making a poor impression is perhaps overstating it."

"But if I had truly taken her advice, Grand Matriarch Jiang might well have formed a first impression that my taste was mediocre, and her regard for me might have suffered."

"Of course, that's only my guess. Perhaps she had other motives as well."

Zisu clenched her jaw and said through gritted teeth: "Is it possible Grand Matriarch Qin put her up to it?"

"Probably not." Qin Ruyin shook her head. "I just tested her — she was very frightened at the idea of coming with me to Grand Matriarch Jiang's courtyard."

Zisu thought for a moment and gave a small nod. "Miss, regardless of the reason, Zicao cannot stay by Miss's side any longer."

"Keeping a viper like her close by — one day she might strike Miss with a truly venomous bite..."

Qin Ruyin's gaze drifted forward, calm and settled. "It's fine. I have my own plans. Go send a trusted maid to report this to Mother."

Zisu immediately agreed and turned to step out.

Qin Ruyin studied her reflection in the bronze mirror, then dipped her fingertip into some rouge and applied a thin, even layer to her cheekbones.

It made her look all the more vivid and lovely, glowing with spirit.

Before long, Zisu returned. "Miss, it is as you instructed — this servant has taken care of it."

"Good." Qin Ruyin nodded. "We'll go to the Grand Matriarch's courtyard now."

When she arrived at Shouhe Hall, she had barely finished bowing to Grand Matriarch Qin when the old woman stopped her with a smile and had someone help her up.

Smiling, she said to Qin Ruyin: "Sixth child is here? Come, come, quickly — come pay your respects to Grand Matriarch Jiang."

Then she smiled and introduced her to Grand Matriarch Jiang: "Grand Matriarch Jiang, this is my youngest legitimate granddaughter — Sixth child."

Grand Matriarch Jiang's eyes crinkled with a smile, and she examined Qin Ruyin with a warm, affectionate gaze, speaking gently: "So this is Sixth Miss — what a lovely and graceful little thing she is!"

Grand Matriarch Qin felt a small prick of irritation.

Grand Matriarch Jiang had come to the Qin residence last time and lavished praise on her Fifth granddaughter — she had taken it to mean the old woman was quite satisfied.

Now that it was this Qin Ruyin instead, she seemed to like her well enough too... but could this girl compare to her own Fifth granddaughter?

Grand Matriarch Qin was displeased in her heart, though she showed nothing on her face. Her gaze was cool, but her expression remained smiling. "Grand Matriarch Jiang is too kind. This Sixth child of mine is fine in every respect — except that she tends to be careless about everything. After she is in your care, I hope you will do her the kindness of guiding her."

What a thing to say.

To the ear it sounded like pleasantries — polite, even complimentary.

But in truth, it painted Qin Ruyin as thoroughly unfit.

A daughter-in-law in a great family of officials and scholars feared, above all, being too fragile to bear the weight of responsibility.

Moreover, Qin Ruyin was to marry from two ranks Senior Jiang Jiuxiao.

Once she became Jiang Jiuxiao's wife, she would inevitably be called upon to navigate the full spectrum of social engagements between aristocratic wives.

If Sixth Miss Qin was truly someone who could not hold things together, would her son not become a laughingstock?

Grand Matriarch Jiang kept her expression neutral, but was not pleased inside.

"Who said so? I think our Sixth child is perfectly wonderful!" Grand Matriarch Jiang smiled and looked at Grand Matriarch Qin. "Why, I took an immediate liking to Sixth child the moment I saw her!"

Having said this, Grand Matriarch Jiang turned to Qin Ruyin and beckoned to her with a kind smile. "Come here, good child — let your grandaunt have a proper look at you..."

"May Grand Matriarch Jiang enjoy boundless blessings!" Qin Ruyin curtsied, then said a gentle and composed "Yes," and walked gracefully forward.

The moment she stood before Grand Matriarch Jiang, the old woman reached out and clasped both of Qin Ruyin's hands in hers, smiling as she looked her over from head to toe.

"What a lovely child! Your grandmother is getting on in years, but looking at our Sixth child makes me feel as though I've shed a few years myself!"

Qin Ruyin let herself be examined with perfect composure — not fidgeting, not bashful.

Hearing Grand Matriarch Jiang's praise, she smiled sincerely and said to the old woman: "Grand Matriarch Jiang was already young to begin with. In Sixth child's eyes, you are not a great many years older than my mother."

"Oh, you child!"

Who doesn't like to be told they look young?

The older a woman grows, the more she treasures hearing it.

"Your grandmother is at least ten years older than your mother — how could we possibly compare?" Grand Matriarch Jiang said, then turned to look at Grand Matriarch Qin. "Would you not agree, Grand Matriarch Qin?"

What could Grand Matriarch Qin say?

She had no choice but to smile along and agree: "Grand Matriarch Jiang simply has a youthful appearance!"

Though in truth, she was several years younger than Grand Matriarch Jiang.

And compared to Lady Li, she — a stepmother-in-law — looked considerably older.

She hadn't known that Qin Ruyin had such a gift — with just a few words, the girl had managed to make the most formidable and hard-to-please woman in the entire noble ladies' circle glow with delight...

Even Lady Tong, who had been sitting quietly to one side without saying much, glanced at Qin Ruyin several times in marked surprise.

She did not think too deeply about it.

She simply assumed that since Qin Ruyin was about to marry into the Jiang household, it had been Lady Li who had coached her daughter at the last minute on how to flatter her future mother-in-law...

"Good child! Old grandaunt has a wonderful affinity with you — take this bracelet!"

Grand Matriarch Jiang slid a bracelet from her left wrist — luminously translucent, clearly priceless — and slipped it onto Qin Ruyin's wrist in one smooth motion.

When Grand Matriarch Jiang had arrived at the Qin residence that day, her two wrists had been adorned with different bracelets.

On her right wrist was a pale, translucent green jade bracelet she had bought in her youth.

On her left wrist was the priceless, finest bloodstone red jadeite bracelet — an heirloom from her grandmother, given to her as part of her own dowry.

And now she had given it to Qin Ruyin at their very first meeting.

This meant she was thoroughly pleased with Qin Ruyin as her future daughter-in-law.

Qin Ruyin had not anticipated that the old woman would hand her such a magnificent bracelet at their first meeting.

In her shock, she immediately cried out "Grandmother."

In formal terms, Grand Matriarch Qin was her elder — and if Grand Matriarch Jiang wished to give her a gift, the decision of whether she could accept it rested with the elders of the household.

Grand Matriarch Qin was certainly a woman who knew the value of such things. Seeing Grand Matriarch Jiang hand over something this priceless at the very first meeting, her feelings were deeply conflicted.

When Grand Matriarch Jiang had met her Fifth granddaughter for the first time, she had given only a white jade bracelet of decent quality.

In terms of value, that white jade bracelet was not even fit to hold the shoes of this bloodstone red jadeite bracelet, which was so rare as to be almost priceless...

"Grand Matriarch Jiang, this... this is far too precious!" Grand Matriarch Qin's heart smarted with resentment, and on impulse she moved to refuse the gift on Qin Ruyin's behalf.

chapters.title: Joining Forces with Her Future Mother-in-Law
chapters.content:
Lady Tong, watching Grand Matriarch Jiang shower Qin Ruyin with such favor, kept the smile on her face but let her gaze go cold.

"Grand Matriarch Jiang's gift upon meeting is truly too precious — our Sixth child is still so young, I'm afraid she might not be able to bear the weight of it..."

The two of them — that mother-in-law and daughter-in-law — were not being polite. Grand Matriarch Jiang, who had been navigating the inner chambers of great households for more than half a lifetime, certainly understood their meaning.

This pair of women simply could not stomach seeing their future mother-in-law take such a liking to Sixth child, and giving her so much face.

Grand Matriarch Jiang laughed coldly inside. She was going to give Sixth child all the face in the world.

So before Lady Tong could finish speaking, she cut her off with a look of mild displeasure.

"What is Second Madam Qin saying! The young ladies of the Qin family are each and every one of them wonderful — how could they not be worthy of the gift this old woman is offering?"

Seeing Grand Matriarch Jiang shield her this way, Qin Ruyin felt a wave of relief.

If Grand Matriarch Jiang was willing to protect her like this, then her visit to the Qin residence today was not about the bride price after all.

Lady Tong, having been so directly turned away by Grand Matriarch Jiang, felt her face flush hot.
