Grand Matriarch Qin was also caught off guard.

She had long heard that Grand Matriarch Jiang was blunt and uncompromising, but she hadn't expected her to be so openly dismissive of Lady Tong's dignity even while she was a guest in their home.

Lady Tong held an imperial title of no small standing!

And what did Grand Matriarch Jiang mean by that remark?

The young ladies of the Qin family were each and every one of them wonderful — meaning Sixth child was better than her own treasured granddaughter Fifth child?

Grand Matriarch Qin was displeased as well.

But she dared not offend Grand Matriarch Jiang.

So she simply directed all her swallowed grievance at Qin Ruyin, shooting her a cold and chilling glance.

Meanwhile, Grand Matriarch Jiang was also quietly displeased.

She had come to the Qin residence today at the behest of her most worrisome fourth son, who feared that the Qin second branch would look down on her future fourth daughter-in-law and had specially asked his old mother to make the trip...

Not to mention that the Qin second branch and Grand Matriarch Qin had pulled a last-minute substitution in the engagement, toying with the Jiang family repeatedly — she, as the elder, could not simply swallow that.

"Grand Matriarch Qin, forgive this old woman for asking out of turn — is our Sixth child's mother, First Madam Qin, busy managing the household accounts at the moment?"

Grand Matriarch Jiang continued: "I have met Second Madam Qin a few times, but have yet to meet First Madam Qin. Since we are about to become proper relatives, this old woman thought that since she's here today, she'd also like to have a word with First Madam Qin."

Lady Tong, upon hearing this, was fuming inside.

They weren't even properly related yet, and Grand Matriarch Jiang was already going around calling her "our Sixth child"?

And she was deliberately bringing up that Lady Li, that woman of no consequence.

Everyone in their social circle knew it was the second branch that ran the Qin household.

Lady Li managing the household finances? Was she even qualified?

And calling Lady Li "proper relatives" — this was a blatant dismissal of her, Second Madam Qin, without any pretense at all!

If the occasion had been appropriate, Qin Ruyin would have applauded Grand Matriarch Jiang.

Now she understood completely.

Grand Matriarch Jiang was doing this on purpose.

She couldn't let Grand Matriarch Jiang fight this battle alone.

"To answer Grand Matriarch Jiang — in this household it is Second Aunt who manages the accounts. My mother's quarters are some distance away, so I imagine she has not yet heard that you have come to call. Otherwise, she would surely have come out to welcome you first thing."

The moment Qin Ruyin said this, the faces of both Grand Matriarch Qin and Lady Tong changed color.

Even Grand Matriarch Jiang was caught momentarily off guard.

Then she smiled.

This Sixth Miss Qin showed little on the surface, but was inwardly astute.

What a perfect opening she had just provided!

She looked at Grand Matriarch Qin. "Grand Matriarch Qin, it's no concern of mine whose responsibility the household accounts fall under. But First Madam Qin is about to become my proper relative — surely you would not object to this old woman paying her a visit?"

Grand Matriarch Qin felt as if her face were burning.

Lady Tong felt as though she had been given a hard slap.

Grand Matriarch Jiang's amused, half-smiling expression was a nightmare for them both.

This was an open and unabashed mockery of the Qin family for disregarding the proper seniority between main and second branches, of how they were deliberately suppressing the main branch...

A strange and awkward tension settled over the room.

Neither Grand Matriarch Qin nor Lady Tong wanted to be the first to speak.

Saying too much would only invite more trouble.

At that moment, a young maid stepped forward to announce that "First Madam has come to pay her respects to the Grand Matriarch."

Grand Matriarch Qin's expression grew even darker.

That first daughter-in-law of hers!

Of all times, she had to come now.

She had doubtless heard that Grand Matriarch Jiang had come to the residence and rushed over to curry favor with her.

She had very much not wanted Lady Li and Grand Matriarch Jiang to have this meeting.

But now that the woman was already here, there was nothing to be done.

Especially when she saw Grand Matriarch Jiang's face light up with excitement, her body leaning forward slightly as she looked toward the doorway — Grand Matriarch Qin felt her chest tighten further still.

Qin Ruyin, for her part, thought Grand Matriarch Jiang's expression looked positively gossip-hungry.

This old matriarch had come out victorious from the inner-chamber battles of a great household — she had likely already calculated that Lady Li's arrival would bring her some fresh entertainment.

That was why she made no effort to conceal her expression. She might even be deliberately trying to vex Grand Matriarch Qin and Lady Tong.

The moment Lady Li walked in, before she could even finish her greeting, Grand Matriarch Qin cut across her and seized the first word.

"First daughter-in-law, you have been so busy these past days preparing Sixth child's dowry. I didn't know you'd found a free moment today."

"Had I known, I would have sent someone to invite you over to keep Grand Matriarch Jiang company."

The implication was that she had not deliberately excluded Lady Li from welcoming Grand Matriarch Jiang.

Rather, it was that Lady Li had been too preoccupied with her daughter's dowry to be disturbed.

Grand Matriarch Qin's words were plausible enough — as long as Lady Li, for the sake of the Qin family's reputation, chose not to contradict them.

Unfortunately, Grand Matriarch Qin was quickly disappointed.

Lady Li said with wide-eyed astonishment: "Oh, Grand Matriarch — with Sixth child having you to dote on her, and her second uncle and aunt to cherish her, what need is there for me to trouble myself over that child's dowry!"

Grand Matriarch Qin and Lady Tong both felt a jolt of alarm.

But it was already too late.

Lady Li smiled and said to Grand Matriarch Jiang: "Grand Matriarch Jiang, please do not laugh at us — our main branch of the Qin family has many children, and plenty of people outside say we are rather strained for means. But our Sixth child has her grandmother and her second uncle and aunt to love and care for her. Our Grand Matriarch reassured us long ago, and told us to put our minds at ease — our Sixth child's dowry will be taken care of by them."

Grand Matriarch Jiang understood at once the deeper meaning Lady Li had woven into her words.

So the main branch of the Qin family was also seething with resentment over the substitution.

Lady Li wanted to use the opportunity of her presence here today to openly announce that Grand Matriarch Qin and the Qin second branch would be supplementing Sixth Miss Qin's dowry... and so she was naturally going to help Lady Li along.

She followed Lady Li's cue and showered Grand Matriarch Qin and Lady Tong with lavish praise.

This meant that even if Grand Matriarch Qin and Lady Tong had been planning to offer only show dowry with no real substance, they could no longer do so.

The pair of them were nearly livid enough to spit blood.

But with Grand Matriarch Jiang present, there was nothing to be said.

They could only direct their hatred at Lady Li.

Lady Li paid them no mind whatsoever.

She had come not only because of Grand Matriarch Jiang.

She had come even more for her beloved little daughter!

After exchanging a few polite words with Grand Matriarch Jiang, Lady Li feigned mild exasperation with her little daughter.

"You child — you're altogether too soft-natured!"

Grand Matriarch Qin and Lady Tong both tensed.

Grand Matriarch Jiang's eyes immediately lit with keen interest.

"First Madam Qin, what is the matter? Our Sixth child is so obedient and gentle — why would you come in and chide her the moment you arrive?"

chapters.title: Goal Achieved
chapters.content:
Grand Matriarch Jiang was protecting Qin Ruyin.

Lady Li's eyes brightened at once. Knowing that Grand Matriarch Jiang intended to join forces with their main branch, she immediately launched into her complaint.

"Grand Matriarch, you don't know — this child of mine is the kindest and most uncontentious soul, always taking pity on those weaker than herself... She has always been generous toward the servants who wait on her, and in her indulgence has allowed some of them to grow more and more presumptuous."

Grand Matriarch Jiang immediately frowned. "Sixth child being benevolent is a virtue, but she cannot be bullied by insolent servants either."

"You are absolutely right — that is exactly what I said." Lady Li nodded in vigorous agreement.

Lady Tong, watching Lady Li and Grand Matriarch Jiang play off each other so smoothly, finally could not hold herself back. With a smile that did not quite reach her eyes, she looked at Lady Li and said: "Elder Sister-in-law, Sixth child is about to marry into Grand Matriarch Jiang's household — how is it that she cannot even manage the people around her?"

"Elder Sister-in-law should privately coach Sixth child rather than bringing this kind of matter to Grand Matriarch Jiang's ears, lest Grand Matriarch Jiang think less of us."

Grand Matriarch Jiang's brow furrowed slightly.

She gave Lady Tong a mild look.

As a second branch daughter-in-law, to speak to the main branch's elder sister-in-law in such an impatient, almost admonishing tone — and to do so right here, in front of an outsider like herself.

It was evident just how overbearing Lady Tong was toward Lady Li, the senior sister-in-law, in their day-to-day lives.

So Grand Matriarch Jiang set down her teacup without drinking from it.

Slowly and pleasantly, she said to Lady Tong: "This old woman does not find it a laughing matter — Second Madam Qin, please do not misread me."

Lady Tong: "..."

Her teeth ached!

This Grand Matriarch Jiang truly gave no one any face...

Grand Matriarch Qin looked at Grand Matriarch Jiang with a complicated expression.

With age comes wisdom — she had come to see Grand Matriarch Jiang's game clearly.

Only, being this blunt was not the kind of bluntness one expected.

It was not as though Grand Matriarch Jiang could not manage her words — she had lost her husband in middle age, raised all six of her legitimate children by herself, seen her sons succeed and her daughters make fine marriages. Could a woman who had accomplished that truly be lacking in tact?

Moreover, she treated Lady Li and the Sixth Miss Qin mother and daughter pair with perfectly gracious warmth!

It was plain then. The Jiang family was pragmatic.

They gave face only to the person the Jiang family was marrying.

Seen in this light, Sixth child was... truly about to leap from branch to branch and become a phoenix.

And with a match like the Jiang family behind the main branch — one that placed real importance on them — the main branch's standing would never be the same again.

Grand Matriarch Qin suddenly felt a surge of deep regret...

While she was lost in thought, Grand Matriarch Jiang was quietly questioning Lady Li about something else.

Lady Li's voice rose suddenly.

"This child of mine... a maidservant who waited on her was being careless and dropped a jade hairpin that our Grand Matriarch had given the girl..."

"If it had only been one of her own hairpins that was dropped, one could let it pass — but to go and drop one that our Grand Matriarch had given her as a gift!"

"Sixth child could not bear to see the maid punished and came to ask me to show the girl mercy and keep the matter from the Grand Matriarch..."

Grand Matriarch Qin truly could not bear to listen any longer to Lady Li chattering away at Grand Matriarch Jiang.

She frowned and looked at Qin Ruyin. "Sixth child, this kind of behavior simply will not do! You are the mistress — when your servants do wrong, you must guide them. Administering rewards and punishments with a clear hand is the proper way to manage those beneath you!"

Hearing her words, Qin Ruyin inwardly rolled her eyes.

This old woman never tired, did she.

Every word dripped with motive.

Lady Li, hearing her words, twisted her lips.

With some difficulty she said: "But... but Grand Matriarch, the one who did wrong was Zicao!"

"It was Zi-..." Grand Matriarch Qin nearly bit through her own tongue. "Our household has always been clear about rewards and punishments. There is merit, so there is reward; there is fault, so there is consequence."

"However, Sixth child's reluctance to punish the maid speaks to the bond between mistress and servant. A light reprimand will suffice."

Zicao was her plant.

Grand Matriarch Qin made a show of raising the matter, then set it down lightly.

Lady Li gave a cold laugh inside, and said: "Grand Matriarch's words are precisely right — that was my thinking as well. But little did I know the girl would have the audacity to talk back to Sixth child..."

She left these words deliberately unfinished, and looked at Grand Matriarch Qin with an expression of implied meaning.

Grand Matriarch Qin, finding her intolerable, was about to speak.

Qin Ruyin curtsied respectfully.

"Grandmother, this is not the first or second time Zicao has talked back to me... She has always relied on being a maidservant from Grandmother's courtyard to lord it over the others in mine."

Grand Matriarch Qin's eyes went cold, quite displeased that Qin Ruyin was making this kind of complaint in front of Grand Matriarch Jiang.

But she had to respond appropriately on the surface. "Oh? To think such a thing has been happening? Why did Sixth child not mention this to Grandmother before?"

Qin Ruyin thought to herself: My whole family lives under your thumb and the second branch's hand — would the original girl have dared say anything to you?

But now, she was not afraid.

"In reply to Grandmother — Sixth child thought she was someone Grandmother had given me, and endured it. Only... times are different now. Sixth child is about to be married and must bring people with her. Zicao is simply not suited to come along."

Grand Matriarch Qin's face cycled through alternating shades of dark and pale.

Meeting Grand Matriarch Jiang's half-amused, half-probing gaze, she wished she could sink into the floor.

Fortunately, Lady Li said: "Grand Matriarch has always shown such loving care to the younger generation — perhaps you could appoint another good maid to serve Sixth child?"

Grand Matriarch Jiang raised her handkerchief to her lips as though to dab at the corner of her mouth.

This pair of mother and daughter were truly too entertaining!

"Any maid from this old woman's own courtyard who relies on that connection to bully her mistress will not be let off lightly by me!"

"Sixth child has been put through this, and that is my fault." Grand Matriarch Qin smiled at Qin Ruyin — a smile that reached nowhere near her eyes. "How about this: I'll have your Second Aunt select a few girls of good character and capable bearing to serve as your trousseau maids?"

Qin Ruyin let out a quiet sigh inside.

This old woman — she was truly set in her ways.

Did she still think that she and her mother were as easy to handle as before?

Did she not see what the situation was now?

Having Lady Tong handpick girls to serve as her trousseau maids.

chapters.title: A Fine Match Indeed
chapters.content:
Was she also going to send several eyes and ears straight into her side all at once?

Grand Matriarch Jiang watched Qin Ruyin with a faint, pleasant smile, waiting to see what the girl would say and do.

Anyone with half a wit would know that the girls Lady Tong chose were bound to be planted as informants at her side.

Qin Ruyin tilted her head. "Grandmother, I'd like to make my own selection."

Grand Matriarch Qin took it calmly enough, but Lady Tong could not restrain herself from letting her expression go stiff.

"Sixth child, are you saying you don't trust your second aunt? That she wouldn't choose good girls for you?"

Qin Ruyin smiled and shook her head. "Second Aunt misunderstands. Sixth child also wants to learn more — Second Aunt once told Fifth Sister that choosing attendants is itself an important skill."

Lady Tong had been deflected by Qin Ruyin and felt a knot of frustration tighten in her chest.

And there was nothing she could say.

To make matters worse, Grand Matriarch Jiang offered a word of praise: "Our Sixth child is truly an eager little learner. She has initiative and decisiveness — that is exactly as it should be!"

Grand Matriarch Qin had no choice but to say: "What Grand Matriarch Jiang says is right — then it will be as Sixth child wishes."

Qin Ruyin immediately curtsied her thanks.

Then, with her eyes wide and innocent as dark jade, she looked at Grand Matriarch Qin artlessly and said: "Grandmother, shall Sixth child appoint Zicao as a third-rank maid then?"

"Cough, cough..."

Grand Matriarch Qin's mood soured, and she broke into a fit of coughing.

"Sixth child, that Zicao girl is a poor sort who acts against her mistress — you'd best send her to your grandmother. I'll have someone give her a thorough education..."

"Yes, as Grandmother wishes."

Having achieved her aim, Qin Ruyin smiled even more innocently.

Grand Matriarch Jiang looked at the girl — beaming, radiant, and utterly untroubled — and could not quite determine whether this young woman was truly guileless... or simply playing the part.

She thought: whether she is genuinely innocent or not, she is my Jiang family's daughter-in-law now. As her mother-in-law, I shall protect her without question.

That was also the wish of her fourth son!

After Grand Matriarch Jiang bid her farewells and departed, Grand Matriarch Qin looked at Lady Li and Qin Ruyin with a darkening expression.

Qin Ruyin, as if sensing it, met Grand Matriarch Qin's gaze.

"Grandmother, is something the matter? Has Sixth child done something wrong?"

Grand Matriarch Qin paused — still the same slow-witted girl.

Then she instantly rearranged her expression into a warm smile, and said gently: "What are you saying? Sixth child did wonderfully — look at how much Grand Matriarch Jiang liked you!"

Then she turned to look at Lady Li. "Don't you agree, First daughter-in-law?"

"Indeed — I could see too that Grand Matriarch Jiang is very fond of my Sixth child!" Lady Li replied.

Her goal accomplished, Lady Li could not quite conceal her delight.

She had also noticed that the Jiang family had clearly been nursing their own grievance — otherwise, why would Grand Matriarch Jiang have gone to the trouble of personally calling on them to lift the main branch up and humiliate Grand Matriarch Qin and the second branch?

Grand Matriarch Qin and the second branch had schemed to swap her Yin'er out of a good match, and had ended up dropping a rock on their own feet!

Served them right!

"And we must also thank the Grand Matriarch for coming up with the idea of this substituted engagement!" Lady Li said cheerfully, then looked at the stiff-faced Lady Tong. "Second Sister-in-law — would you not agree?"

"This match may have been swapped, but it has left everyone happy. What more could one ask for? I, as elder sister-in-law, also hope that the Wang family will take a liking to your Fifth child."

In high spirits, Lady Li was in no hurry to leave.

She was absolutely determined to needle these two — the old matriarch and her daughter-in-law Lady Tong.

After just a few exchanges, Grand Matriarch Qin and Lady Tong both realized that Lady Li was deliberately taking her revenge.

She was riding the wave of Grand Matriarch Jiang's favor, with the Jiang family's match behind her — and there was nothing the old matriarch and Lady Tong could do to her for it.

And if they tried to suppress the main branch again in the future, they would have to weigh their actions far more carefully.

Grand Matriarch Qin, out of consideration for the Jiang family's standing, was unwilling to openly offend Lady Li — yet she could not bear the sight of Lady Li's smug, taunting face.

She made a decisive move and dismissed the mother and daughter at once.

Lady Li, having already had her fill of satisfaction, did not linger. After Qin Ruyin curtsied goodbye to the pair of them, Lady Li took her daughter by the arm and walked out.

Lady Tong watched the mother and daughter lift the curtain and leave, then could not help saying, with cold simmering fury:

"Mother, I didn't think the Jiang family would be so impatient — they're not even proper relatives-in-law yet, and they've already rushed over to pick a fight with us..."

Grand Matriarch Qin rubbed her aching temples. "That wasn't impatience. They had calculated that lifting the main branch up was something we could do nothing about..."

Grand Matriarch Jiang truly was an old woman who refused to suffer even the smallest slight!

She had originally thought that no matter what, the Qin second branch was far more powerful than the main branch.

Even with a substituted engagement, the Jiang family, out of consideration for her second son's face, ought not to have behaved this way...

She had miscalculated.

As for the Jiang family going forward — she would have to tread carefully.

But for now, Fifth child's marriage was the most pressing matter. The Wang family alliance was what the Qin family needed most.

"Mother, never mind all the rest — did you see how presumptuous Elder Sister-in-law was just now? How could she dare conduct herself like that in front of you?"

"The way she was preening — getting above herself the moment she got lucky... and Sixth child hasn't even married into the Jiang household yet!"

The implication was plain: if Sixth child did go to the Jiang household and earned Jiang Fourth Master's favor, there would be no telling how far Lady Li's arrogance would go.

Grand Matriarch Qin understood.

But she cast Lady Tong a cold, sidelong glance.

"If Sixth child truly earns Jiang Fourth Master's affections after she marries in, we cannot afford to judge her by our old standards."

After a pause, her tone carried the weight of a warning. "Second daughter-in-law — you must not let the petty grievances between women cloud your judgment when the careers of the men in this household are at stake."

Lady Tong's face went pale, and she immediately lowered her head. "This daughter-in-law would not dare!"

Grand Matriarch Qin was satisfied with her compliance.

Her tone softened slightly.

Lowering her voice, she said: "However, we must have someone keep a close watch on the main branch — and if necessary..."

Lady Tong's heart eased.

The old matriarch still held the second branch closest in her heart.

Of course — their lord was Grand Matriarch Qin's own flesh and blood.

If not for the other side occupying the elder son's position, their lord would be the rightful First Master of the Qin family.

And the second branch, by rights, ought to be the Qin main branch...

Seeing a strange expression cross Lady Tong's face, Grand Matriarch Qin frowned. "You know what to do?"

Lady Tong snapped back to attention and bowed her head respectfully. "Mother need not worry — this daughter-in-law understands..."

Lady Li swept Qin Ruyin into her own courtyard, her whole person buzzing with energy.

When she told her two daughters-in-law about how Grand Matriarch Jiang had protected Qin Ruyin that day...

Both daughters-in-law were glad for their young sister-in-law's sake.

Yet seeing how openly overjoyed their mother-in-law was, they could not help feeling a touch of unease inside.

And a touch of amusement too.

When the substitution was first announced, their mother-in-law had refused to see anything good in the Jiang family's match — as though Sixth child were being flung into a fire pit.

They could hardly show this on their faces, of course.

After Lady Li sent the two sisters-in-law off to their tasks, the eldest sister-in-law Lin and the second, Meng, pulled Qin Ruyin aside for a quiet word.

Lin and Meng were both sharp-witted women.

Both of them were privately wondering about Grand Matriarch Jiang's true purpose in calling on the Qin residence that day...

But seeing how happy their mother-in-law was — positively exhilarated — they, as daughters-in-law, could not bring themselves to dampen her spirits.

Even so, what needed to be said to their young sister-in-law had to be said.

Of course, they both genuinely wished the best for her.

In their estimation, this match was an excellent one.

Compared to the Wang family's engagement, the Jiang family's was incomparably better in every way.

Aside from the fact that Jiang Fourth Master was considerably older than their sister-in-law, what else was there to find fault with?

He had been married before, yes — but he had waited three full years after his first wife's passing before taking a second wife.

How many men of any means in this world could show that kind of constancy?

And besides, Jiang Fourth Master and his first wife had been married three or four years without producing a single child.

What both sisters-in-law privately considered the finest quality of all was this: Jiang Fourth Master had no weakness for feminine beauty. His inner quarters were entirely clear — not even a cohabiting maidservant to be found.

Their own husbands were good men who treated them well and held them in genuine respect.

Yet even so, the Grand Matriarch had still given each household two girls who had been formally initiated as cohabiting maidservants.

The fact that Jiang Fourth Master could hold fast against the pressure of elders trying to push women on him and keep his household so quiet and clean — that was yet another great point in his favor.

And then there was his official standing — remarkable!

He was a senior second-rank official of the imperial court, at only twenty-eight years of age!

And in the crown prince's youth, he had served as the prince's teacher, though for only a little over a year.

His career truly was extraordinary.

The crown prince's leg had been injured, and many at court had misgivings about him — but the current emperor still held great paternal feeling for him.

And the current emperor, who valued talent deeply, also placed great reliance on Jiang Fourth Master, who had once been the crown prince's teacher. Jiang Fourth Master's future prospects were immense.

In the two hundred years since the founding of the Great Ying dynasty, only two or three men of such breathtaking brilliance had ever appeared.

Grand Matriarch Jiang held an imperial title.

Once their sister-in-law married in, as long as she did not make Jiang Fourth Master dislike her — even if they were merely a couple who treated each other with formal mutual respect — Jiang Fourth Master would certainly petition for her to be granted an imperial title as well.

An imperially titled lady of the second senior rank!

That was a rank higher even than that second aunt of theirs, who seemed to spend her days hoping the main branch would never rise again!

When their sister-in-law came home to visit in the future, by protocol that second aunt would have to greet her first — and then their sister-in-law would return the greeting as to an elder.

As for the rumor circulating outside that Jiang Fourth Master had some unspeakable affliction...

Had anyone actually seen Lord Jiang keep a male favorite?

chapters.title: The Jiang Household Has Its Own Mess
chapters.content:
Seeing is believing; hearing is not.

If Jiang Fourth Master truly was that kind of man...

Nothing had been discovered before the wedding, no evidence had surfaced, and with the second branch domineering and their lord having the final say, the main branch would have no ground to stand on if they tried to contest it.

Therefore, this engagement had no choice but to proceed, and they would simply have to take each step as it came.

All they could do was hope that their husbands' careers would flourish one day, so they would have someone to stand up for the sisters of their own family.

Qin Ruyin listened as her two sisters-in-law shared their little tricks for pleasing a husband and winning over in-laws... and gradually felt herself growing drowsy.

It was only when Lady Li's senior maid came to call the three of them to Lady Li's courtyard for the evening meal that Qin Ruyin perked up again.

Lin and Meng watched her dart off faster than a rabbit, and could only shake their heads with fond exasperation.

Meng turned to Lin with a thoughtful look. "Elder Sister-in-law, hasn't Yin'er been eating more heartily lately?"

"The moment she heard it was time to eat, she lit up... just like my eldest boy."

Lin was momentarily taken aback, her smile still not quite faded.

"Now that you mention it... you're right! I thought I was imagining things, but Yin'er really has filled out a bit."

"Yes, she has." Meng nodded, and added: "It suits her, though — she looks lovelier for it..."

"Doesn't she?"

The two sisters-in-law linked arms and made their way to their mother-in-law's courtyard for dinner.

Meanwhile, over at the Jiang residence.

After Grand Matriarch Jiang returned from the Qin household, she immediately summoned the women of the family.

Fourth Master of the Jiang family was to be married, and the womenfolk of the household had responsibilities to attend to — the more hands to help, the better.

No matter what anyone privately thought, when a joyous occasion arose in the Jiang household — and one that concerned Fourth Master at that — everyone had to appear enthusiastic and willing on the surface.

Once the tasks were assigned, Grand Matriarch Jiang kept the women in her courtyard for the evening meal.

During the banquet, the Jiang family's First Madam, seeing the old matriarch in good spirits, gave a look to a young daughter-in-law from one of the collateral branches.

The young woman knew what was expected of her. She found a pretext and asked Grand Matriarch Jiang about her visit to the Qin household that day.

Grand Matriarch Jiang lifted her eyelids. "We're all family here — there's no harm in telling you."

Everyone's expressions shifted in various ways, but all ears perked up to listen.

Grand Matriarch Jiang, unconcerned with their reactions, continued in her unhurried way: "When this old woman had someone go to the Qin household to arrange the engagement, the person I proposed was the somewhat older Fifth Miss Qin."

"At the time, the Qin Grand Matriarch and the Second Madam Qin — who is Fifth Miss Qin's birth mother — both seemed agreeable when they heard the proposal."

"But who would have guessed — within days, the Qin Second Master came to our Jiang household himself, requesting a substitution."

"Our Fourth Master is a man of benevolence. Unable to bear the thought of harm coming to the Qin young ladies' reputations, he agreed to the substitution."

Having said this, Grand Matriarch Jiang let her old eyes travel across the faces of the daughters-in-law one by one.

The women all exclaimed in astonishment.

"Ah... that was truly improper of them!"

"Are they making sport of our Jiang family?"

"And to think there was such a thing — if Grand Matriarch hadn't told us, we wouldn't even have known..."

"However it's framed, the substitution was the Qin family's doing — the fault lies entirely with them!"

Grand Matriarch Jiang curved the corner of her mouth. "This old woman went to the Qin household today for two reasons: one, to have them give an account; and two, to personally go and look at the Sixth Miss Qin whom Fourth Master had agreed to take."

At this, the round-faced and pleasantly plump First Madam of the Jiang family, a woman named Fang, spoke up.

Her tone was respectful. "Mother, the wedding date has already been set — was it not a little late to go asking for an account at this point?"

Grand Matriarch Jiang shook her head. "As long as our Jiang family truly intends to go and demand one, it is never too late!"

"They behaved this way toward us — our Fourth Master was too benevolent to make an issue of it, but this old woman was not about to let the Qin second branch off without some reckoning."

After a pause, she glanced again at the assembled daughters-in-law. "Besides, our Fourth Master personally agreed to this match, and the Qin main branch is in a weak position. Going there was also a way of making the Qin household understand who they were dealing with."

At this, the faces of Grand Matriarch Jiang's three legitimate daughters-in-law all took on complicated expressions.

They had, in private, already found out the details — the Sixth Miss Qin who had been swapped in for their Fourth Brother-in-law was the youngest legitimate daughter of the Qin main branch.

The Qin main branch had long been suppressed by the Qin second branch, and Sixth Miss Qin had grown up since childhood as little more than Fifth Miss Qin's shadow.

She had likely adopted a meek and compliant manner just to avoid being picked on too much.

Originally, the three of them had not thought much of this little slip of a girl — but for her sake, the Grand Matriarch had gone in person to prop up her standing and give her face?

"Grand Matriarch, you really do dote on our future little Fourth Sister-in-law!" the young daughter-in-law from the collateral branch said in a sweet, admiring tone.

Then her manner shifted. "Only, Grand Matriarch — this niece-in-law has heard the Qin main branch is not well-off. I'm afraid that when the time comes, if the dowry is too meager, it might bring embarrassment on our Jiang family as well."

Grand Matriarch Jiang gave her a mild glance. "How considerate of you to worry about that, daughter-in-law of Rong — but there's no need."

The young woman received a small pointed look from Grand Matriarch Jiang, and her heart gave a startled thump. She dared not say another word.

Grand Matriarch Jiang did not hold it against her.

On the contrary, she had genuinely needed someone to bring up Sixth Miss Qin's dowry.

This gave her the perfect opening to casually mention that Sixth Miss Qin's dowry would be supplemented by Grand Matriarch Qin and the Qin second branch.

When the assembled daughters-in-law heard that there was this unexpected bonus to speak of, they all quietly felt envious of Sixth Miss Qin.

What remarkable fortune.

This substituted engagement not only had her becoming the principal wife of a senior second-rank official — the matter of her dowry, which would otherwise have been easy to look down on and mock, had also been made whole.

Under ordinary circumstances, a private grand matriarch with a large personal fortune would only give an additional dowry portion to a granddaughter she had personally raised.

For a second-branch uncle and aunt to also provide an additional dowry portion was an extremely rare occurrence.

"Mother, by that account, Sixth Miss Qin truly has a charmed lot."

First Madam Fang smiled at Grand Matriarch Jiang. "A girl with such fortune, marrying our household's Fourth Master — it could not be more fitting."

Grand Matriarch Jiang sighed quietly to herself.

This eldest daughter-in-law, who had married her eldest son as his second wife, came from a decent family and had no lack of ability.

She was not a bad person either — she simply had too much of a tendency to fret and scheme.

What was the point in elevating Sixth Miss Qin so dramatically?

She was not like that muddled old fool of a grand matriarch in the Qin household.

The Qin family had a legitimate eldest son and heir, and yet she had broken all proper precedent to suppress the first branch — the children of the first wife — in every possible way.

And yet she still wanted to save face and couldn't bring herself to let the main branch split off and establish their own household.

She insisted on keeping them all under one roof, then handing the management of the house to the second branch daughter-in-law.

She herself would never overstep the eldest daughter-in-law and put the fourth's new wife in charge of the Jiang household accounts.

Not unless the eldest daughter-in-law proved incapable — in which case, she would simply choose the most suitable candidate from among the other branches' daughters-in-law.

As things stood, even though the eldest daughter-in-law was also a second wife — a successor to the first —

Since she had taken over the household accounts, she had managed things to everyone's satisfaction.

After the evening meal, Grand Matriarch Jiang had her attendants see the women of each branch back to their quarters, and kept only First Madam Fang behind.

Fang was from the Fang family of Tongcheng's prominent clan, a collateral branch — and it so happened that Grand Matriarch Jiang herself was also from the Tongcheng Fang family, though she was from the main branch.

The Fang family had in recent years produced many capable sons, with more than a dozen of them serving as officials in the capital.

Fang herself had been raised from childhood as a carefully nurtured legitimate daughter of the family.

Her appearance was ordinary, but she was impeccably versed in propriety and carried herself with composed dignity.

In the year she turned twenty, she was still unmarried — and the eldest son's first wife had died of illness, three years having passed.

The eldest son's two daughters were one six and one four years old, and it was time they had a proper mother to raise them.

Grand Matriarch Jiang had admired Fang's ability, judgment, and shrewdness, and so had asked a well-respected older woman to go to the Fang household to propose the match.

Grand Matriarch Fang had been immediately and thoroughly pleased. Though she requested two days to consider, when the matchmaker came again, the Fang family accepted at once.

Over the years, Grand Matriarch Jiang had always been satisfied with Fang, and had often given her recognition and support.

With Fourth Master finally willing to take a new wife, there were important matters that needed to be discussed clearly with Fang, who oversaw the household accounts.

"Mother!"

When Fang heard Grand Matriarch Jiang say that the betrothal gift money to be sent to the Qin household the day after tomorrow amounted to ten thousand and one taels of silver, she was so shocked she lost her grip on her teacup.

chapters.title: What a Mother-in-Law and Daughter-in-Law
chapters.content:
Grand Matriarch Jiang glanced at Fang and gave a signal. Her attendant Hua nanny immediately beckoned the young maids forward to tidy up.

Fang's face had turned a deep red.

She looked at Grand Matriarch Jiang and stammered: "Mother... your daughter-in-law is... your daughter-in-law is..."

"Sit yourself down properly." Grand Matriarch Jiang waved a reassuring hand at her. "As long as you weren't burned."

Fang's face grew even redder. "Thank you for your concern, Mother... your daughter-in-law... this daughter-in-law was not burned."

Grand Matriarch Jiang nodded, and said again in a gentle voice: "Good then."

Then she said to Hua nanny: "Have someone bring First Madam a calming tea."

Hua nanny acknowledged and stepped away.

Grand Matriarch Jiang looked at the anxious face of First Madam Fang and said: "Don't read too much into this. As for the bride price set for Sixth Miss Qin, this old woman was just as astonished when she first heard it from Fourth Master."

"However, Fourth Master said it was a number he himself wished to give, and that the bride price would come entirely from his own private treasury — not a single coin from the communal household funds. This old woman thought it over and, since he was finally willing to remarry, decided to let him do as he pleased."

First Madam Fang kept her gaze lowered, staring at her own feet.

In her heart, she thought: even if this ten-thousand-tael bride price was entirely Fourth Master's own money, not touching the communal funds by so much as a coin — the sheer size of it was still alarming!

Was it necessary to be this ostentatious for a second wife?

When her own husband had married her, the bride price had been one thousand six hundred taels...

At the time, her grandmother and mother had been absolutely delighted.

They said the first wife had received one thousand eight hundred taels, and that one thousand six hundred for her — a second wife — was already without precedent among the families in the capital who took second wives.

At the time she had felt genuinely content — the Jiang family valued her, her husband valued her, and her heart had been thoroughly satisfied.

But now Fourth Master was also taking a second wife, and she had originally thought: times are different, Fourth Master's official rank is high.

Besides, Fourth Master had been close friends since youth with Xu, the eldest son of the imperial merchant family, and had likely accumulated considerable personal wealth through that connection over the years.

But by custom, the bride price for a second wife ought not to exceed what the first wife had received.

Fourth Master's first wife had received a bride price of two thousand taels.

Even if...

Even if Fourth Master harbored resentment toward his first wife and deliberately set a bride price to surpass hers — it still should not have reached ten thousand taels!

Why would Fourth Master do this?

Grand Matriarch Jiang waited patiently as her eldest daughter-in-law turned things over in her mind.

At last, Fang raised her head.

"Mother, this daughter-in-law has no objection. After all, this is Fourth Master's own affair — and his matter of a lifetime."

"Only, this bride price of just over ten thousand taels — is it not a bit... how to put it... even the imperial family's bride prices for their daughters-in-law have often not reached this number."

"It's of no consequence. Fourth Master, since he dares to use this number, must be quite certain it will cause no problems."

Having said this, Grand Matriarch Jiang's brow furrowed slightly. "However, this matter is not suitable for too many people to know. This old woman told you so that you would have a sense of things."

"Understood... your daughter-in-law understands." Fang nodded with a complex expression.

After a pause, she spoke up once more. "Mother, with such a large bride price, what is the Qin family to do?"

Grand Matriarch Jiang smiled. "Ah, but that is no longer our Jiang family's concern — that is something the Qin family ought to be thinking about."

Fang was momentarily stunned.

Then, all at once, it struck her.

Could it be... Fourth Master was retaliating against the Qin family for their last-minute substitution, and had deliberately done this?

With a bride price of ten thousand taels going out, the Qin family would have to bring in a dowry of at least twenty thousand taels in return!

To put it plainly, in the circle of families who cared about their reputation, any family marrying off a daughter with a dowry that merely equaled the bride price would be laughed at behind their backs.

Many prepared dowries three or four times the value of the bride price.

And the Qin main branch was famously scrimping in the capital — expecting them to bring in a dowry of ten thousand miles of red was entirely out of the question.

Thinking further, the Grand Matriarch had mentioned earlier that Sixth Miss Qin's dowry would come in three portions... and so, if the Qin family did not want to lose face, they would have no choice but to have Grand Matriarch Qin and the Qin second branch make up the difference.

And why had the Grand Matriarch returned from the Qin household today, not even stopping to change her clothes, and immediately invited all the women of the Jiang household to dine together?

Which meant that everyone now knew Sixth Miss Qin's dowry was coming in three portions.

The Jiang household had no small number of women, and by tomorrow, word was sure to spread — that Grand Matriarch Qin and the Qin second branch were each preparing a separate dowry portion for Sixth Miss Qin...

Fang's heart was a tangle of sourness and grudging admiration for this mother-in-law of hers, who was approaching her sixtieth year.

This depth of strategy and foresight — she could spend a lifetime studying and never match it.

"First daughter-in-law, I know that Fourth Master's bride price has made your heart uncomfortable."

Grand Matriarch Jiang smiled at her eldest daughter-in-law.

"But you are a mother yourself — your heart, more than anything, is with your own children."

"Fourth Master is my own flesh and blood. In his earlier years, he sacrificed a great deal for the sake of this family, and endured tremendous injustice — all of which you know well."

Grand Matriarch Jiang let out a sigh.

"These years have not been easy for him... Even when he had climbed to where he is now in his career, the court is a treacherous place — has he not walked every step there on the edge of a blade, his heart never quite at rest?"

"Besides, this bride price is coming from his own private treasury... You are his elder sister-in-law. Don't let your thoughts run away with you over something like this."

"And for that matter, your own husband has treated you very well over the years. To know warmth or cold, one must feel it from the inside — as long as you are living well, what does it matter how others fare? Who can say what anyone else's life truly holds?"

Grand Matriarch Jiang picked up her teacup and took a small, quiet sip. "Don't you think?"

Fang understood that her mother-in-law was guiding her.

She thought it over carefully — her mother-in-law was entirely right.

She had lived very well these years. Which of her sisters, full or cousins, did not envy her?

Her husband respected her, her children were filial, and even her mother-in-law often lifted her up and helped her manage the more difficult daughters-in-law below...

Besides, had not her mother-in-law also hinted — who could say what anyone else's life truly holds?

After all, one's lot in life was not decided by how large a bride price one received.

With this thought, the sourness and envious ache in her heart receded by seven or eight parts.

Fang bowed gratefully to Grand Matriarch Jiang. "Mother, please rest assured. With Fourth Master's wedding, Elder Master and I will spare no effort."

Grand Matriarch Jiang nodded with satisfaction and said approvingly: "Your mother knows. You are the one among all my daughters-in-law who puts this mother most at ease — and the most capable of all!"

Fang heard these words and her eyes immediately brightened. She said, a little self-consciously: "Mother, you flatter me far too much..."

"You deserve every word." Grand Matriarch Jiang said with a warm smile.

She turned to Hua nanny. "What became of that set of cat's-eye green jewelry I sorted through yesterday? Find it quickly and give it to First Madam to take home."

Hua nanny smiled and acknowledged.

"Mother, you mustn't!" Fang started, and immediately waved her hands in protest. "Mother, that is the only set of cat's-eye green jewelry you have — it's your most treasured piece..."

It was priceless and practically impossible to find on the market — even money might not be able to buy it!

"Your mother loves you best, and of course the thing she loves most must go to you! Don't refuse — or Mother will be cross."

Grand Matriarch Jiang cut her off.

"This old woman is especially giving it to you now, before your Fourth Brother-in-law's new wife has even come through the door."

Having said this, Grand Matriarch Jiang deliberately gave a little conspiratorial wink.

Fang's eyes grew warm and moist, her heart flooded with a deep, spreading warmth.

With a mother-in-law who treated her this sincerely, what more was there to say?

She could only repay her by managing the household with wholehearted care, and by throwing herself just as wholeheartedly into the preparations for Fourth Master's wedding!

The conversation had more or less come to its natural end.

Grand Matriarch Jiang did not keep Fang any longer, and had Hua nanny personally escort her out of Rong'an Hall.

Once Hua nanny returned, she looked at Grand Matriarch Jiang with a worried expression. "Grand Matriarch, this old servant noticed on the way out that First Madam kept her head down the whole time, looking very pensive — could it be the knot in her heart is still not entirely untied?"

Grand Matriarch Jiang waved a hand dismissively. "Don't worry. That child is a sensible one — she'll think it through and understand."

Then she added: "In our Jiang household, the eldest son is the nominal head — but who doesn't know that the true head of the Jiang family is Fourth Master?"

"Only, Fourth Master is generous and broad-hearted toward each of his brothers, and doesn't bother over trifles."

"The eldest son's first wife gave him two daughters. Fang herself also bore two daughters before finally producing a son."

"Little Junér is only six now — the eldest son is no one to rely on, so who else can they count on but Fourth Master?"

"As for Fourth Master — he may say he doesn't care who he marries, but you've seen for yourself: even if he doesn't feel anything for Sixth Miss Qin of the Qin family right now, once she's married in, they'll see each other often enough. How could he not come to care for her in time?"

"Once a man has given his heart to someone, whoever wrongs the one he holds dear will not go unpunished. The eldest daughter-in-law — as long as she still has little Junér's future to think of — ought not to be at odds with Fourth Master's wife."

"How thoroughly you have thought everything through, Grand Matriarch!" Hua nanny said with wholehearted admiration.

The mistress and attendant were still deep in conversation when a young maid came from outside to announce: "Grand Matriarch — Fourth Master has come to pay his evening respects."

Grand Matriarch Jiang smiled at that and said with a fond, mock-scolding laugh: "What is he doing coming to pay his respects at this hour? He's been coming quite frequently these past few days!"

chapters.title: The Fourth Lord Smiled Several Times
chapters.content:
Jiang Jiuxiao wore a thick dark-blue cloak that made his already tall, slender figure look even more upright, like a towering pine.

Lady Jiang, the old Matriarch, studied him carefully. His hair was slightly damp, and the cloak draped over his shoulders was wet as well.

She asked urgently: "Oh my, is it raining outside?"

Jiang Jiuxiao nodded and unfastened the cloak, which had been dampened by the fine rain. He did not wish for the chill to reach his aging mother.

Nanny Hua stepped forward personally to take the wet cloak.

Lady Jiang called for someone to bring a large clean cotton towel.

"Dry your hair properly, and your face too — mind you don't catch cold." Lady Jiang gave her instructions while watching him. "You still came to find your mother in the rain — what is it this time?"

"I came to ask Mother whether she met Sixth Miss Qin when she visited the Qin household today."

Jiang Jiuxiao accepted the dry cotton towel from Nanny Hua and spoke with Lady Jiang at the same time.

"My, my, that doesn't sound like you at all." Lady Jiang broke into a grin. "You've actually started to care about that little girl?"

Jiang Jiuxiao was momentarily taken aback. Without answering his mother's question, he changed the subject: "When Mother visited the Qin household today, did the Qin family have anything to say?"

Lady Jiang noticed that her son had sidestepped her question but did not mind. She smiled cheerfully and said: "What face would the Qin family have to say anything?"

"But Sixth Miss Qin did surprise Mother today. Having met her in person, she is quite different from what the rumors say."

Hearing his mother say the same thing, Jiang Jiuxiao felt a faint smile rise in his eyes.

He had known this before his mother had.

But Lady Jiang misunderstood.

"Fourth Son, you and Mother are alike — neither of us can stand foolishness. Even a little bit of cunning is better than being an idiot."

"Before, Mother was still worried that Sixth Miss Qin might truly be as the rumors said... what good is it to be meek and obedient? A household like ours has no shortage of scheming and dark dealings in the inner quarters. Now that I've seen her, Mother feels much more at ease."

"Fourth Lord, please have a cup of hot tea to drive away the chill." Nanny Hua personally brought over the tea.

Jiang Jiuxiao accepted it, lifted the lid, and bent his head to take a few elegant sips.

After he set down the teacup, Lady Jiang looked at him again with great interest. "Today Mother also met your future mother-in-law. Now, she is quite an interesting person too."

Jiang Jiuxiao had nothing much to say about his future mother-in-law.

"If Mother finds her agreeable, then she must be agreeable." He glanced at the hour. "Mother, it's getting late — your son will not disturb your rest any further. Please retire early."

He rose to leave.

Lady Jiang called after his retreating figure: "That Sixth Miss Qin girl is a beauty. Mother likes her very much."

Jiang Jiuxiao's steps faltered briefly, then he strode on and left quickly.

"Matriarch, the Fourth Lord has already left the courtyard." A small maidservant came to report.

Lady Jiang nodded.

Nanny Hua waved the little maid away, and once she had withdrawn, turned to Lady Jiang with a smile. "Old Nanny must congratulate the Matriarch."

Lady Jiang looked at her and said with a laugh: "You old thing — didn't you already congratulate me when Fourth Son announced he was taking a new wife?"

Nanny Hua said at once: "But did the Matriarch not notice? The Fourth Lord smiled several times this evening."

Lady Jiang fell into thoughtful silence.

Nanny Hua continued: "Matriarch, just think — the Fourth Lord must be very satisfied with Sixth Miss Qin of the Qin family..."

"But that is strange. Given the Fourth Lord's status, and his age, it would be impossible for him to have ever met that little girl before. Previously... it was only something he agreed to for the sake of the family. How could he suddenly be satisfied with her?"

Nanny Hua did not understand it either. She only said: "Then perhaps the Fourth Lord and Sixth Miss Qin are simply fated for each other?"

Lady Jiang was delighted to hear it. She slapped her knee. "Good! Fate is good — fate is wonderful!"

"Oh yes — the day after tomorrow the betrothal gifts are to be sent to the Qin family. Nothing has gone wrong with them, has it?"

Nanny Hua covered her mouth to hide a smile. "Matriarch, you've already asked six or seven times. Rest assured — old Nanny personally oversaw the inspection three times over. Nothing can go wrong!"

Only then was Lady Jiang reassured.

The Second Branch of the Qin Household.

Second Master Qin had not returned home immediately after leaving work that day. He had gone out with a few colleagues to enjoy himself.

By the time he returned home slightly tipsy, a maidservant sent by Second Madam Qin came at once to summon him to the inner quarters.

In truth, the first half of his evening had indeed been spent drinking with his colleagues; the second half had been spent visiting the tender young beauty, barely eighteen, whom he kept hidden away as his mistress.

Having feasted his eyes on that soft, dewy flower bud, every fiber of his being now rebelled against going to the inner quarters to face his lawful wife, whose beauty had already faded.

He dismissed the maidservant with a perfunctory excuse.

The maidservant, seeing that Second Master was unwilling to go to Second Madam's courtyard, was so frightened that her legs buckled and she fell to her knees.

"Second Master! Please allow old servant to report... Lady Jiang, the old Matriarch, visited the household today. Second Madam wishes to ask you to come and discuss certain matters."

The hint of a tipsy haze vanished entirely from Second Master Qin's eyes.

"What?"

"Lady Jiang, the Matriarch, came here today?"

He said no more and delivered a kick at the maidservant.

"Useless creature! Why didn't you say so sooner..."

The maidservant was sent sprawling onto her back, yet she bit back the pain and scrambled to her feet, retreating to one side and trembling all over.

When Second Madam Qin — née Tong — saw her husband come rushing in, his clothes soaked through, she quickly called for someone to bring dry garments.

She then personally picked up a dry cotton towel to wipe his hair and face.

He shoved her away.

"Tell me first what business brought Lady Jiang, the Matriarch, to the household today!"

Second Madam Qin was startled, but dared not defy him. She relayed everything that Lady Jiang had said during her visit.

"Oh?" Second Master Qin was astonished.

"She came to call us to account?"

"The betrothal is already settled, and the betrothal gifts are to arrive the day after tomorrow — and yet she comes here today to call us to account?"

"What is that old woman scheming?"

Second Madam Qin let out a sigh.

"Your concubine has already discussed this carefully with Mother-in-law. When all is said and done, I fear this may not be a scheme on the part of Lady Jiang at all... The real worry is that today's visit was the Fourth Lord Jiang's idea."

Second Master Qin's thick brows furrowed. He bared his teeth, and the face that still wore a veneer of scholarly refinement tightened for a moment.

Then he let out a cold snort.

"Even if Fourth Master Jiang holds a higher post than I do, he has no right to treat people with such contempt!"

Tong-shi said ingratiatingly: "That Li-shi — the one who knows no propriety — showed up uninvited at Mother-in-law's courtyard and was practically thick as thieves with that old Lady Jiang..."

"Master, you don't know how it upset Mother-in-law."

Second Master Qin's expression darkened. "How is Mother now?"

"Mother is all right for the time being. It seemed Lady Jiang was hinting at wanting to stand up for the First Branch... She even raised the matter of Sixth Miss Qin's dowry."

Second Master Qin fell silent.

When it came to Sixth Miss Qin's dowry, he truly had no wish to give more.

But there was no help for it.

It was Second Branch's idea to propose the exchange in the first place. For Fourth Master Jiang to have agreed, he had no choice but to compensate with Sixth Miss Qin's dowry.

Second Madam Tong knew how tightly her husband guarded his private treasury and did not count on him.

"Mother-in-law's view is that Sixth Miss Qin's dowry will need to be increased by another ten to twenty percent... Otherwise, I fear the Jiang household will keep making trouble."

Second Master Qin's heart was full of vexation.

An additional ten to twenty percent — from both his own mother and the Second Branch!

He did not want to pay it.

Even if his mother and Tong-shi contributed from their own dowries, every extra portion they gave out was one less portion for him and his children.

But there was nothing to be done about it for now.

"Very well. These inner-quarters matters will be left to Madam's labors."

"Master..." Tong-shi glanced at Second Master Qin and hesitated.

Second Master Qin grew even more irritable. "What else does Madam have to say?"

chapters.title: Fifth Miss Qin Has Been Reborn
chapters.content:
Tong-shi read the impatience in his face, and her heart ached.

It had been over a month since the Master had spent a night in her courtyard.

Even sharing an evening meal with her — that too was gone.

"Why aren't you speaking?" Seeing his wife frozen on the spot, Second Master Qin looked at that face of hers, no longer smooth but now lined with wrinkles, and felt only mounting irritation.

Tong-shi came back to herself.

She hurried to say: "Master, it's our Fifth Miss... that child cried dreadfully today."

"Fifth Miss cried? In this household, who would dare aggrieve her?" Second Master Qin's eyes widened immediately.

He might be impatient with his wife, but he cared deeply for his children — especially the three born of the legitimate wife: two sons and a daughter.

He had always doted on them.

And Fifth Miss Qin was his only legitimate daughter — and his youngest girl.

Not only was she beautiful, her learning was also exceptional. The zither, chess, calligraphy, painting, and needlework — she was accomplished in all of them.

She enjoyed quite a fine reputation in the circles of noble young ladies in the capital.

Whenever he gathered with his colleagues, a compliment or two about her was never wanting.

If he had not kept a tight mouth and refused to commit, his Fifth Miss would have been betrothed long ago — she would never have remained unmarried until seventeen, only to have her match decided after that incident.

And all because he, as a father, could not bear to see his most beloved daughter leave home too soon.

Seeing that he still doted on their Fifth Miss as always, Tong-shi felt considerably better.

A look of helpless resignation crossed her face. "It is still over the dowry..."

"The child learned that Mother-in-law had decided to add another ten to twenty percent to what was already set aside for Sixth Miss — which is already quite a bit more than what was originally prepared for Fifth Miss. The poor girl was unhappy about it, so of course she cried bitterly."

Second Master Qin's brows twitched. "Then let us add more to Fifth Miss's dowry as well. We certainly cannot leave her short, or have her look worse than Sixth Miss."

"And besides — she is the elder. Since when has a younger sister's dowry ever surpassed an elder sister's?"

Tong-shi listened to him airily suggest giving Fifth Miss enough to match Sixth Miss's dowry... and felt even more helpless.

"Master, Shuo-er has still not taken a wife yet. Li-yiniang's Lei-er and Liu-yiniang's Ye-er are still young too — do we not need to set things aside for them as well?"

"Your concubine's dowry can't withstand being added to again and again..."

Second Master Qin knew she was speaking the truth.

If nothing else, the fact that she had mentioned his two concubine-born sons made it hard for him to reproach her.

He thought it over and said: "You need not worry about this. I will go to Mother's quarters tomorrow after work."

Tong-shi's heart eased.

Her eyes flickered darkly for a moment. "If it were not for the First Branch going too far, our household would not have been forced into this predicament..."

Second Master Qin's eyes also turned cold.

But he still said: "The matter of the exchange cannot be allowed to create any more complications. The most important thing now is to see both girls in this household safely married off."

Tong-shi curtsied and softened her voice. "Yes, your concubine understands."

"Master... it is still raining outside. Perhaps Master would stay the night here with your concubine—"

"No. I drank some wine tonight, and I'll surely be thirsty in the night. You are no longer young, Madam — rest well. I will not disturb you."

He rose and left.

Tong-shi sprang to her feet, then froze.

What had he said?

He said she was no longer young?

He thought she was old?

She watched helplessly as Second Master Qin walked out of her courtyard. Tong-shi pressed her hand to her chest and sank down onto the heated couch.

"Second Madam, Second Master is only thinking of your wellbeing — please don't read too much into it!"

Nanny Qiu, Tong-shi's wet nurse, stepped forward to support her, her face full of worry.

Tong-shi stared blankly at Nanny Qiu. "Nanny Qiu, am I old?"

Nanny Qiu looked at the girl she had raised from infancy and caught a glimpse of a few white hairs at her temple. Her heart clenched.

But she still forced a smile and said: "What is the young lady saying? The young lady is still young. Why, even I don't think myself old — and I nursed the young lady and raised her myself. How can the young lady say she is old?"

Tong-shi knew Nanny Qiu was comforting her.

Composing herself, she gave a cold command: "Nanny Qiu, send someone to make some inquiries — find out where the Master went today and whom he saw."

Nanny Qiu's heart lurched.

"Nanny must go herself." Tong-shi said coldly.

Nanny Qiu's expression became grave, and she bowed in assent.

After Nanny Qiu departed, Tong-shi roused herself and had her senior maid help her change into fresh clothes. She then draped a hooded cloak over her shoulders and made her way to her daughter Fifth Miss Qin's courtyard.

Qiwu Garden.

Fifth Miss Qin sat before her mirror having her makeup removed, attended by two senior maids.

"Miss, you really outdid yourself today — your eyes are all red and swollen. Servant feels so sorry for you."

Senior maid Hong-xiu gazed at the reflection of her mistress's beautiful face in the mirror — flawless in every regard, save for the red-rimmed, swollen eyes that made her look pitifully forlorn — and could not help but say so.

The other senior maid, Xi-xin, added: "Miss, Second Madam is your own mother — if you have grievances, just tell Second Madam. Second Madam will certainly side with you."

In other words: between mother and daughter, there was no need to cry so earnestly for real.

Fifth Miss Qin looked at the two pretty senior maids reflected in the bronze mirror and gave a faint curve of her lips.

"You two — try thinking a little more."

Her expression then became deeply meaningful.

"Mother may dote on me, but in the end I am only a daughter. The one she loves and pampers most is Shuo-er."

"If I had not wept bitterly in front of her today, how would Mother have put her heart into finding ways to add to my dowry?"

The two senior maids dared not say more.

Fifth Miss Qin looked at her reflection — eyes puffed and red — with some dissatisfaction and furrowed her brow.

Then the small maid guarding the door came in to report that Second Madam had arrived.

Fifth Miss Qin immediately stood up and thought for a moment, then closed her eyes and rubbed them hard several times with her hands.

Her eyes grew even redder.

What Second Madam Qin walked in on was a daughter with swollen, scarlet eyes.

At once she was filled with tender concern.

And then indignation rose right alongside it.

"Are all of you who attend her as useless as the dead? Your mistress's eyes are puffed up like this and not one of you had the sense to go to the main kitchen and ask for a boiled warm egg to press against her eyes?"

The mistress's anger fell on them, and the servants in Qiwu Garden dropped to their knees in a mass.

In truth, Fifth Miss Qin had deliberately told her senior maids not to fetch boiled eggs from the main kitchen.

She was planning to go and cry in front of her grandmother tomorrow as well.

Seeing her mother's anger, she quickly intervened.

Having lived a second life, she was no longer the useless girl of her previous one who had casually trampled and beaten her servants at will.

In this life, she intended to cultivate those who served her — Hong-xiu and Xi-xin especially.

These two senior maids each had their own little schemes, but their loyalty was beyond question.

"Mother, it is not their fault. They were going to fetch the boiled egg from the main kitchen, but your daughter saw it was raining outside and could not bear to have them walk through the rain and get their shoes wet."

"You — always too soft-hearted... Servants who do not attend to their duties must be corrected in good time!"

"Your daughter understands." Fifth Miss Qin helped Tong-shi take a seat, then personally removed her rain cloak and handed it to senior maid Xi-xin.

She also directed Hong-xiu to bring a cup of warming ginger tea.

"It's still raining outside — why did Mother come to your daughter's courtyard?" she asked softly as her mother accepted the ginger tea.

chapters.title: Fifth Miss Qin Fights for Her Dowry
chapters.content:
Tong-shi took two large gulps of the ginger tea, then winced at the heat and set it down.

"Is it not because of your dowry?"

Still smarting from Second Master's rebuff, Tong-shi's tone was less than gentle.

"Just now Mother sent for your father to come to my courtyard and discuss things. Your father said he would go and speak to your grandmother about it tomorrow."

Fifth Miss Qin lowered her head, hiding the sharp gleam in her eyes, and paid no mind to Tong-shi's tone.

She said softly: "Thank you, Mother! Mother is truly the one who loves Fifth Miss best..."

Tong-shi let out a sigh and said quietly: "Fifth Miss, don't say that. Your father loves you dearly too — otherwise he would not go out of his way to speak to your grandmother on your behalf..."

Fifth Miss Qin responded with an "yes," while inwardly she let out a cold laugh.

Did her father truly love her?

She could still recall that in her previous life, after her younger brother entered officialdom, he got above himself and offended members of the imperial family. His very life hung in the balance...

By that time their grandmother had already passed. Even after her mother had emptied out her own dowry it still was not enough, and her father had personally drawn out fifty thousand taels of silver from his private treasury to spread around before her brother was barely able to escape with his life.

As for her mother — she had entered the Qin family with ten li of red-lacquered dowry trappings. Her trousseau was substantial, and she was skilled at managing farmlands and shops.

She had been married into the Qin family for twenty-one years. In those twenty-one years, the income that accumulated annually from her dowry properties alone was already quite considerable.

When her elder brother married two years ago, her mother had privately subsidized him with upwards of thirty thousand taels, not to mention the fertile fields, manors, and prime shops in the capital — all slipped to him in private.

For her father as well — there had been generous subsidies. He had privately been given five manors with over eight hundred mu of farmland each, and four prime shops in the capital.

And when it came to herself? Her mother was only willing to contribute three thousand taels as trousseau silver.

She said that ever since she was born her mother had been accumulating a dowry for her — that those farmlands and manors were all out in the provinces, and that all the dowry goods combined amounted to no more than five thousand taels.

Her father's private subsidy for her had amounted to no more than two manors and two shops.

The same as in her previous life, down to the last detail.

Originally, even if the exchange of betrothal partners had never occurred, she had made up her mind to marry into the Wang family and to demand more from her family on the matter of the dowry.

Now that Sixth Miss Qin was marrying up, her dowry certainly could not be stinted.

What a perfect excuse to use Sixth Miss Qin to extract more dowry for herself.

Fifth Miss Qin felt not the slightest pang of conscience about it.

Having endured that stifling first life, the one thing she now understood better than anything else was this: a woman could do without a man, but she could never do without money!

This betrothal to the Wang family was a match she herself had schemed and plotted to snatch out of Sixth Miss Qin's hands.

She had no intention of releasing it.

The Wang family was not wealthy, which was precisely why they preferred to marry daughters of prosperous households.

In her previous life, Sixth Miss Qin's dowry had been modest — not too small, but not too large either — yet it was said that Wang Er-lang had climbed the official ranks so rapidly precisely because Sixth Miss Qin had spent no small amount of her dowry smoothing the way for him.

If someone like Sixth Miss Qin could manage it, then how could she, Fifth Miss Qin — so much more capable than her — fail?

She would take as much as she could from her family for her dowry!

If they would not give willingly, she would fight for it herself!

"Mother, I say nothing else — I am only worried that if my dowry is less than Sixth Miss Qin's, the Wang family will use it to mock me."

Tong-shi felt a headache coming on and let some impatience slip into her voice.

"What are you worried about? Even if your grandmother refuses to add any more to your dowry, with what your father and I have privately provided for you, the Wang family will treat you with the utmost regard!"

"Mother has worked it out for you — your grandmother's current additions alone include six full sets of jeweled headpieces, and two thousand taels in trousseau silver. Your elder sister-in-law's trousseau silver for you amounts to another thousand taels. Add Mother's three thousand taels..."

"Then add what your maternal grandmother and aunts-in-law are giving as gifts, and just the trousseau silver alone will be over ten thousand taels for you."

Tong-shi pressed earnestly: "And the Wang family are upright scholars who have never been flush with money. With all of that... the Wang family will certainly treat you with courtesy and care. Just go and live your life well — there is nothing to fear!"

She paused, then added: "To be frank — if you were to take these same dowry goods into the Jiang family, that might truly look rather thin. But the First Branch is useless — they cannot give her too much! And anyway, it is a good thing that Sixth Miss is the youngest child of the First Branch. Once those two have married off Sixth Miss, their duty as parents is considered done."

In other words: you are already very well provided for — don't be ungrateful. There is still a younger brother to think of.

Fifth Miss Qin had long since predicted this response from her mother.

Her mother had always favored her youngest brother most.

She did not mind.

She only said softly: "Mother, setting everything else aside — I am simply afraid that if my dowry is less than Sixth Miss Qin's, the Wang family will make me a laughingstock over it."

"Think about it — if one day I were to come to blows with a sister-in-law and she said to me: 'Even in your parents' eyes you don't rank as highly as that cousin of yours who married into the Jiang family. Look at how your legitimate parents clearly furnished that cousin of yours with a better dowry than yours...'"

"Mother, how would you have your daughter answer her back?"

Tong-shi startled.

She reached for the stinging ginger tea and took a fierce gulp.

Had she not already thought of this?

She had thought of it long ago.

She simply had not wanted to dwell on it.

But with her daughter laying it so plainly before her face, she could no longer pretend she didn't know.

"Hmm. It was Mother who was not thinking carefully — I had not considered this... Very well, Mother will add another thousand taels of trousseau silver for you."

Fifth Miss Qin said nothing. She only gazed at her mother with quiet, lingering eyes.

Tong-shi could tell her daughter found it too little.

"Mother, let me tell you something first." Fifth Miss Qin leaned toward Tong-shi and murmured a few words in her ear.

Tong-shi's eyes lit up. "You mean that?"

Fifth Miss Qin stepped back two paces, putting some distance between herself and Tong-shi, and gently shook her head. "Your daughter dreamed this last night... but whether a dream is true or not, no one can say."

Tong-shi was somewhat excited.

She studied her youngest daughter intently and turned it over in her mind.

If that Wang Er-lang were truly to achieve such a brilliant official career in the future, then Fifth Miss, as his principal wife, and the Qin family, as his in-laws, would stand to gain enormously.

With such a capable brother-in-law backing him, how could Shuo-er's prospects be anything but bright?

"Fifth Miss, regardless — since you had such a propitious dream, we should believe it rather than dismiss it!"

Tong-shi steeled herself and said, with evident pain: "This way then — Mother will also give you a prime shop in the capital, and a fertile manor of six hundred mu in the outskirts of the capital."

Seeing Fifth Miss Qin's expression brighten somewhat, she let out a relieved breath.

She added eagerly: "To speak plainly, that prime shop and the manor were being kept for your brother Shuo-er..."

"But Mother believes that you, as his elder sister, would certainly not forget this brother of yours. Consider it a gift from him to his sister for her trousseau."

Fifth Miss Qin said immediately: "Mother, rest assured — what elder sister would ever forget her own brother? Your daughter won't forget Elder Brother either, nor Father and Mother — not a single one!"

Tong-shi's mood lifted instantly on hearing this.

She reached out and patted her forehead, smiling. "Mother's Fifth Miss is the most sensible and the most lovable!"

Fifth Miss Qin smiled shyly.

Then she said again: "Your daughter knows that over the years Mother has given generously to help Father and Elder Brother advance their careers. Shuo-er will need just as much money when he enters officialdom in the future. Mother has already given so much to your daughter — it is already a great deal..."

Then a troubled look crept over her lovely face. "But Mother, I am afraid that even with all of this, it still will not be enough."

"Mm. Mother also hopes your grandmother will add a little more — then perhaps it will be enough to equal Sixth Miss Qin's dowry."

"Even if Grandmother adds a little more, I'm afraid it will still fall short." Fifth Miss Qin shook her head.

Tong-shi frowned. "Why is that?"

chapters.title: The Jiang Family's Betrothal Gifts
chapters.content:
Fifth Miss Qin still remembered that in her previous life, when the Jiang family sent betrothal gifts for her, the betrothal gold had been three thousand taels.

In her previous life, she had been a second wife — a replacement bride — and yet the betrothal gold she received had been a thousand taels more than what Jiang Si's first wife had received.

In truth, the Jiang family had privately given even more than that.

At the time, both her grandmother and her parents had been overjoyed.

They believed that the Jiang family was showing how much they valued her, and that was why so much had been given.

In this life, the Wang family was likewise an excellent match, and Wang Er-lang was taking her as his original principal wife.

So in this life, the dowry her family had prepared for her was no different from the previous one.

But there was one point of difference.

That formidable old Matriarch of the Jiang family had personally come to the Qin household after the exchange of brides had been agreed upon...

She had sent her confidants to find out — it was plain that the old Matriarch of the Jiang family intended to stand up for Sixth Miss Qin.

This meant it was quite possible that the Jiang family's betrothal gold for Sixth Miss Qin would be more than what they had given for her in her previous life.

Though it grated on her, she did not see it as a bad thing.

It would give her leverage to press her father and grandmother into producing more for her dowry.

So whether or not the Jiang family would give Sixth Miss Qin a larger betrothal gold than they had given for her in her previous life, she could make use of it either way.

"Mother, I did not give it much thought before — but now that Lady Jiang, the Matriarch, has visited our household today... your daughter suspects that the dowry being prepared for Sixth Miss may not look very impressive."

Seeing her daughter's brow furrowed, Tong-shi was startled. "How do you mean?"

Fifth Miss Qin looked earnestly at her mother. "Mother, the day after tomorrow the Jiang family is coming to send—"

Tong-shi nodded, then gave a disdainful laugh.
