"On the surface, yes. As for how much the First Branch can privately subsidize Sixth Miss beyond that, well — that depends on what they're capable of."

"Besides, the Jiang family is taking a replacement bride — a second wife. Sixth Miss cannot compare to my daughter, who is going to be a man's original principal wife!"

Thinking about this, Tong-shi no longer envied the Jiang family their status or Fourth Master Jiang his official career.

The Wang family was not bad either!

Wang Er-lang of the Wang family was also a man of exceptional talent — no worse than Fourth Master Jiang in his younger years.

And besides, yesterday Fifth Miss had even had that auspicious dream — such things carry significance.

In the future, Wang Er-lang's prospects would outshine Fourth Master Jiang's by far more than a little...

The more Tong-shi thought about it, the more she felt her daughter was worrying over nothing.

"My dear child, there is no need to fret so much."

"And besides — by custom, the betrothal gold for a second wife cannot exceed that given for the original wife."

"You are young and do not know — the family of the man who preceded Fourth Master Jiang's first wife is not to be trifled with. Even if the Jiang family wanted to give more betrothal gold for the second wife than for the first, they would still have to weigh what the first wife's family might think, would they not?"

Fifth Miss Qin looked at her mother with complicated eyes.

In her previous life, she had always thought her mother was shrewd and capable — able to see through people and situations at a glance.

After living two lives, she now realized that in her previous life, her mother had simply had more years and more experience than her.

Now she herself had more experience than her mother.

She could see further and more clearly.

But she could not be impatient.

She personally poured a cup of reviving black tea for Tong-shi, then said gently:

"Mother, it is better to err on the side of caution. Your daughter can see that Lady Jiang, the Matriarch, is not the sort of person who shies away from a fight."

"Let's do this... Mother said your daughter had an auspicious dream last night. Tomorrow, when I go to pay my respects to Grandmother, I will ask Grandmother to help me interpret this auspicious dream too."

"You should also go to pay your respects to Grandmother, and while you are there, casually mention to Grandmother the matter of the betrothal gold the Jiang family will be giving Sixth Miss Qin."

She paused and added a few more words: "Mother, you should also tell Father about it. Because if... if Wang Er-lang's official career truly does soar in the future, it will be greatly beneficial for Father and Elder Brother too."

That last part, Tong-shi understood perfectly well without Fifth Miss Qin saying it.

Nothing concerned her more than her husband's and sons' careers.

"Good. Mother understands. There is no time to lose — Mother will go to your father's study right now and speak to him about this..."

Fifth Miss Qin made a show of urging her to rest first and not rush.

Tong-shi paid her no heed, rose, refastened her cloak, and hurried through the rain to Second Master Qin's study.

......

Very soon the day arrived when the Jiang family was to send betrothal gifts to Qin Ruyins household.

The Jiang family's display was enormously grand.

It seemed as though they feared the citizens of the capital would not hear that this day, the Jiang family was delivering betrothal gifts to the Qin household for the Fourth Lord's betrothed.

The procession of betrothal gifts was immense, startling the citizens of the capital into opening their doors and crowding around to watch.

Two carriage-loads of celebratory cakes and sweets.

Eight carrying poles of longan and peanuts, each weighing a hundred jin.

A carriage-load of rare delicacies from mountain and sea.

A carriage-load of ceremonial fish and meat...

Most dazzling of all was the betrothal gold of ten thousand and one taels of silver, bright enough to blind the eye.

Beyond this, there were many more large gift items.

When the betrothal gift list was placed in the hands of Old Madam Qin, the little old woman had not finished reading it before her body went stiff.

Her eyelids twitched furiously, and she nearly threw the list to the ground...

Ten thousand and one taels of silver in betrothal gold!

Just to take a second wife?

Or was it to embarrass the entire Qin family?

At this thought, the old woman's expression grew complicated, and she drew in a sharp, pained breath.

Her Fifth Miss!

The betrothal gold the Wang family gave Fifth Miss could never hope to exceed what the Jiang family had given Sixth Miss.

Ten thousand and one taels!

Was the Jiang family trying to tell her, and through her the entire world, that Sixth Miss Qin was that one-in-ten-thousand exceptional young woman they had chosen?

Thinking this, deep regret began to gnaw at her.

Had she known the Jiang family would be so lavish in taking a second wife, she should never have agreed to let Fifth Miss swap this match!

And then she thought back — when Fourth Master Jiang had taken his first wife, the betrothal gold had been two thousand taels. At the time, everyone had thought it generously high.

After all, the First Branch's own eldest granddaughter, Qin Rujin, had just had her betrothal gifts sent at the same time.

Qin Rujin's betrothal gold had been only six hundred taels, and even Li-shi — that shallow creature — had been rather envious of the two thousand taels Fourth Jiang had given his first wife back then.

Round and round, and now Fourth Master Jiang was here to send betrothal gifts for her youngest daughter, and had gone directly up to ten thousand taels...

Li-shi must be on the verge of fainting with joy right now, mustn't she?

She looked up and found Li-shi narrowing her eyes, one hand pressed over her heart and her body tipping backward, so overcome with excitement she was practically about to faint...

Indeed, Li-shi had nearly fainted with excitement.

But she had acutely sensed Old Madam Qin's eyes on her and snapped back to her senses at once.

"Mother-in-law, am I reading this right?"

"The Jiang family... the Jiang family really gave our Sixth Miss ten thousand taels in betrothal gold? Oh — ten thousand and one taels — one-in-ten-thousand! What a wonderful omen!"

"My, my, my... this has truly opened your daughter-in-law's eyes!"

"Yes — of all the betrothal gifts, the betrothal gold is what matters most!" Li-shi's eyes shone as she fixed her gaze on Old Madam Qin.

"And yet the Jiang family has not only given our Sixth Miss ten thousand taels in betrothal gold — the rest of the gifts are enough to frighten a person to death as well!"

"Mother-in-law, you have lived a whole lifetime — surely you have never seen any daughter in our family receive betrothal gold in this kind of number, have you? Ha ha ha..."

Li-shi went on crowing and showing off, making no attempt whatsoever to conceal her exhilaration and excitement.

Old Madam Qin's eyelids twitched.

Second Madam Tong felt as though she could not draw breath, and longed to step forward and slap a few hard smacks across Li-shi's smug, insufferable face.

At this moment, looking at the long betrothal gift list, Second Madam Tong could not deny that her heart was bleeding.

All of this... had originally belonged to her daughter.

What that shameless creature Li-shi said was not wrong.

Usually, the most important part of the betrothal gifts was the betrothal gold. A decent number of forty to fifty carrying poles of goods on top of the gold was generally considered sufficient.

But the Jiang family had not counted things by carrying poles alone — they had counted by carriages!

Old Madam Qin, having lived the most years, was the first to come back to her senses.

A matter of this magnitude was no longer something she, a single old woman, could handle on her own.

She called out in rapid succession: "Quickly, quickly! Go and fetch the First Master and the Second Master — the faster the better!"

chapters.title: Betrothal Gifts So Lavish They Could Frighten a Person to Death
chapters.content:
Lady Qin, the Matriarch, understood clearly in her heart that the betrothal gold and betrothal gifts the Jiang family had sent were startlingly extravagant — more lavish than anything she had encountered in all her years.

More lavish than anything she had ever handled firsthand.

With this, the dowry for Sixth Miss Qin had no choice but to be increased again.

Thinking of such an enormous stroke of good fortune — such an enormous gain of face — that had originally been destined for her Fifth Miss.

That had originally belonged to the Second Branch!

What merit did that girl Sixth Miss Qin have — what virtue, what blessing — to be granted such fine fortune and such splendid fate?

When the Jiang family's betrothal gifts were carried into the First Branch's courtyard, Fifth Miss Qin was busy entertaining her maternal relatives — the young ladies of the Tong family who had come for a visit.

Today the Jiang family, the in-laws of Sixth Miss Qin of the First Branch, was coming to the Qin household to deliver betrothal gifts — a proper family occasion of joy.

The families of the Second Branch and the other branches would all send people to offer congratulations and observe the ceremony.

Fifth Miss Qin's cousins from her maternal grandmother's household were naturally entertained by Fifth Miss Qin.

Fifth Miss Qin had already brought her Tong-family cousins into her mother's courtyard for conversation, when a clever little maidservant rushed in to report the betrothal gifts the Jiang family had sent.

As this quick-witted little maid rattled off the gifts one by one, the smile on Fifth Miss Qin's face gradually became impossible to maintain.

Her cousins from the Tong family also drew sharp successive breaths.

"What a lavish hand..."

One cousin spoke.

"Indeed... I had not expected the Jiang family to be so generous — causing this kind of stir just to take a second wife."

Another exclaimed enviously: "Fifth Miss, your Sixth Younger Sister truly has fine fortune — this hardly looks like taking a second wife. Even a prince of the imperial family taking an imperial consort as his principal wife would not surpass this."

"Although that would have to be a prince of rank, at least an imperial prince or above, taking a princess consort, would it not?"

"The Jiang family is truly generous..."

The more Fifth Miss Qin listened, the worse she felt.

Her gaze grew darker and darker.

"Enough! Stop reporting the gifts — just tell me: how much is the betrothal gold?"

Seeing her expression had soured, one of Fifth Miss Qin's Tong-family cousins cut in and interrupted the little maid.

The little maid looked timidly at Fifth Miss Qin.

Fifth Miss Qin forced a smile onto her face. "Listen to Cousin Fu — just tell me the amount of the betrothal gold."

"Yes..." The little maid began to tremble. "Sixth Young Miss's betrothal gold is... is ten thousand and one taels of silver..."

"How... how much?" Fifth Miss Qin's face turned ashen in an instant.

"In reply to Fifth Young Miss — it is... it is ten thousand and one taels of silver." The little maid could hear that her mistress's voice was wrong and became even more flustered.

This time, every single person in the room drew a sharp breath in unison.

Ten thousand and one taels of silver in betrothal gold!

All those seated were young ladies carefully cultivated in the Tong family — none of them were fools.

The moment they heard "ten thousand and one," they all understood that the Jiang family was making a grand public declaration to the world: Sixth Miss Qin was the one-in-ten-thousand bride they had chosen for their household...

Ten thousand and one taels of betrothal gold to take a second wife...

What on earth was the Jiang family thinking?

With this precedent, among other noble families — never mind taking a second wife — even taking a principal wife, how could the betrothal gold compare?

This ten-thousand-and-one-tael betrothal gold had made those households that offered only a few hundred taels to their brides' families look worse than mud.

After a long silence, one of the Tong-family young ladies pursed her lips and spoke.

"When the founding emperor opened this dynasty, nothing displeased him more than the custom of competing over betrothal gold — how bold of the Jiang family!"

The other Tong-family young ladies all turned looks of sympathy toward Fifth Miss Qin.

What dreadful luck.

Even the Tong family had originally considered Fifth Miss Qin's exchange — marrying into the upright Wang scholarly family — to be the better match, and more advantageous for the Qin household.

Yet now the Jiang family had delivered betrothal gifts on this scale: not only had it thoroughly overshadowed Fifth Miss Qin, it had dealt the Second Branch of the Qin family a resounding slap across the face...

When the Wang family came to send betrothal gifts for Fifth Miss Qin, what would the betrothal gold amount to?

If it reached a thousand taels, that would already be an extraordinary show of regard from the Wang family toward Fifth Miss Qin.

But against the Jiang family's "one-in-ten-thousand" gold for Sixth Miss Qin — it would not even be fit to carry her shoes...

The Tong-family young ladies all felt rather awkward.

Yet they could not say nothing.

Fortunately, several of the older ones forced smiles onto their faces and offered some dry words of comfort to Fifth Miss Qin.

Fifth Miss Qin was dazed.

How could the Jiang family's gifts to the Qin household have been so much better than in her previous life?

The Jiang family had been so generous with Sixth Miss Qin's betrothal gold?

"One in ten thousand"...

Was this Fourth Master Jiang's idea, or the old Matriarch Jiang's idea?

She had spent over ten years in the Jiang household before Fourth Master Jiang dismissed her with a letter of divorce and sent her packing back to her parents' home.

Whether it was Fourth Master Jiang or the old Matriarch Jiang, putting out ten thousand taels in betrothal gold was something either of them could do without a second thought.

The Jiang family's public funds could never have agreed to so much.

Fourth Master Jiang's own brothers, not one of them was easy to deal with.

Not to mention Fourth Master Jiang's uncles, who were even more difficult to contend with, each one an old and wily hand.

They would absolutely never agree to Fourth Master Jiang taking a replacement wife at the expense of the Jiang clan's traditions.

She was therefore quite confident that those ten thousand taels could only have come from Fourth Master Jiang or from the old Matriarch Jiang — or both mother and son together.

"Cousin Qin, all those betrothal gifts the Jiang family gave to Sixth Miss Qin — that isn't necessarily a good thing... They're like a bird that has flown too high — it's the conspicuous ones that get shot down. You yourself..."

One steady-headed Tong-family young lady stepped forward to comfort Fifth Miss Qin, whose expression kept shifting.

Fifth Miss Qin came back to herself.

She thought: she had nearly made a fool of herself in front of her cousins.

She, who had already lived two lives, truly should not have!

So she lifted a smile to her face and said to the Tong-family young ladies: "There is no need for all of you to worry on Fifth Miss's behalf. In truth, Fifth Miss feels it is a good thing that the Jiang family holds my Sixth Younger Sister in such high regard."

Seeing that her smile and her words were both flawless, the Tong-family cousins were puzzled.

In the past, this Fifth Miss of the Qin family had always been a bit competitive at heart.

Knowing that the Wang family coming to send betrothal gifts for her in the future could absolutely never surpass the Jiang family's gold in amount — how could she still smile as though she didn't care at all?

......

In the warm inner chamber of Old Madam Qin's Shouheng Hall, only the First Branch couple and the Second Branch couple were left to speak.

"Old woman will speak first — the Jiang family gave so much — now let us all say what you think."

Old Madam Qin opened with a stern expression.

Tong-shi had been turning everything over in a turmoil before, but had at last calmed down — though she was still in poor spirits and had no desire to speak.

Second Master Qin sat with his head down, turning the white jade thumb ring on his right thumb, his thoughts unknown.

Li-shi was about to open her mouth when the First Master gave her a look.

What was there to say?

Was it not perfectly obvious?

The Jiang family's betrothal gold and gifts had been on this sort of scale — far beyond anyone's prior expectations.

No matter which party had been involved, the dowry already prepared for Sixth Miss was certainly now nowhere near sufficient!

The Matriarch wanted the younger generation to speak first.

Was it not just a matter of saying straight out that the dowry for Sixth Miss needed to be increased — multiplied several times over? What was there to deliberate about?

"Why is no one speaking? Were you all so thunderstruck you still haven't recovered?" Old Madam Qin said this while her old eyes swept over the First Branch couple.

First Master Qin sat perfectly at ease and simply refused to meet Old Madam Qin's gaze.

Li-shi took her cue from her husband and followed suit, even deliberately lifting her teacup and sipping from it with leisurely little sips.

Old Madam Qin was exasperated.

"First Son! First daughter-in-law!" Even with all of Old Madam Qin's practice at composing herself, anger now showed plainly on her old face.

"Sixth Miss is your own daughter — you two speak first as her parents!"

chapters.title: Discussing Adding More to the Dowry
chapters.content:
Seeing that Old Madam Qin had lost patience and called on him directly, First Master Qin straightened himself, rose, and clasped his hands in a bow.

"Mother... it is not that your son does not wish to speak. In truth, your son is still rather dazed."

Old Madam Qin was left at a loss for words.

She had nothing to say.

What ability did the First Son have? That the Jiang family's grand display would stun him went without saying.

She gritted her old teeth, and did not even spare Li-shi a glance.

She turned instead to look at Second Master Qin.

"Second Son, you say — what is to be done?"

Second Master Qin also gritted his teeth inwardly, grinding his molars until they ached, before he let his lips part.

"In reply to Mother — your son had not expected the Jiang family to hold Sixth Miss in such esteem... But however esteemed she may be, this level of betrothal gold and gifts is still excessive."

"By custom, whatever the betrothal gold and gifts amount to, the dowry must at minimum be double."

These words were said with resignation — and with pain.

Tong-shi, thinking that a mere Sixth Miss Qin would need a dowry of at least thirty to forty thousand taels for the trousseau alone, felt as though she could not breathe.

"Then let it be done as Second Son says."

Old Madam Qin looked once again at the First Branch couple.

"You two heard what Second Son said — the dowry for Sixth Miss needs to be increased further. You two calculate — how much more can you produce?"

Li-shi could no longer contain herself.

"Gracious, Mother — as you know, our First Branch has always been in poor straits...

What with our many children, the elder ones have already married off, the younger ones have also been matched, and now Sixth Miss here is marrying high — your son and daughter-in-law have already given all we had!

I won't deceive Mother or my Second Uncle and Second Aunt — your son and daughter-in-law still don't know how Jinhao-er's wedding costs will be managed..."

Whatever Old Madam Qin did with her rolling eyes, crying poor was the right move!

This time, First Master Qin did not stop his wife either.

He even gave the faintest curve of his lips.

Second Master Qin's eyelids twitched in agitation.

Tong-shi nearly couldn't stop herself from pointing at Li-shi and screaming what a shameless creature she was.

Was this the sort of thing to say?

Whose daughter was Sixth Miss Qin anyway?

It was as though the Second Branch was the one marrying off a daughter.

Having them beg the Second Branch and the Old Madam for the dowry like this — what incredible thickness of face!

Old Madam Qin was also cursing this couple to their faces in her heart?

But there was nothing she could do about it.

This matter had to be resolved.

The Jiang family had already made their move. If the Qin family did not respond, they would never be able to hold their heads up in the capital again.

She pressed her hand to her forehead and rubbed it, then cut Li-shi off.

"Enough! First daughter-in-law, rest your mouth and have some tea."

Then she turned to the Second Branch: "Second Son, Second daughter-in-law — how much more can you produce for Sixth Miss's dowry?"

Tong-shi felt a bitterness rise in her throat.

Second Master Qin also felt the wrench of it, and tugged painfully at the corner of his mouth.

But he thought the same way as his mother: whatever the Jiang family had intended — whether deliberate or not — since they had made their move, and it was the Second Branch that had first proposed the exchange of brides...

This had to be resolved.

And resolved immediately.

Otherwise, come tomorrow at court, he would be drowned in the spittle of a hundred officials.

"Mother, Elder Brother, Elder Sister-in-law — the Jiang family has shown Sixth Miss such regard, and as her Second Uncle I am glad for her."

"Since Elder Brother and Elder Sister-in-law genuinely cannot produce any more for Sixth Miss's dowry, I as Second Uncle have no choice but to step forward and shore up her dignity on her behalf."

He then cast a veiled glance at Tong-shi.

Tong-shi had no choice but to force a smile and add: "And as her Second Aunt, I will lend a hand too."

First Master Qin immediately rose and clasped his hands in a bow to the couple.

Since they were genuinely extracting gains from this scheming pair, Li-shi did not mind bowing her head a little.

She followed in her husband's wake and gave the Second Branch couple a curtsy.

"Then this Elder Sister-in-law will thank her Second Uncle and Second Aunt on Sixth Miss's behalf for their care of her."

Second Master Qin couldn't even bring himself to look at her.

Tong-shi also turned her face away in fury.

Old Madam Qin was similarly infuriated by Li-shi's smug "got a good deal and now acting gracious" expression.

She only looked at the Second Branch couple and asked: "Second Son, Second daughter-in-law — what do you calculate you can add, and how much? What I cannot cover, Old Woman will make up. If it is still not enough, let each of the other branches contribute a share..."

Second Master Qin sat with a dark expression for a moment, then lowered his head with a pained look and replied: "With two daughters in the household both about to be married off... your son has very little left. I can only take what was set aside for Shuo-er first."

Tong-shi also replied through gritted teeth: "So can your daughter-in-law. I can only take what I had set aside for Shuo-er to fill the gap first. As for Shuo-er... we will save again for him."

"Ah!" Old Madam Qin let out a deep sigh. "I suppose there is no other way for now."

"On my end, I will add two manors in the Daning County on the outskirts of the capital for Sixth Miss — both are prime farmland manors, a little over a thousand mu each."

"I will also add two prime shops in the capital — one is a cosmetics shop, one is a roasted goods shop. Both yield good returns."

Mother is producing two prime thousand-mu farmland manors with the best yields, and the finest-location prime shops in the entire capital!

All of this had originally been for him and his children!

Second Master Qin felt the pain so acutely he nearly could not stay in his seat.

Tong-shi also felt such pain that her eyes blazed with rage.

These fine farmland manors and fine shops — they had originally belonged to her children!

Now, she was forced to watch helplessly as the Old Madam gave them to that little chit Sixth Miss Qin!

In contrast to the Second Branch couple, the First Branch couple's eyes both lit up.

It was just that it would be unseemly to show it too plainly, so they held their expressions under immense strain.

"These two farmland manors and two capital shops alone will be worth about ten thousand taels."

Of course none of the four knew that Old Madam Qin was inwardly both regretful and resentful, thinking: had she known it would come to this — this drain on her funds — she should have long since given the best of it first to her own eldest grandsons and their family...

She had finished on her part, so she now signaled with her eyes to the Second Branch couple.

She had already given so much — the Second Son and his wife need not give anything too valuable.

"Then... daughter-in-law has a courtyard property in Daxing outside the capital that came with my dowry — I will add it to Sixth Miss's—"

Li-shi raised an eyebrow and looked at Tong-shi with a half-smile. "Second Sister-in-law, that Daxing property isn't worth very much, is it?"

Tong-shi nearly spat blood in fury.

She was willing to give it, and this shameless creature was turning her nose up at it!

But she still kept her rigid smile and explained.

"Elder Sister-in-law, that is not right. That courtyard is a four-entry compound — the land area is extremely generous. The rooms and corridors are all very spacious, the layout is excellent, and there is even a large rear garden. It is a very fine prop—"

Li-shi cut her off before she could finish.

"Second Sister-in-law, I am naturally grateful to you, and on Sixth Miss's behalf, thank you. Of course the First Branch will gladly accept it."

Then she changed her tone. "I only worry that when the Jiang family sees on the dowry list that it is a gift from Sixth Miss's Second Aunt... they might look down on it."

Two patches of unnatural dark red rose up on Tong-shi's cheeks.

She longed to step forward and tear off Li-shi's endlessly chattering, maddening mouth.

Old Madam Qin couldn't stand it any further and looked coldly at Li-shi. "First daughter-in-law, that is enough!"

Then she said to Tong-shi: "Second daughter-in-law, a single Daxing courtyard is not sufficient — add another thousand taels of trousseau silver as well."

Tong-shi swallowed the sweetish tang rising in her throat and said submissively: "Yes."

Li-shi then shifted her gaze from Tong-shi to Second Master Qin.

Second Master Qin felt as though he were being watched by a venomous snake and shuddered involuntarily.

"Second Uncle of the children, what will you add for Sixth Miss?" Li-shi asked directly, all smiles.

chapters.title: Embezzlement
chapters.content:
Watching Li-shi, that mercenary woman, make so little attempt to conceal her greed, Second Master Qin drew a long, deep breath to keep himself from lashing out.

He also ignored Li-shi entirely, and addressed only Old Madam Qin. "Mother, since Elder Sister-in-law looks down on my wife's Daxing courtyard, I will add a house within the capital city instead."

When these words were spoken, Second Master Qin felt as though his heart and liver had been struck with a heavy hammer.

Even the typically silent First Master Qin looked at his second younger brother in surprise.

Was he that generous?

A house inside the capital was one thing, but a good property in the city was literally priceless — money could not buy it.

He knew his second brother well.

Not only did Second Brother have a talent for officialdom, he also had a considerable knack for commerce and managing assets.

He had quite a few properties and a substantial sum of money in hand.

But he was tight-fisted.

Even privately, he did not like to give too much to his own children.

His sons and daughters all depended on the Old Madam and Tong-shi to provide dowry and bride prices from their own pockets.

Over there, Tong-shi was already drawing in a pained breath.

Old Madam Qin knew her son had quite a nest egg, and seeing him willing to give up a house inside the capital city, her old face showed some pained distress as well.

She had already given so much herself — how did this son of hers still not understand, and went ahead and offered up a capital-city house?

"This house is in Baoping Alley — it is a small three-entry compound. Though not large, its location is excellent, being very close to the Imperial Academy..."

When Old Madam Qin heard that this house was near the Imperial Academy in Baoping Alley, she immediately frowned.

Tong-shi's heart clenched as well.

The house was certainly valuable — that went without saying — but a house near the Imperial Academy was something money simply could not buy!

"Master!" Tong-shi couldn't hold it back and let out a cry.

Second Master Qin shot her a look, signaling her not to interrupt.

Tong-shi could only close her mouth, her heart feeling as though it were being fried in oil.

"Mother, Elder Brother, Elder Sister-in-law — to speak plainly, I spent great effort acquiring the Baoping Alley house from a retired Imperial Censor. I bought it for the entire Qin family..."

"As Elder Brother knows, in all these years the Qin family has not produced a single good student able to enter the Imperial Academy. That house has sat empty all this while. Now that the Jiang family has shown Sixth Miss such regard, let the house go to Sixth Miss as part of her trousseau."

Li-shi's initial delight curdled the moment she heard this, and her face fell.

First Master Qin also fell silent.

Old Madam Qin and Tong-shi, however, visibly brightened.

Second Master Qin was doing this deliberately.

Once this house was given to Qin Ruyin as part of her trousseau, the Second Branch would have gained a fine reputation in the eyes of outsiders.

Internally, they could also hint to certain members of the clan that the Baoping Alley property, bought specifically for the family's benefit, had been forced into being given as trousseau for the First Branch's daughter because of pressure from the First Branch couple.

In this way, certain clan elders who had previously sympathized with the First Branch, and privately disapproved of the Second Branch's overbearing oppression, would — for the sake of their own sons' and grandsons' futures — no longer stand on the side of the First Branch.

Moreover, if Sixth Miss Qin later failed to find favor after marrying into the Jiang household, the Second Branch could maneuver through the clan to reclaim the house from Sixth Miss Qin's hands.

This way, not only would they lose nothing, they would gain several advantages at one stroke!

First Master Qin held his silence precisely because he had seen through his second brother's scheme and was thinking of a countermeasure.

He had turned it over every which way, and concluded that if he wanted to break this scheme, the only way was to refuse the Baoping Alley house.

But if the First Branch spoke up and declined, they would fall right into Second Brother's other trap.

After all, if the uncle offered such a fine trousseau gift and the First Branch still was not satisfied and continued to pick and choose, they would be the ones to lose all moral high ground.

It would also damage Sixth Miss's reputation.

"First Son — look at how good your Second Brother is to you, and how much he loves Sixth Miss his niece. Are you still dissatisfied?"

Old Madam Qin was inwardly delighted at her own son's clever scheme, and as she looked at this stepson of hers, a faint note of triumph crept into her old eyes.

Li-shi could see the first layer but not the second — that refusing would spring another trap — and so she looked at her husband, thinking if it came to it, they could just decline and press the Second Branch to offer something different.

Just then, First Master Qin rose to his feet.

He clasped his hands in a bow to Second Master Qin. "Then Elder Brother will thank Second Brother on Sixth Miss's behalf for this trousseau gift."

Second Master Qin's eyes narrowed slightly.

He gave several cold inward laughs.

That greedy?

He actually dared take it?

Old Madam Qin and Tong-shi also cursed in their hearts.

But the words had already left Second Master Qin's mouth, and there was no taking them back no matter what.

So the house was given away — that was final.

If the First Branch dared to take it, let him not blame his younger brother for playing foul afterward...

"We are all family — there is no need for formalities. It is staying within the family after all, not going to outsiders." Second Master Qin had already calculated his next moves, and kept up appearances admirably.

His words also carried a layered meaning.

Old Madam Qin suppressed her reluctance and resentment, and said to the First Branch couple: "Second Son's house is rare. Even though it is a bit small, it will be worth a few thousand taels."

Her implied meaning: you have already gotten a very good deal, do not be greedy.

First Master Qin and Li-shi both understood when to quit while ahead.

First Master Qin, though he knew the house Second Brother offered was given with ill intentions, was confident he could nullify Second Brother's scheming.

"The care Second Brother has for his niece — Elder Brother takes note of it." First Master Qin gave Second Master Qin a meaningful glance in return, then turned to Old Madam Qin with a word.

Old Madam Qin, feeling inwardly stuffy, did not notice that this stepson, whom she had never regarded as any threat, now had a different look in his eyes than before.

She only said mildly: "Old Woman also has very little of real value left. The children of the First Branch are nearly all paired off. The Second Branch still has the legitimate Fifth Miss who has not yet married, and there is also Shuo-er."

"Your Fourth, Fifth, and Sixth Younger Brothers have only a few legitimate children between them, but the concubine-born ones number more than ten in total — Old Woman will still need to prepare something for each of those children when they come to marry."

"Therefore, Old Woman will not add any more manors, shops, or farmlands for Sixth Miss. In addition to the trousseau silver already agreed upon, I will only give a few more fine jeweled headpiece sets, and have some four-season garments and silks and brocades made up for her."

chapters.title: A Shameless Old Woman
chapters.content:
Even so, she could not help but feel her heart pounding.

How did this bastard child know?

Clearly...

The first Madam Wei had indeed left behind two large chests of authentic masterworks of ancient paintings and calligraphy.

Madam Wei was the descendant of a renowned minister in history, her family having produced more than ten officials who reached the rank of Chief Minister.

The Wei family truly had a certain character about them.

They did not care for gold and silver, but favored refined and elegant things.

They especially loved to collect ancient paintings, antiques, calligraphy, and fine porcelain...

By Madam Wei's generation, she was the only daughter in the family. Her trousseau had little in the way of gold and silver but was exceedingly rich in things of refinement.

At the time, when the old patriarch's family was still prosperous, they had not needed much gold or silver from Madam Wei for maneuvering in officialdom, but her refined dowry pieces had been put to considerable use.

Later, when the old patriarch rose to the rank of a second-rank official, Madam Wei's refined dowry gifts had played no small part.

In his old age, the old patriarch came to love these refined things as well, and what remained were all things of exceptional rarity and priceless value.

The old patriarch had indeed said while alive that since those preserved treasures were originally Madam Wei's trousseau, after his passing they should be returned to the First Son — for the First Son was, after all, the only living blood Madam Wei had left in this world.

But how could she bear to give them up?

She had gone back to her parents' home and inquired of her father and brothers, and learned that some works by great painters and calligraphers could fetch ten thousand gold pieces per scroll!

Ten thousand in gold — not ten thousand in silver!

From that moment she understood: what Madam Wei had left behind was the greatest treasure the entire Qin family possessed!

As for the antiques and the fine ceramics passed down from dynasty to dynasty...

At least the First Son had not set his sights on those yet!

"First Son, what kind of talk is this?"

"Were the things your mother left behind not all properly distributed among you brothers before your father passed?"

First Master Qin laughed coldly inwardly.

Have a stepmother and you'll have a stepfather.

What is more, by the time the old man was on his deathbed he was already confused, and what he said was all rambling — no one could make sense of it.

This old woman had huddled close to the old man's ear and pretended to listen to his dying words, when in fact she only said what she wanted to say...

Did she think everyone was a fool?

But at that time he had already been ground down to nothing, and knowing the truth was of no use.

It was only in these last years that he had quietly gathered his strength, laid his plans over a long time, and then — unexpectedly — come to be tied by marriage to the Jiang family... For his children's sake, it was time to slowly draw the net closed.

Today was a test.

"As it happens — a few days ago, old Steward Sun, who had served as Father's personal attendant his entire life, had someone pass me a few account books, and inside the account books were several slips of paper..."

First Master Qin watched Old Madam Qin's face darken in an instant, and continued at an unhurried pace.

"The slips recorded which items from my mother's trousseau had entered Father's private treasury. But after Father passed, the private treasury no longer contained those items from my mother's trousseau that were listed on the slips."

Old Madam Qin stared at him coldly.

Second Master Qin also went rigid, but a moment later stepped in front of First Master Qin, blocking his line of sight to Old Madam Qin.

"Elder Brother, what is the meaning of this? Are you accusing Mother of secretly helping herself to your birth mother's trousseau?"

At this moment, Li-shi and Tong-shi both wore expressions of shock.

Though the two women's inner states were entirely different.

Li-shi's shock quickly gave way to outrage.

That was her birth mother-in-law's trousseau!

And this old witch had secretly pocketed it for herself?

This old witch who always wore the face of a bodhisattva — she was this shameless?

This would not do!

Her birth mother-in-law's dowry could only belong to her husband and their children — she had to find a way to take it back from this old witch!

Tong-shi, meanwhile, was thinking: Heavens! Her lawful mother-in-law, Madam Wei, was a daughter of the Wei family of Xianyang — a lineage that had been passed down for hundreds of years right up to this day!

The Wei family of Xianyang had always kept a low profile. The family's descendants were no longer willing to apply themselves in officialdom; they devoted themselves entirely to scholarship, and to the arts of painting and calligraphy...

Yet everyone knew their foundation was extraordinarily deep — more than a match for certain century-old aristocratic families!

When the old patriarch had married Madam Wei, it had truly been a stroke of great fortune.

The old patriarch had been a distant nephew through the maternal line of Madam Wei's mother's family. Madam Wei was her mother's late-born child, also the only daughter of that generation.

She was deeply cherished and beloved by all the elders in the household.

But it was a pity her mother had only conceived her past forty, surviving on preservation tonics, and she had been born with a weak constitution and was constantly ill.

In those days, countless renowned physicians had been engaged to treat her body, all of them saying that even if she married in the future, it would be best for her not to bear children. Otherwise it would shorten her life.

The Wei family had been open and honest about it, telling any matchmakers who came to call the truth of their daughter's poor constitution and difficulty in childbearing.

As a result, those who were mismatched in status or circumstance — but who coveted the Wei family's prestige — all withdrew their intentions.

At that time, the old patriarch of the Qin family had just come of age, held the title of Juren, and the Qin family was already in decline — yet the old patriarch had been a man of great ambition.

Having carefully weighed the situation, he steeled himself and personally went to Xianyang to live with the Wei family for several months.

It was during that time that the sole daughter of the Wei family gave her heart to him in secret.

The Qin family invited the honored Qingning County Princess from the capital to personally go to Xianyang and propose the match on the old patriarch's behalf.

Moved by the Qin family's sincerity, and unwilling to tear apart a pair of young people who were clearly fated for each other — and above all, out of love for their own daughter — the Wei family agreed to the match.

Before the old patriarch of the Qin family married Madam Wei, he had already had a woman he loved — and that woman later became Old Madam Qin.

But for the sake of the Qin family's future and advancement, he had still calculated and set his sights on the Wei daughter.

Later, as was feared, Madam Wei's constitution deteriorated further after bearing a child. When First Master Qin was only seven years old, she passed away.

Madam Wei's several elder brothers resented the old patriarch for allowing their sister to conceive a child, causing her early death. And since the Wei family's political views clashed with those of the Qin old patriarch, he seized the opportunity to sever ties with his in-laws.

Shortly after — against the Wei family's objections — one year after Madam Wei's passing, he immediately elevated the concubine who had been his true love — Old Madam Qin — to the position of principal wife.

First Master Qin was then held tightly under Old Madam Qin's control.

The Wei family had already broken with the Qin family, and First Master Qin's several maternal uncles — all upright gentlemen — wished to advocate for him but could not intervene.

Moreover, they had lived their entire lives in their ancestral home of Xianyang, and their influence in the capital was far inferior to that of a long-established family of officials like the Qins...

The more Tong-shi thought about it, the more agitated she became.

Whatever of Madam Wei's trousseau had survived to this day... every single piece was a rare treasure!

She cast a veiled glance at her mother-in-law.

This old woman truly knew how to hide things!

Without a sound, without a word, she had swallowed up the trousseau of her first predecessor Madam Wei... and had not told even her own son, her own husband's family's master.

"Embezzlement?" First Master Qin gave a slight smile. "How can Second Brother speak of Mother in such a way?"

"Is that not exactly what you are implying?" Second Master Qin's face had turned thoroughly ugly.

"I originally thought that after Father passed, Mother had simply not noticed that Father had temporarily borrowed those antiques, paintings, and calligraphy masterworks from my mother's trousseau for appreciation. Seeing that even Second Brother did not know, it seemed Mother had indeed not noticed those items."

The Qin family now was entirely under Second Son's control — even the clan members in recent years had gradually grown accustomed to deferring to the Second Branch.

In these years, Second Son had secretly bestowed all sorts of favors on certain influential figures in the clan.

Therefore, the clan elders who sided with the Second Branch had grown more and more numerous.

His maternal family — though they had been quietly reconnecting — unfortunately his three maternal uncles and their sons all stayed out of officialdom, so his maternal family could not help him much at present.

For now, he could only use the Jiang family's prestige to reclaim whatever he could.

Old Madam Qin had not expected First Master Qin to put it in these terms, and the tightness in her chest eased somewhat.

Her expression also improved.

"First Son, if you put it that way, it sits better with Old Woman's heart. When your father passed, you were all grown up, and whatever was to be distributed had already been properly divided."

"However, there were indeed some personal effects he intended to leave to Old Woman."

"Old Woman is getting on in years and cannot bear to look at things that remind her of him. Those effects he left to Old Woman — I had someone register them roughly in a ledger and put them in storage without examining them carefully."

"Only, Old Woman has truly never seen any antiques, paintings, or calligraphy masterworks left by your birth mother..."

"Mother, I still remember some of them. For instance, Master Li Youru's Beauties of the Twenty-Four Solar Terms — a set of twenty-four paintings of ladies in their seasonal dress, each painted according to one of the twenty-four solar terms."

First Master Qin's gaze burned as it fixed on Old Madam Qin.

This shameless old witch, wearing a face of perfect innocence — truly a remarkable talent!

Old Madam Qin was shaken inwardly, though her expression was aggrieved.

Second Master Qin's face changed.

There was actually a set of Li Youru's Beauties of the Twenty-Four Solar Terms!

chapters.title: There Is Evidence
chapters.content:
Now he was truly certain that what Elder Brother had said was no lie.

Because... three months ago, wanting to advance further in his official post, he had gone looking for a famous painting to present to his superior, Minister of Public Works Wei-daren.

His superior happened to be fond of famous masters' paintings of court beauties.

When he discussed it with his mother, she had smiled at the time.

She said the old patriarch had been fond of collecting famous paintings and calligraphy in his lifetime, and that right there in her private treasury she had Master Li Youru's Spring Equinox Beauty, one of the finest paintings of court beauties ever made.

At the time, he had been beside himself with joy, calling out "Mother" in a cascade of delighted cries, urging her to quickly have someone bring it to him...

Hearing what Elder Brother was saying now, it seemed the whole set had fallen into Mother's hands — all twenty-four beauties?

His eyes blazed with excitement, while at the same time resentment began to stir in his heart.

Why had Mother hidden a matter this significant from him?

Did she not know that to continue climbing the official ladder, he needed to keep greasing the wheels above him without stopping?

And those masterworks by great masters — priceless, impossible to come by — were the finest and most useful keys to open any door!

Of course, now that Elder Brother wanted them back, that was absolutely out of the question!

What did Madam Wei's trousseau mean to him?

Once Madam Wei died, those trousseau items had belonged to the Qin family.

They were the Qin family's property now!

The Qin family now was run by him — so naturally they were his property!

First, regardless of why Mother had kept it from him, he had to protect those fine things for her.

Second Master Qin's eyes shifted slightly, and landed on Tong-shi.

Tong-shi was also at that very moment thinking about the Spring Equinox Beauty that the Old Madam had given her husband...

Husband and wife's thoughts aligned in an instant.

Just as Tong-shi was about to open her mouth, Old Madam Qin gave a faint shake of her head. "Old Woman truly knows nothing of any set of Twenty-Four Solar Terms Beauties — I have never seen such a thing."

Second Master Qin and Tong-shi both felt their hearts ease.

Li-shi, however, became anxious.

Just as she was about to speak, First Master Qin curved his lips slightly and said with cool composure: "It is no matter if Mother does not know — simply open the private treasury and take stock of the personal effects Father left to you."

First Master Qin could say this because he was confident: the greater part of his mother's trousseau was in the old witch's private treasury.

Old Madam Qin's old face went stone cold in an instant. The unflappable composure of before was gone.

"First Son, you actually intend to force Old Woman to open her private treasury and let you inspect it?"

A stepchild forcing his stepmother to open her private treasury for inspection — if word of this got out, First Master Qin's reputation would be finished entirely.

The idle post he held in office would certainly be lost as well.

Old Madam Qin was betting that First Master Qin would not dare.

Second Master Qin also immediately saw the point, and stared hard and coldly at First Master Qin. "Elder Brother, think carefully about what you are saying!"

First Master Qin smiled lightly and said without hurry: "What are Mother and Second Brother getting so anxious about?"

He continued: "My mother's dowry list at the time was made in three copies. My mother kept one copy and brought it to her husband's household with her. One copy was kept by my maternal grandmother's family. One copy was kept at the Xianyang County Yamen."

"When my mother passed, I was still young. The clan at the time decided that Grandmother should manage my mother's trousseau on my behalf. After Grandmother passed as well, my mother's trousseau was managed by Father."

He paused, and First Master Qin swept a glance at Second Master Qin, who was standing in front of Old Madam Qin.

"Second Brother holds the rank of Associate Third Grade. You know as well as I do that as long as a woman's dowry list survives, it is always possible to trace where those items came from."

Second Master Qin's expression froze.

Of course it was traceable.

But what made this hardest to deal with right now was the fact that Madam Wei's dowry list had been made in three copies, one of which was still at the Xianyang County Yamen!

Mother had certainly not known about this.

Whether or not Father had known about it was hard to say.

He surmised Father had not known either; otherwise Father could not possibly have failed to tell his mother about it.

In the early days of the Great Ying dynasty, a bride's dowry list could be registered with the local authorities — it was not compulsory, mainly out of consideration for the face of the bride's new household. In general, even when a family registered in secret for fear that their daughter's dowry might be swallowed by her in-laws, they often even kept it from their own daughter, terrified of the consequences if anyone found out.

The Wei family had presumably not trusted the Qin family from the outset, which was why they had secretly filed a copy of Madam Wei's dowry list at the county yamen...

The more Second Master Qin thought about it, the more agitated he became.

In fact, his deduction was entirely correct. The old patriarch of the Qin family had truly not known about this.

It had been done quietly by the Wei family patriarch when he was still alive.

Only the Wei family's eldest son — First Master Qin's eldest maternal uncle — had been told. Even First Master Qin's own mother, the person most directly involved, had been kept in the dark.

First Master Qin had only learned of this because his eldest maternal uncle, upon hearing that Sixth Miss Qin's match with the Jiang family had been settled, had sent a trusted man galloping post-haste from Xianyang to the capital to inform him.

His eldest uncle's view was: after all these years, with the Jiang family's power to back them, the time had come to reclaim what rightfully belonged to the Qin First Branch.

First Master Qin was still level-headed: given the control Second Son's branch had over the Qin clan in the capital, recovering everything at once would not be easy.

Second Master Qin had spent over twenty years navigating officialdom — he was not a man to trifle with.

He quickly calmed himself.

It was clear that First Son had known for some time that Mother had privately held back some of Madam Wei's trousseau...

The reason he had chosen to expose this now was obviously to make use of the Jiang family's influence.

But what was most troubling was that First Son was using a straightforward strategy — a move that left both him and his mother with no counter.

Fortunately, First Son was also cautious: he was afraid of provoking both Mother and himself to the point where everyone would throw caution to the wind and make a reckless mess that no one could clean up afterward.

So he had only mentioned letting Mother produce those famous paintings and calligraphy masterworks left by Madam Wei to add to Sixth Miss's trousseau.

This way, everyone's dignity was preserved.

"Mother, since Elder Brother has said as much, please be so good as to go and take stock of the private treasury. If those Twenty-Four Solar Terms Beauties Elder Brother mentioned are indeed there, bring them out for—"

Seeing Second Son stand down, First Son gave a cold inward laugh.

This was only the beginning!

Li-shi, however, was not willing.

She set down her teacup and said in a quiet, lingering tone: "Second Uncle, that is not right. If those Solar Terms Beauties are found in Mother's private treasury, then they are Sixth Miss's own paternal grandmother's gift for her trousseau."

When she spoke these words, everyone in the room — except First Master Qin — changed expression.

But what of it?

What she said was fact.

Li-shi was also deliberately putting Old Madam Qin to shame.

She was making a pointed reminder: your whole life you are nothing more than a replacement wife, and yet you dared to take the first wife's trousseau for yourself — on that count alone, Old Madam Qin could be held by the scruff of her neck for the rest of her days.

Old Madam Qin was so enraged she nearly choked on air.

She could not help but direct some of her resentment at her own son.

If she would not admit it — so what?

Was First Son truly going to take this outside and let people laugh at the Qin family?

That would be a laughingstock for the entire Qin clan, and the clan itself would never allow him to do such a thing!

Old Madam Qin was betting that First Master Qin did not dare.

But Second Son had already spoken, and what was done could not be undone.

There was nothing for it...

"First Son, after your father passed, Old Woman was overwhelmed and truly did not have the mind to think of everything. Those personal effects he left to Old Woman — I have truly not inspected them to this day..."

"If they are truly found among the effects your father left to Old Woman, Old Woman will naturally give them all to Sixth Miss as trousseau gifts — you can rest easy on that count!"

First Master Qin gave no answer.

But inwardly he was even more certain that this shameless old witch was now preparing for the day the truth would come fully to light.

Old Madam Qin, her old face crimson with the effort, swallowed her humiliation and said through gritted teeth: "Old Woman is, after all, Sixth Miss's grandmother too. For her trousseau, Old Woman will not be stingy — Old Woman will add four thousand more taels of trousseau silver for her, and that will be that."

By this reckoning, she had already been forced to produce an extra six thousand taels in trousseau silver for Qin Ruyin.

Normally, in the Qin family, marrying off two legitimate granddaughters — the trousseau silver she gave as grandmother was already sufficient!

Of course it hurt — beyond all bearing.

Tong-shi's eyes also reddened with pain.

The Old Madam still had a solid foundation.

She could reach into her pocket and pull out another six thousand taels just like that.

Her Fifth Miss had fawned and flattered for all she was worth, and this old woman had only been willing to add two thousand more taels of trousseau silver for her...

It was simply not fair!

"Mother still loves Sixth Miss best of all. The Wang family are upright scholars — our Fifth Miss will not have nearly this much to call her own when she marries into them."

Tong-shi could not help but speak up, pointing out that Old Madam Qin had added far more to Sixth Miss Qin's trousseau than to Fifth Miss Qin's.

And she was brimming with resentment.

"Let us not speak of this any further. Elder Brother, Elder Sister-in-law — would you, as the younger generation, truly wish to search Mother's private treasury?"

"Think it over carefully: if the treasury is searched and there are no Twenty-Four Solar Terms Beauties as Elder Brother described — then what?"

chapters.title: Setting Herself Free
chapters.content:
First Master Qin's lips curved into a dangerous arc.

He gave Tong-shi a cool glance.

"Is Second Sister-in-law trying to make a bet?"

Tong-shi was caught off guard. Having her thoughts exposed to her face, she went red at once.

Second Master Qin was quietly furious at Tong-shi for making things worse, and said to First Master Qin:

"Mother herself is uncertain whether the private effects Father left her include anything from Elder Brother's birth mother. Mother is also willing to open the private treasury for stock-taking herself."

"Elder Brother, why bother having words with a woman? We are family, blood and bones — if there is something to be resolved, let us resolve it."

Li-shi gave a cold laugh. "I'm afraid certain people are simply hoping for a stroke of luck."

Tong-shi was seething and wanted to argue with Li-shi, but Second Master Qin shot her a fierce glare.

"Enough! Sixth Miss is about to be married — her wedding is the most important matter at hand. You women — when will you stop making a scene?"

Li-shi's eyebrows shot up. She was not one to be pushed around.

The First Branch had been pressed down and held under by the Second Branch for all these years, yet had never been entirely subdued. Beyond First Master Qin's quiet endurance and long-laid preparations, there was also Li-shi's unyielding backbone.

She gave a cold laugh. "Qin Er-lao-ye, if you want to scold your own wife, scold your own wife — but dragging in 'you women' is asking for trouble!"

"What is it — do you look down on women? Then tell me, didn't you come crawling out of a woman's belly?"

Second Master Qin's face went green and white in an instant.

Having spent over twenty years in officialdom, when had anyone ever pointed a finger at his nose and scolded him like this?

Old Madam Qin's and Tong-shi's faces also darkened.

Tong-shi also could not restrain herself. She mimicked Li-shi's cold laugh — though it was merely parroting, and had none of the original's spirit.

She also did not have Li-shi's recklessness, and only dared to mock: "Elder Sister-in-law is still, after all, an official's wife. Such coarse and impolite speech — you are in effect cursing Mother as well!"

"Ha, Second Sister-in-law, my own family cannot compare to your Tong family's wealth and standing. And what is there to be done — Mother only has her heart set on her own sons. From Second Son down to Fifth Son, which of those sons' wives has a lesser pedigree than mine?"

"My background may be a cut below, and my manners may be a bit lacking. You just have to put up with it. What else can be done, when Mother's heart only goes out to her own flesh and blood — and for the son she bore from the first wife, she could only manage a daughter-in-law like me?"

"You are all respectable ladies from good families, so if you want to maintain your dignity as great ladies, you had best not bother wrangling with the likes of me!"

Old Madam Qin nearly toppled backward with rage.

Not in her wildest dreams had she imagined that in her old age, she would be pointed at like this and scolded by this daughter-in-law of lowly origin.

Tong-shi also dared not say anything more.

She could only curse Li-shi in her heart, up one side and down the other.

And Li-shi felt as though something had come unblocked in her, all at once grasping what her Yin-er had said — "when someone wants to keep face, they have no power over one who has nothing left to lose" — and savoring its full benefit.

Indeed!

Watching the way Tong-shi's face had cycled through every color in a dyer's shop, Li-shi felt a great satisfaction in her chest!

She simply let herself go.

She shot Tong-shi a contemptuous look. "And Second Master Qin — did he not come crawling out of the Old Madam's belly? The only one he can scold like this is his own wife. What is this talk of 'you women'? Is that something a person says?"

"Second Sister-in-law, you still have the face to come and blame me? Think about it — it was Second Master Qin himself who was the one being unfilial, bringing his own mother into it with 'you women'!"

At the word "unfilial," Second Master Qin could no longer contain himself.

First Master Qin curved his lips with a look of deep and fond indulgence for his wife.

It was Old Madam Qin who finally gave the table before her a hard slap, silencing everyone.

She could not even be bothered about Li-shi slapping her in the face right now.

Her own son was a third-rank official of the court. If a charge of "unfilial" were to stick to him, his career would certainly suffer greatly going forward.

She could see it now: Li-shi had been ground down by years of hardship into someone with nothing left to lose. Now, with the Jiang family match to stand on, she was even harder to manage.

For the moment, there was no use dealing with this shrew. The important thing was to get past this step with First Son first.

"First Son, First daughter-in-law — please go back. Sixth Miss's dowry is not your concern as her parents — Old Woman and Second Son will see to it properly."

"Old Woman must also hurry and go through the private treasury herself — better to clear up any misunderstanding early."

With that, Old Madam Qin set down her teacup in the gesture of dismissal.

Seeing her show them out with the tea, First Master Qin did not linger. He bowed in a brief salute and led Li-shi — who gave only a perfunctory curtsy — out of the room.

"Mother..." Seeing the First Branch couple leave, Tong-shi turned to Old Madam Qin with an anxious look.

"Second daughter-in-law, you go and tend to your own affairs. Mother wishes to speak with your husband privately."

Tong-shi was stuck.

Old Madam was actually sending her away?

In a matter this significant... she was to be kept out?

Seeing that she had still not moved, Second Master Qin shot her a cold look. Tong-shi's heart lurched, and she had no choice but to say through gritted lips: "Yes. Your daughter-in-law takes her leave."

"Mother?" Second Master Qin called out, inquiring with a look.

Old Madam Qin let out a heavy sigh.

"That is right — back then your father did pass those masterworks and calligraphy by the great masters to Mother to keep. Mother went back to her parents' family and asked your maternal grandfather and your eldest uncle. They both said it was better for Mother to hold on to them for the time being."

Old Madam Qin glanced at Second Master Qin. "They also meant well — they were thinking that one day when you brothers entered officialdom, you would all inevitably have use for such things."

Old Madam Qin had no wish to admit in front of her own son that it was her own greed that had led her to pocket Madam Wei's trousseau.

So she shifted the blame to her late father and elder brother.

In any case, those two were already in their graves.

No one could prove what she said was false.

No one knows a mother better than her son.

Second Master Qin said with a furrowed brow: "Mother gave me that Spring Equinox Beauty... are the remaining ones truly all twenty-three of the set?"

Old Madam Qin gave a pained nod. "There are indeed still twenty-three."

"Elder Brother specifically named the full set of twenty-four... and that Spring Equinox Beauty has already been given away." Second Master Qin's brow was deeply furrowed. "A gift once sent out cannot be taken back."

Old Madam Qin also had no solution, and could only say: "Then simply deny the whole thing outright — perhaps Elder Son is only bluffing us..."

Second Master Qin shook his head. "I know him well. He would not say something like this without proof, and would not dare to say it."

Old Madam Qin grew frantic and blurted out: "Then what is to be done?"

"Mother, the only course available now is to first give those twenty-three to Sixth Miss as trousseau additions. As for the Spring Equinox Beauty that is already gone, just say it could not be found — it has gone missing."

"Ah, I'm afraid that if Elder Brother truly has evidence, he certainly won't let the rest go without a fight."

Second Master Qin gave a cold laugh.

"First stall for time. If Sixth Miss finds favor after marrying into the Jiang household, we will slowly look for a way out... If she does not find favor, then there is nothing more to be said."

Over these twenty-odd years he had scraped and schemed for every advantage, yet the fine things he had in hand could not match the value of these twenty-four Beauties.

And yet there were those who had been born into countless priceless treasures.

Heaven was just that unfair.

Old Madam Qin was still reluctant to let them go.

One painting alone was priceless — let alone twenty-three of them!

She stared ahead with a darkened face and asked her own son: "Second Son, is it truly necessary to give those twenty-three Beauties to that girl Sixth Miss for her trousseau?"

chapters.title: A Flash of Sharpness
chapters.content:
"They absolutely must be given." Second Master Qin's voice was grave. "Mother, the most frightening thing is not Elder Brother's copy of his birth mother's dowry list, nor the copy kept with the Wei family."

Old Madam Qin's face was etched with concealed pain.

Afraid she would still not grasp the severity, Second Master Qin gritted his teeth and pressed on. "What is most frightening of all is that the Wei family, behind our backs, filed a copy of the dowry list at the Xianyang County Yamen!"

"Xianyang is the Wei family's ancestral stronghold. Even if we have connections there, we cannot reach that far."

"If he is pushed to the wall and Elder Brother, throwing all face aside — his own and the First Branch's — makes this a public affair, he need only bring up that copy at the Xianyang Yamen... and your son will have no future left at court."

"And it will not only be your son — Fourth Brother, Fifth Brother, and Sixth Brother will all lose face as well. The harm would extend to their children and grandchildren..."

At these words, Old Madam Qin's old face went completely white. She shook her head immediately. "That must never happen!"

Nothing mattered more than the futures of her sons and grandsons!

"It is good that Mother understands. Fortunately, Mother handled things well today. Even if Elder Brother and his wife spread word of it, Mother has her version of events to stand on."

"Good! Mother will listen to you..."

......

"Madam, today... how did you come to speak to the Old Madam and Second Brother's household the way you did?"

After returning to their own courtyard, First Master Qin asked his wife about it.

Li-shi was busy settling him down, taking his cloak, and calling out in a stream of instructions for a small maid to bring tea.

She stole a glance at her husband, then set aside the cloak and bent over laughing, covering her mouth.

First Master Qin, seeing her laugh like that, could not help laughing himself.

"What is so funny?" he asked gently.

Li-shi straightened up with her hands on her hips. "How could it not be funny? Your concubine unleashed her full might today, gave that old woman and that venomous Second Branch couple a proper tongue-lashing — that was a real venting of long-suppressed rage!"

"And besides, they didn't dare talk back to your concubine... Oh, that's right, Master — what is all that about our first Madam's dowry? You've never mentioned it before."

First Master Qin looked at his wife, as lively and full of spirit as the day she had married him in her youth, and the fondness in his eyes deepened.

He said to her: "I have known for a long time that Mother's dowry was secretly pocketed by that old woman. But at the time we were too weak and powerless. Telling you would only have added to your troubles."

Li-shi thought about it and gave a light nod. "True enough. If I had known when I was young, I would certainly have gone to make a scene — but I would certainly have lost the fight and only alarmed them into being more careful."

"Now it is different." A cold glint flickered briefly in First Master Qin's eyes.

Li-shi smiled and added: "It is different. And Yin-er also told your concubine — she said now that her marriage is settled, and to such a fine family as the Jiang household, she told me not to keep swallowing my grievances when I speak to the Old Madam and that venomous Second Branch couple."

"Oh?" First Master Qin blinked in surprise.

Li-shi then told First Master Qin everything that Qin Ruyin had analyzed for her.

The more First Master Qin listened, the brighter his eyes became.

After the midday meal, he sent for Qin Ruyin to come to his study.

Qin Ruyin did not know why her father was calling her to his study at this hour.

But she knew her father had always been exceptionally good to the original occupant of this body, and that he would only summon her to the study for something important.

Still, she felt a flicker of unease — two parts of it — afraid that her father, who had always kept his own counsel beneath a veneer of submission, might have sensed that the person inside her had changed.

She tidied herself briefly, draped a lotus-pink cloak over her shoulders, and headed over with only her second-rank maid Huangqi for company.

After the page boy served tea, First Master Qin looked at her with a smile. "Our Yin-er has truly grown up — she already knows how to teach her mother how to handle the Old Madam and the other elders."

At these words, Qin Ruyin understood at once.

It must have been the analysis she had shared with her mother yesterday — and her mother had put it to use today against Old Madam Qin and the Second Branch couple.

Judging by her father's smiling expression, her mother must have won a great victory — and must have thoroughly vented the long-pent-up resentments of both herself and her father.

Thinking this, she felt the tension in her chest ease, and she smiled too.

"How did Yin-er come to think of all that?" First Master Qin asked warmly, looking at his youngest daughter — the last of his children — with a father's tender, doting gaze.

"Father, Yin-er only thought: all these years you and Mother have endured so much, and it has all been for us children's sake. But now we have all grown up!"

"Yin-er's match — they tried to lift a stone and dropped it on their own feet, and it turned out unexpectedly well. You and Mother need not go on enduring things as you did before."

"Mm, Yin-er is right about that." First Master Qin told her to drink her tea and warm herself, then asked: "But your two elder brothers still do not yet have their full wings. To make sworn enemies of them entirely may not be wise."

Qin Ruyin held the teacup and sipped from it in small, delicate mouthfuls.

Then she said with a smile: "Father, what are we afraid of? In your daughter's view, Elder Brother and Second Brother's futures do not depend on them at all — and not only do they not depend on them, but if those people refrain from sabotaging them in secret, that will already be a stroke of luck."

"Father, think about it — all these years they have pressed down and suppressed us First Branch so relentlessly, isn't it because they fear that if we rise, we will go back and pay them back in kind?"

"If we keep holding ourselves back for fear of provoking them and being punished, they will only use that to control us."

Qin Ruyin narrowed her eyes slightly.

"But if we make a sudden reversal and become aggressive in our turn, they will be the ones who worry we will kick up a great fuss."

"After all, our First Branch has nothing left to lose — nothing but our lives. But they do have things to lose!"

"As for our lives — we cannot lose them, but they cherish their lives even more. The saying goes that on a narrow road, the bolder one wins. Now the roles of attacker and defender have reversed between our two branches — we need not endure them any longer."

First Master Qin broke from his usual reserved and somber manner and suddenly stroked his beard, laughing heartily.

"Who would have thought it... who would have thought our Yin-er would turn out to be such an inwardly wise and clever little strategist!"

Qin Ruyin immediately played the part of a spoiled daughter and said: "Father, don't make fun of me."

That sent First Master Qin into another peal of laughter.

He then teased his youngest daughter: "So that is why you told your mother: if the betrothal gold the Jiang family gives you today is a large sum, there is even less reason to be afraid of them?"

Qin Ruyin smiled with a touch of embarrassment.

"Yes, Father — your daughter's thinking was simple enough. If the Jiang family gives your daughter a large betrothal gold, regardless of the reason, people outside will at least assume that the Jiang family holds your daughter in high regard."

"Then our household can use the Jiang family's name to put on a show of borrowed authority before the Old Madam and the Second Branch. Second Uncle, being the official-obsessed man he is, would certainly not dare make any rash moves."

......

First Master Qin watched his youngest daughter leave, and let out a long sigh.

His longtime steward, Ning-shu, saw this and smiled. "Master, Sixth Young Miss is so inwardly wise and clever — you ought to be happy!"

First Master Qin shook his head. "This child was also forced to grow up overnight. What worries this old man is her life after she marries into the Jiang family..."

Ning-shu's smile faded a little at this.

He thought for a moment, then offered comfort: "Master, rest easy. The Jiang family's betrothal gold and gifts for Sixth Young Miss have been so lavish — once Sixth Young Miss marries in, they will certainly treat her well."

First Master Qin gave a quiet nod. "Mm. May your words prove true. I only hope Yin-er will live a life of ease and contentment!"

"Let us not speak of that for now. The Jiang family has given Yin-er such a lavish betrothal — I, as her father, cannot offer so little in return. Ah-ning, help me think it over and plan what more to add..."

Before First Master Qin could finish speaking, a messenger arrived from Old Madam Qin's side summoning him, saying there was urgent business to discuss.

First Master Qin and Ning-shu exchanged a glance.

chapters.title: Victory
chapters.content:
"Master, since the Old Madam has sent for you, you had best go first. Old servant will think carefully about additions to Sixth Young Miss's trousseau."

First Master Qin nodded and turned to leave.

Ning-shu gave a signal, and a brown-clad figure flashed past, swiftly falling into step behind First Master Qin.

Shouheng Hall.

Old Madam Qin and Second Master Qin had already been seated and waiting for some time.

When First Master Qin arrived, Second Master Qin did not rise, only giving him a nod in greeting.

First Master Qin paid him no mind, and simply bowed to Old Madam Qin before asking directly: "The Old Madam sent for me — has there been some progress on my birth mother's dowry?"

"Mm... hmph!" Old Madam Qin had not expected him to come in asking this and could not help letting out a few labored false coughs.

"...Much time has passed, and the private effects your father left to Old Woman are quite numerous. In my urgency today I rummaged through some of them — and lo, I actually did find those beauties you spoke of."

"Very good." First Master Qin clasped his hands behind his back and gave a slight nod.

Old Madam Qin then changed her tone. "However, only twenty-three were found. One could not be found no matter what. Perhaps you are misremembering — perhaps there were only twenty-three?"

First Master Qin gave a cold inward laugh.

Was she trying that with him?

Was it not Second Master Qin who had taken that Spring Equinox Beauty and presented it to his superior, Minister of Public Works Wei Shulin?

Second Master Qin had been watching First Master Qin's expression closely and, seeing things were going wrong, spoke up immediately.

"Elder Brother — this way: since the twenty-three beauties have already been found, let them first be sent to Sixth Miss. As for the missing one, Mother can have people look for it more carefully."

"And if it still cannot be found?" First Master Qin looked at him. "My birth mother's dowry list records it clearly. And since it is a masterwork by a great artist, even if it was given away as a gift, there would be a record of it."

Second Master Qin ground his teeth inwardly. These words sat very ill with him.

It was as though Elder Brother knew where the Spring Equinox painting had gone.

Trying to affect composure while his dense black brows furrowed with impatience, he said with studied calm: "If it truly cannot be found, and Elder Brother insists on pursuing it, then there is nothing for it but for me, as his younger brother, to compensate Elder Brother on our father's behalf."

First Master Qin, though he was getting on in years himself, still found Second Master Qin's ugliness of character remarkable.

He had blatantly taken it to curry favor with his superior — yet without so much as a flutter of the eyelids, he was now dumping this black mark onto a father dead these dozen-odd years.

Though, of course, that father who had been dead a dozen years was no great person himself.

"Second Brother — let us take things one at a time. There is no need to speak such high-minded words ahead of time. And certainly do not, without even having investigated, slap a great black mark onto our father who has been dead for over a decade."

First Master Qin assumed a stern expression and pronounced two words with heavy deliberateness: "Unfilial."

Second Master Qin clenched his jaw so hard his face went green.

"Enough!" Old Madam Qin called out darkly. "Both of you, less talk! Old Woman will have people look into it carefully. First Son, you need only rest easy — if in the end that one still cannot be found, Old Woman will give you an accounting!"

"Since the Old Madam herself has said so, then let it be as the Old Madam says." First Master Qin clasped his hands in a bow.

"There is no other business. I had you come to Shouheng Hall for two reasons: one, to tell you of this; and two, to have you personally inspect those twenty-three beauties. If all is in order, take them back and hand them to Sixth Miss."

First Master Qin had no desire to complicate things further. Getting those twenty-three beauties into Sixth Miss's hands first was better than anything.

He examined the twenty-three beauties using the method his eldest maternal uncle had taught him to distinguish genuine works from forgeries, and confirmed they were authentic.

He then took the twenty-three beauties and left Shouheng Hall.
