That same day, First Master Qin had the twenty-three beauties delivered to Qin Ruyin's courtyard.

It was only then that Ruyin learned from her father's own mouth that the Old Madam had secretly pocketed the trousseau of the original Ruyin's paternal grandmother.

That old woman... she had truly cast all shame aside!

First Master Qin told her to concentrate on her wedding preparations and not worry about those matters.

He told her those were things for the adults to handle.

Not wanting her father to worry about her, Qin Ruyin docilely agreed.

She reflected that today her father had summoned her to his study, and she had said things no ordinary girl would have said... she must have frightened him.

He was probably worried she would go and do something rash on her own and make a mess of things.

But she had no intention of acting on her own.

Her father was not the kind of spineless, pressed-down man he appeared to be on the surface.

He had his own plans and schemes, and both his vision and his judgment were sound.

If not, he could never have endured being suppressed for decades by his own father and stepmother and their sons — and yet still maintained a harmonious and loving marriage with his wife, and raised every child his wife bore him to adulthood.

Her father also spoke with her about the Jiang family's lavish betrothal gold and gifts.

As for the betrothal gold Fourth Master Jiang had given her — she had her own sense of what that figure would be and was not surprised.

But when her mother joyfully told her about it today, she then learned that the Jiang family's other betrothal gifts were also exceptionally generous.

Her mother even gestured to show her: gifts that lavish could not have come to less than two or three thousand taels all told.

The dried fruits, pastries, and sweets were the finest that could be had anywhere in the capital.

The dried goods, mountain delicacies, sea foods, and tonics were also all of the highest quality.

Most valuable of all were the large-ticket items and the imperial-grade silks and brocades...

"Father thought that the Jiang family's betrothal gold and gifts had exceeded the customary scale by too much, and that what Father had originally set aside for you was no longer adequate."

First Master Qin smiled as he looked at her. "Father will go back and take stock with Ning-shu, and add a little more to your trousseau."

Qin Ruyin quickly waved her hands to say it was not necessary.

First Master Qin smiled and shook his head. "You do not understand. The face the Jiang family has given you is also face given to our First Branch and to the Qin family. That face must be matched."

"If we cannot match it, the Qin family will become the laughingstock of the entire capital..."

"As for those beauties — they are fine things left by your paternal grandmother. If she were still with us, she would certainly be glad to have them go to Yin-er as trousseau additions."

"Yin-er need only know that, and think no more of it." Those were the last words First Master Qin said to her.

......

Shortly after First Master Qin left, Elder Sister-in-law and Second Sister-in-law came together to see her.

Though they said they came to talk, they were really there to give her trousseau gifts.

The First Branch had been suppressed for years, yet the family backgrounds of her two sisters-in-law were actually quite good.

Elder Sister-in-law Lin Xi's father had been a classmate and close friend of her own father, and had been the better student of the two. But he had valued her father's character so highly that he had voluntarily offered his legitimate second daughter — Elder Sister-in-law — as a wife for her elder brother.

Second Sister-in-law Meng An's family was a branch of the great Meng clan of Lin'an, settled in the capital. Her Second Sister-in-law's birth third uncle had once been a colleague of her father's. He too had held her father in high regard and had personally acted as matchmaker, bringing about the match between Second Sister-in-law and her second brother.

Qin Ruyin had taken stock of things long ago: though the First Branch of the Qin family appeared on the surface to be everywhere constrained and suppressed by the Second Branch, its foundations were actually very solid.

Rising to prominence was only a matter of time.

After pressing her two sisters-in-law to sit, Qin Ruyin personally took the tea from the maidservant's hands and served it to them.

The two sisters-in-law beamed at her. "You are practically a bride already — how can you still be doing things like this?"

Qin Ruyin smiled too. "It's precisely because I am about to leave that I cherish each chance. Once I am gone, the times I see you two sisters-in-law will be fewer and fewer. Each cup of tea I pour for you is one fewer left."

The three women — two sisters-in-law and one sister — laughed and joked for a while, and then Elder Sister-in-law Lin Xi reached into the wide sleeve of her robe and produced a small wooden box.

Second Sister-in-law Meng An also produced a small box of similar size with a smile.

The two of them handed them to Qin Ruyin together.

chapters.title: Deep Sisterly Bonds, Trousseau Gifts for Ruyin
chapters.content:
"These are your trousseau gifts from your elder brother and me." Lin Xi looked at Qin Ruyin with gentle warmth.

Second Sister-in-law Meng An also smiled and nodded. "And these are from your second brother and second sister-in-law."

Qin Ruyin accepted them graciously and thanked them with a smile.

"Your Elder Sister and Second Sister will also be coming soon to bring their trousseau gifts, so Second Sister-in-law and I will head back first and leave the three of you sisters to talk."

Lin Xi rose, smiled at Second Sister-in-law Meng An and said: "An-an, let us go."

Meng An also smiled and nodded. She rose and looped her arm through Elder Sister-in-law Lin Xi's, saying gently to Qin Ruyin: "Rest well — there is no need to see us out."

Qin Ruyin rose as she said: "How could I not?"

As she saw the two sisters-in-law to the moon-gate near the courtyard, she caught sight of Elder Sister Rujin and Second Sister Ruxiu coming arm in arm.

The sisters-in-law and sisters all chatted happily at the moon-gate for a long while, until Elder Sister-in-law Lin Xi, worried they would catch a chill, urged Qin Ruyin to quickly take her two sisters inside.

After bidding farewell to her sisters-in-law, Qin Ruyin walked with one arm through each sister's, leading them back to her inner room.

Qin Ruyin studied her two sisters carefully.

Elder Sister was dressed in a pale green silk robe embroidered with the blessings character motif, and her expression was happy.

Qin Ruyin noticed she had applied a very thick layer of powder, yet it still could not fully conceal the dark shadows under her eyes.

Something stirred in her heart.

Second Sister wore a sky-blue Hangzhou silk robe trimmed with gold thread at the edges, its pattern of lucky clouds and intertwining branches beautifully rendered. Her complexion was rosy and her whole person radiated energy.

Today was their youngest sister's joyful day. The Jiang family's betrothal procession had streamed through the Qin family's front gate in an unbroken flow, and all the Qin family members — including every relative and guest — had been watching.

While the two of them, along with their two sisters-in-law, helped their mother Li-shi entertain the guests, they heard no shortage of envious and admiring remarks.

For so many years the First Branch had been firmly held down by the Second Branch, and both sisters had long been smoldering with frustration.

Today, on their little sister's day of joy, they could at last hold their heads high.

As for the match with the Jiang family — they had worried on their little sister's behalf just as their mother had.

Later, when their mother told them that their little sister was actually quite willing to accept the match, and given that two days prior the Jiang family's Old Madam had personally come to the Qin household clearly intending to stand up for their little sister, they had already begun to feel happy on her behalf.

They had not expected today's betrothal to be on such a magnificent scale. The last traces of worry in their hearts mostly dissolved.

What remained was only concern about their little sister's life after she married into the Jiang household.

The three sisters squeezed together on the daybed to talk.

Seeing the two small boxes sitting on the little tea table beside the daybed, Elder Sister Rujin lightly tapped Qin Ruyin's forehead.

"You are practically a bride — how are you still so careless? Such valuables and you did not think to have someone put them away safely."

Qin Ruyin rubbed her forehead with an embarrassed smile. "I was rushing to see Elder Sister-in-law and Second Sister-in-law out just now and forgot."

Second Sister Ruxiu also furrowed her crescent brows. "Sixth Miss has so few reliable people serving her to begin with."

Rujin shot her a glance. "Weren't you and I in just the same situation when we were still daughters at home? We were even worse off."

Ruxiu fell silent.

They both understood — and their parents and brothers understood too — that during all those years of living under another's thumb, it was not wise to have too many clever, capable people serving openly by your side.

She and Elder Sister had only been able to openly keep beside them the people their mother had trained for them after they had married out.

"No matter. Second Sister and I came here today specifically to bring our Sixth Miss some people she can rely on."

Rujin pulled a sheaf of papers from her wide sleeve and passed them to Qin Ruyin with a warm voice. "Sixth Miss, take these — these are your Elder Sister's trousseau gift for you."

Ruxiu also produced a sheaf and pressed it into Qin Ruyin's hands.

"Thank you, Elder Sister!"

"Thank you, Second Sister!"

Being too ceremonious between real sisters would be hurtful. Qin Ruyin accepted both sisters' trousseau gifts with delight.

"Originally, Second Sister and I were thinking of giving you a shop or a manor as a trousseau gift. But Mother told us that marrying into a household like the Jiang family, you cannot manage without enough reliable people around you."

"Mother had already trained several good people for you, along with two accompanying households. But since this is truly a high marriage, that number of people is simply not enough."

Rujin held Qin Ruyin's small, pale, smooth hand and spoke to her softly.

"So Elder Sister discussed it with me," Second Sister Ruxiu added in, "and we decided it would be better to give you some capable and useful people instead."

"And with the Jiang family having given you such a lavish betrothal this time — not to mention that Father and Mother still have more trousseau additions to give you, and Grandmother and the Second Branch cannot escape doing the same..."

"This means that the manors and shops coming your way as dowry, Sixth Miss, will already be quite numerous."

After Ruxiu finished, Rujin continued: "My trousseau gift to you is three accompanying households — one family of four, two families of six. All the bond papers are here."

She then changed her tone and said approvingly: "I heard from Mother that you yourself intend to choose some people and place them in Mother's courtyard first, to have Nanny You train them. That is very wise."

Qin Ruyin raised her brows with an air of mock pride.

That made both sisters laugh.

Then Ruxiu said: "Second Sister is also giving you one accompanying household. It is one Mother originally gave to me — you know them too. It is the Miao Dacheng family."

"After Second Sister married, Miao Dacheng has been working with his wife and two daughters on Second Sister's manor — he is a skilled hand at running farmland."

"As for his two sons — they have both been trained up in Second Sister's shop, and are just right for you to put to use."

"There are also six trained maids. They work hard and conduct themselves well. You can look them over later yourself — promote those who serve you well, and sell off those who don't..."

Elder Sister Rujin's eyes sharpened and she added: "Even though they are people given by your two sisters, don't indulge them! If they work hard and behave well, that is all well and good — but if any of them are the disloyal, lazy, slippery type, don't be gentle. Beat them and sell them off — that's the sensible thing to do!"

Qin Ruyin felt a warm glow in her heart. She pulled both sisters closer to her with one hand each, and rested her head on their shoulders.

"How did I come to have such wonderful sisters? I am truly so fortunate..."

"Look at this girl — isn't she being silly?" Ruxiu laughed helplessly.

Rujin also shook her head with an indulgent smile, and reached out to tap the tip of Qin Ruyin's nose. "Our Sixth Miss is not silly at all!"

Ruxiu thought of what their mother had told the two of them about this little girl — how the things she had said had made Old Madam Qin and the Second Branch couple livid enough to spit blood — and laughed freely.

"Yes, yes, yes! Our Sixth Miss has grown up, and she is no longer that meek and mild little fool she once was!"

Qin Ruyin whined and played the coquette with her two sisters. Suddenly she remembered the dark rings Elder Sister had been unable to conceal even with thick powder...

She gave a start and lifted her head, looking seriously into Elder Sister Rujin's eyes.

"Elder Sister, I am not a child anymore. Please tell me honestly — have you been dealing with something serious lately?"

Rujin was momentarily taken aback.

Ruxiu, hearing this, immediately sat up straight, turning to look at their little sister in surprise.

Then she glanced at Elder Sister Rujin.

But Rujin was absorbed in her surprise and did not notice Second Sister looking at her.

Her fingers, holding her handkerchief, tightened slightly. She forced a smile and looked at Qin Ruyin. "Sixth Miss, what wild ideas are you getting? Why do you ask that?"

Qin Ruyin shook her head, her expression grave.

"Elder Sister, I am not getting wild ideas. Go look in the mirror — your thick powder cannot even hide the dark circles under your eyes." Qin Ruyin said it plainly.

Rujin: '...'

Then followed a long silence.

It was Ruxiu who could not bear it any longer and spoke. "Elder Sister, Sixth Miss has truly grown up — she is no longer the little shadow who used to trail behind us."

"There are some things you can tell me, or you can tell Sixth Miss. Otherwise you will only make her worry."

Rujin's eyes reddened at once.

In truth, she had been ashamed to speak of her present circumstances to her youngest sister, who was only sixteen.

Everyone said she had made a fine match — her husband was promising in his career, and devoted only to her.

But as they say: the person who drinks the water knows whether it is cold or hot.

Her husband, Han Zheng, was caring and cherishing toward her. Yet there were places in the inner quarters where a man's hand could not reach...

With Elder Sister's silent permission, Ruxiu took Qin Ruyin's hand and sighed: "It is Elder Sister's mother-in-law pressing her for a child."

"But is having children something Elder Sister alone can control? If one keeps staring at the woman's belly, shouldn't one also wonder whether there's a problem on the man's side?"

Ruxiu said this with some indignation — and there was also a grievance against Han Zheng, her elder sister's husband, in her voice.

"Ruxiu!" Rujin said with a tearful, exasperated laugh. "Tell Sixth Miss properly — don't say too much."

"Elder Sister, are you still defending Brother-in-law?" Ruxiu stared at her.

Rujin also let out a sigh, and a deep, dissolving grief rose in her eyes.

She still gently began to explain to her two sisters.

"Your Elder Sister-in-law has been a wonderful husband all these years, caring and protecting me — even though there has been no movement in my belly since we married, he has been the same as always, shielding me and encouraging me."

"But however unconcerned he is, his father and mother are anxious..."

Ruxiu's crescent brows shot upward. "So this time around? Your mother-in-law wants him to take a concubine, and she has already picked someone out — surely Elder Sister cannot truly mean to agree?"

Qin Ruyin's delicate brows also knitted. "Elder Sister, has it truly come to this already?"

Rujin smiled bitterly and shook her head at her youngest sister. "Your Second Sister's temper is always in a rush. How could it have come to that?"

"'How could it have come to that?' — why didn't Brother-in-law firmly stand on your side and refuse his mother's plan to put someone in his inner quarters?"

Qin Ruyin also fixed her gaze steadily on her Elder Sister.

"You both don't understand," Rujin finally said. "That Miss Liu is no ordinary young woman from a common family — she happens to be the only child of my mother-in-law's youngest sister."

"That Miss Liu's mother passed away last month. My mother-in-law, in her grief for her youngest sister, made a promise at her youngest sister's deathbed that she would find Miss Liu the best possible match."

"The thing is, that youngest aunt clung to my mother-in-law on her deathbed and said that only if Miss Liu was brought into the Han household would she be able to leave in peace..."

"Oh, so then Miss Liu the cousin is to become a concubine in the Han household? A concubine — what kind of 'fine match' is that? I think your mother-in-law is simply muddleheaded!"

Ruxiu was incensed.

Her angry cheeks puffed out.

"Xiu-jie, you are being rash again." Rujin looked at her impetuous second sister with disapproval.

"In truth, my mother-in-law has been a very good mother-in-law to me all these years. Even with no child showing for so long, she has never humiliated me to my face. The matter of Miss Liu is, one could say, a kind of..."

Qin Ruyin was frowning through all of this.

Her Elder Sister had once been a woman of her own quiet plans and ambitions. But these years of marriage without conceiving a child had ground away her confidence entirely.

To the point that she was now entertaining thoughts of compromising with her mother-in-law.

So Qin Ruyin cut in at once.

"Elder Sister, you are only twenty-four. There is no need to rush so over the matter of children. And besides — what Second Sister said just now, that children are a matter for both husband and wife together, is entirely right!"

"All these years it has been you seeking physicians and taking medicine. Shouldn't Brother-in-law also see a physician?"

Rujin was somewhat dazed by this flurry of questions from the little sister she had always held in the palm of her hand.

chapters.title: Elder Sister Rujin's Marriage Shows Red Flags
chapters.content:
Rujin gave a bitter smile.

It was not as though she had not thought of what her little sister was saying.

But this was the husband she loved dearly — she did not want to think ill of him...

Seeing her expression, Qin Ruyin knew what she was feeling.

Elder Sister and Brother-in-law Han Zheng had been drawn to each other at first sight — a young couple, deeply in love. That Elder Sister wore rose-tinted glasses where he was concerned was perfectly natural.

Ruxiu, ever the plain-spoken one, turned to Rujin. "Look — these are words only Sixth Miss can say to you. If I said them, you'd give me a good scolding."

Rujin said nothing.

"Therefore, right now Elder Sister should speak plainly with Brother-in-law about her own thoughts, and also listen carefully to what is truly in his heart." Qin Ruyin said.

Rujin looked up at her youngest sister with a gaze full of complex feeling.

Qin Ruyin had a sudden intuition. She asked immediately: "Has Elder Sister already argued with Brother-in-law about the matter of the Liu family cousin?"

Rujin's aggrieved eyes turned red at once. She pressed her lips together and opened her mouth with difficulty.

"He... at the time, he told me he was not close with the Liu cousin and only felt sorry for her mother."

"After that... I couldn't bear to listen any longer and snapped back at him a few times. He... he was angry too, and went to spend the night in his study. And then shortly after, he was sent out of the capital on official business..."

No wonder, on a great day like today, Brother-in-law had not come to the Qin family with Elder Sister.

"I also don't know if he went out of the capital on his own initiative for work, or if it was his superior who sent him." Rujin said.

When she said this, she could no longer hide how hurt she was.

"When was this?" Qin Ruyin asked.

Rujin dabbed at her eye with her handkerchief and said in a low voice: "Four days ago. He left in a hurry — the bureau handled all the packing. He only sent someone home to say it was an urgent matter and he had to leave the capital immediately."

Qin Ruyin thought to herself: it must be that Brother-in-law's official business was extremely pressing, and that was why he had not been able to write to Elder Sister in time to explain.

The matter of the Liu cousin would of course have to wait until Brother-in-law returned.

She thought it over and shared her view with Elder Sister.

After hearing it, Elder Sister shook her head slightly. "Your Brother-in-law and I have been married all these years and never has he been like this — he has never had cross words with me and then gone to spend the night in the study. I always feel..."

"I know what Elder Sister means." Ruxiu cut in. "Are you not just worried that if Brother-in-law truly has a mind to take the Liu girl as a concubine, what you should do about it?"

Rujin said nothing.

Qin Ruyin didn't overthink it. She asked her Elder Sister directly: "Elder Sister — are you willing to spend a lifetime with a husband who has taken a concubine..."

"Managing his household, overseeing his concubines, using your dowry to fill the communal accounts, and raising concubine-born sons and daughters with your own dowry?"

In an instant, the face of dignified beauty went completely white.

She had never envisioned such a life!

She dared not even think of it!

Seeing her shake her head in horror, Qin Ruyin's heart eased.

Ruxiu also breathed a sigh of relief.

She exchanged a glance with her little sister.

"Elder Sister does not want that life, then we must not go and live it!" Qin Ruyin said.

"But for now, Elder Sister need not think so far ahead. Everything must wait until Brother-in-law has finished his official business and returned to the capital."

She paused, then continued: "I just thought of something. Before this, when the Old Madam used to show off that she treated all her granddaughters equally, she would always take me and Fifth Miss to the temple to offer incense..."

"Once I heard a lady who had had no children for many years come to give thanks to the Fertility Goddess, saying she had finally had a child after years of marriage..."

"The presiding master of the Fertility Goddess hall said directly that this lady had adopted a child, and that it was not that she herself was destined for children, but that the child she adopted was destined to bring siblings."

Qin Ruyin looked seriously at her Elder Sister. "Elder Sister — once Brother-in-law comes home, if you are truly of one heart and mind, and he has no desire for a concubine, then steel yourself and insist on first adopting a child from Brother-in-law's clan."

"Adopting a child?" Rujin murmured.

Ruxiu clapped her hands in agreement. "Elder Sister, this is a wonderful idea! My own muddlehead of a husband said as much before — he said Elder Sister worries about children every day, so why not first adopt a child, and having a child about the house would lift her spirits!"

"And as the presiding master of the Fertility Goddess hall said — maybe after Elder Sister and Brother-in-law adopt a child, it will bring Elder Sister and Brother-in-law a child of their own?"

Rujin shook her head helplessly.

"Before, your Brother-in-law also mentioned several times to his parents wanting to adopt an orphaned girl from the clan to raise as a daughter..."

"But the two old people were opposed. They said that with Brother-in-law being so outstanding, he must certainly have his own legitimate sons and daughters — how could he go and adopt?"

Qin Ruyin thought about it — of course, given Brother-in-law's gifts and talents, his intelligence being what it was, whatever she could think of, he had certainly thought of as well.

It was just that, as a filial son, there were often things one could do nothing about with one's own parents.

Especially in a feudal society that placed paramount importance on filial piety.

In the world she had once come from, the decision to adopt a child would only need to be made by the husband and wife together.

Elder Sister had certainly also wanted to adopt a child before.

But there were too many obstacles.

And yet, things were different now.

"Elder Sister, if you are certain that Brother-in-law is of one mind with you and still has no intention of taking a concubine, then stand firm. Insist on first adopting a child."

"If your in-laws object, you can ask them directly: would they prefer to adopt one from the Han clan, or from the Qin family's branch..."

Qin Ruyin had not finished when Ruxiu clapped her hands and burst out laughing.

"Brilliant! Our Sixth Miss is truly formidable!" She tilted her head to look at her little sister, her eyes full of delight and admiration.

She then turned to look at Elder Sister Rujin. "Elder Sister — before this, our First Branch has always been kept down, and there was nothing we could do. Even when Father and Mother and our brothers wanted to stand up for you, the Han clan never really regarded us as anyone..."

"But now it is different!"

"Yes! Elder Sister, things are different now! At least now, I can stand up for you!" Qin Ruyin said.

Rujin reached out and touched Qin Ruyin's forehead, moved in spite of herself.

She said: "Foolish girl — after you marry into the Jiang family, take good care of yourself and live well. Seeing you happy and content is what would make Elder Sister truly glad! You have not even married yet — how can you already be talking about standing up for Elder Sister?"

"Whatever happens to me after I marry into the Jiang family, the Jiang family has given me such great face for now. Outsiders will have to think twice before they act. There is no need to worry about me, Elder Sister — I know what I'm doing."

"Our Sixth Miss has truly grown up!" Ruxiu also looked at Qin Ruyin with deep affection, and was genuinely happy for her.

She then said to Rujin: "Elder Sister, listen to Sixth Miss this time. Never mind anything else — the Master Jiang that Sixth Miss is marrying is an Associate Second-Rank Left Vice-Minister of the Board of Personnel!"

"The Board of Personnel controls the appointment, dismissal, and assessment of officials across the realm. Even if Brother-in-law is a man of upright and unyielding character, your in-laws and the Han clan only know how to size people up according to their station."

"If you ask them whether they want to adopt from the Han clan or the Qin family's branch, they will understand — and they will no longer dare to push you around."

"Mm, I will think it over carefully." She paused, then, worried her two sisters might form a poor impression of her husband, she spoke to smooth things over on Han Zheng's behalf. "Don't blame your Brother-in-law — he is always good to me."

Qin Ruyin sighed inwardly.

She too had been careful not to say anything the least bit resentful of Elder Sister-in-law Han Zheng, not wanting to cause Elder Sister embarrassment.

Yet when all was said and done, was the problem not precisely with Brother-in-law Han Zheng?

chapters.title: Is Brother-in-law a Great Paragon of Filial Piety? Giving Elder Sister Advice
chapters.content:
If Han Zheng had stood firmly on Elder Sister's side, how could his parents possibly dare give Elder Sister a hard time?

Han Zheng wanted to be a paragon of filial piety. His parents were not saints. Then the one to bear the endless grievances would be his wife — her Elder Sister.

How hard it was to be a woman in ancient times.

To be a wife was even harder.

A woman as dignified, well-mannered, virtuous, and beautiful as her Elder Sister was being driven to the brink — with nearly no way out.

If the original Ruyin had not had that accident, followed by the unexpected swap of betrothal that landed her in the Jiang family's match, Elder Sister's future would truly have been uncertain.

"Elder Sister, as long as you understand — before this, all of us in the First Branch had to hold ourselves back, and even you and Second Sister, having already married out, had to endure in your husband's households as well."

"From today onwards, what you should not endure, endure no longer! Sometimes, if you firmly defend your own interests, others will have to give way — even if they are your elders!"

She had come here. She would give everything she had, and would absolutely not let her sisters sink into the mud.

Qin Ruyin said this to Rujin, and watching the flickers of light rise in Elder Sister's red and swollen eyes, she understood that Elder Sister had been persuaded.

Rujin nodded, looking earnestly at her little sister, and said solemnly: "Sixth Miss need not worry for Elder Sister. Elder Sister just had a momentary lapse — but hearing your words and Second Sister's, I see it all clearly now!"

She truly did see it clearly.

Though it was somewhat shameful to admit, the truth was the truth.

With Sixth Miss having this match with Master Jiang, the First Branch of the Qin family was different from this day forward!

As they say: when one person ascends, even the chickens and dogs rise with them.

They, as married-out daughters, would inevitably carry a different weight in their husbands' households!

Seeing Elder Sister regain her spirit, Ruxiu quietly gave Qin Ruyin a thumbs-up.

"All right — enough about my affairs. Right now the most important thing is Sixth Miss's great happiness."

Rujin was the Elder Sister, and she had the manner of one to the end — her mind was always on her sister's lifelong affairs.

"The Jiang family sent their betrothal gifts today, and Sixth Miss's wedding day is drawing closer and closer." She gazed at Qin Ruyin with fond helplessness. "Sixth Miss is wonderful in every way, but as for needlework — no matter how Second Sister and I tried to teach you, you never mastered it."

Ruxiu remembered little sister as a child, being driven by their mother, those tender little white hands pricked swollen by the embroidery needle, and the butterfly she had embroidered that looked more like a fat bumblebee — and could not help laughing.

She looked at Qin Ruyin and said merrily: "Old Madam said for you to embroider your trousseau, but there is no need for you to actually take that seriously!"

"Your trousseau — Second Sister and Elder Sister started embroidering it several years ago. When the time comes we'll put it all together, and you just put in a few stitches for appearance's sake."

"Wonderful, wonderful, how wonderful!" Qin Ruyin nearly had tears in her eyes. She scrambled off the daybed and clasped her hands together in a bow to her two sisters. "Thank you, Elder Sister and Second Sister, for saving my life!"

Ruxiu laughed until she could barely stand, then pretended to give her bun a light tap. She turned to Elder Sister Rujin. "Look at this girl — she has truly lost all restraint! Who says things like that?"

"If she's a 'dog'... then what does that make us?"

"She is still young, and so are you — so why are you talking nonsense along with her?" Rujin scolded Ruxiu with utmost seriousness, yet could not bring herself to say a harsh word to her little sister.

But thinking of her little sister's comical words and comical look, she too could not help laughing.

For a moment the small inner room was filled with merry laughter.

It had been a long time since the three sisters had laughed this happily.

After the two sisters left, Qin Ruyin sorted out the trousseau gifts the two sisters-in-law and two sisters had given her and put them together.

She took a look: Elder Sister-in-law's box held the land deed for a small manor, along with six hundred taels of trousseau silver.

Second Sister-in-law's box held the property deed for a small shop, along with six hundred taels of trousseau silver.

The two sisters, in addition to the accompanying households and maids, had each also given Qin Ruyin six hundred taels of trousseau silver.

Once Zisu had put everything away, she came to attend to her mistress.

Seeing that her mistress was writing and sketching on a sheet of paper, she did not dare make a sound.

Qin Ruyin finished writing her plans — the kind only she herself could decipher — and turned to find Zisu had come.

"You have come at just the right time. Tomorrow, find a moment to instruct your brother: have him find a few quick-witted people to go to Elder Sister's husband's household and look into several matters..."

Zisu hurried to agree.

"Mm. Today is a happy day — take a handful of silver seeds to your brother as a treat from me, your mistress. Tell him to buy himself some snacks. But no drinking — drinking causes trouble."

Zisu cheerfully agreed.

"Oh yes — I don't shortchange people who do work for me. Tomorrow also take twenty taels of silver to your brother."

This time Zisu said: "Miss, it is only a matter of asking a few people to make some inquiries. Twenty taels is too much!"

Miss, even if you are flush with money now, you cannot go throwing it around like this.

When Miss marries into the Jiang family as a new bride, there will be even more places to spend money...

"Listen to me on this. Once your brother gets the silver he will understand. He will do it well." Qin Ruyin smiled. "If he does not do it well, then this will be the last time he helps your mistress with anything."

Zisu immediately fell to her knees. "Miss, your servant's brother will absolutely do his best for Miss and would not dare be sloppy or disloyal!"

Qin Ruyin personally helped her up, smiling. "What is this? Get up quickly. I trust you and your brother — I am even planning to bring your whole family with me when I marry into the Jiang family."

"Of course, that is only your mistress's own wish. Whether or not you are willing is also for your family to decide." Qin Ruyin looked at her.

Zisu was so moved her knees went weak and she nearly knelt again.

Qin Ruyin caught her with one hand. "All right, all right — I don't like all this kneeling for every little thing. There has been no great offence. No kneeling over nothing."

Zisu was so excited she could barely form words, but kept nodding over and over, saying her whole family would absolutely be willing to follow the Miss when she married...

Following Miss to the Jiang family — what an honor, what an immeasurable stroke of good fortune that would be!

"Miss, you were busy before and servant did not dare tell you — today when the Fourth Master Jiang's betrothal gifts were carried in one after another into our First Branch's courtyard, the servants of the household were all wide-eyed with amazement..."

"After that, quite a few people came to find servant, asking servant to put in a good word for them with you, Miss — begging you to quietly choose their whole families as accompanying households to go with you to the Jiang family!"

Qin Ruyin gave a slight smile. Quietly choose them... what a nerve.

"Servant knows some people are just toadies. Servant couldn't look at them — and I don't know how they had the nerve even to think of it!"

Zisu thought of certain people's expressions and felt thoroughly disgusted.

"Never mind — pay no attention to those people. Accompanying households, or people going with me to serve in the Jiang family — besides the few of you who have been with me since childhood, the rest are all for the elders to decide on."

"You only need to know that — and that is what you can tell any of them who come to petition you."

Zisu was loyal, and also always quick-witted. During all those years of the First Branch being kept down, she had not seen much from her original mistress — but once properly trained, she would be Ruyin's reliable right hand.

These past days, Qin Ruyin had been observing this girl, and was very satisfied with what she saw.

Here, Zisu listened to her mistress's instructions and nodded vigorously.

Qin Ruyin smiled and sent her off to work.

She had barely dispatched Zisu when second-rank maid Yimi hurried in through the door curtain. "Miss, Seventh Miss of the Third Branch has come."

Yimi's face was unwell, which clearly meant something was amiss with Seventh Miss of the Third Branch.

chapters.title: The Strange Matter of Seventh Miss Qin of the Third Branch
chapters.content:
Since arriving in this body, Qin Ruyin had not yet met Seventh Miss of the Third Branch.

She thought carefully, and a stream of information surfaced in her mind.

The Third Branch of the Qin family was concubine-born.

When the original Ruyin's paternal grandmother was gravely ill, she had elevated a loyal senior maid to concubine status, in order to oppose the then-favored and not-yet-principal-wife Old Madam Qin.

That concubine became the Third Master's mother — Concubine Grandmother Chen.

The year after Old Madam Qin gave birth to Second Master Qin, Third Master Qin came along.

Old Madam Qin had naturally employed no few poisonous methods in her attempts to rid Concubine Chen of her child.

It was the original Ruyin's paternal grandmother Madam Wei who had risked everything to protect Concubine Chen, and thus Third Master Qin came into the world.

The Third Master's inner quarters were relatively peaceful — only one wife and one concubine.

His wife had borne him two legitimate sons; his concubine had borne him two daughters.

This Seventh Miss was Third Master Qin's eldest concubine-born daughter, Qin Ruyan.

In the Qin family's genealogy, all daughters of Ruyin's generation took their given names with the character "Ru" — there was no distinction made between legitimate and concubine-born.

Speaking of Third Master Qin — he was also a man of good fortune.

He was only a little over a year younger than Second Master Qin, and in his childhood both he and his mother had been protected by the original Ruyin's paternal grandmother.

After the paternal grandmother was gone, First Master Qin — even while being ground down hard by Old Madam Qin himself — had still exerted himself to the utmost to shield him.

Old Madam Qin and Second Master Qin mother and son were mostly focused on suppressing the First Branch. As long as the Third Branch did not stand out, they could not be bothered to spare the effort to oppress them as well.

Besides, Third Master Qin was completely without distinction or ability. It was Second Master Qin who, for the sake of appearances, had managed to buy him an honorary post.

It sounded respectable on the surface — a seventh-rank official — but in reality it was a figurehead, the kind of post the court turned a blind eye to in order to give noble families a face-saving cover for their undistinguished sons.

Qin Ruyin drew on the original Ruyin's memories and reflected. She had no favorable impression of the Third Uncle she saw only a few times a year.

There was even some resentment.

She felt Third Uncle was an ingrate.

Her father had been in such dire straits and had never abandoned him, shielding him well — yet Third Uncle, fearing perhaps that he too would be dragged down and suppressed by Old Madam Qin and her sons, had voluntarily distanced himself from her father.

He had also completely ignored all of these nephews and nieces.

And this Seventh Miss — the original Ruyin had barely seen her even a few times a year. What was she coming to her courtyard for now?

Qin Ruyin had no expectation of her coming to give a trousseau gift.

Third Aunt had already come on behalf of the Third Branch to give her trousseau gifts today.

Though Seventh Miss was the eldest daughter of the Third Branch, she was concubine-born, was not in favor with the Third Branch's mistress — her Third Aunt — and was younger than Qin Ruyin by several months, making her the younger sister.

Even if she wanted to give a trousseau gift to this elder sister of hers, she would not have the means.

Qin Ruyin had no expectation of a trousseau gift from her.

But now that she had come, they were all family — there was no reason to turn her away.

"Please ask Seventh Miss in to speak." Qin Ruyin instructed.

"Miss, Seventh Miss looks to be unwell — her eyes are all red from crying..." Yimi hurried to report.

Qin Ruyin waved her hand. "Show her in and we will see."

Yimi agreed and started to withdraw. Qin Ruyin added one more instruction: "Send a quick-witted little maid to make discreet inquiries at the Third Branch."

"Yes, Miss!" Only then did Yimi withdraw.

A short while later, Yimi led Seventh Miss in.

Qin Ruyin looked over at her.

Seventh Miss wore a half-new water-blue robe embroidered with narcissus vines, and inside it a pale pink short jacket over a long skirt. She was quite fresh-faced and delightful.

Though her stature was short, this only added to her petite, sweet-natured charm.

Qin Ruyin could not help but marvel: there was not a single plain-looking girl among all the daughters of the Qin family.

Each had her own charm and manner.

"Greeting Sixth Sister." Seventh Miss curtsied to Qin Ruyin. Qin Ruyin rose and returned the greeting between equals.

"Seventh Sister has come — please, no need to be so formal. Come and sit." She smiled.

Toward this cousin of the Third Branch, she could not be as warm and intimate as she was with her own two sisters or two sisters-in-law.

Seventh Miss shyly thanked her and took a seat below Qin Ruyin under Yimi's guidance.

"Seventh Miss has come to congratulate Sixth Sister and also to bring a trousseau gift."

She bit lightly at her pearlescent lower teeth, and from the sachet at her waist produced a little bundle of red cloth.

She had actually come to give a trousseau gift?

Then what were those red and swollen eyes about?

Qin Ruyin showed nothing and smiled warmly. "Thank you, Seventh Sister. But Seventh Sister is younger than me — how could I accept a trousseau gift from you, a younger sister? Please take it back!"

"Sixth Sister, this is a small token of my sincerity. If you will not accept it... then I, then I..."

The little girl was both bashful and flustered, her powdery face flushing crimson.

Looking at her like that, Qin Ruyin was reluctant, but truly did not want to take her things.

Just from her dress and appearance one could see that life was not easy for her in the Third Branch.

And coming today, using congratulations and a trousseau gift as a pretext to see her — there was in all likelihood some request behind it.

In that case, this so-called trousseau gift would be a hot potato.

Though this little girl was only fifteen or sixteen and looked timid and gentle, Qin Ruyin did not dare underestimate her in the least.

"Sixth Sister, don't say that. I have always longed to be close to First Elder Sister, Second Elder Sister, and you, Sixth Sister. It is only that our Third Branch's circumstances... I never dared to."

"Now Sixth Sister is also about to marry out. If I cannot even give Sixth Sister a trousseau gift, I am afraid I will have no more chances to be close to Sixth Sister in the future."

After saying this, she opened the red cloth in her hands with a show of good cheer. "Sixth Sister, look — this bracelet was passed down to me by my own maternal grandmother. The jade quality is quite good..."

Something stirred in Qin Ruyin's heart.

She had not missed those words just spoken — "afraid I will have no more chances to be close to Sixth Sister in the future."

She had also not missed that this little girl said the bracelet was from "her own maternal grandmother"...

She was concubine-born, so in name she was counted as the Third Branch mistress's daughter.

If she said "grandmother," that should refer to Third Aunt's mother.

But this little girl said her own maternal grandmother.

That would mean her concubine-mother's mother.

Either this little girl was exceptionally deep and calculating, or she was a person of utterly genuine sincerity.

Qin Ruyin fixed her gaze on her.

Though the little girl still wore a timid look, she bravely met Qin Ruyin's eyes for a moment.

Those eyes were entirely clear and transparent.

Qin Ruyin's gaze softened. She reached out and wrapped the little red bundle back up in her hands.

Under Seventh Miss's surprised eyes, Qin Ruyin held her hands and pushed them back.

"This was given to you by your own maternal grandmother. You should keep it as a keepsake. It should not be given to someone else."

"Sixth Sister..." The little girl's small face went white. Apart from this bracelet given to her by her own maternal grandmother, she had nothing else of value.

She was no fool. From the age of ten, she had seen clearly: everyone in the Third Branch lived under the shelter of their First Uncle and First Aunt.

Her concubine-mother had also told her: when the First Uncle was in the most desperate of times, he had still sent First Aunt with silver to relieve their household...

She had no way to repay the First Uncle's family.

But in her heart she had always longed to be close to the First Uncle's family, and was very willing to repay them.

Only, she feared she would have no such chance now...

chapters.title: Seventh Miss Brings a Trousseau Gift and Falls into the Second Branch's Hands
chapters.content:
"Sixth Sister, I truly want to give you this as a trousseau gift. This bracelet may not be worth much, but it is my sincerest wish..."

She had in fact already noticed the change in Sixth Sister's eyes — from indifference at first meeting to now a reluctance to take from her.

Sixth Sister felt sorry for her!

Noticing this, Seventh Miss felt a leap of joy in her heart.

Only — Sixth Sister had said to keep the bracelet as a keepsake. But how could Sixth Sister know that she was giving Sixth Sister this bracelet precisely to give her a keepsake?

Of course she knew Sixth Sister had never been the sort to be snobbish about wealth, and would not look down on this younger sister's trousseau gift for being inadequate.

But if Sixth Sister did not want it, there was nothing she could say.

She had said the words, but in her heart she felt ashamed.

Seeing her say this, Qin Ruyin sighed.

"I am not at all looking down on what you have brought. For one thing, it was given to you by your own maternal grandmother. For another, I know the circumstances in your Third Branch these years — they were not much easier than ours in the First Branch."

Qin Ruyin saw the little girl's eyes grow even redder and felt very conflicted herself.

"And besides — I suspect, Seventh Sister, that you have very few nice things to call your own, which is precisely why I insist you keep it."

At these words, Seventh Miss was immediately cheered, and her brows and eyes smoothed open.

She looked up and gave Qin Ruyin a bright-eyed glance.

"Sixth Sister's heart — I have always known it!" She broke into a shy little smile and gathered her courage, pressing the red-cloth-wrapped bracelet firmly into Qin Ruyin's hands.

"Sixth Sister, you must accept it! Otherwise I am going to be heartbroken..." Afraid Qin Ruyin would still refuse, Seventh Miss shoved the bracelet out and immediately got to her feet and bolted.

"Sixth Sister, you must be happy! I wish you and Brother-in-law Jiang a lifetime of harmony and peace!"

She held up her skirt and ran, still turning back to call her blessings to Qin Ruyin.

"Hey... Seventh Sister, don't run!" Qin Ruyin was too slow to react, and too late to rise.

She watched Seventh Miss turn a smile as radiant as a blazing sunset sky upon her, then run headlong into Yimi, who was just coming in through the door curtain.

"Seventh Miss, you—" Yimi scrambled to help Seventh Miss steady herself. Seventh Miss waved her off quickly: "I'm fine — hurry and go attend to Sixth Sister!"

And she hitched up her skirt again and ran.

Yimi had something urgent to report to her mistress, and truly could not spare attention for Seventh Miss. She went in through the curtain.

"Miss—" but she saw her mistress with her head down, putting on her shoes, clearly about to give chase after Seventh Miss. Yimi quickly said: "Seventh Miss is running fast — you would not catch her now."

Qin Ruyin sighed. "That girl — I don't know what's come over her, but something feels ominous."

"Oh yes — go find a steady little maid to follow after Seventh Miss. I am not easy about it."

Yimi agreed and went first to carry out her mistress's instructions.

Once she had done the instructed task and came back into the room again:

"Miss, servant has news."

"Go ahead," Qin Ruyin nodded.

"Nothing else unusual in the Third Branch — it is only that today Seventh Miss had a quarrel with Third Branch's Concubine Lu, and then Seventh Miss cried and ran to find you, Miss..."

Qin Ruyin fell into a brief reverie.

Could it be that Seventh Miss and her own birth-mother Concubine Lu had quarreled over this bracelet she had wanted to give as a trousseau gift?

The bracelet was celadon green — the jade quality was indeed quite good, but the color was not one coveted by noble ladies and great madams.

So however fine the jade quality, the bracelet was of ordinary value.

She did not look down on it at all.

"Yimi, go and find a small jewelry box."

Seventh Miss had not even been able to produce a decent jewelry box for this bracelet — which showed well enough how impoverished her everyday life was, along with her younger sister Eighth Miss.

Yimi went and found a small jewelry box, and Qin Ruyin personally placed the bracelet inside.

She then took the box and went to find her mother, Li-shi.

......

Seventh Miss had barely run out of the First Branch's west courtyard when a strapping, sinister-looking old maidservant called out to her in a low, eerie voice: "Seventh Miss — good day."

Seventh Miss was so startled her soul nearly flew from her body.

"Seventh Miss need not be afraid. Old Servant attends in Second Madam's courtyard. Second Madam has sent old servant to invite Seventh Miss to the east courtyard for a visit."

At the mention of the east courtyard, Seventh Miss shook several times with fright.

The old maidservant gave her a disdainful look.

"Seventh Miss, the east courtyard is hardly a flood or a wild beast. What sort of performance is that? Come along quickly — we cannot keep Second Madam waiting!"

Seventh Miss lowered her head, biting her lip in humiliation until pink marks appeared on her soft lower lip.

"Yes, I will come with you, Mama..."

Here, Qin Ruyin brought the bracelet and went to find Li-shi, and told Li-shi everything about Seventh Miss coming to give her a trousseau gift.

Li-shi listened to it all and let out a sigh. "Poor Seventh Miss — she is a good-hearted child."

Qin Ruyin also openly admitted that she had initially suspected Seventh Miss's motives.

Li-shi said to her: "That child is a person of truly gentle and sincere goodness. It is a pity she was born of a concubine — a shame, truly."

"Accept this bracelet. It would fulfill the little sisterly feeling Seventh Miss has for you."

Her mother approved of the little girl, and Qin Ruyin too felt her heart ease, and felt even more tenderness.

"Seventh Sister lives so hard a life, with so few decent pieces of jewelry to call her own, and this one was given to her by her own maternal grandmother. I truly don't want to take it..."

Li-shi waved her hand and said: "First hold on to this bracelet. You feel sorry for her, and you will have chances in the future to return the favor."

She paused, and then laughed coldly.

"Speaking coldly — your Third Aunt is the sort who only takes and never gives. If she knew Seventh Miss still had a decent piece of jewelry hidden away..."

"You gave it back, and it would only end up going through her hands, and then Seventh Miss would not have it anymore. So you would only be hurting Seventh Miss's kind heart."

Li-shi's words were not finished when Zisu, who had finished her errand, came in to seek an audience.

Zisu was one of her mother's people to begin with, and what she had to report was not sensitive, so after paying her respects she reported directly to Qin Ruyin.

"Miss, after servant returned to the courtyard, Yimi told servant something: the Seventh Young Miss of the Third Branch, after leaving Miss's courtyard today, was taken away by Second Madam's people..."

Li-shi heard this and frowned, cutting Zisu off. "Why would that girl be taken away by Second Branch's wife?"

Qin Ruyin immediately rose.

"Could it be that Seventh Miss was in tears today because Second Branch was bullying her?" Li-shi said, and at once sent people to the Second Branch's side to find out.

Qin Ruyin was worried too, and did not leave her mother's courtyard. She waited for the people her mother had sent to return.

A quarter-hour later, those sent to inquire returned with news.

chapters.title: Trouble Stirs Again in the Qin Household; Wang Er-lang Returns Home
chapters.content:
"In reply to the First Madam, Sixth Miss..."

The one who came to report was a quick-witted little maid with her hair in twin loops.

After hearing the little maid's full report, Li-shi let out a breath of relief.

She said to Qin Ruyin: "Sixth Miss, set your mind at ease. Your Second Aunt is one who always loves to make a good show of herself in front of outsiders."

Qin Ruyin gave a lazy little curve of her lips.

Li-shi also gave a cold laugh and, worried her little daughter might not grasp it, proceeded to analyze things for her.

What Tong-shi had told outsiders was that she had turned up quite a lot of fabric when sorting through things to add to the trousseau — and knowing Seventh Miss Qin had come by this way, she had called Seventh Miss over to pick out some good cloth to take home.

This had several layers of meaning.

First, it told outsiders that she, as Second Aunt, was very fond of her nieces — the niece was about to marry out, and she was still actively searching for good things to add to the trousseau.

Second, even the niece who had no betrothal yet — even a concubine-born niece from the Third Branch — had not been forgotten. She had shared some good things with that Third Branch niece who had also come of age.

Third — if the Second Branch was beginning to scheme and maneuver to win over the Third Branch...

Qin Ruyin listened to her mother's analysis and sighed inwardly.

She had, after all, fought her way through school and earned herself a decent job.

But even with her brain, in this ancient inner household — without someone to guide her — she would probably have ended up dead very miserably.

For instance: the twists and turns her mother had just analyzed — she had not thought of those at the time.

What she had focused on was: Seventh Miss had only just left her courtyard — how had Second Branch's people appeared so quickly to take her away?

"All right, Yin-er — you are tired today and Mother is tired too. Since your Seventh Sister has come to no harm, settle your mind and go back. Rest early. There is plenty more to keep us busy ahead."

Qin Ruyin also saw the fatigue on her mother's face, and quickly and thoughtfully urged her to wash up and retire for the night. She herself bowed and withdrew with Zisu.

Li-shi looked at her youngest daughter with eyes full of tenderness. "Mother knows. Watch your footing on the way back — it is already dark. Have Zisu light the lantern properly."

Qin Ruyin agreed.

She took Zisu out of the room. Outside, it was already pitch dark. The early spring wind was still cold and biting, cutting against the face like a knife.

Walking along the covered walkway through the courtyard, Qin Ruyin said in a low voice to Zisu: "I keep feeling there is something not right about Seventh Miss. Have two sharp-eyed maidservants keep a quiet watch on that side."

Zisu responded in a murmur, then said: "Miss, is it because you are worried that Second Branch might be bullying Seventh Miss?"

Qin Ruyin's gaze cooled. "It is only a guess. I hope it is not so."

There was no shortage of power struggles within a family.

The Second Branch was already so powerful — if they were still bullying a teenager like this, they were truly contemptible!

"Miss, servant has something to say — I am not sure if I should..." Zisu looked at her mistress, hesitating.

"Say it. You are now the most trusted and capable person by your mistress's side — what is there you cannot say?" Qin Ruyin laughed, teasing Zisu.

Zisu was embarrassed to be teased by her mistress, but she was pleased.

Then her expression turned grave. "Miss, your compassion for Seventh Miss speaks to your kind heart — but if, as First Madam suspects, the Second Branch is beginning to win over the Third Branch and going about it through Seventh Miss... then your kindness toward her would be wasted."

Zisu said this boldly.

She was also worried about being scolded by her mistress.

But Qin Ruyin praised her.

Then shook her head. "In my view, even if the Second Branch wants to win over the Third Branch, they should not be going about it through Seventh Miss."

Seventh Miss Qin and her sister Eighth Miss were both born of Third Master's Concubine Lu, and therefore not in favor with Third Aunt, the legitimate mistress. They had always been kept down.

She had no standing whatsoever in the Third Branch. If the Second Branch was courting allies, they should be courting Third Aunt, who was always sharpening her head to please Tong-shi.

After all, Third Aunt was the mistress of the Third Branch, and had both a voice and influence there.

"Just have people keep a careful watch on the Third Branch's side. If Seventh Miss is truly being mistreated, Third Uncle as her father should not stand by and watch."

Qin Ruyin added to herself: as long as that Third Uncle was not a complete fool.

......

Having given her instructions, Qin Ruyin put the matter of Seventh Miss aside.

After all, the two cousins of the Third Branch had rarely seen the original Ruyin — even if it was not the children's own wish, even if it was unavoidable, the reality was the reality.

Close contact had been few, and the bond of feeling had not grown deep enough.

Her willingness to intervene was entirely because she herself was a woman.

She could not bear to see a teenage girl, who had done no wrong, be schemed against and have her life destroyed by the so-called family around her.

......

The magnificent spectacle of the Jiang family sending betrothal gifts to the Qin family's Sixth Miss spread throughout every street and alleyway of the capital overnight.

How many were envious, how many burned with jealousy, how many gnashed their teeth in resentment.

The Wang Household.

Lady Wang had returned yesterday from offering trousseau gifts at the Qin household, and had not slept all night, staying up until both her eyes were red and the bags under her eyes had turned dark.

She had originally been displeased with this substituted match that the Qin family had engineered — but her husband said the new match was better, and she had talked herself into accepting it.

Fifth Miss Qin had not yet married into the Wang family, but since the betrothal was settled, the two families visited one another as relatives.

On the day of Sixth Miss Qin's betrothal, the Wang family was also required to go and give trousseau gifts.

But she had not expected the Jiang family's betrothal gifts to be on such a grand scale — the betrothal gold alone was a "one-in-ten-thousand."

Was this not deliberately humiliating both the Qin family and her Wang family?

What the Jiang family had done was truly relentless!

They had clearly never thought anything of her Wang family or of the Qin family.

The Qin Second Branch had brought this on themselves — they deserved it.

But what had her Wang family done to deserve being dragged into this humiliation?

Lady Wang was seething with resentment. She had been so furious last night she had not slept a wink, lying there with her eyes open until dawn.

Now a servant came to report that breakfast was ready, but she had no appetite whatsoever.

A short while later, another little maid came to report that the Second Young Master had taken leave from his teacher and had come to pay his respects to his mother.

Lady Wang grew even more irritated.

Second Young Master Wang Huaimin was currently studying at the Imperial Academy in the capital.

He had heard of the Jiang family's betrothal gifts for Sixth Miss Qin from his classmates yesterday, and first thing this morning had taken leave from his teacher and hurried home.

First Master Wang had already left for the bureau early. Wang Huaimin had no choice but to come to his mother first.

"You still know to take leave and come back? You also heard about the Jiang family giving Sixth Miss of the Qin—?"

Without waiting for her son to bow and greet her, Lady Wang already looked at him with a cold face.

The young man was of incomparable refined handsomeness, dressed in a robe of cloud-brocade white, tall and straight as he stood, ethereal as an immortal.

This son had been the pride of herself and her husband, and even of their branch of the Wang clan.

Yet he had chosen precisely this critical juncture of his marriage to make a muddle of things, causing the present predicament.

If not for him drinking too much and being goaded into blurting out in front of others that he "wished to marry no one but Fifth Miss of the Qin family," the Qin Second Branch would never have been able to use that as justification to swap the betrothal.

Wang Huaimin had always been used to being the golden child. He was self-assured even before his own father, First Master Wang.

The only person he feared was his mother.

One could also say: the person he respected most was his mother.

"Mother, your son..."

The golden child flushed red from his mother's sharp words, started to argue, then caught his mother's slightly disappointed gaze and went mute.

His gaze also turned uncertain. "Mother, your son does not understand — why does the Jiang family hold Sixth Miss Qin in such regard? Sixth Miss Qin, she..."

chapters.title: Seventh Miss Qin Attempts to Take Her Own Life After Being Coerced by the Second Branch
chapters.content:
"What? Sixth Miss Qin — she was originally meant to be betrothed to you, wasn't she?" Lady Wang gave a cold laugh.

Wang Huaimin's exceptionally handsome face flushed crimson.

No one knows a son like his mother.

But the more she understood his feelings, the angrier Lady Wang became.

She scolded him: "Have you ever thought — if not for your drunken nonsense that day, Sixth Miss Qin would have been betrothed to you long ago!"

"Mother, please calm yourself. At the time... it was also the Minister of Public Works, Excellency Wei himself, who personally approached Father about the switch. Our family could not exactly..."

"Our family settling on Sixth Miss Qin was entirely proper and justifiable. If not for concern about what you had said in public when you were drunk, your father might not have agreed!"

"Mother, it is too late to say all of this now." Wang Huaimin furrowed his brow and bowed to Lady Wang. "Please, Mother, stop being angry over this matter."

"Hmph!" Lady Wang let out a heavy cold snort.

How could she not be angry?

"Look at this now — the Jiang family's betrothal gold for Sixth Miss Qin is a 'one-in-ten-thousand'! Even if Mother were to pledge her entire dowry, it would not be enough to raise ten thousand taels in cash."

Wang Huaimin argued back: "Mother, our family has been upright scholars for generations. We do not compete with others over such things."

"You say you don't compete — but can you truly just not compete? Your mother needs to go out and socialize too. When people start pointing fingers and mocking me behind my back, you men do not care!"

Wang Huaimin had rarely seen his mother this agitated.

In his heart, he understood that his mother was not truly concerned about how much betrothal gold any family gave their daughter-in-law.

What his mother felt more strongly was still her dissatisfaction with the Qin family's swap.

This also meant his mother had genuinely liked Sixth Miss Qin as her daughter-in-law.

"Mother, your son always feels that the Jiang family holding Sixth Miss Qin in such regard is not ordinary. In the circle of ladies you move in, have you ever heard of anything exceptional about Sixth Miss Qin?"

Lady Wang looked at him and actually gave it some thought.

Then she shook her head. "No, I have not. If there is truly anything exceptional about her, it would be that Sixth Miss Qin is very beautiful."

Wang Huaimin wrinkled his brow. For families like theirs, in choosing a principal wife, looks were not the primary concern.

Though of course he also liked a beautiful girl as a principal wife — who would not want to add beauty to beauty?

"To really speak of it, Fifth Miss of the Qin family is somewhat better-looking than Sixth Miss." Lady Wang looked at her son. "In taking a wife, virtue matters. And Fifth Miss Qin's looks suggest she is not a settled, steady sort."

Still, she disliked Fifth Miss Qin.

At this point, Wang Huaimin had no wish to hear about that.

So he said: "It is not the time to speak of these things now. What concerns your son more is: the Jiang family has deep foundations — I fear they know something others do not, which is why they hold Sixth Miss Qin in such regard."

Lady Wang, seeing her son raise this question again, calmed down and thought it through, and understood.

"You suspect Sixth Miss Qin's origins are problematic?" she asked.

Wang Huaimin looked at her, his expression unreadable.

"That Fourth Master Jiang has been renowned since youth — he is no pushover! If he did not truly hold her in high regard, the betrothal gold for a replacement wife could not possibly be this lavish. He even chose the auspicious phrase 'one-in-ten-thousand' — it all looks as though he is trying to ingratiate himself with someone..."

If Sixth Miss Qin were truly the daughter of some imperial family or powerful noble, raised in the Qin First Branch — how could he not be bitter about that?

But Lady Wang laughed at this.

"Impossible! You are overthinking it!" she shook her head.

If Sixth Miss Qin were truly of some extraordinary status, how could she be placed in the Qin First Branch, rather than the Second Branch?

"How can Mother be so certain?" Wang Huaimin frowned.

In his heart, he thought: his mother was wise, but ultimately she was confined to the inner household and could not see far.

This matter still needed to be discussed with his father.

Lady Wang said quietly: "The Qin Second Branch has always been dominant. If there were truly such a golden opportunity to rise through the ranks, they would never allow it to go to the Qin First Branch."

Wang Huaimin made no comment on his mother's words.

There were times when, for the sake of a clan, things were done for show for outsiders — that was a possibility.

These things he had no intention of discussing with his mother. Even if he told her, his mother would not understand.

"Mother is quite right. Your son only took half a day's leave from his teacher today and must return to the Academy."

Lady Wang immediately said: "You came back in such a rush, you have not had breakfast yet, have you? It so happens that Mother hasn't eaten either — stay and eat with Mother."

No matter how angry she was at her son, a mother always worried about whether he had eaten and drunk enough.

Wang Huaimin clasped his hands in a bow. "Thank you, Mother. Your son bought breakfast on the road and ate it in the carriage. Mother please eat a good breakfast. Your son takes his leave."

Seeing her son eager to go back to the Academy, Lady Wang could not say anything.

She could only give instructions to those attending her son, give out reward money, and specially had someone fetch silver to supplement her son, telling him to eat and drink well at the Academy and not let himself starve.

The moment Wang Huaimin stepped out of his mother's courtyard, his expression turned dark as he gave instructions to the page boy walking behind him.

"Have several quick-witted people keep a watch on the Qin First Branch. And look carefully into Sixth Miss Qin."

"Young Master, Lady's meaning is not to..." the page boy said in surprise.

"Are you the young master, or am I? Go when told to go. So much talk!"

The page boy shuddered all over and hurried to agree.

Wang Huaimin knitted his handsome brows.

If it were truly found that Sixth Miss Qin's origins were extraordinary...

He would have to plan carefully!

Time always seems to fly when one is busy.

Qin Ruyin's wedding day was now only half a month away.

One day, Qin Ruyin was adding a few stitches to some robes for Jiang family elders that the seamstresses had embroidered. Huangqi came rushing in, face white, lifting the door curtain.

"Miss, something terrible has happened... Seventh Miss of the Third Branch has hanged herself from a roof beam with a white silk..."

Qin Ruyin's hand jerked, and the embroidery needle drove into the pad of her finger. Blood immediately welled up.

Seeing this, Huangqi panicked even more. "Miss, your hand... don't be alarmed — Seventh Miss has already been cut down."

Qin Ruyin felt no pain at all. She waved a hand at Huangqi while giving instructions: "Hurry and help me change — we are going to the Third Branch!"

A few days prior, Seventh Miss Qin had left her courtyard and been taken away by Second Branch's people. Tong-shi had indeed sent many bolts of good fabric to the Third Branch sisters.

At the time, the people she had set to watch had also reported that when Seventh Miss left the Second Branch, her face wore a smile.

Seeing this, Qin Ruyin had not sent people to keep watching the Third Branch.

Whatever choice Seventh Miss made, as long as she was not harming anyone in the First Branch, it was none of her concern.

Even if she had gone over to the Second Branch — that was just her way of making her own days a little more bearable.

Only, she would then have no more wish to be close to the Third Branch sisters.

And yet, only a few days later, Seventh Miss had attempted to take her own life.

Huangqi was so frantic she was all fumbling hands. Qin Ruyin had her stand to one side and dressed herself.

She asked as she did: "How is Seventh Miss now? Does Mother know?"

"In reply to Miss — the Third Branch has already called a physician... First Madam has already gone over with our Elder Young Mistress and Second Young Mistress."

"Do you know why Seventh Miss tried to take her own life?"

Huangqi shook her head in bewilderment. "I... I do not. Not even a whisper had come out before this."

Qin Ruyin nodded.

A girl just barely in her teenage years, in the full bloom of youth — how desperate must she have been to steel herself to tie a white silk around her own neck?

"You go first, as fast as you can, to the Third Branch. Find Elder Young Mistress quietly and ask her whatever happens, to keep the Third Branch people steady until I arrive!"

chapters.title: The Third Branch's Mistress Is Foolish; Ruyin Goes to the Rescue
chapters.content:
Huangqi's small face still showed alarm, to which a new trouble had now been added.

"What is the matter?" Qin Ruyin asked gently.

Huangqi had originally been a third-rank maid. Qin Ruyin, seeing that she went about things with care and caution and was fairly loyal to the original Ruyin as her mistress, had specially promoted her to second-rank.

But the girl was only fourteen, and had little experience weathering major events.

Meeting something as serious as Seventh Miss's attempted suicide, her alarm and fear were perfectly understandable.

Qin Ruyin did not reproach her.

She could see that Huangqi was a person of great potential, and wished to give her some tempering.

"In reply to Miss — there is no time to go and find Elder Young Mistress anymore. You will likely not be able to get into the Third Branch's courtyard yourself... Second Madam has already sent people to blockade all the paths leading to the Third Branch's courtyard."

Qin Ruyin drew a slow breath.

The moment Seventh Miss had an incident, Tong-shi had moved this fast to blockade the Third Branch's courtyard.

She was even more convinced that Seventh Miss's attempt on her life had something to do with the Second Branch.

Was the Second Branch about to have someone's life on their hands?

Before, there would perhaps have been no one who dared to intervene.

But she had come.

"Huangqi, don't be afraid. You are going to follow me to the Jiang family from here on out. If you cannot even handle something like this, how would I dare entrust things to you once we are at the Jiang family?"

Huangqi immediately made to kneel.

"No kneeling. Right now, go out and gather all the stout and argumentative mamas and aunties from our courtyard."

"Tell them: their Miss is worried about Seventh Miss and intends to take them along to force her way into the Third Branch."

"Tell them to do their best. If things go well, there will be good rewards for them all!"

Huangqi steadied herself, and her gaze gradually hardened with resolve. She answered firmly: "Yes, Miss! Huangqi will go at once!"

Qin Ruyin tucked a pearl flower into her hair bun and went out the door.

Huangqi had already assembled everyone as instructed.

When these women caught sight of Qin Ruyin standing on the steps outside the door, every face lit up with eagerness. They all curtsied and greeted her together.

"Sixth Miss, greetings!"

Huangqi also curtsied to her mistress.

She lowered her voice to report: "Miss, everyone is assembled. Servant also sent someone to wait at the back gate for Elder Sister Zisu, to tell Elder Sister Zisu to bring people to the Third Branch courtyard to reinforce you as soon as she returns."

"Well done!" Qin Ruyin praised her.

She thought for a moment, then added another instruction: "Send another person to the back gate to tell the one waiting for Zisu — have her ask her brother to prepare ahead of time. Gather all those in our First Branch's courtyard who have some skill in a fight."

Huangqi's expression sharpened, and she answered firmly.

Her Miss was going to war!

With such a good match as the Jiang family, Miss should no longer have to swallow humiliation from the Second Branch!

And yet Miss was still this kind-hearted, standing up for Seventh Miss of the Third Branch...

What right did the Second Branch have to keep Miss from going to the Third Branch?

She could not help thinking: just now when she went to gather the mamas and aunties, every single one of them had been pushing and shoving to get in on it...

Was it not because those mamas were eager to prove themselves before Miss?

Who among those serving in Miss's courtyard did not want to follow Miss to the Jiang family?

And thankfully, Miss had already let on that she would be bringing her.

She absolutely could not let Miss down!

The mamas below were all eager to catch the young Miss's eye, every one of them looking fierce and formidable — truly like soldiers following a general into battle.

Qin Ruyin was satisfied.

Before they set out, Huangqi gave them all one final rallying talk.

Leaving Yimi behind to watch the courtyard and giving her a round of instructions, Qin Ruyin led her people toward the Third Branch's courtyard.

Arriving outside the Third Branch's gate, she saw as expected: seven or eight servant women standing in tight formation, guarding the main entrance to the inner courtyard.

When they caught sight of Qin Ruyin coming over with so many people behind her, the fat, pale-faced Liu Mama who led them was startled inwardly.

Thinking of her own mistress, this mama put on a dark face at once, stepped forward to block Qin Ruyin, and gave a perfunctory bow.

Qin Ruyin recognized her.

She was a second-rank mama from Tong-shi's courtyard, surnamed Liu. She was also held in great favor by Tong-shi.

"Sixth Miss, why have you come?" Before Qin Ruyin could say anything, this mama spoke up first, presuming on her age to question the young mistress.

She was a person favored by Tong-shi, so of course her whole heart went to Tong-shi. Since the Jiang family's lavish betrothal had put Tong-shi off her food for several days, this mama had also taken a dislike to Qin Ruyin.

After all, her whole family depended on Second Madam for their livelihood.

If anything happened to Second Madam, where would she find such standing?

And how would her family get their share of the fat?

Qin Ruyin did not miss the resentment in Liu Mama's squinting eyes. She only looked coldly at this Liu Mama.

Huangqi stepped forward with a cool face. "What would stop our Miss from coming? Does our Miss need Liu Mama's permission to visit Seventh Miss and Eighth Miss?"

"And Liu Mama — you attend in Second Madam's courtyard. What are you doing here with all these people, blockading the Third Branch courtyard?"

Liu Mama, being challenged like this by a girl in her teens, was instantly furious.

Seeing that Huangqi had already spoken the words that needed to be said on her behalf, Qin Ruyin had no time to wrangle with a servant.

She glanced at Huangqi.

Huangqi read the signal and immediately called out loudly to the mamas behind her: "Aunties and sisters — this insolent servant is relying on Second Madam's backing to bully our mistress. What do you say we do about it?"

"Our Miss is about to be married to the Fourth Master Jiang, the Honorable Vice-Minister. How dare she bully our Miss! Sisters — are you going to stand for this?"

The mamas behind Huangqi immediately roared into action — tightening their sashes, rolling up their sleeves. Two to one, they surrounded Liu Mama.

The rest found their own opponents and began grappling.

Those with a quick and sharp tongue followed close behind the larger, sturdier mamas and began shouting.

Seeing that the mamas from her own courtyard had cleared a path for their mistress, Huangqi at once shielded Qin Ruyin and led her into the Third Branch's courtyard.

The Third Branch had few members to begin with, so the courtyard was not large, and the number of servants was small.

What with the great commotion happening in the Third Branch, some servants had been sent to guard the two young misses' courtyards, while others had slipped off to watch the excitement...

By the time Qin Ruyin walked in, it was as though she had entered an unguarded place.

One could see that the Third Branch's mistress, Third Aunt, was simply not up to the task — she could not even manage her own little plot of ground, and it showed.

Such a great event had occurred, and she had not deployed people to seal every inch of the courtyard. It was riddled with gaps.

Following the path to the courtyard where Seventh Miss lived, Qin Ruyin was met with a jostling crowd of men, women, old, and young pressing together.

Whatever Qin Ruyin's usual inclination to ignore things, at this moment she felt a surge of righteous anger.

Her mother Li-shi, though not a particularly formidable mistress either, kept the First Branch's courtyard clean and orderly.
