Even the spies Old Madam Qin and Tong-shi had planted there dared only do their deceiving in secret.

Coming to the Third Branch was simply more than the eyes could bear!

A moment's thought told Qin Ruyin why: the servants in the Third Branch were almost all people belonging to Old Madam Qin and Tong-shi.

Third Aunt was not a capable woman, and how many people she had truly won to her side was genuinely uncertain.

But Seventh Miss's life mattered more. Qin Ruyin kept her face cold as she entered Seventh Miss's courtyard.

From a distance she could already hear Old Madam Qin's voice scolding Third Branch's mistress, Zhou-shi.

As Qin Ruyin reached the doorway, the two mamas standing guard were greatly startled — but they reacted quickly, and both simultaneously extended their hands to block her.

"Sixth Miss! The Old Madam has given orders: apart from the masters of the household, no one else may enter!"

chapters.title: Mother in-law and daughter in-law
chapters.content:
"Step aside!" Qin Ruyin's voice rang out with cold authority.

Huangqi immediately stepped forward and shoved the two matrons' hands away, snapping, "Who do you think you are, daring to block our young mistress?"

The two matrons' faces darkened.

They were the Grand Matriarch's people! Even the Second Mistress would never have shown them such disrespect.

"Sixth Young Miss, you may be the mistress, but even you cannot simply ignore the Grand Matriarch's orders..."

Qin Ruyin's eyes flashed with a sharp chill. She cut them off in a cold voice: "What do you mean? I've come to visit my Seventh Sister — surely the Grand Matriarch, in her wisdom, wouldn't stand in the way of two sisters sharing their bond?"

The remark was so pointed that the two matrons couldn't respond.

Block her or not block her — both options were impossible.

If they blocked her and the Seventh Young Miss had already attempted to take her own life... and if the Sixth Young Miss went around spreading word that the Grand Matriarch had stood in the way of the sisters' bond — how would outsiders view the Grand Matriarch?

But if they didn't block her, they'd have no way to answer to the Grand Matriarch.

Truly a dilemma.

Qin Ruyin spoke deliberately loudly, making sure everyone inside the room could hear.

Just as she expected, everyone seated inside heard her. They all recognized her voice.

The Old Matriarch's face immediately darkened. She was so agitated she forgot to even reprimand Third Mistress Zhou.

She called out with feigned ignorance: "Who is making all that noise outside?"

Qin Ruyin replied brightly, "Grand Matriarch, it is Sixth Girl, come to visit Seventh Sister."

Before the old woman could speak, Tong Shi frowned and said, "Your Seventh Sister has had a little incident — this is no matter for a young girl like you. You had best return to your own courtyard, Sixth Girl."

Tong Shi then shot a pointed look at Li Shi, taunting, "Since Sixth Girl is soon to marry into the Jiang family, her manners ought to be properly taught, Elder Sister-in-law!"

Li Shi's expression darkened. Without a second thought, she fired back, "Second Sister-in-law, what a laugh! My Ruyin's manners are perfectly fine! Unlike some people who raised a daughter that tries to steal other girls' good marriage prospects!"

"You—!" Tong Shi nearly choked on her breath. "Elder Sister-in-law, have some shame in what you say!"

By then, Qin Ruyin had already led Huangqi past the two blocking matrons and entered the room.

Hearing Tong Shi's words, she smiled.

"Second Aunt, the world has always had shameless women who snatch others' good marriages. My mother said nothing about anyone in particular — why are you hollering at her?"

Tong Shi nearly fainted from rage.

The Old Matriarch frowned deeply, but still needed to maintain the image of a kind, benevolent grandmother.

She looked at Qin Ruyin and drew a breath, about to speak.

Qin Ruyin spoke first: "Where is Seventh Sister? How is she? Let me go see her!"

The Old Matriarch swallowed her fury, barely keeping her composure.

She said only: "Your Seventh Sister had a small incident, but she's all right now. Don't worry yourself. Run along home — this truly is not a matter for an unmarried young girl to be involved in."

She added, "Look around — are there any unmarried young ladies here?"

Qin Ruyin looked at her steadily.

"There are hardly any unmarried girls left in our household apart from the younger ones. But Seventh Sister has had such a serious incident, and yet Fifth Sister hasn't come — that is quite unexpected."

"Why are you dragging your Fifth Sister into this? Your Fifth Sister has always observed proper conduct..." Tong Shi snapped irritably, but was too choked with fury to finish the sentence.

"Oh, so in Second Aunt's view, sisterly feeling is inferior to that cold thing called 'propriety'." Qin Ruyin gave a cold smile.

"I once thought that Fifth Sister valued sisterly bonds most of all. It seems I was wrong about her. Or perhaps I wasn't — perhaps it is Second Aunt who has taught her badly."

Tong Shi's face turned deep crimson. She could only manage one word: "You—"

Qin Ruyin had already lost interest in sparring with Tong Shi.

Today she intended to make a scene. She reckoned neither the Old Matriarch nor Tong Shi would dare do anything to her.

She turned to Third Aunt Zhou Shi and asked, "Third Aunt, what on earth happened to Seventh Sister, for things to come to this?"

"Ah? Nothing... your Seventh Sister is fine. Where did you hear such nonsense?" Zhou Shi's face flushed red. She stammered, eyes darting away, not daring to meet Qin Ruyin's gaze.

She was an expert at reading the wind. She knew this particular niece of the First Branch was not someone she could afford to offend.

"Third Aunt, this Third Branch courtyard is full of gaps. With an incident this serious, the servants aren't just failing to do their duties — they're swarming around in three layers, gawking at the spectacle."

"Before tomorrow, news of Seventh Sister's incident will have spread throughout the entire capital."

"What... what?" Zhou Shi went pale with shock, instinctively looking toward the Old Matriarch.

The Old Matriarch nearly spat blood.

This Zhou Shi was an utter fool!

She turned to Zhou Shi in fury: "Why are you looking at me? This courtyard is the Third Branch's — you're the Third Branch mistress. You should be in charge here. You can't even manage this?"

Zhou Shi was full of grievances.

She said plaintively: "But Mother, when you arrived, didn't you say I needn't worry, and that Second Sister-in-law had already sent people to surround the Third Branch courtyard?"

Tong Shi, seeing the fire leap to her own feet, immediately couldn't hold back. She clutched her handkerchief and stood up: "I had people guarding the Third Branch courtyard for you, but the servants inside are your responsibility, Third Sister-in-law!"

Li Shi couldn't restrain herself any longer. She said coldly to Zhou Shi, "Third Sister-in-law, shouldn't you be sending a capable person outside to take a look right now?"

Only then did Zhou Shi seem to find her footing. She quickly whispered several instructions to her wet nurse Feng Mama, who composed her aging face and left to handle things.

The Old Matriarch sat turning her prayer beads, expression cold, and said nothing.

"Third Aunt, may I go see Seventh Sister?" Qin Ruyin looked at Zhou Shi.

Zhou Shi didn't know what to do and looked again at the Old Matriarch.

Qin Ruyin sighed softly. "Did the Grand Matriarch not just say that this is the Third Branch's courtyard, and Third Aunt should make her own decisions?"

The Old Matriarch's brows sharpened. The hand turning her prayer beads went still. She spoke coldly: "Sixth Girl, are you truly determined to behave so improperly?"

"I would respectfully ask the Grand Matriarch — going to see Seventh Sister is improper behavior? Then what, pray tell, is proper?"

The Old Matriarch's heart gave a start.

This girl — she was a different person today.

Since entering, she hadn't called her Grandmother even once. It was "Grand Matriarch" this and "Grand Matriarch" that.

Clearly she was trading on the prestige of that good marriage into the Jiang family, and no longer placed even this old woman in her eyes.

What a shallow, foolish little girl, drunk on sudden good fortune.

Her mood souring, she could no longer maintain the kindly facade.

"Sixth Girl, you're still young, and you have a long road ahead. Be careful that one moment of triumph doesn't lead to ruin."

Qin Ruyin curved her lips. "Grand Matriarch, have you perhaps heard the old saying: 'When life is good, drink deep of the pleasure'? Sixth Girl is actually grateful..."

She turned her head deliberately toward the cold-faced Tong Shi. "...Grateful to the Grand Matriarch and Second Aunt for giving Sixth Girl this very opportunity to feel pleased with herself!"

The Old Matriarch nearly shot up from her seat.

She drew a furious breath, barely keeping herself from choking.

Tong Shi's nails were nearly digging into her own palm.

Zhou Shi stole glances back and forth between them.

She finally understood one thing very clearly...

Neither the Old Matriarch nor Tong Shi — these two overlords of the Qin household's inner court — dared do anything to Sixth Young Miss, who was about to marry into the Jiang family.

What did that mean?

It meant the winds in the Qin household were about to change.

Then was it not foolish to keep clinging to those Tong clan women — mother- and daughter-in-law?

Moreover, if not for that vile Tong Shi, would the Third Branch have ended up in such chaos?

What bad luck — when her husband came home, she didn't know how angry he'd be with her...

"Well then... Sixth Girl, your Third Aunt understands now that you have a true sister's heart toward your Seventh Sister."

Zhou Shi immediately changed her tune, pretending to furrow her brows with deep worry as she looked at Qin Ruyin.

"Your Seventh Sister is not doing well at the moment. She's been rescued, but she's still crying and wishing to die... won't you go in and speak with her?"

chapters.title: A Scandal That Cannot Be Kept Secret
chapters.content:
"Third daughter-in-law!" The Old Matriarch rose abruptly to her feet, brows drawn together in fury, glaring hard at Zhou Shi. "You fool!"

The Seventh Girl's birth mother, Concubine Lu, was still there — and she feared that woman's love for her daughter might override her warning, leading her to confess everything to the First Branch...

Zhou Shi trembled for only a moment before steadying herself.

"Mother, this is such a large scandal... even if we wished to keep it hidden, it cannot stay hidden now. Better to let Sixth Girl fulfill her sisterly love."

She continued: "Though the Seventh Girl did not come from my womb, she is properly my daughter. I never had a daughter of my own, so naturally I care for her too."

What Zhou Shi was thinking was this: Sixth Young Miss of the Qin family had clearly made up her mind to wade into the Third Branch's mess, and the First Branch women had made no move to stop her. That meant the Seventh Girl still had some fortune on her side.

As long as she stopped trying to kill herself, the Third Branch wouldn't have to lose so much face.

Furthermore, if the Seventh Girl was lucky, perhaps the future wife of the Left Vice Minister of Personnel could help find her a good match — which would also benefit her two sons.

Qin Ruyin gave the ashen-faced Old Matriarch a cool sidelong glance.

People were, in the end, practical creatures.

Did the Old Matriarch really believe she could continue to rule the Qin household's inner court with an iron fist forever?

"Mother, your daughter-in-law, along with Second Sister-in-law, would also like to accompany Sixth Girl to see Seventh Sister."

First Sister-in-law Lin Xi and Second Sister-in-law Meng An exchanged a glance and spoke to Li Shi.

They shared the same thought: with them accompanying Sixth Girl, things would be handled better.

Li Shi said, "Since you two also care for Seventh Girl, go in quickly."

She would stay here to fend off the old woman and Tong Shi herself.

As for Third Sister-in-law — today Li Shi saw her clearly at last.

The disappointment and contempt were almost overwhelming.

She supposed she had been too naive. Her husband had warned her years ago that the Third Mistress had been "carefully selected" by that old hag, and it was best to keep interactions minimal.

She had thought that while the Third Son was nothing remarkable, he was more than a match for this Zhou Shi. As long as Zhou Shi had half a brain, she'd make a decent life with him, with the First Branch standing behind them to give the Third Branch some cover — so the old woman and the Second Branch wouldn't pay them too much attention.

Yet it turned out: this foolish woman saw her own household's daughter being driven to her death, and still wanted to latch onto the oppressor's feet.

If not for her Ruyin's determination to come and stand up for the girl, the Seventh Young Miss would eventually have been destroyed entirely.

Li Shi also understood perfectly why her youngest daughter had insisted on coming to stand up for Seventh Young Miss.

She was striking while the iron was hot — using the incident of the Seventh Girl being pushed to suicide to deal a hard blow to the old hag and the Second Branch.

She was a mother. Of course she worried that her youngest daughter was taking too great a risk, and feared it might damage her reputation.

But since her daughter had already acted, there was nothing to do as a mother but stand firmly behind her.

Even her two daughters-in-law knew to stand behind Sixth Girl — how much more so should she, who was the girl's mother?

Qin Ruyin and the two sisters-in-law entered Seventh Young Miss's inner chamber.

Seventh Young Miss's birth mother, the concubine, was holding her and weeping bitterly.

"Seventh Young Miss, no matter how hard things are, you're so young — you mustn't give up! If something happened to you, how would I and Eight Young Miss survive? Oh, oh, oh..."

Qin Ruyin looked over. Seventh Young Miss was conscious, lying there staring blankly at the canopy of the bed, a strip of white gauze wrapped around her neck to hide the marks from the cord.

"Seventh Sister, I and my eldest sister-in-law and second sister-in-law have come to visit you."

Qin Ruyin looked at the young girl — a flower just beginning to bloom — lying there without any spark of life, like a living corpse. She couldn't help but feel a pang in her heart.

She spoke, but it was as though Seventh Young Miss didn't hear her at all. She continued to stare blankly, eyes empty, at the canopy above.

It was Concubine Lu who, upon hearing Qin Ruyin's voice, whipped her head around in shock and surprise.

She let go of her daughter and came scrambling forward, throwing her arms around Qin Ruyin's and First Sister-in-law Lin Xi's legs.

"First Young Mistress, Second Young Mistress... Sixth Young Miss, save us! Please save us!"

"This concubine begs you to save Seventh Young Miss, I beg you... I beg you. From now on, I will eat vegetarian meals and pray every day to protect you all, to protect everyone in the First Branch..."

A mother's love for her child had ground her dignity into the dust.

"Concubine Lu, please stand up! You must not do this — you are half a senior to us, you absolutely cannot behave this way!" Lin Xi reached out to help her up.

Meng An also bent to support her. "Concubine Lu, as my eldest sister-in-law says, please don't do this! Seventh Young Miss and Eighth Young Miss still need you!"

Concubine Lu wept harder.

Even the First Branch's young mistresses could see it clearly — her poor Seventh Young Miss and Eighth Young Miss had no one to rely on but her alone.

Which meant everyone sitting outside, save for the First Branch's matriarch, saw her Seventh Young Miss as nothing.

Her Seventh Young Miss truly had no way to go on living.

If it came to that, then she, as her mother, would rather die than fail to demand justice for her Seventh Young Miss...

"What my sisters-in-law say is right — be strong for your children. Even if you are a concubine in this household, in this family, you and Third Master are perhaps the only ones who truly think of Seventh Young Miss and Eighth Young Miss."

"Come, sit and talk." Qin Ruyin said this, and Huangqi immediately stepped forward to support Concubine Lu onto the small cushioned stool by the table.

Qin Ruyin walked to Seventh Young Miss's bedside and reached out to take her pale, cold hands.

Lin Xi and Meng An followed closely behind.

"Seventh Sister, don't worry. I promise — no one will dare force you to be anyone's concubine again."

"Rest and recover. I'll send two maids to the Third Branch to look after you."

"And promise your sister — no more thoughts of ending your life."

"You are barely a teenager. Your road is long. In the future you will marry a decent and upright husband, have a few clever and adorable children, and live the life you want to live. Only then will your life have been worthwhile."

Concubine Lu surged forward. "Seventh Young Miss, quickly promise your Sixth Sister! Your Sixth Sister has a good heart, she truly wants to protect you! She will soon be the most honored young mistress in our family — she has the power to shield you!"

She was desperate for her daughter to agree. She didn't want to lose her; this time, her very heart had shattered.

Lin Xi and Meng An also persuaded her, urging Seventh Young Miss to cherish her life and never do such a foolish thing again.

At last, Seventh Young Miss burst into tears.

"Good, good — crying is good, crying is good!" Lin Xi wiped her own eyes with joy.

Qin Ruyin also breathed a sigh of relief.

Seventh Young Miss finally cried her fill, then grabbed Qin Ruyin's hand with both of hers, her face full of terror as she shook her head: "Sixth Sister... Sixth Sister, please don't get involved with the Second Branch on my behalf!"

"Second Aunt dared do this to me because she had Second Uncle's and the Grand Matriarch's permission. Our Third Branch cannot fight them..."

"I also don't want to drag you into this..."

So she had endured in silence all along, and when she went to pay respects in the First Branch, she had revealed nothing.

"I had already written a farewell letter to my mother, asking her to say after my death that I had died of illness... so it would not affect our sisters' marriage prospects."

"Sixth Young Miss, that day when Seventh Young Miss came to bring you the trousseau gift, this concubine begged her to seek you or the First Mistress for help, but this foolish child simply would not..." Concubine Lu wept, her face streaked with tears.

"This concubine knows she is being selfish, but she is a mother... she truly had no other way."

"Concubine Lu, no matter what you may say about me behind my back, I must tell you plainly: Seventh Young Miss being sent to the Anwang Mansion was approved by your household's mistress!"

The chamber door swung wide. Tong Shi walked in supporting the Old Matriarch.

Li Shi and Zhou Shi followed behind.

Hearing Tong Shi's words, Zhou Shi's face filled with embarrassment and discomfort.

"Moreover, since ancient times, a daughter's marriage has been the parents' command and the matchmaker's word. What gives a mere concubine the right to decide a young miss's marriage?"

Tong Shi looked down with contempt at Concubine Lu, who was kneeling before the Old Matriarch, and laughed coldly.

Then she turned her cold gaze to Qin Ruyin. "And Sixth Young Miss — Seventh Young Miss is a cousin from a different branch. For an unmarried young girl to really stir up trouble like this..."

"Aren't you afraid of how the Jiang family might talk about you?"

Qin Ruyin looked over with a calm, steady gaze.

Her cool eyes met Tong Shi's vicious stare in the air between them.

"Oh? So Second Aunt also knows the principle that a daughter's marriage is the parents' command? Then why did you not discuss it with Third Uncle? Instead, while Third Uncle was away, you tried to forcibly send someone's daughter off to be a concubine?"

Tong Shi's expression turned sinister.

She said coldly: "It depends on whose concubine one is being. Prince An is of royal blood, a Prince of the First Rank. To be his concubine — that is Seventh Girl's great blessing!"

Qin Ruyin asked mildly: "Oh? Then shall Second Aunt take this great blessing for herself? Would she want it?"

Tong Shi's outstretched finger trembled.

"Ruyin." Li Shi coughed once, hinting to her daughter not to go too far — it wouldn't be good for the young girl herself.

But Qin Ruyin only smiled. "Oh, I spoke too hastily. At Second Aunt's age, even Second Uncle, who is well past forty, likely finds her tiresome — how could she possibly become Prince An's concubine?"

"What Niece means to say is: since being Prince An's concubine is such a great blessing, why did Second Aunt not bestow this great blessing upon the daughter from her own womb — Fifth Sister?"

chapters.title: “Blessing”?
chapters.content:
Before Qin Ruyin could ask anything, Concubine Lu burst into a shrill accusation, “She’s a mother too, she has daughters of her own—how can she be so vicious?”

“Start from the beginning,” Qin Ruyin reminded her.

Through Concubine Lu’s grief-stricken account, Qin Ruyin, Lin Xi, and Meng An—the two sisters-in-law—finally learned the truth behind Qin Seventh Miss’s suicide attempt.

Tong Shi’s cruelty truly sent chills down one’s spine.

In order to pave the way for her precious eldest son, she had planned—while Third Master Qin was away—to send Qin Seventh Miss in a small sedan chair to the back gate of Prince An’s residence, forcing her to become the prince’s concubine without so much as a proper explanation.

Prince An was the late Emperor Wen’s youngest son. Emperor Wen was the previous emperor.

Before his death, the late emperor had grasped the current emperor’s hand, tears streaming down his face, and entrusted his most beloved young son to him.

The current emperor pitied the younger brother who was three years younger than his own crown prince.

For the sake of the empire’s stability—and also so the current emperor would not be burdened by his indulgence of the youngest son—the late emperor, before his passing, ordered his favored consort, Dowager Consort Yu, to die by a strip of white silk, explicitly stating he could not bear to part from her and had her buried with him.

Prince An thus grew up peacefully. The current emperor raised him like a son, granted him the title “Prince An,” hoping he would remain safe and healthy, and bestowed upon him the rank of a first-class prince.

Tong Shi’s eldest son, Qin Ronggong, had caught the attention of the similarly young Prince An in childhood, and the prince requested from the emperor that Qin Ronggong be made his study companion.

Second Master Qin, wishing to hedge his bets, readily agreed.

As for Qin Ronggong himself—he was charming, elegant, and handsome.

Prince An was fond of beauty and knew that the Qin family, whether sons or daughters, were exceptionally good-looking. He therefore dropped many hints in front of Qin Ronggong.

A legitimate daughter of the Qin family could not be a concubine.

So Qin Ronggong hinted to his mother, Tong Shi, that for the sake of his future, it would be best to choose a Qin family concubine-born daughter and send her in a small sedan chair to Prince An’s residence.

Qin Seventh Miss might have been timid and weak, but she would rather die than become a concubine—even to imperial royalty.

Before the Jiang family sent betrothal gifts for Qin Ruyin, Tong Shi had coerced and tempted Third Madam Zhou. Zhou Shi could not resist her, and with selfish thoughts of her own, she agreed.

Her reasoning was that by the time her husband returned from his trip, it would already be a fait accompli.

Tong Shi had promised that not only would her two sons benefit, but her husband would as well. Presumably, he would not blame her for making the decision on her own.

After all, she was only a concubine-born daughter.

Not someone she had given birth to herself—naturally she wasn’t valuable.

“No wonder… no wonder Seventh Sister sent my two girls and boy those finely crafted cloth tigers and tiger-head shoes yesterday…” Lin Xi reached out tenderly to touch Qin Seventh Miss, who still could not speak.

Meng An’s eyes reddened. “She sent some to my children too. You could tell from the stitching that Seventh Sister made them herself.”

Qin Ruyin also understood now why, that day, Seventh Sister had come to give her bridal gifts with red eyes. That bracelet had been the best thing she owned.

So she had already resolved to die back then.

“Sixth Miss, Seventh Miss had already planned to take her life. But the Jiang family had just sent betrothal gifts for you a few days ago, and she didn’t want to bring bad luck to you… She originally planned to wait until after you were married…”

Hatred flooded Concubine Lu’s eyes, her still-charming face twisting with fury.

“But that vicious woman Tong Shi sent people yesterday to pressure Seventh Miss, saying she would be sent to Prince An’s residence tomorrow…”

“Our Seventh Miss truly had no way out… sob… Please don’t blame her… sob…”

Concubine Lu cried bitterly, as if she wished she could tear Tong Shi apart alive.

But she couldn’t.

Her Seventh and Eighth daughters still depended on her protection.

How could Qin Ruyin blame that pitiful yet noble girl?

She immediately shook her head. “Seventh Sister’s character is pure. She is not wrong to refuse being a concubine. The fault lies with those who forced her. She had no other choice, which is why she took such extreme action. I only feel heartache for her—how could I blame her?”

Lin Xi suddenly turned to Qin Ruyin. “Yin-niang, we learned early because Second Sister-in-law and I sent return gifts to Seventh Sister.”

Qin Ruyin instructed Huangqi to comfort Concubine Lu.

She herself walked to Qin Seventh Miss’s bedside, sat down, and held her hands.

“Seventh Sister, don’t worry. I guarantee no one will dare force you to be a concubine again.”

“Focus on recovering. I’ll send two maids to Third House to take care of you.”

“And you must promise your sister—never think of ending your life again.”

“You’re only in your teens. Life is long ahead. In the future, you’ll marry a proper gentleman, have a few clever, lovely children, and live the life you want. Only then will your life be worthwhile.”

Concubine Lu threw herself forward. “Seventh Miss, Seventh Miss, quickly promise your Sixth Sister! Your Sixth Sister is kind—she truly cares for you! She will soon be the most honored young lady in our family. She can protect you!”

She anxiously urged her daughter to agree, unwilling to lose her—this time, her heart was breaking.

Lin Xi and Meng An also persuaded her, telling her to cherish her life and never do something foolish again.

At last, Qin Seventh Miss burst into tears.

“It’s good that she’s crying… it’s good…” Lin Xi wiped her own tears, relieved.

Qin Ruyin also let out a breath.

After crying her fill, Qin Seventh Miss suddenly grabbed Qin Ruyin’s hand, shaking her head in fear. “Sixth Sister… Sixth Sister, don’t offend the Second House for my sake!”

“Second Aunt dares to treat me like this because she has Second Uncle and Grand Madam’s approval. Our Third House can’t fight them… I don’t want to drag you in…”

That was why she had endured it all and, even when she visited the main house, revealed nothing.

“I already wrote my mother a farewell letter, telling her to say I died of illness… That way it wouldn’t affect the other sisters.”

“Sixth Miss, when Seventh Miss went to give you bridal gifts, I begged her to ask you or Madam Li for help—but this foolish child refused…” Concubine Lu sobbed uncontrollably.

“I know I’m selfish, but I’m her mother… I truly had no other way.”

“Concubine Lu, no matter how you slander me behind my back, I’ll make it clear—Seventh Miss entering Prince An’s residence was approved by your household’s mistress!”

The door swung open. Tong Shi entered, supporting Old Madam Qin.

Madam Li and Madam Zhou followed behind.

Hearing Tong Shi’s words, Zhou Shi looked embarrassed, her face extremely unpleasant.

“Besides, since ancient times, marriages are decided by parents and matchmakers. How can a mere concubine presume to decide a young lady’s marriage?”

Tong Shi looked down at Concubine Lu, who knelt to greet the old madam, and sneered repeatedly.

She then cast a cold glance at Qin Ruyin. “And Sixth Miss as well—Seventh Miss is only your cousin from another branch. You’re not even married yet—do you really intend to meddle?”

“Aren’t you afraid what the Jiang family might say when they hear of it?”

Qin Ruyin looked back calmly.

Her cool gaze met Tong Shi’s vicious one in midair.

“Oh? Second Aunt knows that marriages should be arranged by parents and matchmakers? Then why didn’t you consult Third Uncle? Instead, while he was away, you forcibly sent his daughter to become a concubine?”

Tong Shi’s expression darkened.

“It depends on whose concubine she becomes. Prince An is imperial royalty, a first-rank prince. Becoming his concubine is a great blessing for Seventh Miss!”

Qin Ruyin asked lightly, “Oh? Since it’s such a great blessing, Second Aunt—do you want it?”

Tong Shi trembled with anger, pointing at Qin Ruyin.

“Yin-niang.” Madam Li coughed, reminding Qin Ruyin not to go too far—it wouldn’t look good for a young girl.

But Qin Ruyin smiled. “Oh, I spoke too hastily. At your age, Second Aunt, even Second Uncle—who’s already well past forty—might find you undesirable. How could you become Prince An’s concubine?”

“My meaning is—since becoming Prince An’s concubine is such a great blessing, why not give this blessing to Fifth Sister, who came from your own womb?”

chapters.title: This Great Blessing — Do You Want It?
chapters.content:
Qin Ruyin had no intention of letting Tong Shi off. She continued: "Second Aunt is also a mother with a daughter of her own — how could she bring herself to harm someone else's child?"

Tong Shi clutched her chest, her eyes rolling back as she began to sway.

"Second Mistress!"

Her entourage of servants surged into chaos, some supporting her, some steadying her — and in the commotion, they nearly knocked the Old Matriarch off her feet.

Fortunately, the servants around the Old Matriarch were no slouches either. They swiftly steadied her before she fell.

The Old Matriarch, having had quite a scare, closed her eyes briefly. Her temper was still simmering, and seeing the mess around her, she bellowed in fury: "What is the meaning of this?!"

Tong Shi, seeing that her mother-in-law was not furious at Sixth Young Miss for her outrageous behavior, but was instead angry at her own servants for nearly knocking into her, felt layer upon layer of resentment creep through her chest.

She simply let herself go limp and pretended to faint.

Li Shi had already leapt nimbly to one side when Tong Shi's entourage descended into chaos.

Now, seeing Tong Shi put on the act of fainting, she jumped forward two steps, pressed her thumbs firmly onto the woman's philtrum, and twisted the flesh back and forth with practiced force.

All the while she cried out in alarm: "Oh dear! The Grand Matriarch has frightened Second Sister-in-law into a dead faint!"

Tong Shi clenched her teeth tight, the skin around her eyes reddening more and more.

Li Shi, watching the faint tremor of Tong Shi's eyelids, gave a cold inward snort.

Hmph! You vicious woman — still trying to fake a faint, so word spreads that my Ruyin frightened a maternal elder into unconsciousness?

Forget it!

Li Shi's cry also successfully fed the Old Matriarch's already blazing fury.

Glaring coldly at Tong Shi, she also perceived that the woman was performing.

Instantly furious, she snapped: "Second daughter-in-law, how old are you? How can you be so incapable of composing yourself?"

The philtrum area was burning with fiery pain, but Tong Shi endured it, determined to pin the crime of frightening a maternal elder on Qin Ruyin's head.

Yet after Li Shi's cry, the old woman's tone was nothing but cold, and even her words served to slap Tong Shi in the face...

She gritted her teeth.

With a sharp cry, Tong Shi snapped her eyes open — red-rimmed and glaring.

Then she shoved hard, intending to push Li Shi away.

Li Shi had long anticipated this move. When Tong Shi shoved, she stepped nimbly aside and deliberately moved toward the Old Matriarch's side.

Tong Shi missed Li Shi entirely, and instead smacked her head squarely into the Old Matriarch's knee.

The Old Matriarch's face went chalk white in an instant, her back teeth nearly grinding to dust from the pain.

Li Shi moved several steps further away, but kept crying out: "Oh dear, Second Sister-in-law, how did you bump into the Grand Matriarch again? You know perfectly well the Grand Matriarch's knees are bad..."

Tong Shi was terrified as well. Her eyes went red and she began to weep, insisting again and again that it had been unintentional.

The Third Mistress, Zhou Shi, had been crouching in the corner like a wilted quail, trying to stay out of sight.

But what unfolded before her eyes — she had never seen anything like it in her life.

She was so excited and inwardly thrilled it cost her tremendous effort to keep it in!

Qin Ruyin watched as Tong Shi — who had always prided herself on being a lady of distinguished birth — cast aside all dignity and resorted to crying, making a scene, and pretending to faint. She found the whole thing absurdly funny.

Tong Shi had always been proud of her name as a nobly born lady of quality. Yet these inner-court tricks of hers were no different from those of a common street shrew.

The expression on the Grand Matriarch's face had turned black as the bottom of a wok, and the fury in her heart burned ever hotter.

She genuinely did not understand how Tong Shi — always the most pleasing of her daughters-in-law — had become so incapable of keeping up appearances.

Seeing the disorder going on and on, Li Shi was finally forced to speak up with a weary sigh.

"Are you all dead? Lift the Grand Matriarch and Second Mistress back to their feet!"

"Grand Matriarch, are you all right? Shall we ask the household physician to come have a look?"

Li Shi suppressed her laughter and offered the concern with exaggerated sincerity.

"No need!" The Old Matriarch shot Li Shi a cold glance. "I am not dead yet!"

Qin Ruyin sighed softly as well.

"Why does the Grand Matriarch say such inauspicious things?"

Then she switched to a smiling expression.

"Speaking of which — the other day when I went out to try on some jewelry, I happened to run into the wife of the Right Vice Minister of Personnel. She recognized me, and we had tea together on the second floor for a while. Reading between the lines, it seems Second Uncle may be in line for a promotion of half a rank before year's end."

This was not a fabrication. It had actually occurred.

The official ranks and titles of the Great Ying Dynasty's Six Ministries were not entirely identical to those Qin Ruyin had learned of in her previous life.

In the Great Ying Dynasty, the Left Vice Minister was of second rank, while the Right Vice Minister was half a rank below — third rank.

The Old Matriarch's expression immediately cleared like the sky after rain, her knee suddenly painless, a gleam of pleasure lighting up her aged eyes.

Tong Shi stopped weeping as well, scrambling up from the floor in a flash. Smug triumph was nearly spilling from her eyes.

But then Qin Ruyin followed up, calm as still water: "If you were gone, Grand Matriarch, it would all be for nothing. Second Uncle would certainly have to observe three years of mourning. Just keeping his third-rank post would be an achievement — let alone a promotion."

The Old Matriarch nearly toppled forward, only now understanding why Tong Shi had almost fainted earlier.

She forced herself to stay steady, taking a labored deep breath...

The color of her face was truly something — like a recently unearthed ancient corpse.

And for some strange reason, Tong Shi found that watching the Old Matriarch get twisted up by Qin Ruyin exactly the same way, still struggling to hold on — she felt a secret, deep satisfaction.

Her eyes rolled, and Tong Shi immediately demanded of Qin Ruyin: "Sixth Girl, is that true? Did the Right Vice Minister's wife truly hint that your Second Uncle would be promoted this year?"

"Do I look like someone whose words Second Aunt would dare repeat?" Qin Ruyin smiled at her.

Tong Shi choked. She had been thinking that if Qin Ruyin's words proved inaccurate, she'd go find the Right Vice Minister's wife and have it out with her.

But who would be the most embarrassed by that? Her own husband...

"Nothing in life comes with a complete guarantee." Qin Ruyin said, unhurried.

"Moreover — at this critical moment when Second Uncle is hoping for a promotion, Second Aunt has gone and committed the scandal of forcing a niece into concubinage, nearly driving her niece to her death..."

"If word of that reaches the ears of those censors who will seize on anything with the faintest scent of blood, what fate would befall Second Uncle?"

Qin Ruyin looked away from Tong Shi's expression and turned to the Old Matriarch.

"Grand Matriarch, even if the matter of Seventh Sister being driven to take her life could be concealed from outsiders, it cannot be hidden from 'family.''

She pressed down heavily on those last three words.

"With Second Aunt having committed such a great wrong, she will inevitably become a grip for those whose politics are opposed to Second Uncle's. How do you see this, Grand Matriarch?" She dragged out the final syllable of her question.

The Old Matriarch's heart lurched.

She stared hard at Qin Ruyin.

Was this girl threatening her — demanding that she punish Tong Shi?

Her meaning was: if Tong Shi's punishment was not to her satisfaction, this girl would let "family" spread word of what Tong Shi had done?

She had guessed correctly.

That was precisely what Qin Ruyin intended.

This was a rare and perfect opportunity. She intended to rid the household of the poison that was Tong Shi.

As for the Old Matriarch — once she was married out, she'd have plenty of time... to play slowly.

"Grand Matriarch, there is one more thing Ruyin has not yet said." Li Shi spoke up slowly.

"A household like ours has a third son serving as a companion to Prince An. To then send a daughter into the Prince's household as a concubine as well — once that gets out, the marriage prospects of every unmarried girl in the Qin family will become very difficult to manage."

Qin Ruyin's eyes brightened.

Mother really was the perfect ally.

The Old Matriarch immediately grasped the full meaning of what Li Shi had said.

Both Sixth Girl and Fifth Girl were already promised. Sixth Girl in particular — the Jiang family had already made an extravagant betrothal gift. That marriage would not fall through.

The Qin family was ruled by the Second Branch. Even if some scandal emerged, it would be unlikely to easily affect the First Branch.

Next in line was the Fifth Girl.

Although the Fifth Girl had agreed to a match with Wang's Second Son of the Wang family, the Wang family had not yet formally gifted the betrothal presents to Fifth Girl.

If it spread that a Tong Shi had driven a niece toward concubinage, causing her to attempt suicide, the Fifth Girl — as Tong Shi's own biological daughter — would likely face the Wang family reneging on the engagement entirely. That family held their reputation more sacred than life itself.

And then there were the girls from all the other branches of the Qin family whose marriages would also be affected.

The Old Matriarch's fondness for Second Son was real, but Fourth, Fifth, and Sixth Sons were also her biological children.

They all had more than one legitimate daughter.

She could not possibly allow the actions of a daughter-in-law born to another family's name to ruin the marriage prospects of her own true-blooded granddaughters.

Tong Shi watched the Old Matriarch's gaze grow fiercer and fiercer, and felt a chill of fear.

"Mother, I..." She wanted to explain, but was silenced by the Old Matriarch's sharp reprimand.

"Silence!"

Gone was the kindly grande dame. The Old Matriarch's aged face was all sharp edges and fury.

"All of this is your doing!"

Tong Shi was speechless. She could not very well say that pushing the Third Branch's concubine-born daughter into concubinage had also been for the sake of the Old Matriarch's own beloved eldest grandson's advancement...

"Sixth Girl, you are right — your Second Aunt's mind was clouded by greed, and she made an error."

"In your view, how should this matter be resolved?"

Qin Ruyin said: "Since the Grand Matriarch asks for my humble opinion, I'll share a few thoughts."

"Go on."

The Old Matriarch waited to hear what punishment Qin Ruyin had in mind for Tong Shi.

"First, Seventh Sister's marriage must not be decided by anyone other than herself. Even when prospective matches are being considered, Seventh Sister must first see the young man in person before any decision is made."

"This—"

The Old Matriarch was naturally unwilling.

Granddaughters' marriages — legitimate or concubine-born — all had to be decided by her, the family matriarch!

"Seventh Sister is someone who has already died once. What does the Grand Matriarch think she has left to fear?"

"And let the Grand Matriarch not assume that a girl confined to the inner chambers cannot do anything. One should know: geese leave their traces, and the wind leaves its sound."

"Even if she truly died, there is still our First Branch. There is still Third Uncle."

Such utterly blatant threats!

The Old Matriarch gritted her teeth.

"Very well. As Sixth Girl wishes." Knowing when to yield was the mark of a wise person.

The Old Matriarch's aged eyes calculated. "Then tell me — how should your Second Aunt be dealt with?"

"Mother!" Tong Shi was stunned, then outraged.

She was a senior — how could the fate of a senior be decided by a junior? On what grounds?

Yet she did not know that in the Old Matriarch's heart, she — the most valued of her daughters-in-law — had already become a dead piece on the chessboard.

"Second Aunt is still my elder, after all. How she is to be dealt with is naturally for Second Uncle and the Grand Matriarch to decide." Qin Ruyin recognized the old woman's calculation and bounced the ball back.

But then she pivoted.

"As my mother just said, Prince An is not someone just anyone can court or dare to court."

Qin Ruyin stepped closer, looking down at Tong Shi, who had gone limp in her maidservant's arms.

"Second Aunt, you come from the distinguished Tong clan of Hexi — surely you understand the principle that each new emperor brings his own court?"

Qin Ruyin turned and performed a slight bow in the direction of the imperial palace.

"Our current Emperor has been governing independently for several years now, and under his reign all is peaceful and prosperous. What is his greatest concern? Naturally — instability in the court, and the people's unease."

Tong Shi's face went three shades paler than snow.

Her eyes were hollow with despair.

The Old Matriarch also understood exactly why Qin Ruyin had said this.

If the Censorate's old dogs came after the Qin family with a charge of colluding with Prince An...

She dared not think further!

Tong Shi understood now — Qin Ruyin had spoken these heart-stabbing words to deliver the final, killing blow.

"Grand Matriarch, it would be best to discuss with Second Uncle soon, and see whether the Qin family's reputation can weather Second Aunt's damage to it."

When Qin Ruyin finished speaking, the Old Matriarch rose abruptly to her feet.

"Enough! Everyone disperse! Today's matter ends here. No one is to speak of it again!"

The Old Matriarch had already made up her mind.

Her aged eyes sharpened, and she delivered pointed warnings to both Li Shi and the perpetually silent Zhou Shi.

"You two sisters-in-law must keep your own courtyard servants under control. If any of today's business leaks out, I will not show leniency."

She glared fiercely at Zhou Shi. "Third daughter-in-law especially — manage your own people!"

Qin Ruyin was displeased.

Even at this point, the old woman was still putting on the airs of a mother-in-law in front of her own mother.

She had a backer now — why should she still suffer in silence?

She shot back immediately: "Grand Matriarch, it's hardly fair to blame Third Aunt for not managing her people. After all, most of the servants in the Third Branch courtyard are either your people or Second Aunt's people — Third Aunt has no power to command them either way, does she?"

The Old Matriarch: "..."

What a pestilential creature.

She really was going to kill this old woman...

Qin Ruyin's thinking was simple: now that she had a backer, who wanted to keep up the pretense of civility?

Plain speaking and a decisive blow — now that was satisfying!

The Old Matriarch left with Tong Shi in tow, both of them looking thoroughly disgraced.

Zhou Shi watched their retreating figures until they disappeared, then turned to look at Qin Ruyin with undisguised awe — and a tinge of sourness.

"Our Sixth Young Miss truly was impressive today! Even the Grand Matriarch and the Second Mistress who runs this household were cowed by you."

Zhou Shi kept babbling on.

"It really is true that a good marriage means a straighter spine! Before this, Sixth Girl used to trail behind Fifth Girl like a meek little kitten..."

How insufferable this woman was.

Li Shi, Lin Xi, and Meng An all had darkened faces.

Even Concubine Lu looked at her with barely disguised contempt.

She endured — and then could endure no more.

"Mistress, today it was Sixth Young Miss and the First Mistress, and the First and Second Young Mistresses, who saved the Seventh Young Miss's life! They also restored the Third Branch's reputation. You shouldn't..."

"Slap your own mouth!" Zhou Shi erupted with fury, jabbing her handkerchief toward Concubine Lu. "You're a concubine — you dare climb over your mistress? Who gave you permission to speak here?"

Qin Ruyin said idly: "Is it not because Third Aunt has a head stuffed with straw and no ability, which is why she couldn't manage her own courtyard or save her own household's daughter?"

Zhou Shi froze.

When she came to herself, she realized Qin Ruyin had just called her a thick-headed fool...

"Goodness gracious... heavens, the Grand Matriarch and your Second Aunt have offended you — fine! But Third Aunt has done nothing to you, Sixth Girl — why do you speak to me like this?"

Besides, Seventh Young Miss hadn't even come from her womb.

The fact that she hadn't made things worse was already generous of her.

Qin Ruyin couldn't be bothered to trade barbs with this fool.

She listened as her own mother gave a cold laugh: "Third Sister-in-law is truly ungrateful — even the Buddha would lose patience with someone like you."

The implication was clear: you were rightfully insulted. Keep pushing, and I'll insult you too.

Zhou Shi: "..."

Li Shi's eyes were too terrifying. She didn't dare say another word, afraid this mother-daughter pair would say something even more humiliating.

Li Shi then looked at the lifeless Seventh Young Miss and let out a long sigh.

"Since Third Sister-in-law cares nothing for Seventh Girl's life or death, and pays no attention to Eighth Girl either, why not let Seventh Girl and Eighth Girl be adopted into our First Branch?"

Li Shi was not saying this to deliberately humiliate Zhou Shi.

It was something her husband had mentioned years ago, saying both Seventh Girl and Eighth Girl were pitiful.

But at the time, the First Branch was itself in a precarious position. Sticking their neck out then would only have brought more trouble on themselves.

So they had politely declined.

But now — someone had nearly died. She truly could not bear to stand by and do nothing.

"Elder Sister-in-law! What do you mean?"

Li Shi ignored her, and spoke only to Concubine Lu: "The matter of adoption has nothing to do with you. When Third Master returns to the household, you explain things clearly to him. For now, I will take Seventh Girl and Eighth Girl back with me to our First Branch."

Concubine Lu was so overcome with gratitude she could not find words. She fell to her knees before Li Shi with a thud.

"You need not worry," Li Shi told her. "While the children are with me, they will not be mistreated."

Concubine Lu was perceptive. She understood that Li Shi was shielding her — keeping her from speaking, lest Zhou Shi find trouble with her.

If Li Shi had said as much, Zhou Shi would have to think carefully before going after Concubine Lu.

Because if Seventh Young Miss and Eighth Young Miss were truly adopted into the First Branch, Concubine Lu would no longer be someone Zhou Shi could touch.

...

At the Jiang mansion.

Jiang Jiuxiao had come home from court earlier than usual today. He went straight to his study and began reading the official reports.

"My lord, your servant has news from the Qin family to report..."

At the words "Qin family," Jiang Jiuxiao's gaze lifted from the page to Li Yi's face.

It was not Li Yi who had been attending him today — Li Yi had been given the day off.

That he had come back even on his rest day to report something from the Qin family meant something significant had happened.

"Speak." Jiang Jiuxiao returned his gaze to the reports.

"My lord, today the Seventh Young Miss of the Qin family had an incident. Sixth Young Miss flew into a rage... on behalf of the Seventh Young Miss, Sixth Young Miss had it out with the Grand Matriarch and the Second Mistress of the Qin family..."

"My lord, as you know, Sixth Young Miss has an upright nature and a delicate constitution..."

Sensing his master's displeased gaze, Li Yi's body stiffened, and he immediately quickened his pace.

"The Grand Matriarch's and Second Mistress's methods... how could Sixth Young Miss have handled them? One doesn't know how wronged she must feel right now!"

Jiang Jiuxiao's handsome face turned cold as frost. Without warning, he rose to his feet.

"Prepare the carriage. We're going to the Qin family."

That old scoundrel Qin Mingwei.

He apparently thought Jiang Jiuxiao was someone easily manipulated.

To actually allow his household's women to bully the person Jiang Jiuxiao had chosen!

chapters.title: Ruyin's Great Victory; Lord Jiang Stands Up for His Own
chapters.content:
"My lord, there is one more thing — I'm not sure I should say..." Li Yi hesitated.

"Speak."

Jiang Jiuxiao extended both arms to either side, unhurried and dignified.

His young book-boy was helping him put on his cloak.

Li Yi steeled himself and said: "I'm told that the Second Mistress of the Second Branch was trying to force a niece into concubinage... she intended to have the Third Branch's concubine-born young miss sent in a small sedan to the back gate of Prince An's mansion."

"Prince An's mansion?" Jiang Jiuxiao's handsome brows drew together.

Li Yi nodded immediately. "My lord, I didn't dare bring it to you before — I was afraid your servants below had heard incorrectly."

"The First Son of the Qin Second Branch served as a companion to Prince An from a young age and has maintained ties with the Prince's mansion ever since. By rights, there was no need to send yet another concubine-born daughter to Prince An's household..."

That would be far too conspicuous.

Jiang Jiuxiao said mildly: "Even you can see that much."

Li Yi, who understood his master better than most, knew the master disdained to pass judgment on others easily. But Li Yi could read him.

His master must find the Second Master Qin to be a reckless fool.

And foolishness was another word for stupidity.

His master had the deepest contempt for stupid people.

Heh! It looked like someone was about to have very bad luck.

"Li Yi — send someone with nimble hands and feet. Have a note written and tossed through the window into the study of Chief Censor Fan..."

Li Yi's whole body went rigid.

His master was calling on Chief Censor Fan, the head of the entire Censorate!

So it wasn't that Second Master Qin was just about to have bad luck — he was about to have catastrophically bad luck!

He thought as much — his master was the most protective of his own people. How could he stand by and watch Sixth Young Miss suffer such humiliation?

...

Second Master Qin Mingjie had barely stepped through the front gate when he was intercepted by one of the Old Matriarch's people, waiting by the door, who promptly escorted him to Shouhe Hall.

The Old Matriarch, seething with both grievance and fury, relayed to Qin Mingjie everything that had happened in the Third Branch's courtyard that day.

Throughout the account, Qin Mingjie drank cup after cup of tea.

The refined composure on his face gave way, more and more, to a twisted expression.

By the time the Old Matriarch finished with the last few words, Qin Mingjie nearly hurled his teacup at the wall.

He stopped himself only by remembering he was in his mother's chambers — he didn't want to frighten her.

"That idiot Tong Shi! She's getting more and more muddleheaded — a true disappointment!"

He had long found that yellowed-faced wife of his intolerable.

He had tolerated her before because she was the children's mother, and because she had managed the household without any major blunders.

But who could have known she was this foolish — unable to handle even something small! Couldn't keep one concubine-born Third Branch girl under control, and let that girl cause such a huge scene.

Bad enough in itself — but then to be outmaneuvered by that little chit from the First Branch, that Sixth Young Miss, and make both him and his mother lose face completely.

"Don't just be angry," said the Old Matriarch. "Tong Shi may be lacking, but she is still your principal wife and the children's mother. You must find a way to outwardly maintain her position — it is the least you can do to avoid wounding your sons' and daughter's feelings."

The Old Matriarch had already decided what to do with Tong Shi in her heart.

But she would never say so outright to her son.

If she did, sooner or later it would be resented by the grandchildren, and the Qin household would one day pass into the grandchildren's hands. She still depended on their incense offerings after her death.

The Old Matriarch massaged her brow wearily and continued: "Besides, Tong Shi did all this for our beloved eldest grandson. Who could have expected things to turn out like this?"

"Hmph! That Third Branch concubine-born girl is also unable to see what's good for her. Given her birth, being made Prince An's concubine was already a heaven-sent fortune for her!"

Hearing her son say this too, the Old Matriarch suddenly shivered.

In her mind, she recalled what that little chit from the First Branch — arrogant on borrowed power — had said to Tong Shi.

That great blessing — shall Second Aunt take it for herself? Would she want it?

"Don't speak like that anymore. Today Tong Shi said the same thing, and that Sixth Young Miss gave her an enormous dressing-down for it."

The Old Matriarch said, teeth aching.

Qin Mingjie grew angrier. "I intend to find Eldest Brother and have a proper talk with him about how he raises his children. Does that chit Sixth Young Miss have any filial respect for her elders?"

At this point he also regretted agreeing to the match swap.

If there had been no swap, the First Branch would forever have had to keep its head down and bow before his Second Branch.

None of this — a meek little rabbit turned fox using borrowed prestige to wreck the Second Branch's plans — would have happened.

But it was too late to say so now.

"Fine, fine — Sixth Young Miss... I suppose we all misjudged her."

"But some things she said... after the fact, when I thought about them, she wasn't entirely without reason."

The Old Matriarch's brow remained heavy.

"Take this business of Seventh Girl's attempted suicide... I also hadn't anticipated that fool Third daughter-in-law would lose her head completely, and that concubine Lu would find a way to let word leak out. We must prepare a response."

"That's easy enough — anyone who dares spread the news, find them, beat them to within an inch of their lives, and sell the whole family off to the salt mines in some frozen wasteland!" Qin Mingjie's gaze was vicious.

"Servants can be dealt with, but that chit Sixth Young Miss threatened me: if Seventh Girl doesn't get a satisfactory resolution, she won't let the matter rest."

"Even if Eldest Brother himself had said it, we'd have ways to suppress him. But it had to be that girl Sixth Young Miss..."

"Now that the Jiang family holds her in such esteem, we can't lay a finger on her — not only that, but we must treat her with the utmost care."

The Old Matriarch pressed a hand to her chest. "Just thinking about it... my heart feels like it's being seared in oil."

"That girl even said: 'A new emperor brings his own court.' Hinting obliquely that our current sovereign will certainly not tolerate his ministers cultivating ties with Prince An."

"Then she mentioned that our family already has Eldest Son Rongzong walking Prince An's path. To then send a concubine-born daughter as well... you can imagine the rest yourself. I dared not think further."

Qin Mingjie's brow darkened. "What could a girl kept deep in the inner chambers know? She speaks recklessly about court affairs — truly outrageous!"

"Ah — one cannot say that!" The Old Matriarch shook her head in disagreement.

"Fifth Girl and I often mocked her, saying that girl loves reading unofficial histories and miscellaneous records... yet I find myself thinking — the more one reads, the more wisdom one gleans."

This son of hers — apart from his own mother, he never listened to what any woman said.

"Mother!" Doubted by his own mother, Qin Mingjie was displeased. "You must not let yourself be intimidated by the Jiang family. Sooner or later I will find an opportunity to properly teach that bold and shameless chit a lesson!"

The Old Matriarch saw that her usually deep-scheming son was being particularly... and sighed.

"Forget Sixth Young Miss — let's first discuss what to do about Tong Shi."

At the mention of this, Qin Mingjie calmed and resumed his refined, measured air.

He considered for a moment, then said: "Though I resent her, Mother, as you just said — she is still my principal wife. She gave me several sons and a daughter. A light punishment and stern warning should suffice. Surely we cannot truly act out of spite and deal with her harshly?"

The Old Matriarch looked at this son of hers with something close to disappointment.

Eldest Son had his legitimate heir status and the powerful Wei clan of Xianyang as his mother's family. Second Son could let that slide.

But Third Son — all he had was his birth to a concubine. Surely Second Son could crush him.

"Don't threaten the dog or the dog will bite!" the Old Matriarch said pointedly. She knew that quiet dogs were often the most dangerous.

"If Third Son wants to snarl, I'll pull out every tooth in his mouth and break both his legs!" Qin Mingjie said savagely.

"Fine, fine — have it your way!" The Old Matriarch pressed her temple, ill-tempered.

Mother and son were nearly at the point of parting on bad terms when Qin Mingjie's personal attendant rushed in.

"Second Master! Left Vice Minister of Personnel, Lord Jiang, has sent in a calling card — and he is already at our front gate..."

Both mother and son were startled.

"Why has Jiang Sijiu come to our Qin family at this hour?" The Old Matriarch looked at her son in confusion.

Qin Mingjie rose, straightened his collar, and allowed himself a measure of self-satisfaction.

"He must have an important matter to discuss with me. Perhaps it is about the marriage with Sixth Girl, or perhaps it concerns the good news Sixth Girl heard from the Right Vice Minister's wife."

The Old Matriarch was somewhat uneasy.

Qin Mingjie reassured her: "Put your mind at ease, Mother. If it concerns the marriage — look how Jiang Sijiu, Left Vice Minister, still comes to find me, his Second Uncle, to consult on matters? Even if it concerns my likely promotion before year's end, it only proves Jiang Sijiu values me — not my Elder Brother and his family."

The Old Matriarch was reassured.

There was nothing else it could be.

"Receive him properly. Although you are about to become his maternal clan's Second Uncle, the man outranks you. Especially since he holds high position in the Ministry of Personnel — all the officials under heaven answer to them..."

Qin Mingjie cut her off impatiently. "These small matters, I know. Mother, rest easy. I'll go greet Lord Jiang."

He made his way from the inner courtyard to the front gate at an unhurried pace, as if strolling through his own garden.

What did it matter that Jiang Sijiu outranked him by two grades?

He was the younger — Qin Mingjie was the elder!

Once he married Sixth Young Miss, wouldn't he bow and call him Second Uncle with clasped hands?

He had no need for Jiang Sijiu to show him any special deference.

All the men of the court were seasoned political operators.

Everyone would know that Jiang Sijiu had married a Qin family woman, making him part of the Qin family. And anyone who wanted to ingratiate themselves with Jiang Sijiu but had no other access would inevitably come looking for him — and that would be the Qin family's time to rise.

"Lord Jiang gracing our humble home — we are filled with light and glory! Your servant failed to come out to meet you sooner and begs your forgiveness!"

When Qin Mingjie laid eyes on that tall, elegant figure — dressed in black, standing like a radiant sun above the clouds — he felt another pang of regret for his daughter Qin Wuyue.

But he quickly steadied himself.

He stepped forward with his most affable manner.

"Master Qin." Jiang Jiuxiao, who had been waiting inside the carriage for a quarter of an hour, was composed and unhurried, his bearing as effortless as ever.

He showed not the slightest impatience at Qin Mingjie's late emergence.

"I wonder what brings Lord Jiang to visit at this hour?"

Jiang Jiuxiao glanced at him mildly. "Master Qin, let us speak in the study."

"Oh... yes, of course. Please, Lord Jiang, this way." Though dissatisfied with Jiang Jiuxiao's cool manner, Qin Mingjie — having spent over two decades navigating the court — showed nothing on the outside. Not a flicker.

"Please take tea, Lord Jiang." Qin Mingjie gestured welcomingly and asked with a smile, "Has Lord Jiang come today regarding the marriage with our Sixth Girl?"

"Master Qin jokes. If it were about the marriage, I would naturally go to my father-in-law." The words landed on Qin Mingjie like a slap to the face, causing him to clench his fingers together.

He thought of what he had just said to his mother... and here was Jiang Sijiu slapping him in the face so fast.

Qin Mingjie was silent for a long moment.

He was stewing with irritation, deliberately leaving Jiang Jiuxiao to wait.

Jiang Jiuxiao held his teacup with a calm and placid expression.

Seeing that the man's self-composure was quite remarkable, Qin Mingjie could only give in.

"Then... is Lord Jiang here on official business?"

He chuckled and shook his head. "That shouldn't be so either — Lord Jiang, you are a senior official in Personnel. I work in Public Works. Even if there were something, surely it wouldn't require your personal presence."

Only then did Jiang Jiuxiao set down the teacup — the lid of which he had in fact never opened, so he had not drunk a single sip.

He raised his head and looked at Qin Mingjie. "I should not have come to the Qin family today. But for the sake of Sixth Young Miss, I had no choice."

Qin Mingjie inwardly sneered.

So it really was for Sixth Young Miss.

How sour.

He had always known Jiang Sijiu had changed his ways and developed an interest in women. He shouldn't have gone along with Wuyue's whim and agreed to the swap.

After all, it was Wuyue who lacked foresight. Even to benefit the Qin family, what was the harm in marrying into the Jiang family and enduring a little hardship?

Composing himself, Qin Mingjie's smile grew more generous.

"Then please, Lord Jiang — speak plainly. Your servant will take the liberty, as one who may soon be called your Second Uncle, to say: whatever Lord Jiang's request, Second Uncle will certainly..."

"Master Qin again jokes." Jiang Jiuxiao's lips barely moved, and Li Yi — who knew his master — recognized this as the prelude to impatience and disdain.

"This visit is not about personal matters. It concerns the entire Qin family."

Jiang Jiuxiao's pace quickened slightly.

Qin Mingjie's expression finally shifted.

Coming for the entire Qin family?

That was presumptuous!

"This official also cannot afford to linger long in the Qin household. I have only two things to say."

"Please speak!" Qin Mingjie cupped his hands, all pretense of the condescending elder gone.

"First — a close acquaintance of mine has informed me that someone has submitted a memorial accusing Master Qin of abusing his position for personal gain..."

Qin Mingjie's heart lurched. He immediately rose and clasped his hands toward the direction of the palace, his face a mask of righteous indignation:

"May the Emperor judge this! May Lord Jiang also serve as witness! This official has been clean and upright both in public and in private, without the slightest..."

The Ministry of Personnel had supervisory authority over all officials. That Jiang Jiuxiao had come with such a purpose — small wonder Qin Mingjie was frightened.

Jiang Jiuxiao waved a hand to cut him off. "As the marriage date with Sixth Young Miss draws near, I do not wish to alarm her at this time. So I had someone set that memorial aside for now."

Qin Mingjie's heart eased.

His whole body went slack with relief. "Many thanks, Lord Jiang..."

If that memorial had truly landed on the Emperor's desk, his hopes for a year-end promotion could be buried...

"Don't thank me yet, Master Qin. The second matter is this: I am afraid that memorial may not be able to stay suppressed."

Qin Mingjie reeled as though struck by lightning.

"This... why is that?"

Jiang Jiuxiao gave a soft sigh and looked at him. "This official left court early today and caught wind of something: word is that your household intends to send a concubine-born young miss to Prince An's mansion as a concubine."

Qin Mingjie shot to his feet in horror. "There is absolutely no such thing..."

"I hope indeed there is not." Jiang Jiuxiao rose elegantly. Qin Mingjie assumed he was about to take his leave and inwardly scrambled, eager to sweep up the loose ends of the Seventh Young Miss's suicide attempt. He was desperate for the man to go.

His lips, though, offered a hollow invitation: "Lord Jiang, won't you have another cup of tea..."

Jiang Jiuxiao shot him a sidelong glance. "No."

Then, with a languid air, he added: "Even if the Qin family had nothing to do with it, Master Qin would do well to be on guard."

"While my carriage waited at your front gate, a colleague from my ministry suddenly sent someone to inform me that a person has written an anonymous letter to Chief Censor Fan, exposing this very matter."

Jiang Jiuxiao finished speaking and took his leave.

Qin Mingjie tottered, grabbing the edge of his desk to stay upright, his face drained of color. "Lord Jiang, please stay!"

chapters.title: Second Uncle Outwits Himself; Lord Jiang Pays a Visit to Deliver a Blow
chapters.content:
"My household's womenfolk did make some mistakes, but surely it need not go as far as reaching Chief Censor Fan!"

Qin Mingjie bit down hard and performed a deep bow, hands clasped. "I beg Lord Jiang to mediate on behalf of the union between our two families. I, Mingjie, will hold this in my heart!"

Chief Censor Fan was the most inflexible and impartial man in the Censorate. If the matter truly reached him, he would send a swarm of equally inflexible subordinates after Qin Mingjie and never let go.

Although Qin Mingjie had many back channels he could work in private, none of them could be used at this moment.

Personal favors wore thin the more they were spent.

But Jiang Jiuxiao was different. He was about to become the Qin family's son-in-law. Surely, for the sake of his wife's family, he would intervene.

He thought so, at least, until this point.

Jiang Jiuxiao, however, was unmoved.

What was there to say to a man whose mouth was full of lies?

Just as he was stepping over the threshold of Qin Mingjie's study with Li Yi at his side, a woman dressed in a servant's clothes and with a servant's hairstyle suddenly lunged forward and knelt before Jiang Jiuxiao.

Li Yi had already extended his foot when he saw the woman kneeling on the ground, and immediately pulled it back.

"My lord..." He turned to look at his master.

This Qin family was truly extraordinary. It kept opening his eyes.

"Who are you, and what business do you have blocking my way?" Jiang Jiuxiao's gaze held a flicker of interest.

"Lord Jiang — this concubine is Concubine Lu of the Third Branch of the Qin family. I have come to bring charges against Second Master Qin Mingjie! The women in the inner court follow whatever the men of the house decree. His wife daring to force the Seventh Young Miss into concubinage must be at his direction..."

Before the woman could finish, Qin Mingjie cut her off with a furious shout.

"Are you all dead?! How did this madwoman get in here and startle our honored guest?"

He immediately caught his book-boy's eye. "Get people over here at once to drag this lunatic away!"

"Stop." Jiang Jiuxiao spoke. He looked down at the woman in servant's clothes kneeling before him.

"You say you are a concubine of the Third Branch?"

"In answer to Lord Jiang — this concubine is Concubine Lu of the Third Branch, and also the birth mother of the Seventh Young Miss who attempted to take her own life..."

Jiang Jiuxiao nodded, then asked: "You said just now that you wish to bring charges against the Second Branch Mistress?"

"Lord Jiang, this is a matter of the inner court — nothing to do with this official. If Lord Jiang would be good enough to spare this step..." Qin Mingjie said immediately.

By the laws of the Great Ying Dynasty, a complaint brought in person before an official of Jiang Jiuxiao's rank — Left Vice Minister of Personnel — must by law and procedure be investigated on the spot, regardless of the complainant's station, and regardless of time and place.

This investigation would also be recorded in the official case archives.

In matters involving inner-court impropriety, most officials would turn a blind eye — as long as it wasn't a life-or-death affair, they would generally give face to both sides and allow it to be settled privately, without recording it.

This was why Qin Mingjie asked Jiang Jiuxiao to skip the formality.

He still believed, at this point, that Jiang Jiuxiao — as the Qin family's prospective son-in-law, eager to preserve his wife's family's reputation — would surely comply.

What he did not know was that Jiang Jiuxiao had come on this very errand. Even without this Concubine Lu and her desperate mother's love, he would not have left without acting.

...

First Branch, Qin household.

Master Qin of the First Branch had returned from court at dusk and gone straight to his wife's chambers.

After hearing his wife recount everything that had happened in the household that day, he immediately furrowed his brow and called for Qin Ruyin.

Aside from supper and a brief rest, Qin Ruyin had been listening to a loving "reprimand" from her father ever since.

A servant woman outside came in to report something, and only then was she temporarily released.

After hearing the servant's account, everyone fell silent for a long moment.

Jiang Jiuxiao had arrived at the Qin household after nightfall.

He had spoken with Qin Mingjie in the study for a while. As he was about to leave, Concubine Lu — disguised in servant's clothes — suddenly rushed forward and knelt before him to bring charges against Qin Mingjie.

Concubine Lu returned safely to the Third Branch.

And Tong Shi had come down with a sudden illness, sent by Qin Mingjie in the middle of the night to the suburban estate to recuperate.

"At such a critical juncture, Tong Shi gets sent to the countryside... and for 'recuperation'?"

Li Shi clapped a hand over her mouth, eyes wide as saucers.

But First Master Qin was looking only at Qin Ruyin.

"Ruyin, do you understand why your Second Aunt was sent to the countryside in the middle of the night to 'recuperate,' right after Lord Jiang's visit?"

Qin Ruyin lowered her head like a meek little quail.

"Father, your daughter was wrong. Your daughter will never act so rashly again."

"You say this now, but in your heart you still feel you did nothing wrong?" First Master Qin looked at that small look on her face and nearly burst out laughing despite himself.

"A young girl throwing her weight around in the inner court can at most intimidate the women within. But she cannot intimidate someone like your Second Uncle, who has spent over twenty years navigating the court." He instructed her.

"If not for Lord Jiang coming himself today, and being seized upon by Concubine Lu to tear away the Second Branch's fig leaf — your Second Uncle would not only refuse to back down or apologize, he would have come to find your father to lay charges against you."

"Caring for your sisters is admirable, and paying debts of grievance in kind is sometimes not a bad thing — but you cannot underestimate your opponent." First Master Qin sighed.

"You thought you were trading on the Jiang family's prestige and could force your Second Uncle to submit. But little did you know your Second Uncle has backing that even the Jiang family cannot afford to antagonize."

"If you had truly enraged him — made him think you were not only useless to him but his enemy — you would have been in real danger!"

"Enough, enough — you've been scolding the child long enough. Look how wilted she's become," Li Shi said reproachfully, shooting First Master Qin a look.

"And see how well things turned out! A true tiger appeared, and that Tong Shi has been sent off to the countryside, hasn't she?"

Li Shi was genuinely overjoyed.

The venomous sister-in-law who had oppressed her for over twenty years had suddenly been packed off to the countryside — even if she'd eventually be brought back, it was enough to make her rejoice.

If not for the lateness of the hour, she'd have happily asked someone to warm a jug of fine wine for her and her husband to get thoroughly drunk together.

"You also mustn't always indulge the child. The Jiang household's inner court is no peaceful place either..."

"Yes, yes! Our lord is wisest!" Li Shi humored him, then caught Qin Ruyin's eye. "You must remember your father's teaching and never act rashly again."

"Go ahead and apologize to your father!"

Qin Ruyin obediently did so.

Her father's words made good sense. She had genuinely recognized that today she had been a little rash.

If only she had a bit more audacity — she could have first sent someone to inform Jiang Jiuxiao of the matter.

As it turned out, she hadn't done that... and Lord Jiang had come to her rescue anyway.

In that sense, she and Lord Jiang really did have a kind of predestined connection.

"Wait — Father, something doesn't add up!"

Qin Ruyin suddenly realized something.

First Master Qin looked at her.

"Even if Seventh Sister's attempt on her life may have leaked, the reason behind it shouldn't have been known outside. So why did Lord Jiang come to the Qin family today?"

First Master Qin let out a breath. "When one reaches Lord Jiang's position, much that others do not know finds its way to them anyway. Don't overthink it — just focus on preparing for your wedding."

"You need only understand this: if not for Lord Jiang's personal visit today, the Second Branch would never have given ground."

Qin Ruyin nodded obediently.

Her father was right not to press further, and she asked no more.

It seemed that lying low and staying out of things was truly not an easy matter.

Father had kept his head down for years, quietly grooming her two brothers while the First Branch crouched and bided their time. Even with the Jiang family as backing, they were still no match for the Qin Second Branch.

Trading on Jiang Jiuxiao's prestige to project power had its uses, but the method could not be overused.

She had originally planned to seek out Uncle Ning after she was married. Now it seemed she should do so sooner.

She needed to secure more leverage.

First Master Qin, seeing those eyes of hers rolling busily, knew the girl still hadn't taken his advice to heart. He sighed softly.

"Ruyin, your father will speak plainly: why the Jiang family holds you in such regard — even your father has not yet fully pieced it together."

"But the unknown is always most dangerous. Relying on the Jiang family to do things that benefit yourself is one thing — but you cannot use the Jiang family to shield your own natal home."

"Lord Jiang rose to such heights at so young an age through his own abilities. He is not someone like your Second Uncle, who schemes and scrambles for every small advantage. Such a man — especially such a man — will not like being used. And especially not being used by his own betrothed bride..."

Qin Ruyin understood that her father was worried, and was teaching her the wisdom of surviving as a wife in a husband's household.

But she did not entirely agree.

"Father, your daughter truly and willingly wants to marry him. Even if he has no genuine feeling for me, he should still protect me — my face is his face! And I haven't ruined his reputation or done anything in his name..."

"...It was just that I made use of the fact that others were afraid to provoke him, and so dared not provoke me. If he can't even understand that much, then what would be the point of marrying him?"

First Master Qin stared at her.

"Father." Qin Ruyin moved closer and took her father's cool hands in both of hers. "Don't worry. Your daughter believes Lord Jiang will understand. And your daughter also knows he is a very good man."

"This child..." First Master Qin shook his head in helplessness, but much of his worry genuinely lifted. The most important thing in marriage was for husband and wife to be of one heart.

Even Li Shi said with a smile: "Look — your youngest daughter really does have a connection with that Lord Jiang. The very first time I told her about him, she said the match was not a bad one. And she's never even met him once, yet she's already saying he's a very good man."

First Master Qin looked down at the small hands holding his large ones and thought: youngest daughters were the sweetest — even at this age, she still willingly reached out to her parents.

His heart swelled with tenderness for his youngest.

He drew his hands free and gave the back of her hands a gentle pat. "Good child. If you have a clear sense of things, that is the best possible outcome."

Qin Ruyin said sweetly: "I learned it from Father and Mother, didn't I? Father and Mother have spent half a lifetime just like this — supporting each other and trusting each other."
