First Master Qin and Li Shi glanced at each other, and both smiled.

Their eyes filled with deep, quiet affection.

Just then, a servant outside the screen called in: "Lord Jiang has been escorted by the Second Master to come and see the First Master."

"At this hour?" Li Shi was surprised. She looked at First Master Qin. "I assumed Lord Jiang had already returned to his own household."

He told her: "Then I'll go receive Lord Jiang. Wife, have someone escort Ruyin back to her own courtyard. For the next few days, make sure she has several quick-witted servants accompanying her wherever she goes."

Li Shi told him not to worry, and urged him out to entertain their future son-in-law.

First Master Qin personally went to escort Jiang Jiuxiao to his study. Qin Mingjie, face ill-humored, still tried to follow.

Jiang Jiuxiao turned around and said mildly: "Master Qin has many household matters to attend to, and must be weary after such a long day. Please, there is no need to accompany me further."

Qin Mingjie's face flashed with startled disbelief, quickly giving way to awkwardness.

First Master Qin inwardly laughed, but betrayed nothing.

"Thank you, Second Brother, for personally escorting Lord Jiang here. You have an early court session tomorrow — you really should go rest."

Qin Mingjie bid farewell with great reluctance.

"The night is cool and damp, Lord Jiang — please have some warm water." This was the first time the two — father-in-law and son-in-law — had been in each other's presence at such close range.

After all, this prospective son-in-law outranked him by far. First Master Qin was rather stiff with formality.

He explained: "By rights I should warm some wine to accompany Lord Jiang — but I thought that since Lord Jiang must attend morning court tomorrow, drinking might interfere with matters."

Jiang Jiuxiao bowed in a deep, formal salute. "Jiuxiao thanks my father-in-law for his concern."

First Master Qin was so flustered that he nearly slid off the edge of his chair. He waved both hands emphatically. "Please, please don't — Lord Jiang, rise quickly, rise quickly!"

He thought for a moment, then hurried forward to personally lift the man up, fussing about until he had pressed Jiang Jiuxiao into the senior guest seat.

He stole several glances at his prospective son-in-law from close range. First Master Qin was very pleased indeed.

A widower this future son-in-law may be, but his talent, bearing, and looks were truly beyond reproach.

His manners were excellent too!

They weren't even married yet, and technically "father-in-law" was premature — but Jiang Jiuxiao had chosen to use the title anyway. That was an honor bestowed.

First Master Qin could not hold back his delight; it showed plainly on his face.

"Please be seated as well, father-in-law. Jiuxiao came today having heard that Sixth Young Miss was wronged by the Second Branch of the Qin family..."

First Master Qin's heart stirred.

Lord Jiang had come for Ruyin indeed.

He knew Jiang Jiuxiao had his own channels of information — he wouldn't dare ask about them.

Jiang Jiuxiao laid out everything he had said to Qin Mingjie in full for First Master Qin.

First Master Qin was quietly moved — the more he looked at this prospective son-in-law, the more he liked him.

He thanked Jiang Jiuxiao on his daughter's behalf, then, as if in passing, sighed: "I won't hide it from you, Jiuxiao — Ruyin is still young after all. She acted with nothing but goodwill in her heart, and still ended up trading on your prestige for some borrowed power..."

Jiang Jiuxiao's eyes flickered.

This father-in-law of his was quite a character — most people would never say such a thing, let alone admit it so readily.

"Father-in-law speaks too gravely. Jiuxiao rather admires Sixth Young Miss's chivalrous spirit. And to be able to support Sixth Young Miss — that is something Jiuxiao does willingly."

The smile in First Master Qin's eyes nearly brimmed over.

But his tone turned helpless, punctuated with a few rueful chuckles.

"Ah, Jiuxiao, you don't know — when I returned home today, I called that girl over and gave her a thorough dressing-down..."

"Who could have known she's goodhearted and also a bit of a handful. She insisted she genuinely wants to marry you and already believes you are a good man, so she is sure you won't blame her for trading on your prestige..."

"Tell me — what kind of thing is that to say?"

With that, he watched Jiang Jiuxiao's face closely.

What he did not see, beneath the wide sleeves of his cloak, were Jiang Jiuxiao's hands clenched tight — and in those refined eyes, the warm glow of stars.

"I ask that you and my mother-in-law not be too hard on Sixth Young Miss. Sixth Young Miss is quite right, and Jiuxiao will absolutely not blame her." Jiang Jiuxiao said warmly.

chapters.title: Father- and Son-in-Law Meet; Lord Jiang Is Quietly Pleased
chapters.content:
First Master Qin put on a show of gratitude and remorse, bowing once more.

But the corners of his mouth kept curving upward.

Jiang Jiuxiao gently asked him not to stand on ceremony. It was getting late, and he should be returning to the Jiang mansion.

First Master Qin personally accompanied him out to the front gate and watched him board the carriage.

Once Jiang Jiuxiao was inside, he lifted the curtain and said warmly to First Master Qin — who stood straight as a pine tree beneath the brilliant red lanterns — "The night is cold and the wind harsh. Please, Father-in-law, go inside to rest."

...

Li Shi was in her chamber, pacing in circles around the table.

Just as her wait reached its most anxious, her senior maidservant reported that the Master had returned.

Li Shi hurried to meet him at the doorway, accepting his overcoat from his hands, and immediately asked: "My lord, why did Lord Jiang come?"

First Master Qin's smile could not be contained. He looked at Li Shi. "I think our Ruyin's fortune is a great one."

"How so?" Li Shi pulled him down to sit.

...

In Shouhe Hall.

Though it was deep into the night, Shouhe Hall blazed with candlelight.

The legitimate young masters and young ladies of the Second Branch had all been summoned to the Old Matriarch's chamber.

The atmosphere was quiet and heavy.

The Old Matriarch turned her aged, sunken eyes over the grandchildren of the Second Branch and spoke in a voice hoarse with weariness.

"Your mother's actions — Grandmother has explained everything to you clearly... Your father had no choice, and sent your mother to the countryside in the dead of night. Do not blame your father."

"This is already the most fitting way to protect your mother. If it were not done so, there might be catastrophe for all of us in the Qin family. The children must be kept safe first — then other matters can be spoken of."

"Your mother was also willing. No one forced her. She is a good mother — she did all this for you, and for your father and the Qin family. She is a fine wife to the Qin family as well..."

The Old Matriarch spoke beautifully. None of the Second Branch grandchildren would have cause to resent her.

"Grandmother, it must have been Sixth Young Miss who invited that Jiang Sijiu here — she was deliberately seeking revenge on Mother!" Second Branch youngest legitimate son Qin Rongshuo's eyes were red as he bellowed furiously.

"Nonsense!" Qin Mingjie's face darkened. He frowned and shot a sharp look at his youngest legitimate son.

"Lord Jiang arrived this quickly today — it is plainly because he has his own information channels. I have already looked into the First Branch. None of them have the means to inform Lord Jiang in so short a time."

If he didn't refute it, it would mean that even a young girl from the First Branch could ride over his head, harnessing the Jiang family's power to suppress him, the family patriarch.

Of course, if Jiang Jiuxiao had truly come on personal grounds, that was something Qin Mingjie could still navigate. It was clearly someone in the court who had begun paying attention to the Qin family.

He still believed that Jiang Jiuxiao had come to warn him.

Even if he felt resentment toward Jiang Jiuxiao, for now he needed the man to protect the Qin family's standing, and his own reputation in the court.

Qin Rongshuo, already reeling from the thunderbolt of his mother being sent to the countryside, and now scolded by his father on top of it, couldn't hold back the tears.

He was only fifteen, and had been so thoroughly indulged by Tong Shi that he could not withstand setbacks.

The Old Matriarch gestured for him to come over. Qin Rongshuo tried not to cry as he made his way to her side.

"Good boy, don't worry. Your mother has gone to the countryside to lie low for a while. Once the storm passes, your father will personally bring your mother back. You just study hard with your tutor and wait for your mother's return..."

"Father, Grandmother — when can we bring Mother back?" It was Fifth Young Miss Qin Ruyue who spoke. Her complexion was terrible, as though she had fallen gravely ill.

The Wang family had not yet sent the betrothal gifts for her. And now, of all times, her mother had gotten into trouble.

And moreover — this sending of her mother to the suburban estate in the dead of night had never happened in her previous life.

In her previous life, her mother had been fine. It had been Third Aunt Zhou Shi who was resented by Third Uncle for the rest of their lives, the two of them forever estranged.

In her previous life, her mother was unscathed — but Third Aunt Zhou Shi suffered. The couple became completely coldly distant.

The Old Matriarch knew what she was thinking, but had little she could say.

Her original plan had been to negotiate with the First and Third Branches — then, once Tong Shi finished managing both the Fifth and Sixth Girls' weddings, send her off to the suburban estate on the pretext of exhaustion from managing two large events in succession.

As for how long she would stay there — that was negotiable. As long as she could keep the First and Third Branches from making trouble, letting Tong Shi stay in the countryside for three to five years — and bring her back when Rongshuo was of marriageable age — would also work.

"Fifth Girl, don't worry. Even with your mother away, Grandmother is still here. Grandmother will oversee your marriage arrangements. Put your mind at ease." The Old Matriarch truly did love this granddaughter of hers.

Qin Ruyue noticed how the eldest daughter-in-law, Sun Shi, glanced at her with an unreadable look, and quickly said: "Grandmother, I am not worried about myself — I just want Mother to come back soon. Mother is of distinguished birth and must suffer terribly in a crude countryside estate..."

Qin Mingjie cut her off coldly: "That is the consequence of your mother's carelessness. She stirred up a mess and nearly dragged the whole family down with her!"

"I may as well tell you all: if this business with your mother isn't resolved satisfactorily, even my current post may be difficult to hold onto!"

All the Second Branch children were stunned.

None of them had anticipated the matter was this serious!

The Second Branch's second legitimate son, Qin Rongcheng, was ordinary in both looks and talent — the most overlooked of all the children at home. He had long harbored jealousy and resentment toward the parents' and grandmother's favoritism of Eldest Son Qin Rongzong.

Hearing this, he immediately sneered at Qin Rongzong: "Wasn't all of this done for your sake? Yet when Mother was forced off to the countryside estate, you didn't say a single word for her! If Father's official position is dragged down by this, do you feel no shame?"

Qin Rongzong's face went pale. He shot his second brother a vicious glare, then lowered his head and said nothing.

He knew that right now, the more he said, the worse it would be.

Was he not wronged?

He was greatly wronged!

When he had conveyed Prince An's intentions to his parents, they had both been wholeheartedly in favor.

Who could have known that wretched Third Branch Seventh Young Miss would be so ungrateful, and make such a scene by trying to kill herself?

And then some people in the court — who had apparently been watching the Qin family — found out about it...

Qin Rongzong also did not believe the First Branch had the ability to inform Jiang Sijiu about this so quickly.

He thought: had he known things would get this thorny, that wretched girl might as well have hanged herself when she had the chance.

Then they could have gotten rid of her birth mother too.

If both of them were dead, there would be no witnesses, and his grandmother, father, and mother would have had ways to cover it all up.

"Enough, Rongcheng — the more difficult the moment, the more you must stand united, brothers. Stop blaming each other. It's late — all of you go rest. The sky won't fall; life must go on."

The Old Matriarch dismissed the grandchildren and kept only Qin Mingjie behind to speak.

"Old Second, you sent Tong Shi off to the countryside so hastily — aren't you afraid your sons and daughter will resent you?" The Old Matriarch shook her head in disapproval.

"You had them all here before you, yet you couldn't say two soft words to comfort their hearts. Why?"

Qin Mingjie said nothing in return.

Tong Shi's situation... he had been eager to make a deal with Jiang Jiuxiao and had no time to spare thought for anything else.

As for speaking gentle words to a roomful of sons and daughters? Who was the father and who were the children here?

The Old Matriarch knew his rigid, self-righteous nature could not be changed. She could only sigh.

She then asked: "If he only punished Tong Shi... did Jiang Jiuxiao actually agree to help you?"

"He made no promises. Only said that as long as our Second Branch was truly clean, there would be no trouble."

The Old Matriarch grew immediately anxious. "So he still didn't agree!"

"Mother, there's no need to worry about that. Men in his position don't make light promises. I know what I'm doing. And I've already sent people out to sweep up the loose ends."

"If you have it all in hand, so much the better." The Old Matriarch had dominated the inner court for decades, but the twists and turns of the officialdom were not something she fully grasped.

After a moment's thought she said: "So, as you see it — was Jiang Sijiu actually coming to warn you, treating the Qin family as in-laws?"

Qin Mingjie gave a cold snort. "Where would he find such goodwill? He simply still sees some use in the Qin family. And furthermore..."

He glanced at the Old Matriarch. "In this son's view, he is still not over the business of the match swap. He'll not swallow that lightly... He even stated outright that Sixth Young Miss's wedding affairs are to be handled by Li Shi."

In the Qin family, whenever a young miss married or a young man took a wife, it had always been Tong Shi, as the household mistress, who oversaw matters. This included the already-wed children of the First Branch.

The Old Matriarch's expression darkened further.

"With Tong Shi gone, I, the Old Matriarch, will personally oversee Sixth Girl's wedding! Li Shi, with her common origins and incomplete etiquette... how could she possibly manage such a large affair? We'd end up with a spectacle that shames the whole Qin family!"

"I also stated this tactfully to Jiang Sijiu, but that young man said he didn't want Sixth Girl's wedding — her own birth mother can't even have a say." Qin Mingjie's expression was no better than the Old Matriarch's.

The Old Matriarch slapped her thigh in exasperation. "What a curse! If only I had known... well, forget it. In any case, Fifth Girl's dream visions have always proven accurate. Her dream said the Wang family will one day be even more prominent than the Jiang family."

"And marriage alliances are ultimately just exchanges of advantage. Old Second, in private, invite that Jiang Sijiu out for drinks more often, get to know him properly..."

"I know." Qin Mingjie nodded, his gaze turning calculating and cold. "As long as he isn't truly doing all of this for that fool Sixth Young Miss who can only see what's right in front of her, that's fine by me."

"The Qin family isn't so short of girls that we have to depend on just that one Sixth Young Miss. Fourth, Fifth, and Sixth Brothers' households have plenty of legitimate daughters who haven't come of age yet!"

He still thought he could easily sacrifice or exploit them whenever it suited him.

"Old Second, Old Fourth, Old Fifth, Old Sixth's daughters are your genuine nieces — and they're all still children!" The Old Matriarch frowned. "Don't go ruining them!"

"Mother, rest easy — I only spoke offhandedly. As long as Sixth Young Miss, after marrying into the Jiang family, puts her natal home first, I have no reason to move against her." Yet the cold menace in his eyes did not fade one whit.

The Old Matriarch had also reached the peak of her resentment toward Qin Ruyin, and so offered no further counsel.

...

Qin Ruyue walked back to her Qiwu Courtyard with a heavy heart, replaying in her mind along the way everything that had occurred between her and Qin Ruyin before they were both married off in her previous life.

Alas — even though her previous life had been miserable and without dignity, she had lived to a good old age.

Having lived two lives, the events of her previous life were now too distant.

She could only recall the things that had left the deepest impressions. Other memories were hazy fragments without context.

"Young Miss, have some water first to warm yourself. Don't worry about the Second Mistress — the Second Master won't let her suffer in the countryside. Your servant believes the Second Mistress will be back in the household very soon..." Senior maid Red Sleeve attended to her as she sat down.

Qin Ruyue lowered her head, eyes fixed on the small table on the daybed, shaking her head slightly.

"It isn't as simple as you think."

The other senior maid, Xixin, said with a pout at the side: "Not to speak out of turn, but your servant thinks it's all that Concubine Lu's fault. What sort of a person is Seventh Young Miss? For Prince An to take notice of her is her fortune. Otherwise, at her station, she wouldn't even qualify as a common room girl for Prince An, let alone his concubine..."

"Prince An... Seventh Sister..."

A sudden flash of memory struck Qin Ruyue's mind.

She recalled something!

In her previous life, after she was married into the Jiang family, on the day of the return visit to her natal home — Jiang Jiuxiao had not accompanied her back, and she had been utterly humiliated by that.

Going to pay respects to her grandmother, she had rushed so fast that the little maids outside her grandmother's chamber had not had time to announce her, and she arrived before the announcement.

She overheard her grandmother and her mother saying: "Who could have known — we sent the wrong one. Who could have guessed Prince An's interest lay in the First Branch girl?"

The First Branch girl!

Could it be that in her previous life, the girl Prince An had truly wanted was not Seventh Sister, but First Branch's Sixth Young Miss?

At that point in time, the only First Branch girl not yet married was Sixth Young Miss.

Unfortunately, in that previous life she had been in such a hurry to pour out her grievances to her grandmother and mother that she had asked no follow-up questions.

And her grandmother and mother had apparently not wished her to know of such a shameful affair — they had never spoken of it in front of her.

Speaking of Prince An — she could not help but think of his fate in her previous life.

Four years later, the current Emperor fell gravely ill and passed. Before his death, he left an edict: despite the Crown Prince's lame leg, he was still appointed to inherit the throne, becoming the Huiwen Emperor of the Great Ying Dynasty.

Regrettably, after Huiwen Emperor ascended, the leg wound recurred again and again. Across all four seasons, the days he personally presided over court did not even add up to a single season.

In the first two years of the Huiwen Emperor's reign, he heavily favored Jiang Sijiu and those allied with Jiang Sijiu. Under their governance, the empire was reasonably well-managed.

Yet the crippled leg tormented him day and night, leaving him barely human. It was later that Prince An personally sailed overseas and, according to the stories, sought out a miraculous plant from distant islands, brought it back to the Great Ying Dynasty, cultivated it, and when its fruits ripened, compounded them into a medicinal pill that relieved the Huiwen Emperor's pain by half.

From then on, the Huiwen Emperor trusted only Prince An. He subsequently issued an edict naming Prince An as Regent Prince. Prince An served as Regent for over thirty years.

He was, for all practical purposes, the kingmaker without a crown for more than three decades.

He was still serving as Regent when she passed away.

That Seventh Young Miss had been unlucky too.

After entering Prince An's household as a concubine, within three months she had already lost favor.

But her three months of favor had brought great benefits to the Qin family. Prince An thereafter continued to look after the Qin family.

In her previous life, under Prince An's patronage, the Qin family leapt from a second-tier noble clan to a first-tier noble clan.

That was why she had not interfered in Seventh Young Miss being sent to Prince An's household.

Her father had been right, as had her eldest brother. That move had been correct.

She had just never expected that in this life, after she engineered the match swap with Sixth Young Miss, Sixth Young Miss would suddenly become so bold and headstrong, and would actually dare to meddle in the Third Branch's business!

And worse still — as a consequence of her actions, her mother Tong Shi had been packed off to the countryside estate.

Thinking about this, Qin Ruyue clenched the handkerchief in her fist, full of regret.

If only she had known earlier...

If she had known from the beginning that it was Sixth Young Miss Prince An truly had his eye on, she should never have had Sixth Young Miss swapped to the Jiang family.

Having lived two lives, she had more than enough means to scheme Sixth Young Miss into Prince An's household as a concubine!

She bit her lip lightly, and suddenly rose to her feet. "Bring my overcoat. I'm going to the West Courtyard to find Sixth Young Miss!"

"Bring my washing things and my jewelry case. Tonight we are staying in Sixth Young Miss's rooms."

Xixin and Red Sleeve were so startled they couldn't find their words.

Qin Ruyue's wet nurse, Zheng Mama, was also taken aback. She couldn't help but counsel: "Young Miss, you and Sixth Young Miss have never been close. And it is so late..."

"Do as I say!" Qin Ruyue's voice was sharp and high.

The sound of it, so loud and fierce, shocked both the senior maids and Zheng Mama.

...

As the Second Branch's household mistress in the Qin family — though each courtyard locked its gates at night — Qin Ruyue, as a legitimate young miss of the Second Branch, had the freedom to come and go as she pleased.

That was a privilege reserved solely for the Second Branch's direct household members.

Many things had happened that day, and Qin Ruyin was exhausted. She had returned to her own rooms, freshened up, and lay down on the bed.

She was nearly asleep when someone called out to wake her, saying Fifth Young Miss had come.

Qin Ruyin thought she must be dreaming.

"Sixth Sister, I am sorry to disturb your rest. Fifth Sister owes you an apology."

At the sound of Qin Ruyue's voice, Qin Ruyin's eyes flew wide open.

She nearly made a sound she'd regret.

chapters.title: A Glimpse at the Secrets of a Past Life; A Late-Night Visit
chapters.content:
"What brings you here?!" Qin Ruyin's voice nearly broke.

How utterly irritating this woman was!

Barging in at the dead of night to disturb her sleep.

"Sixth Sister doesn't welcome me?" Qin Ruyue's eyes reddened. "Actually, I wanted to come find you yesterday — but then today's incident with Seventh Sister happened."

The great beauty looked aggrieved and pitiable, drawing out every ounce of sympathy.

"Fifth Sister — you and the Second Branch have never been close to our First Branch. And besides, coming to stand over my bed in the dead of night — don't you think that's frightening?"

"Whatever it is, can't it wait until morning?"

Qin Ruyin was not in the least bit gracious.

In Qin Ruyue's eyes, the girl's expressive little face — with its animated, alluring beauty — stirred an unwilling appreciation.

She took pride in her own looks, but having lived through much in her past life, she understood clearly: the kind of unself-conscious, alluring charm Sixth Young Miss possessed was the most lethal poison to men.

No wonder Prince An had taken notice of this girl.

She just didn't know when it was that she had first caught his eye.

"Sixth Sister, whether you believe it or not, I have always thought of you as my own sister. Of all our household's sisters, you and I were the closest before our betrothals."

Qin Ruyue reached out and took Qin Ruyin's hands in hers. "Sixth Sister, today's business with Seventh Sister was a misunderstanding from the start. I also feel deep remorse toward Seventh Sister..."

Qin Ruyin drew her own hands back without expression, frowning in silence.

This woman was laying so much groundwork — what devious plan was she hatching?

Watching her own hands left empty, Qin Ruyue sighed.

"Ah, Sixth Sister must be angry at my mother and for that reason treats me this way. But you and I are both just young girls — even if I felt for you, what could I do?"

Qin Ruyue had come already steeled in her mind and resolved to humble herself.

As long as she could make this wretched Sixth Young Miss trust her again, she would endure.

Face didn't matter.

The substance of what she wanted — she would stop at nothing to have it.

Qin Ruyin said nothing, her expression radiating cold disdain.

Qin Ruyue's eyes dimmed. An aura of aggrieved vulnerability wrapped around her.

Qin Ruyin wasn't moved.

Purple Perilla's face also darkened. She didn't even bother bringing out a cushion stool for Qin Ruyue.

She was the one on night duty tonight.

But their young miss didn't like having the night-duty maid inside the room. Whoever was on night duty slept in the small side room outside the young miss's chamber.

The young miss also needed only one person on night duty. Tonight Fifth Young Miss had barged directly into their young miss's bedroom, catching her completely off guard.

Qin Ruyue's wet nurse Zheng Mama felt her own heart ignite with anger seeing her young miss treated so.

She stepped out and remarked with pointed sarcasm: "Sixth Young Miss is about to become the Left Vice Minister of Personnel's wife. But from what this old servant observes, Sixth Young Miss's chamber servants have no manners whatsoever. Sixth Young Miss ought to properly train..."

Qin Ruyin shot her a cold sidelong glance. Zheng Mama's heart lurched, and her words died in her throat.

Purple Perilla leapt back with a retort: "Zheng Mama is just a slightly privileged old maidservant. Since when did an old slave earn the right to sit in judgment on our Sixth Young Miss?"

Zheng Mama nearly turned over with indignation.

Qin Ruyue shot her a sharp look and scolded: "Mama, don't be rude to Sixth Sister!"

With even their own young miss scolding her, Zheng Mama dared not speak further.

Even Zheng Mama was being reprimanded by their own young miss — Xixin and Red Sleeve's simmering frustration also dared not surface.

"So Fifth Sister's own servants don't have particularly good manners either." Qin Ruyin inwardly praised Purple Perilla as a good girl, and gave Qin Ruyue a cool look.

"If there is something, Fifth Sister, say it. If Fifth Sister came to call me to account, I will keep her company to the end."

Qin Ruyue immediately shook her head. "Sixth Sister, how can you say such a thing? We are sisters — we must not let a misunderstanding divide our hearts."

"And I also heard — today in the Third Branch, my mother wronged Sixth Sister. I apologize to Sixth Sister on my mother's behalf."

"There's no need — Second Aunt should most rightfully apologize to Seventh Sister." Qin Ruyin was merciless.

Gritting her teeth, Qin Ruyue endured. Then her expression fell, and she said with subdued sorrow: "You're right. My mother did wrong. Father has already punished her. She has been sent to the countryside estate..."

Qin Ruyin hadn't expected she would be so capable of humbling herself. She couldn't help glancing at her.

Qin Ruyue was someone with a strong sense of purpose. She definitely had something she wanted from her.

She would not fall for the sisterly-love act.

"Sixth Sister, whether or not you believe it — earlier, when I heard whispers that Prince An had taken an interest in Seventh Sister, I did try to dissuade my mother..."

"But as you know — when the adults have made up their minds, what can young women like us in the inner court do to interfere?"

Qin Ruyue was standing while Qin Ruyin sat on the bed listening, which made Qin Ruyue look rather like a maidservant reporting.

She was trying to draw everyone else out of the blame, leaving Tong Shi — who had already been punished — as the sole person to bear it.

"Is this all Fifth Sister came to tell me? If so, please return and rest — I'd also like to sleep." Qin Ruyin said blankly and coldly.

A Second Branch mother and daughter truly bonded? Nothing more than this.

"No." Qin Ruyue suddenly took on a very grave expression.

Qin Ruyin looked at her.

This woman wanted to share a secret with her?

"My dreams are very accurate. For instance, my maternal cousin's wife finally gave birth to a legitimate son the day before yesterday."

"My eldest maternal aunt's daughter Yunhuan first began talks of a match with the legitimate second son of Dongbo Marquis Household. I dreamed she was carried in a bridal sedan to the Linyang Beihou Household — and sure enough, Yunhuan ended up formally betrothed to the heir of Linyang Beihou..."

Qin Ruyin listened to Qin Ruyue rattling off examples, and her gaze suddenly grew complicated.

Could this woman be someone reborn?

Or someone who had transmigrated into a novel?

Transmigration seemed less likely — this woman's thinking didn't resemble that of a modern person.

Rebirth, then, was most probable.

If Qin Ruyue truly was someone reborn, she would need to watch her every move closely.

This woman had engineered the match swap. She must have known the Jiang match was unfavorable, and the Wang match was good — very good.

Otherwise, no girl of good family who valued her reputation more than her life would have dared to secretly exchange letters with the Wang family's Second Son before the engagement, hiding it from everyone, just to engineer a situation where — once exposed — both families would have no choice but to go ahead with the match.

She could not tip her hand now.

She would watch and wait.

Qin Ruyue spoke until her mouth was dry. She fixed her gaze on Qin Ruyin, and nearly spat blood.

What sort of look was this wretched girl giving her?

Did she think she was a madwoman?

"Sixth Sister, if you don't believe me, you can ask around. All the dreams I've had have come true, and I always told my mother and grandmother in advance..."

Qin Ruyin cut her off: "Fifth Sister dreamed about me?"

Qin Ruyue nodded, then turned and waved at the servants still lingering nearby. "All of you step back."

Then she bent close and whispered a few words into Qin Ruyin's ear.

"Cough, cough..." Qin Ruyin erupted into a sharp, hurried cough.

Qin Ruyue's lips curved, and a meaningful smile flickered in her eyes.

"Sixth Sister need not be too frightened. Once you are married into the Jiang household and become the Left Vice Minister's wife, Prince An cannot do anything to you."

"You only need to avoid places where he is likely to appear. Then nothing will happen."

Qin Ruyin fell silent for a moment, not saying whether she believed it or not.

Qin Ruyue was thinking: she had told her grandmother and mother about her talent, and they had both believed her. How could this fool Sixth Young Miss not believe her?

Qin Ruyin lifted her head and looked at her. "Fifth Sister, if your dreams are truly accurate, then I have a question: why did Second Uncle and Second Aunt originally mistake Prince An's interest as being aimed at Seventh Sister?"

Qin Ruyue looked at those slanted phoenix eyes and felt her heart stir.

"Seventh Sister's eyes look very much like yours. And her figure is similar too."

Both were slender and delicate — graceful as willow branches swaying in the wind.

Qin Ruyue's figure was more rounded — the kind that the capital's noblewomen most favored in a daughter-in-law.

"Before, I often went out with Sixth Sister. It was probably during one of those outings that someone noticed her. Later, for some reason... there was a misunderstanding." Qin Ruyue said quietly.

A cold, sharp light swept across Qin Ruyin's eyes.

Qin Rongzong — using his own cousin's happiness and life as stepping stones for his own advancement. Truly, he had no shame.

"Sixth Sister, I am not trying to frighten you. In my dream, that person became the Regent of our Great Ying Dynasty a few years hence... A person he wants — our Qin family would have difficulty refusing him."

"I know Sixth Sister is not someone who chases high positions and abandons low ones... let's plan further from there..." Qin Ruyue looked like the kindest, most understanding elder sister in the world.

Then her tone shifted. "But as things stand — Seventh Sister chose death to prove her resolve, and the First Matriarch has stepped in to put out word that the First Branch intends to adopt Seventh Sister and Eighth Sister. What do you think should be done?"

Qin Ruyin looked at her coldly. "What does Fifth Sister think should be done?"

Qin Ruyue bit her lip softly and asked: "How is Seventh Sister now?"

Qin Ruyin looked at her with open mockery. "Seventh Sister's heart has gone cold. No one can reach her. Is Fifth Sister still thinking of persuading her to agree — to go ahead and become a concubine, to bring some benefit back to the Qin family first?"

Struck in her very thoughts, Qin Ruyue's face went white.

Then she shook her head quickly. "Sixth Sister has misunderstood. I only wanted to visit her tomorrow."

The idea of using Sixth Young Miss to persuade Seventh Young Miss to enter Prince An's household was clearly not going to work.

"Seventh Sister may not wish to see you." Qin Ruyin was not about to take her words at face value.

Never did Qin Ruyue expect that she had already humbled herself so thoroughly, and this wretched girl still showed her nothing but a face full of hostility.

If this wretched girl had been shown this sort of earth-shattering secret before their betrothals, she'd have been falling over herself with gratitude, throwing herself at Qin Ruyue's feet!

"Let's not talk about Seventh Sister for now." Qin Ruyue took a deep breath, then turned and beckoned to Red Sleeve, who was holding the jewelry case.

"Sixth Sister — I've brought my jewelry case. Whatever you like, take your pick. Anything you want is yours."

She gestured for Red Sleeve to hold out the case before Qin Ruyin.

"Think of it as Fifth Sister's trousseau gift to you!"

Qin Ruyin had no desire for any of her things.

If she truly wanted to give a trousseau gift, she should have prepared it properly in advance and found a proper day to bring it over.

Now she was holding out a random jewelry case and telling her to pick for herself — what was the meaning of that?

"Many thanks for Fifth Sister's kind intention. I've received so many nice things lately — Sister should keep these to wear herself."

Qin Ruyue's expression soured.

"Sixth Sister, you won't accept my trousseau gift?"

"We'll probably both be married around the same time. Let's call it even on trousseau gifts." Qin Ruyin deliberately jabbed at the sore spot.

Her own wedding date had long been set — the Jiang family had already chosen an auspicious day and formally presented the betrothal gifts.

The Wang family, however — despite the match being settled — had delayed sending the formal betrothal gifts for Fifth Young Miss, and no one knew why.

Qin Ruyue, sharp-witted as she was, could see perfectly well that Qin Ruyin was being deliberately pointed.

She swallowed it down and nodded with an ill expression. "Then we'll do as Sixth Sister says."

"It's just that the night is already deep — it's not convenient to disturb anyone at this hour. Sixth Sister, could you take me in and let me spend the night here?"

"No!" Qin Ruyin refused without a second thought.

She replied with a chilly edge: "Fifth Sister's Qiwu Courtyard is so large and fine — why come to my small, cramped, freezing-in-winter-roasting-in-summer courtyard to squeeze in?"

Qin Ruyue was left with no graceful way out.

Purple Perilla curtsied perfunctorily toward Qin Ruyue.

"Fifth Young Miss, with your kind permission — our young miss has never liked others sleeping beside her. Even when we maids are on night duty, we don't sleep on her footstool, but in the small side room outside."

Once Purple Perilla said that, it thoroughly killed any thought Qin Ruyue had of forcing her way to stay.

She — a legitimate young miss of the Second Branch — didn't even qualify to sleep on someone's footstool?

If that got out, she'd have no more face to show anyone.

"Fifth Sister, please go back and rest — I'm truly tired." Qin Ruyin urged, her lips curving slightly.

Qin Ruyue gritted her teeth hard, and only after a moment forced out the words: "I hope Sixth Sister won't let what I told you tonight spread to others..."

"Don't worry. I'm not like you."

Qin Ruyue left with her dignity in tatters.

She endured the shame and returned to Qiwu Courtyard.

Zheng Mama's heart ached for her. She fumed: "Sixth Young Miss is getting above herself. She'll have no good end!"

Qin Ruyue gave a cold, quiet snort.

"Young Miss, you truly didn't need to go tonight..."

"Mama hasn't seen the look in my brothers' and little brother's eyes when I left Grandmother's room."

"They all assumed I only cared about my own marriage and had abandoned Mother's fate. I must put their suspicions to rest."

After one married out, the greatest support was having a capable natal family.

In her previous life, her eldest brother's standing with Prince An had brought enormous gains. It was under his hands that the Qin family rose from a second-tier to a first-tier clan.

The other brothers she cared nothing for.

Her eldest brother — she must win him over.

"Young Miss, what you did to dispel the young masters' suspicions is right. But there was no need to humble yourself so to Sixth Young Miss."

"Sixth Young Miss right now is just a blunt cannon with no head on her shoulders. She doesn't even put the Grand Matriarch in her eyes anymore and says whatever comes to mind... Why suffer that from her?"

"Mama, there are things you don't understand. Getting close to Sixth Young Miss has great use in the future."

"Alright, attend to my rest. I'm truly exhausted today." Qin Ruyue rubbed her aching brow. "By the way — don't forget to spread the word that I went to Sixth Young Miss's chambers last night."

"Young Miss, rest easy!"

The very next morning, the Old Matriarch heard a report from those close to her that Qin Ruyue had gone to Qin Ruyin the previous night.

She had brought washing things and a jewelry case, but was turned away by Qin Ruyin in the end.

"What was Fifth Girl trying to do?" The Old Matriarch's expression soured upon hearing the full account from the servant.

When she heard the servant's explanation, she smiled again.

"Good girl — she can bend and she can stretch. And she knows to protect her father and Rongzong, trying to distance them from that business."

The Old Matriarch shook her head. "But she's still too young. She thinks going to petition Sixth Young Miss, then having Sixth Young Miss put in a good word with Jiang Sijiu after she marries in, will solve everything."

The maid who had reported to the Old Matriarch had in fact been paid off by Qin Ruyue, and was saying only flattering things on Qin Ruyue's behalf.

Qin Rongzong also chose not to go to Prince An's mansion today. After his personal attendant reported to him that Qin Ruyue had gone to find Qin Ruyin last night, the tightly knitted brow he'd been wearing finally eased a fraction.

"Fifth Sister has a good head on her."

Sun Shi gave a cold inward laugh. A good head, yes — but a very cold heart.

Truly not the least bit concerned about her own mother's welfare.

And her own husband was praising her...

She gave a slight shake of her head and concentrated on grinding ink for Qin Rongzong.

Her own family background was a cut above Tong Shi's. Her parents had only one daughter — herself — and doted on her greatly. Her five full brothers all cherished and protected her. They would never let her be wronged.

No matter what became of the Qin family in future, she and her children had nothing to fear.

Qin Rongzong dipped the wolf-hair brush carefully into the ink. Then he heard his personal attendant report from outside the study: "Young Master, Prince An has sent someone to invite you to the mansion!"

Qin Rongzong's pupils contracted sharply. He shot to his feet.

chapters.title: A Person Reborn
chapters.content:
A drop of ink fell from the wolf-hair brush onto the precious paper, spreading into a black blur.

Sun Shi closed her eyes briefly. That piece of calligraphy was ruined.

She stepped forward and gathered up the ruined piece.

She said quietly: "Young Master, please go change your clothes quickly. Don't make Prince An wait."

By the household's family order, she should have called him Third Master.

Qin Rongzong disliked it and had everyone in the courtyard call him First Master.

The Old Matriarch and Qin Mingjie both knew and turned a blind eye — they never said a word about it.

Sun Shi found it highly improper, but since even the elders didn't mind, what could she say?

As for Prince An — she knew him as well.

By coincidence, Prince An and her second brother were on very good terms. Her second brother presented himself to the world as a dissolute playboy, and the current Emperor thought nothing of Prince An being close to him.

Perhaps because of this fondness extending to those nearby, every year for her birthday while she was growing up, she received a birthday gift from Prince An.

Nothing extravagant, nothing particularly special.

Every time it was six twenty-tael silver ingots cast by the imperial workshop, and eight bolts of imperial tribute silk.

After she was married, the gifts stopped.

In those days when she was looking over potential matches and Qin Rongzong was presented to her, she had not been interested.

It was her father who worried that if she refused, it would offend Prince An's face.

After all, Qin Rongzong had been Prince An's companion since boyhood.

Her second brother, dissolute on the surface but brilliant within, could truly get along with Prince An. She understood: Prince An was not truly the lecherous hedonist he appeared to be on the surface.

It was a pity her husband simply could not take her counsel, and had caused Third Branch's Seventh Young Miss to attempt suicide.

What an offense against Heaven!

But this time, that First Branch Sixth Young Miss — she had a newfound appreciation for her.

Her own situation within this grand household was already difficult, her father and brothers suppressed, yet she had dared, out of sheer righteousness, to stand up for a distant cousin from a different branch.

And at that, a concubine-born cousin.

The Old Matriarch and her mother-in-law had both cursed Sixth Young Miss at length for being a petty person momentarily drunk on success, for failing to show proper respect to her elders, for being a blunt cannon...

She had found it almost comical listening.

The Old Matriarch had used the prestige of being an elder to suppress the First Branch for decades.

The Second Branch had also used that prestige for untold advantages.

Did they truly think the First Branch would submit for a lifetime?

On what grounds?

In her view, Sixth Young Miss harnessing the Jiang family's power — daring to speak, daring to act, daring to give pointed remarks, even daring to scold and make a scene — was the cleverest thing she could do...

She was about to be married out. While she could still use the Jiang family's prestige, to vent a proper grievance for her parents and brothers in her natal home — what was wrong with that?

Was she to wait until after she was married and then come back to stand up for them?

By then, the Second Branch would simply say: a married girl has no business meddling in her natal home's affairs — and the argument alone would put her at a disadvantage.

And then there was the First Branch — she had long observed something about them.

If First Master Qin of the First Branch were truly the cowering turtle that everyone assumed, and Lady Li of the First Branch were truly as simple-minded as the Old Matriarch and her own mother-in-law claimed...

Then how had the First Branch raised three sons and three daughters, all grown safely to adulthood, with marriages arranged quite well — was that truly nothing but luck?

And most tellingly: the First Branch was the only branch in the entire Qin household without a single concubine-born child.

That said everything. First Master Qin of the First Branch took full responsibility toward Lady Li, and full responsibility toward the children born between them.

With a father like that, and a mother who played the fool while being anything but — the First Branch's children would not spend their lives bowing before others.

Save for herself — a junior who had married in — there was not a single person in the entire Qin Second Branch who could see that the First Branch held the hidden potential of a dragon waiting to ascend.

And moreover — when she brought her children back to visit her natal home, her father and brothers had all quietly counseled her.

The inner court intrigues of the Qin household were not for her to involve herself in as a Second Branch daughter-in-law. Just observe.

When the First and Second Branches were about to determine their standing, then act accordingly.

But she was done enduring. It was time she made her move.

"Wenlang, I had already sent word to Prince An's household that I was taking leave — why then has he summoned me?" Qin Rongzong was anxious, worried that news of Seventh Young Miss's suicide attempt had already reached Prince An.

But then he reasoned that Prince An had been at the imperial Dayangān Temple for half a month, praying for blessings on behalf of the Emperor and Empress, and had only returned to his mansion the previous day. His heart eased a little.

In his view, Prince An had one weakness — a love of beauty — but otherwise had a fine temperament.

The reason he had been so eager to send Seventh Young Miss to Prince An's household was to provide some comfort and joy for Prince An, who had spent half a month in austere fasting and prayer...

Seeing that he had asked a question, Sun Shi turned her gaze to him with mild eyes and spoke with calm composure: "If Young Master asks this concubine — this concubine believes that no matter why Prince An has invited Young Master, Young Master need only tell him the truth."

"Absolutely not!" Qin Rongzong's handsome face went dark as ink. "If Seventh Young Miss's disgraceful behavior reaches Prince An's ears..."

Sun Shi frowned, and in a rare departure from her usual reserve, she took the initiative to cut him off.

"My second brother has said: Prince An is most protective of those he considers his own. As long as one admits fault and takes responsibility without flinching, Prince An will show leniency."

"If Young Master doesn't trust this concubine, then never mind — but please think carefully about the consequences of deceiving Prince An."

Besides — perhaps Prince An had merely made a passing remark about the Qin family having beautiful daughters, with nothing particular in mind?

It was Qin Rongzong's own scheming nature that had turned an offhand compliment into this disaster.

Sun Shi finished speaking and said no more. She lowered her head and began tidying the brushes and inkstones scattered across the desk.

She was a daughter of the great Jiangdong Sun clan, and she had a streak of pride in her.

If her husband chose to ignore her counsel, she was equally disinclined to waste further breath on him.

Qin Rongzong's face cycled through several expressions. Seeing that his wife had truly dropped the subject, he had no choice but to go change his clothes with a dark look.

Qin Rongzong hurried off in his carriage to Prince An's mansion.

Upon hearing the news, the Old Matriarch was so anxious she could not eat a proper midday meal.

She had people posted at both the front and back gates, with orders to bring the Third Young Master to her the moment he returned.

Sun Shi brought her two senior maids back to her own courtyard at an unhurried pace.

After instructing the wet nurses and senior maids of her two sons to attend to the children properly, she turned to her personal maidservant and said: "Go to the storeroom and find the purple crystal agate jewelry set that my mother gave me as trousseau."

The maidservant smiled and curtsied in reply. She said happily: "Young Miss is so beautiful — she ought to dress up properly!"

Sun Shi shook her head with a smile. "Not for me to wear. It's to be packed up as a trousseau gift for Sixth Young Miss."

The maidservant froze, then said with reluctance: "But Young Miss, the last time the Grand Matriarch gave Sixth Young Miss trousseau gifts, you already included a full set of solid-gold jewelry — and that was unique even among what was given, worth several hundred taels..."

"That purple crystal agate set is priceless — how can you give it away to Sixth Young Miss?"

Sun Shi understood she was hurting on her behalf, and said gently: "This time, Young Master made a mistake, and Sixth Young Miss's future husband caught him at it... I must make a gesture."

"Young Master brought this on himself — why should Young Miss pay for..."

Sun Shi stopped her. "Mind what you say."

She continued: "Also find two thirty-year mountain ginsengs and half a jin of blood swallow's nest from the storeroom. I want to visit Seventh Young Miss as well."

"Yes!" The maidservant curtsied, eyes full of heartache, and went off to the storeroom.

...

Qin Ruyin had just gone to visit Seventh Young Miss.

After her mother had brought Seventh Young Miss and Eighth Young Miss back to the First Branch, she had temporarily settled the two sisters in Xiaguang Pavilion, where Second Sister Ruxiu used to live.

Her mother's words about adopting Seventh Young Miss and Eighth Young Miss as First Branch daughters were not empty talk.

Her father had always pitied the two nieces from the Third Branch, and upon hearing her mother raise the subject herself, he was genuinely pleased.

He said plainly: when Third Uncle returned to the household, the adoption ceremony would be held.

Seventh Young Miss's state was still not good. Fortunately, with Eighth Young Miss by her side for company and care, she had managed to drink some thin congee — but she was still silent, not speaking.

Qin Ruyin made her way back to her own courtyard by way of a grove of early cherry blossoms.

The sunlight was just right, and the cherry blossoms bloomed brilliantly.

A soft breeze stirred, and petals scattered like a rain of pink snow.

She couldn't help but stop and linger.

Then a rush of hurried footsteps rang out along the covered walkway.

A young maid with double bun-knot hair and a pale green vest tumbled into view.

"Sixth Young Miss, Sixth Young Miss — Third Young Mistress is here..."

Qin Ruyin's brows drew slightly together. Sun Shi had come to see her?

Thinking of Sun Shi, the image of a serene and graceful young woman surfaced in her mind.

Now that she thought about it, Sun Shi and the original Qin Ruyin had had little interaction.

They had rarely met, and even when they did, it was nothing more than the surface pleasantries exchanged between a sister-in-law and a young miss.

She recalled that Sun Shi had also sent a trousseau gift along with the other Second Branch women.

"Banjia, have the gardener's woman cut a few branches of the early cherry blossoms with the most flower buds." Qin Ruyin instructed the newly promoted maid Banjia.

Banjia acknowledged the order.

Sun Shi had already been welcomed by Qin Ruyin's senior maid Purple Perilla and was seated on the large daybed by the window in Qin Ruyin's chambers, drinking tea.

"Third Sister-in-law, I hope you're well. I didn't know Third Sister-in-law was coming — Sixth Girl wasn't here to greet you properly. Please don't fault me for it."

Sun Shi looked up.

Qin Ruyin was wearing an embroidered lotus-pink brocade jacket, paired with a moon-white long skirt. She walked in with a few branches of tightly budded early cherry blossoms cradled carefully in her arms — like a fairy stepping out of a painting.

Involuntarily, a glimmer of admiration lit up her eyes.

She had never before seen Sixth Young Miss looking so vivid and alive.

She set down her teacup, rose to her feet, and walked forward to personally take Qin Ruyin's hand and lift her out of the bow she had started.

"Sixth Sister, no need for ceremony. Third Sister-in-law was the one who presumed to invite herself over. Won't you come sit and talk with me?" Sun Shi smiled warmly, putting one at ease as naturally as a spring breeze.

Qin Ruyin didn't know yet what this visit was about, but in terms of presence alone, she felt no dislike toward her at all.

"Third Sister-in-law, the early cherry blossoms in the West Courtyard are blooming beautifully. I had a few branches cut — and since Third Sister-in-law arrived at just the right moment, it must be fate between you and these blossoms. So I'll offer them to you — though I'm giving you your own flowers as a gift."

"Third Sister-in-law won't mind, I hope?"

Perhaps her delight showed plainly on her face — the warmth in Sun Shi's smile became genuinely fuller. She quickly gestured for someone to take the branches of fresh, dewy cherry blossoms from Qin Ruyin's arms.

She smiled and said: "How could Third Sister-in-law mind? I'm overjoyed! Thank you so much, Sixth Sister!"

Qin Ruyin had offered the cherry blossoms partly as a small test of Sun Shi.

Seeing that the pleasure on her face appeared entirely genuine, Qin Ruyin felt a quiet relief in her heart.

Sun Shi's background was the most distinguished of any woman in the Qin household.

She was so beautiful, and she had no wish to make an enemy of Sun Shi.

The two women exchanged a look and a smile, then sat down together.

"I came today for two reasons: first, to separately give Sixth Sister an additional trousseau gift. And second, to bring some medicinal supplements to visit Seventh Sister."

Sun Shi did not waste time on pleasantries but went straight to the point.

Then she turned to the senior maid who had accompanied her: "Present the trousseau gift I brought for Sixth Young Miss."

Qin Ruyin immediately spoke up: "Third Sister-in-law, you've already sent me a trousseau gift. There's no need to send another!"

Sun Shi smiled. "That time was just going along with the group. This time is Third Sister-in-law giving you something from the heart. Take it quickly — or I'll be displeased!"

Qin Ruyin's heart, which had grown warm toward her because of her intention to visit Seventh Young Miss, now also filled with suspicion at this second gift.

"Sixth Sister can set her mind at ease. This is nothing more than Third Sister-in-law's goodwill toward Sixth Sister. If Sixth Sister feels uneasy about it, then consider it Third Sister-in-law making an apology on your Third Elder Brother's behalf." It was as if she had read Qin Ruyin's suspicion. She spoke up.

"All these years, he hasn't been a proper older brother to you, and that has wronged you greatly. Whatever the reasons, the fault lies with him."

Sun Shi said only this much, then smiled and looked at Qin Ruyin.

Qin Ruyin's heart moved.

She was beginning to understand why Sun Shi had come.

When Sun Shi mentioned her Third Elder Brother Qin Rongzong, there was not a trace of love or protectiveness in her eyes.

On the contrary — she had barely concealed a look of disdain...

Qin Rongzong was probably not the husband Sun Shi had chosen for herself in the Jiangdong Sun clan.

After Qin Rongzong had stirred up the scandal of selling out his cousin for his own advancement, Sun Shi immediately sought out Qin Ruyin — a sign that the Jiangdong Sun clan was considering cutting Qin Rongzong loose.

The Jiangdong Sun clan had probably learned of some secret unfavorable to the Qin Second Branch...

If that were true, then the chances of the First Branch's rise were growing considerably.

"All right — then I'll accept it. Many thanks, Third Sister-in-law!" Qin Ruyin quickly returned to herself and curtsied in thanks, accepting the additional gift Sun Shi had brought for her.

Sun Shi took the folding fan handed to her by her senior maid and fanned it at an idle, unhurried pace.

Seeing her accept the gift with open ease and dignity, a streak of appreciation lit up her eyes.

Qin Ruyin, seeing that she was a straightforward person, decided to be straightforward herself.

"Third Sister-in-law, to speak honestly — I like you very much. But I don't like Third Elder Brother, and I like neither the Grand Matriarch nor Second Uncle and Second Aunt." After all, everyone knew it — it wasn't a secret.

"If you've come today to say good things on behalf of the Second Branch or Third Elder Brother, then you've made a wasted trip." Qin Ruyin looked earnestly at Sun Shi's delicate features. "Our First Branch will not submit to the Second Branch for a lifetime."

Sun Shi listened to her speak with such direct sincerity and was first taken aback, then raised her fan to cover a laugh.

"Sixth Sister — the Jiangdong Sun clan has stood for several centuries without falling. Though we now walk the path of civil officials, our ancestors were military men at the beginning."

"Our belief is this: the victor rules. I won't hide it from you — to our Sun clan, whoever wins, we help them."

Then she lowered the fan and looked steadily at Qin Ruyin. "Only — Sixth Sister, there is one thing Third Sister-in-law asks you to remember: the Qin Second Branch is the Qin Second Branch. Your Third Elder Brother is your Third Elder Brother. They have nothing to do with me and my sons, nor with the Jiangdong Sun clan behind me."

Qin Ruyin's pupils contracted, and her pretty little face filled with startlement.

Good heavens!

She hadn't expected that coming to this ancient era, she would meet someone so absolutely to her liking.

And a woman at that — how rare that was!

Her face brightened with a smile, and she said to Sun Shi: "Third Sister-in-law, you must mean what you say!"

Sun Shi countered with a smiling quip: "We Jiangdong Sun clan women always mean what we say. If I don't, you may go smash the placard hanging over my natal home's gate."

Then she turned serious, her smile fading. "But if your First Branch can't hold its own and can't outfight the others, then don't expect Third Sister-in-law to speak up for you."

Qin Ruyin understood clearly: Sun Shi daring to say all of this to her meant she possessed at least some ability to mobilize a portion of the Jiangdong Sun clan's resources.

Saying this much to her was already something quite remarkable.

"I'll remember what Third Sister-in-law has said." Qin Ruyin's expression also grew earnest.

"Then I'll also tell Third Sister-in-law plainly: as long as Third Sister-in-law holds true to what she has promised me today, in the future — whatever comes — Third Sister-in-law and your two sons will have peaceful and prosperous days."

The unspoken remainder was: if she broke her word, they would each rely on their own abilities.

Sun Shi's heart gave a quiet jolt.

This girl.

Very confident.

Her confidence clearly didn't come solely from Jiang Jiuxiao and the Jiang family behind him.

And certainly not from the current First Branch of the Qin family — which was still considerably weaker than the Second Branch.

Then — what other backing did this girl have?

chapters.title: Sun Shi Sends a Precious Gift; Her Husband Leaves Her Cold
chapters.content:
"Young Miss!" Purple Perilla, following Qin Ruyin's instructions, opened the trousseau case Sun Shi had brought and was so stunned her eyes could not move.

"How beautiful!"

Qin Ruyin had intended to have Purple Perilla look over the gift and note it down in the gift register. She was given quite a start herself.

She leaned over to look — and her eyes went still too.

Purple crystal wolf's blood agate!

The purple was so deep and rich. The three largest pieces of agate on the main headdress pin were each bigger than a longan fruit — round and perfect, held tightly in crimson gold settings.

The tops of the six pairs of side pins were set with the same purple crystal wolf's blood agate — about the size of longan seeds, and every one of them was plump and round.

Most precious of all: this was a complete matched jewelry set — the craftsmanship need not even be described.

Qin Ruyin had anticipated that Sun Shi's separate gift would not be shabby, but she hadn't expected anything this precious.

She even felt a little embarrassed.

And moreover — a gift this valuable was not something she could match in kind if she wanted to return the favor.

Unless she used the betrothal gifts the Jiang family had sent her.

That she would never do.

"Young Miss, the Third Young Mistress gave you such a weighty trousseau gift separately — does she have some ulterior motive? Should Young Miss discuss it with the First Mistress?"

Purple Perilla came back to herself, somewhat at a loss, concern flooding her eyes.

Even she as a maidservant knew: the greatest asset of the Qin Second Branch was not the third-rank official Second Master Qin.

It was the Third Young Mistress Sun Shi, born of the Jiangdong Sun clan.

If the Third Young Mistress had some design on or plot against their young miss — what were they to do?

"Of course I'll tell Mother. And Father as well." Qin Ruyin's gaze moved reluctantly away from the jewelry case.

She was already thinking about how to return the favor.

By the customs of the Great Ying Dynasty, no matter who gave a trousseau gift to an unmarried young miss, the young miss was not expected to give anything in return.

But she could not take Sun Shi's gift this valuable and simply feel at ease.

And she couldn't have no gesture at all.

Otherwise, feeling she owed Sun Shi too heavily, that she was in Sun Shi's debt, she'd feel inferior every time they interacted.

A gift should strike the person's heart. Likewise, a return gift that struck the heart of the recipient — that would be ideal.

Sun Shi was of distinguished birth — a true gentlewoman, cultivated with the full force of the entire Jiangdong Sun clan.

What good thing had she not seen?

So what was most important to Sun Shi right now?

She was a mother. To her, what mattered most was naturally her two sons.

Sun Shi's two sons — one was five, already beginning his studies, and by all accounts learning exceptionally well.

The other was only three, not yet formally enrolled, but had already shown a gift for learning.

Both Qin Mingjie and Qin Rongzong father and son placed enormous expectations on the two little boys and were extremely strict about their education.

This had caused Sun Shi no small displeasure.

As Sun Shi herself had said: though the Jiangdong Sun clan now walked the path of civil officialdom, their ancestors had been military men.

She hoped her sons studied hard, but her prerequisite was that they keep healthy bodies.

Her two sons were not particularly robust to begin with, and in spring especially, when the temperature shifted back and forth, both boys frequently suffered coughs and colds.

Sun Shi alone had gotten into cold standoffs with Qin Rongzong multiple times already this spring.

This was gossip that the two sisters-in-law had shared with her.

At the time, she had clucked in sympathy over the two young nephews of the Second Branch — how pitiful — and felt sorry for Sun Shi as their mother.

"Purple Perilla, send someone to find out what books the Third Sister-in-law's two little nephews are studying, and what style of calligraphy they're practicing for their character exercises."

"Once that's done, go out of the household yourself and find the finest furrier's shop. Buy some good-quality pelts and bring them back — I have a use for them."

Purple Perilla knew her young miss was growing more and more capable of thinking for herself, and that the young miss's decisions usually yielded very good results. She stopped worrying.

She curtsied in acknowledgment and went to see to it.

After Purple Perilla left, the senior maid Banjia came in with a smile to report: "Young Miss, the First Mistress asks for you."

Qin Ruyin smiled. Mother must be worried that Sun Shi had come with some bad intent.

"Banjia, come with me to Mother's rooms."

The date of her marriage was drawing closer and closer. Her mother had already sent someone to find out that in the Jiang family, the legitimate young mistresses of Qin Ruyin's generation were each matched with four first-tier senior maids and eight second-tier maids.

First-tier senior maids were generally brought from the natal home. If the number of second-tier maids was short, the Jiang family's side would provide them.

Purple Perilla was naturally her first senior maid. But Purple Perilla was already two years older than her — eighteen this year.

She also needed to plan for Purple Perilla's future. She could keep her by her side for at most two more years.

Banjia and Xinyi had been trained by her eldest sister-in-law and sent to her as gifts.

One was steady and measured, quiet but competent, a mature head on her shoulders. The other was quick and clever, full of ideas but always knowing where to draw the line.

Both were excellent candidates for senior maids — nothing more needed to be said.

Chenxiang and Juhong had been trained by her second sister-in-law and sent to her.

Both had more lively and spirited temperaments, thorough in their work despite an outwardly rough manner, and were also quite good.

Chenxiang was fourteen this year, and Juhong only thirteen.

She had first promoted Chenxiang to senior maid, with Juhong still at the second-tier level, to be promoted when Purple Perilla's position fell open.

Banjia had only been with the young miss a few days, but seeing that the young miss valued her, she was very conscientious in everything she did.

"Young Miss, shall I help you change into a fresh outfit? That tawny yellow Shu brocade magnolia jacket would suit you beautifully."

"No need to change. Just find me a hair comb — the new hair growing at my temples keeps slipping down again." Qin Ruyin said with a smile.

Ten thousand things were sprouting in this spring season, and her hair was growing in thick spurts.

"What about this silver-inlaid pink pearl comb, Young Miss? It pairs perfectly with the lotus pink you're already wearing." Banjia held the comb up and showed it to Qin Ruyin.

"Very good — that one." Qin Ruyin nodded.

Indeed — a maid who truly thought of her young miss wouldn't dress her unflatteringly. She only wanted to make her young miss look her very best.

"Help me put it in."

"Yes!" Banjia happily guided her young miss to sit before the dressing table.

Li Shi, seeing her flower-like young daughter come in, called out in a flurry: "Quickly bring out the lamb's milk custard your eldest sister-in-law set aside for your Sixth Young Miss!"

Qin Ruyin curtsied to her mother with a perfunctory bow, smiling broadly, all teeth and no eyes. "Eldest Sister-in-law left me lamb's milk custard again?"

"Isn't it just so, you little glutton?" Li Shi tapped Qin Ruyin's forehead with loving exasperation.

Eldest Sister-in-law's lamb's milk custard was the best — all the children loved it. The adults in the First Branch had no taste for the soft, delicate texture, but Qin Ruyin was the exception.

Once, Eldest Sister-in-law had fed a spoonful to her second daughter, and Qin Ruyin had leaned over — and Eldest Sister-in-law had given her a taste on a whim.

She'd been struck as if by lightning at the first bite.

From then on, whenever Eldest Sister-in-law made it for the two little girls and the children of Second Sister-in-law's household, she always set aside a bowl for Qin Ruyin.

Li Shi watched with a smile as her little daughter finished an entire bowl of lamb's milk custard, then urged her to rinse her mouth first.

"You greedy little thing." Li Shi's eyes were full of indulgence.

Once Qin Ruyin had rinsed her mouth and sat back down, Li Shi took her hands and asked: "I heard the servants say Second Branch's Sun Shi came to see you?"

Qin Ruyin nodded. "Third Sister-in-law came to give me a separate trousseau gift and also brought medicinal supplements to visit Seventh Sister."

Li Shi's brow lightly furrowed, her tone flat. "She really does know how to make people like her."

She then said with contempt: "That Second Branch — she's the only one who still has any dignity left. At least she still has the decency to personally bring supplements to visit your Seventh Sister. Where is everyone else?"

"Mother, there's no need to worry about them. Seventh Sister doesn't want to see anyone from the Second Branch anyway." Qin Ruyin said coldly. Li Shi nodded in agreement.

"Mm? Did you say Third Sister-in-law gave you a separate trousseau gift?" Li Shi remembered this, and quickly asked another question.

"Yes — a full matched set of purple crystal wolf's blood agate. The craftsmanship is very fine and clearly very valuable. I can't put a price on it myself, but it's likely worth at least two thousand taels..."

Before Qin Ruyin could finish, Li Shi drew a sharp breath.

"Why would she give you such a precious separate gift? On top of the solid gold jewelry set she gave you alone last time, which was already one of a kind and worth a few hundred taels..."

"Did she say anything to you? Did she ask you to do something?" Li Shi was very tense.

Qin Ruyin quickly reassured her.

She then said: "Third Sister-in-law has no other intent. On the surface it's an apology from Qin Rongzong — but really... I think Third Sister-in-law has a very low opinion of her husband."

Li Shi gave a cold laugh, then looked seriously into her daughter's eyes. "Before your betrothal, some things were not appropriate for Mother to say to you. But now that you are almost married, Mother can share some gossip about other people's households."

"Your Third Sister-in-law, in her day — it was truly a case of a phoenix falling into a mountain chicken's nest. Though having fallen in, even a phoenix can become a mountain chicken."

Qin Ruyin was puzzled. "But she and I spoke today, and she spoke with some pride of the Jiangdong Sun clan. With such deep roots, surely if she simply refused, she would never have had to marry into the Qin Second Branch?"

Li Shi gave a snort of contempt, her voice dripping with scorn.

"That was your Second Aunt using underhanded methods. What a waste — a Jiangdong Sun clan woman who'd be more than good enough to be an imperial prince's consort..."

Her mother had said underhanded methods but hadn't specified what they were — and if they were underhanded, they must have been quite sordid.

Qin Ruyin didn't press.

She only said: "Based on Third Sister-in-law's temperament, she doesn't seem like someone who would give in easily. How was it that she..."

"The Jiangdong Sun clan swallowed it in the end and accepted the match, biting down on their teeth — for Prince An's sake."

"I always knew that Qin Rongzong was no good. How trying for Third Sister-in-law!" Qin Ruyin couldn't help but feel more sympathy for Sun Shi.

Life for women in ancient times was truly difficult.

A noblewoman like Sun Shi — the natural target of every clan and notable family — was plotted against, and then, for the family's sake, was forced to swallow it.

She had to marry a man she had no respect for, and bear his children.

And when that odious man's genes were already lacking, he still had to interfere in how she raised the children...

What a piece of work.

"Not one of the old members of their Second Branch is any good! Rotten at the root — of course the upper beam being crooked will make the lower beams crooked." Li Shi said with contempt and spat.

After a brief silence, Li Shi said: "She gave you such a valuable separate trousseau gift. This favor is too great. I'll need to discuss it with your father — we must find a way to return the gesture."

Qin Ruyin had already thought through how to return the favor to Sun Shi.

She told Li Shi: "Third Sister-in-law's separate gift speaks for her own feelings and is her personal affair. I accepted it as my own, and I've already thought of what return gift to send. Mother and Father need not worry about this."

Li Shi said with a smile: "That you want to handle the return gift yourself is your business — but your parents can't be ignorant of propriety either and must also make a gesture."

She paused, then as if remembering something, said with amusement: "As it happens, your father recently acquired some curious and unheard-of Western objects from somewhere. I'll pick a dozen or so to send to your Third Sister-in-law's two sons — perfect."

Qin Ruyin's eyes lit up at her mother's words.

The only phrase she had focused on was: "Western objects!"

That Western objects had appeared in the Great Ying Dynasty at this time — she understood clearly what that implied!

"Mother, does Father have maps of our Great Ying Dynasty in his study?"

"Of course he does. There are others countries' maps too..."

"Mother, I want to have a look!" Qin Ruyin said, quick and eager.

Since coming into this body, so many events had unfolded one after another in rapid succession. She had never thought to take a look at a geographical map of the Great Ying Dynasty.

The ancient "maps" — what the ancients called 'kanyutu' — were originally used by geomancers to read the landscape. Later their uses expanded greatly: battles, taming rivers, and more.

"Oh! And now our little girl is going to do what her two brothers do and study maps? Are you going to become a woman general next?" Li Shi teased.

Qin Ruyin wheedled and cajoled her mother with a childlike air. Li Shi was already preoccupied with the Sun Shi business — and now that she knew Sun Shi had come to show goodwill, her worry was gone.

Unable to resist her youngest daughter's pestering, she quickly shooed her off.

"Goodness — finally got rid of that clingy little thing." Li Shi watched her daughter's cheerful departing figure and couldn't help shaking her head.

The Cui Mama who attended her beside her said with a laugh: "First Mistress, how can you still find yourself disliking Sixth Young Miss coming to wheedle you? A child this big, still willing to be affectionate with her parents — isn't that a wonderful thing? Some mothers would give anything for that and can't get it!"

Li Shi put on a show of superiority.

"That's right! My Ruyin gets along with me best of all!"

Cui Mama just pressed her lips together to hide her smile.

Qin Ruyin arrived at her father's study. Uncle Ning, who was in the study helping the master with account books, startled.

Why had Sixth Young Miss come to the master's study alone with just one senior maid at this hour?

Thinking this, he promptly rose and came out to receive her.

Qin Ruyin saw Uncle Ning was there too, and her face immediately broke into a bright smile.

Seeing Uncle Ning bow to greet her, she took two quick steps forward and extended both hands, catching him before he could complete the bow.

"Uncle Ning, what is this? Everyone in our household knows that you are in truth my father's sworn brother, and also our elder uncle. Such formality will shame your niece!"

Qin Ruyin's words were both graceful and warm — the worldly-wise Uncle Ning was left momentarily stunned.

But the corner of his mouth kept lifting higher, nearly catching a fishhook.

How could this child say such lovely things?

Of course, all the master's children were good children — they always treated him, a mere household man, with tremendous kindness and respect.

But Sixth Young Miss had previously been sweetly endearing. Now she was... hmm, sweetly endearing!

"Sixth Young Miss, you've come to the master's study — is there something you need?" Uncle Ning, who had lived his life without wife or children, was genuinely happy to spend time with the First Branch children. A fatherly tenderness was plain on his face.

"Oh, Uncle Ning, just call me Ruyin or Sixth Girl." Qin Ruyin personally poured him tea.

She smiled and raised her eyebrows. "I want to look at the maps of our Great Ying Dynasty. I was thinking I'd send someone to find you after — and then, how convenient, Uncle Ning is right here in my father's study!"

Uncle Ning was taken aback for a moment. "Sixth Young Mi— oh, Ruyin, you want to find me?" His eyes involuntarily carried a note of concern.

His expression also turned more serious. A young miss had never proactively sought him out before — had she run into some difficulty?

Then his brows grew sharper still. Could the Second Branch still be troubling their Sixth Young Miss?

chapters.title: A Man Already Standing Up for His Own
chapters.content:
Qin Ruyin was so intent on looking at the maps of the Great Ying Dynasty that she failed to notice the concern in Uncle Ning's eyes. "Uncle Ning, let me look at the maps first, and we can talk after."

Seeing her so eager, Uncle Ning had no choice but to take her to see the maps.

"There are two maps in all. This one shows our Great Ying Dynasty, and this one is the Complete Map of All Nations — it took the great explorer Xu Zhen of the previous dynasty over thirty years to complete."

Qin Ruyin was first struck dumb by the vast, spreading territory of the Great Ying Dynasty — shaped like an autumn leaf or a begonia.

Was this not the homeland she had missed so dearly in her dreams?

Looking at the scale, she made a rough calculation — it was more than twice the size of the homeland she had once known.

Her heart leapt with wild joy!

The Great Ying Dynasty's territory must be no less than nearly twenty million square kilometers.

She then turned to the Complete Map of All Nations.

This was essentially a simplified world map.

Looking at the Great Ying Dynasty's northern neighbors — there were the Golden Horde Khanate and the Kipchak Khanate.

In her original world, these two had been a single nation in a certain feudal era. Yet in the Great Ying Dynasty's world, they were two neighboring countries side by side.

And both had very large territories.

With domains that large, and both nations built on horseback — if they combined forces, the Great Ying Dynasty's northern frontier would be in very grave danger!
