Looking further, she saw what amounted to a large-scale Southeast Asia — the names of the many small kingdoms there she couldn't even keep track of.

To the west were the nations of the Western Regions; to the east, the sea — except that it was dotted with many large archipelagoes and enormous solitary islands.

Was this some kind of jumbo-sized version of the Blue Planet?

Qin Ruyin had been overjoyed at the vastness of the Great Ying Dynasty's territory. After seeing the Complete Map of All Nations, she no longer felt the same way.

It was pure envy.

She was starting to resent the successive emperors of the Great Ying Dynasty for lacking sufficient ambition.

"Uncle Ning — speaking plainly — there are so many small, weak nations surrounding our Great Ying Dynasty, sitting on so much land. Is that not a shame?"

"And that stretch of open sea to the east... I can see on the Complete Map that there are so many uninhabited archipelagoes and large solitary islands. Why has our Great Ying Dynasty not sent people to claim them?"

Just looking at it was heartbreaking.

"Ah?" Uncle Ning was thrown completely off balance by the Sixth Young Miss's question.

The master himself, and First Young Master, and Second Young Master had never given this a thought.

His mind turned for a long while before he managed an answer: "Before our dynasty, some of the smaller kingdoms on our borders used to frequently launch military campaigns against the central dynasty. After several dynasties' worth of suppression, they've become much better behaved..."

As for those solitary islands and archipelagoes in the eastern sea, even a man with a desire to expand territory would have been envious.

Yet somehow, the court paid little attention to uninhabited sea islands. The Great Ying Dynasty already had vast resources — the problem was that its population was too sparse relative to the land.

Hearing what Uncle Ning said, Qin Ruyin couldn't help but borrow an ancient lament: "The ancients did not deceive us — these little countries truly do only fear the strong and have no gratitude for the kind!"

At hearing this old saying flow so casually from Sixth Young Miss, Uncle Ning's eyes suddenly brightened.

He had not expected that Sixth Young Miss would understand principles most men did not!

"Ruyin — you are looking at these maps... do you have some plan in mind?" Uncle Ning's sharp mind had already formed a rough guess. He thought he'd ask first.

"Yes, Uncle Ning — I intend to go into business and make money." Qin Ruyin was direct.

"Business?"

Uncle Ning had guessed wrong.

He had assumed Ruyin was consulting him about her dowry properties — shops to the north, estates to the south, and so on.

"The Great Ying Dynasty currently has four stable and thriving trade routes, doesn't it? The betrothal money I've received for this marriage is quite substantial. I'd like to ask Uncle Ning to help me first assemble a small merchant caravan..."

"Ruyin!" Uncle Ning was startled. "You... you..."

Qin Ruyin could see he was agitated — probably unable to understand why a young woman of the inner court would have such audacity and want to form a merchant caravan.

"Uncle Ning need not worry. Business always has its gains and losses — I understand that very well."

"The world is unfair to women. Many things — especially business — are not convenient for me to handle in person. That is why I'm asking Uncle Ning to help me!"

"My father has told me all about you, Uncle Ning. Father trusts you, and told me to trust you as well. So of course I trust you!" She smiled warmly at him.

Uncle Ning rubbed his reddening eyes. His voice was firm. "Ruyin, which trade route did you have in mind?"

He then added: "Generally speaking, the Silk Road to the northwest and the Ancient Tea-Horse Road to the southwest are the most reliable routes. The Ning family has excellent connections along both..."

"Uncle Ning, I want to take the maritime route."

Uncle Ning was startled again.

The Great Ying Dynasty had only begun developing maritime trade routes in the last two or three years — trading with the island nations in the southeastern sea and the small kingdoms of the Southeast Asian peninsula.

These small nations might have little military power, but their territories were relatively vast, their climates favorable, and their resources abundant.

Enormous maritime trading ships loaded with the richest goods of the Central Plains would sail out, sell at high prices along the way, then buy back spices, gems, exotic creatures, rare medicines, and other local specialties at low cost.

As long as the ship made it back in one piece, the profits were invariably staggering.

However, these enormous maritime trading vessels were all built under the supervision of the Ministry of Public Works, and only the Eight Imperial Merchants were eligible to purchase them at astronomical prices — and even then, they were trading on the court's behalf.

Of the profits, the participating Imperial Merchants could keep thirty percent.

That said, as of now, only the Xu family and the Xue family among the Eight Imperial Merchants dared to operate these routes.

His Ning family, wealthy as it was, also had no foot in this door. It wasn't that the court forbade it — private merchants of great means who built their own ships and set to sea only needed to register with the court.

It was just that the Ning family had long been accustomed to overland trade, and was deeply conservative. They didn't dare casually venture into maritime trade — far more dangerous and wholly outside their familiar domain.

Coastal fishermen who feared nothing and went out to sea for their livelihoods would sometimes take a small boatload of goods and try their luck — but that was quite different.

After hearing Uncle Ning's analysis, Qin Ruyin's resolve did not waver.

"One must always try first. If all you see are the risks, nothing ever gets done. But then again — if the risk weren't so great, there'd already be a crowd doing it, and there'd be nothing left for us."

"Mother mentioned today that Father had acquired some Western objects — those must have come through your channels, Uncle Ning?" Qin Ruyin's eyes shone with bright curiosity.

Uncle Ning quickly replied: "Ruyin, those Western objects came overland..."

"Ah, all right. Very well — Uncle Ning will try." He couldn't bear to disappoint the girl, and so agreed, steeling himself.

Qin Ruyin immediately smiled.

"Uncle Ning, rest assured — I'm not the sort who needs ten bulls to drag her back." She said cheerfully.

"First, please send someone to the coastal provinces to make thorough inquiries — assess how the local fishermen take their boats out and conduct trade."

"If those fishermen, who have spent their whole lives fighting the sea and are seasoned from a hundred battles, are willing to work for us, then we'll proceed. And of course, we will not treat them poorly."

"If after your inquiries you still feel the risk is too great and we cannot do it, then I'll do overland trade instead."

Hearing her say this, Uncle Ning finally breathed easy.

"We'll prepare for this first, but even if all goes well, we'll have to wait until after I'm married before we begin."

Qin Ruyin's expression was earnest and solemn. "Uncle Ning need not worry about spending money, or worry about the money going to waste... I won't be satisfied unless we try."

"Good!" Uncle Ning agreed.

Sixth Young Miss was only in her teens and already had this kind of courage and foresight — willing to stake her own wedding funds. He was a grown man from a great merchant family. How could he retreat?

Thinking this, for the first time in all his years, Uncle Ning felt a surge of bold and adventurous spirit.

"Ruyin, shall we inform the master about this?" Uncle Ning asked her.

Qin Ruyin was secretly pleased — the fact that Uncle Ning had asked showed he recognized her as the one in charge and wasn't placing her father above her.

"If Uncle Ning trusts me, then let's not tell Father for now — it would only worry him."

She laughed. "Father is cautious. He wouldn't approve of something this impulsive."

Uncle Ning laughed too.

In his heart, he had to admit — he had always been fonder of the young who dared to charge ahead and fight their way through.

But he understood well: the master had truly not had it easy all these years.

He had originally thought that with the master and himself as a foundation, it might take the First Branch three more generations to produce an offspring bold enough to fight and strive.

Yet here was Sixth Young Miss — this wonderful surprise, far sooner than expected.

If Sixth Young Miss's enterprise succeeded... they'd be counting silver until their hands cramped.

And Sixth Young Miss had the same fierce loyalty toward her own that the master and mistress had. If she succeeded, the First Branch's meteoric rise would not be far off.

What was a great clan without silver to lean on?

Truthfully, Sixth Young Miss was in the right. The established overland trade routes had long since matured — the profits were nowhere near as handsome as they had been in earlier dynasties.

If you wanted to strike it truly rich, how could you avoid risk?

Consider how many early caravans had been lost and destroyed to make today's relatively safe overland routes possible.

Qin Ruyin wasn't worried about this not working out.

She had arrived in this world and found she had no particular special power or golden finger. The only constant was this: whatever she set her mind to do, she managed to accomplish.

So her golden finger must simply be very good fortune.

"Uncle Ning, here are two thousand taels in banknotes. Please take them first. If they're not enough, have someone let my maid Purple Perilla know." Asking someone to do work for you without paying — that wouldn't do, however close the relationship.

Uncle Ning shook his head like a rattle-drum. "Just gathering information is such a small thing — how could I take Ruyin's money? Uncle Ning has his own!"

"That won't do. I want Uncle Ning as a partner in this venture, and since this is my idea, you must take the money. As for how much you'd like to invest as a partner once you've decided the business can be done — we two can negotiate that."

Uncle Ning's eyes reddened again.

He said at once: "Whether it succeeds or not, Uncle Ning is willing to invest in Ruyin's venture. Uncle Ning will put in one share, a dry stake. Since Ruyin has put in two thousand taels, Uncle Ning will add two hundred..."

chapters.title: The Wang Family Is Displeased with Fifth Young Miss
chapters.content:
At the Jiang mansion.

Jiang Jiuxiao had been frantically busy today — like a man at war.

When he finally left the court, Li Yi leapt from the carriage, bowed, and fell into step with his master, whispering a report.

Jiang Jiuxiao had been about to step into the carriage, but his footsteps stopped.

"This is absolutely certain?"

Li Yi responded with due gravity: "My lord, absolutely certain. You know what Lin Er's team can do — every one of them has extraordinary hearing. The little one Lin Er planted to sweep and clean in Master Qin's study courtyard heard it all clearly!"

Jiang Jiuxiao's brow furrowed slightly.

The girl's audacity was truly something.

Then his lips curved into a smile.

Without audacity, how would she have done so many things that even surprised him?

"Li Yi, go personally and let Young Master Xu know — have him quietly give Sixth Young Miss a hand."

Li Yi paused. "My lord, you're not worried that Sixth Young Miss might..."

Lose everything?

"Worried about what?" Jiang Jiuxiao said mildly. "Even if it's a total loss, there is still your lord here to back her up. Consider it her playing around with a bit of pocket money."

Li Yi thought: My lord, is that a 'bit of pocket money'?

But he saw his master glance back at him with that cool, steady look. "Li Yi — do you think your lord could possibly let Sixth Young Miss take a loss?"

...

Li Yi shook his head frantically.

Jiang Jiuxiao clasped his hands behind his back and boarded the carriage. As the carriage curtain fell, he added one more instruction:

"Keep watch on the Ning family. If the one running things on the Ning side is on Ning Haoyuan's side, have Young Master Xu look after them a little as well — and let the Ning family know it's on account of Sixth Young Miss."

Li Yi acknowledged the order, but was inwardly stunned.

Good heavens.

His master was being this protective of Sixth Young Miss's people — did he even realize it himself?

After Qin Ruyin left her father's study, Uncle Ning took out a brand-new account ledger and recorded the two thousand taels Qin Ruyin had given him.

Below that two-hundred-tael entry, he noted it as belonging to the First Branch of the Qin family.

But the money was his own.

He had decided from the start: if Sixth Young Miss's maritime venture succeeded, those two hundred taels representing one share would count as the First Branch's stake — not his personal investment.

He believed that even if Sixth Young Miss succeeded later, the First Branch would never exploit her by demanding further shares.

He was putting in this one share on the master's behalf — and that was already taking advantage. Any more would be unfair to Sixth Young Miss.

...

Qin Rongzong returned from Prince An's mansion at midday.

He had barely stepped through the Qin family's front gate when the Old Matriarch's people came to escort him to Shouhe Hall.

Qin Rongzong's expression was terrible. The Old Matriarch felt a lurch in her heart the moment she saw it.

"Rongzong, what did Prince An say?"

Qin Rongzong forced himself to put on a brave face, but the memory of Prince An's harsh reprimand made his complexion go pale again.

"Grandmother, the business with the Seventh Young Miss has truly reached Prince An's ears... and today in the morning court, several censorate memorials were submitted impeaching Father..."

"How can this be!" The Old Matriarch lurched to her feet in shock, accidentally knocking over the fruit dish on the side table. "That matter... your father dealt with it himself. By rights, news of the Seventh Young Miss's suicide attempt shouldn't have leaked out at all!"

Qin Rongzong hung his head in dejection.

"Never mind Father — even Prince An himself has been personally called to account by Chief Censor Fan in a submitted memorial."

"What... what?!"

"Prince An has asked me to personally bear all the consequences this incident has caused, and to clear Father's name — otherwise Father's official post may not survive."

"As for me — I cannot expect to advance so much as half a step within the next two years..." He was bitterly unwilling. How could this be?

He simply could not understand how things had turned out this way.

The Old Matriarch swayed, then her eyes rolled back and she fainted.

The servants around her flew into a panic...

Qin Rongzong didn't know how he found his way back to his principal wife Sun Shi's Haoyue Courtyard.

Sun Shi saw him enter in a daze, not even registering the maids' and servants' greetings. He drifted like a ghost. She gave a soft, cold smile and told her people to attend to him, while she went back to reading her accounts.

"Wenlang..." Qin Rongzong sat down across from Sun Shi, his face drawn. He told her everything Prince An had said to him.

"Originally, I was planning to endure one more year, then approach Prince An again next year to arrange a few years of training in the Ministry of Personnel. But now..."

"Wenlang, could you ask your eldest brother to help smooth things over for me?" Qin Rongzong looked with hope at his wife's serene, gentle face.

With Prince An now clearly sick of him, and his father in trouble of his own, the Sun family connections were his last resort.

Only then did Sun Shi set down the account book. She looked at him, her expression as composed as ever.

"Prince An has already shown you a clear path forward. If you don't follow it and instead attempt to pull strings behind the scenes, where does that leave Prince An? You must understand — you are Prince An's man. If you defy him openly, who in the future will be willing to associate with you? Who will dare to use you?"

If the Qin family had already sunk into a swamp and still wanted to pull the Sun family down with them, it was simply disgusting.

Qin Rongzong's handsome face turned from white to green.

He saw not a flicker of wifely concern in Sun Shi's eyes.

She was always this calm, this composed, this rational... even in the bedchamber.

What he despised most was this quality in her — she was like a clay Buddha sitting immovably in a shrine.

"I heard from Grandmother that you went to the West Courtyard today to see Qin Sixth Young Miss?" He suddenly changed the subject.

Sun Shi looked at him. A thinly veiled mockery surfaced in her eyes.

"Yes. After you and the Second Branch have offended the First Branch so thoroughly, and Fifth Sister used her scheming ways to steal the marriage that originally belonged to Sixth Sister... and after you served as an accomplice — now that Sixth Sister is no longer the pushover Second Branch people used to bully, I naturally had to clean up some of the mess on your behalf."

"If we've made enemies of her beyond all repair, there's no getting that back."

"What you're saying is — the Second Branch's current precarious situation is the result of Jiang Sijiu pulling strings behind the scenes? He was actually shielding Sixth Young Miss?" Qin Rongzong's face cycled through white to green and back again.

Jiang Sijiu — what cunning! He even came to the Qin household at night playing the good man, so no one would suspect him.

Sun Shi sighed. "You always focus only on your own interests. Who exposed the Seventh Young Miss's situation and got Father impeached by the Censorate — that's no longer the important thing."

"What matters is that someone is targeting the Qin Second Branch. Right now, the Second Branch is in the open while the other party is in the shadows. At the very least, we cannot push the Jiang family into direct opposition with the Qin family."

"And Jiang Sijiu? What if Jiang Sijiu himself is the one pulling the strings?" Qin Rongzong's composure was beginning to crack.

Sun Shi looked at him steadily. "Even then, the Second Branch cannot openly break with the Jiang family."

"Why not?"

"Because your Second Branch has not one shred of evidence."

After the Second Branch's fall from grace, outsiders seeing Lord Jiang arrive after nightfall would certainly assume he had come to help the Qin family on account of the family connection — that was the only interpretation that made sense.

Lord Jiang's visit had, in fact, handed over a kind of vulnerability. If someone used it against him, it would be troublesome for Lord Jiang as well.

But what if Lord Jiang was not afraid of that trouble?

Then the Second Branch was finished. No one would dare come out to help Qin Rongzong's father and son.

Once Qin Rongzong calmed down, he seemed to grasp this logic too.

"And one more thing — because none of your Qin family's officials can outrank Lord Jiang." Sun Shi delivered one more stab to his chest.

Qin Rongzong erupted in fury. He threw his head back and snapped at Sun Shi: "Isn't there still your Sun family? The Jiangdong Sun clan — with the full force of the entire clan behind us, I refuse to believe the Jiang family wouldn't be afraid!"

The beauty of Sun Shi's eyes went even colder.

"The Sun family is my Sun family. Not yours. And certainly not your Qin family's."

A son-in-law who had never once been accepted by the whole clan had the audacity to talk about the Sun family mobilizing their entire clan for him.

His arrogance had grown beyond all sense of proportion.

Qin Rongzong finally lost control. He lurched to his feet and glared at Sun Shi's face. "Wenlang — how can you say such things to wound me? We are husband and wife — one flesh!"

"Are we?" Sun Shi asked him, quiet and tired-sounding. "Are we truly one flesh, you and I?"

"We are!"

Sun Shi smiled. But her eyes were ice. "Think carefully before you answer me again."

Qin Rongzong snarled: "In the six years since I married you, I have not taken so much as a bedchamber servant, let alone a concubine! You alone — is that not being one flesh? You're still not satisfied?"

"Oh, is that so?" Sun Shi looked at him with contempt. "Then tell me — who is the young woman called Huazhi who lives in Yuanbao Lane?"

"You... how do you know about that?"

Sun Shi smiled coolly. "When your family came to propose to mine, you promised no concubines until the age of forty without a son. I gave birth our first year, and Zheng'er was born by year's end. By the third year, Shen'er arrived. Unwilling to wait to take concubines, you secretly kept a mistress outside — a girl of sixteen, a flower just beginning to bloom, yes?"

She still dared to ask how she found out.

When her eldest brother told her himself, she hadn't believed it.

Not until her eldest brother personally took her in a carriage to Yuanbao Lane.

That Huazhi girl was indeed a slender, breathtaking little beauty, her small face glowing with the light of a woman who was cherished.

Unfortunately, proper conduct and virtue were quite beyond her.

That was when she understood — her husband, the father of her children, was attracted to this kind of woman.

How laughable.

Her eldest brother told her to think carefully. If one day she wished to end the marriage, he would personally bring the other brothers to the Qin family to take her home.

She could not bear to leave her two sons. She convinced herself to endure.

She couldn't let her young sons be left without a father or without a mother.

She had given him one chance.

"What is wrong with a man taking multiple wives and concubines? All my colleagues have concubines in their back courtyard. I alone do not — do you know how they mock me behind my back?"

"So you hid it from me and secretly kept a mistress outside?" Sun Shi met his anger without flinching.

"You! You're being unreasonable!" Qin Rongzong's face flushed scarlet. He stood and flung his sleeve savagely. "I'll sleep in the study tonight. See yourself to bed!"

Sun Shi said coldly, "As you wish."

After Qin Rongzong's shameful exit, Sun Shi's wet nurse and maids all looked at her with heartbreak.

"What are you doing? Life goes on. Each of you go attend to your duties." Sun Shi gave a brief smile and waved them away.

...

On the third day after Tong Shi was sent to the countryside estate to "recuperate," Wang Furen also received the news.

She had originally been swallowing her irritation and pinching her nose, preparing to send betrothal gifts to Fifth Young Miss. After much deliberation, she had steeled herself to give two thousand taels as a betrothal sum with sixty trays of gifts — already more than she'd given her own eldest daughter-in-law, and she would have to privately compensate the eldest daughter-in-law for the difference.

But upon hearing the news, she threw down the betrothal gift list on the spot, her face iron-gray with fury.

When Master Wang returned from court that evening, she vented her grievances at him.

"What exactly does the Qin family mean by all of this?"

"This time, the Second Mistress of the Qin family falls sick and gets sent to the countryside to recuperate. Sick with what? Don't tell me she's done so many things against her conscience that she has a guilty heart?"

Wang Furen was brimming with resentment, and couldn't even look at her husband pleasantly.

Master Wang was also taken aback.

At this moment — how had another mess broken out?

"Perhaps my lady should visit the Qin family tomorrow and sound out their Grand Matriarch's intentions?"

Wang Furen flared up at once.

"How can you say such a thing? What wrong has our Wang family done? It is clearly the Qin family causing trouble again and again. Why should our Wang family go groveling to them?"

"If you want to go, you go yourself. After the match swap, this marriage was the Qin family coming to us. I refuse to be looked down on!" Wang Furen said her piece and ignored her husband entirely.

Master Wang was stuck. His lady was in the middle of her anger and he didn't dare push further.

Who could blame her? It was his own failure: his lady had better judgment and hadn't wanted the swap. It was he who hadn't told her first before privately settling things with that old scoundrel of the Second Branch.

No wonder she was so full of deep resentment...

He no longer had even a fraction of his former confident, commanding air.

"Fine, fine — shall we delay the betrothal visit to the Qin family by a few more days?" he ventured.

Wang Furen huffed bitterly. "If not for the sake of our Wang family's good name, I truly would not want to ally with that Qin Second Branch at all!"

Master Wang could only soothe her. "How can that be? Never mind that this engagement is already a tiger we've climbed onto and can't get off — our son Mian'er chose Fifth Young Miss himself. Are you not his mother? Don't you love him?"

Wang Furen drew a deep breath and let it out heavily.

"I must truly be repaying that little rascal for some past debt!"

Master Wang gave a rueful, fond chuckle.

With some slight sourness he thought: Mian'er was a fine scholar, outstanding in both talent and looks with a bright future ahead — even he as the father didn't dare call him a "little rascal." Only his mother could get away with that.

"Then let's do as you say and delay the betrothal visit a few more days. We can say that the Jiang family's betrothal gifts for Sixth Young Miss were so extravagant we've been selling the furniture to scrape together enough to match!"

"My lady — you absolutely cannot say that!" Master Wang said urgently.

Wang Furen shot him an exasperated look.

Did he really think she would actually say it?

...

In Shouhe Hall of the Qin household.

After enormous effort — and in the end, Prince An quietly intervening — the Second Master of the Qin family had managed to put to rest the matter of Seventh Young Miss being driven to suicide.

However, the price paid by the Qin father and son was that neither could be promoted for two years.

Though the cost was steep, at least the matter was settled.

The Old Matriarch was saddened, but also relieved that the affair was over.

For the Second Branch now, the most pressing matter was Fifth Young Miss's marriage.

The Old Matriarch's heart was increasingly uneasy at the Wang family's continued failure to come with betrothal gifts. Qin Mingjie was quietly nervous as well.

But since he had just been in trouble, he had to keep his head down for now. Matters of the children's marriage were better handled by the womenfolk.

So he had the Old Matriarch ask a close acquaintance — a matron the Old Matriarch was on good terms with — to go to the Wang family as a hint.

That matron went to the Wang family and no matter how tactfully she raised the question of the betrothal, Wang Furen simply brushed it off with pleasantries and got around it.

The matron returned empty-handed. With no other recourse, the Old Matriarch went to the Wang family herself.

Wang Furen received her warmly.

When the Old Matriarch had the cheek to raise the matter of the betrothal gifts directly, Wang Furen began to tell her tale of hardship.

Qin Mingjie had been severely reprimanded at court by the Emperor, who declared that he and his son could not be promoted for two years. Though the Emperor had given the Qin family face by not specifying the reason publicly, the Second Branch had still thoroughly lost face.

Wang Furen now had new thoughts about Fifth Young Miss's betrothal gifts.

"Your Ladyship, this concubine has truly no other recourse. The betrothal money and gifts Lord Jiang sent for your household's Sixth Young Miss were so lavish that even if our Wang family were to sell the house, we couldn't match it."

"Ah, I am also a mother. Of course I want my son's future wife to have dignity and face. That is why I am still trying to scrape together enough for the betrothal money and gifts."

"I just don't know how much would be the right amount to gather!"

The Old Matriarch's face flushed crimson, her heart seething with furious indignation.

She knew perfectly well that Wang Furen was saying this on purpose. Wang Furen had likely already become unhappy with Fifth Young Miss — and was deliberately bringing up the Jiang family's gifts for Sixth Young Miss to rub her face in it.

"Oh, dear Lady Wang — please don't think of it that way! The Jiang family had originally taken a fancy to our Fifth Young Miss — those betrothal money and gifts were set long in advance. Our Sixth Young Miss was simply the one who ended up going. You really need not worry about any of that!"

She was still trying to prop Fifth Young Miss up on Sixth Young Miss's coattails? If Fifth Young Miss were truly so exceptional, how could the Jiang family have agreed to swap to Sixth Young Miss?

Wang Furen gave a cold snort inwardly, but showed nothing outwardly.

No matter what — she was determined to press this old woman into openly agreeing that the Wang family need not give Fifth Young Miss too generous a betrothal sum.

"Your Ladyship also knows — whether it's Sixth Young Miss or Fifth Young Miss, our Wang family's fortunes truly cannot compare to the Jiang family's betrothal money and gifts!"

Hearing Wang Furen's words, the Old Matriarch fully understood their meaning. A surge of old blood rose to her throat and her face went completely white...

chapters.title: Fifth Young Miss's Betrothal Is an Insult
chapters.content:
What could the Old Matriarch do? She could only force herself to swallow that surge of blood back down.

The Wang family had been dragging their feet to avoid giving Fifth Young Miss too generous a betrothal sum and gifts. Yet they also wanted to preserve face — they had to be the ones going through the motions of maintaining propriety, forcing the Qin family to come begging them to hurry up with the betrothal, while also asking the Qin family not to make things difficult over the amount.

Sixth Young Miss's wedding date had already been set. By custom, the elder sister was to be married before the younger. The Wang family continued to delay — and when the Jiang family's appointed day arrived, they would come straight to the Qin household to take the bride. By then, the only ones who would be laughed at were Fifth Young Miss and the Second Branch.

Having thought through Wang Furen's intent, the Old Matriarch knew that circumstances were stronger than people. Second Son and Rongzong were already in a run of bad luck, and they absolutely could not afford to antagonize the Wang family further.

"Lady Wang, the Wang family has been a household of scholars and upright men for generations — we all know that. Our Qin family is not one to attach importance to such material things when marrying off a daughter. Dear Lady Wang, please do not trouble yourself over this. Let us simply follow custom and do what is fitting."

The Old Matriarch swallowed her humiliation and said all of this through gritted teeth.

Wang Furen, seeing that the old woman had finally broken and softened, was satisfied.

Yet she still put on a show of embarrassment: "How would that do? By custom, when we welcomed our eldest son's bride, the betrothal sum was six hundred taels with twenty trays of gifts."

The Old Matriarch nearly keeled over with fury — yet she could show nothing, and could only nod through gritted teeth. "That is perfectly fine. Our Fifth Young Miss doesn't care about such things..."

"Oh goodness, Your Ladyship hasn't quite understood me. That was the standard for welcoming our Wang family's eldest daughter-in-law — the standard for welcoming the clan matriarch-to-be."

"Fifth Young Miss is the second son's wife. That is... a different matter." Wang Furen changed her tone and smiled pleasantly at the Old Matriarch.

"Your Ladyship — you see, what is to be done?"

The Old Matriarch's throat filled again with the bitterness of suppressed rage, and every drop of color drained from her aged face.

Wang Furen acted as if she didn't see it. This old woman had risen from concubine status — she was used to swallowing things. She wouldn't choke to death on it.

"Then... what does Lady Wang propose?" The Old Matriarch nearly ground her teeth to dust as she listlessly asked.

Wang Furen lifted her teacup and let her eyes drift lightly.

Then she looked at the Old Matriarch and smiled: "According to our Wang family's rules, the eldest daughter-in-law has the highest standard. All other sons' wives are equal — five hundred taels for the betrothal sum, ten trays of gifts. What do you think, Your Ladyship?"

The Old Matriarch's aged face wrinkled like a dried eggplant. She couldn't help herself: "Is that not rather too little? Our Qin family's dowry will certainly not be lacking..."

Wang Furen immediately said: "Your Ladyship just said Fifth Young Miss doesn't care about betrothal money and gifts, and our Wang family and Mian'er don't care about how much the bride brings in her dowry..."

The Old Matriarch cut her off at once, emphasizing again: "Lady Wang can set her mind at ease — our Qin family loves its daughters, and the dowry will be very generous!"

Wang Furen gave a half-smile. "I have nothing to be uneasy about. That is the Qin family's gift to their own daughter — it represents the kindness of you elders. Fifth Young Miss is a fortunate girl."

She simply would not take the bait.

The betrothal money and gifts — she just wasn't willing to give Fifth Young Miss more.

Either way, she had said it herself: the Wang family didn't care how much the Qin family gave Fifth Young Miss in her dowry.

If the Qin family loved their daughter and chose to give too much, that was the Qin family's affair. A generous dowry gave Fifth Young Miss face; a meager one diminished it.

No matter who Fifth Young Miss married, that money was for Fifth Young Miss herself and her children or husband to use.

Coming to the Wang family, it would benefit her son and grandchildren. More was welcome; less was not complained about — her son's family could still be fed.

Nothing more than a plainer life.

The Wang family had lived that way for generations.

As for the shared household funds? She had her pride. The Wang family would never lower themselves to touch a daughter-in-law's dowry.

"Lady Wang — how about this. The betrothal sum for Fifth Young Miss, six hundred taels, with two fewer trays of gifts." This was already the Old Matriarch's absolute maximum.

Wang Furen raised an eyebrow, smiled faintly, and nodded. "Very well. Your Ladyship insists on only reducing the trays by two — that also works. Then I will gather together a bit more, and once I have it ready, I shall come to your household to present Fifth Young Miss's betrothal. Your Ladyship, what do you say?"

The Old Matriarch nearly went dark before her eyes.

Forcing down one last burst of anger, she sighed and looked at Wang Furen. "Lady Wang — Mian'er is such a fine young man. Can you truly bear to let him be wronged like this?"

"Our Qin family is not fighting over these things. A few hundred taels as the betrothal sum, so be it — after all, this is your Wang family's custom. But a scholar of Mian'er's caliber and talent... this old woman truly cannot bear to have him put to shame!"

The implication being: this also reflects on your son's face.

Wang Furen met the Old Matriarch's gaze with cold composure. "There's nothing to be done about it. In our Wang family, rules are rules. Who told Mian'er not to be born two years earlier as the Wang family's eldest legitimate son?"

Those words had now been said.

If the Old Matriarch pushed any further, she would have no face left at all.

Things had come to this, and the Old Matriarch could not bear to stay in the Wang household another moment. She was anxious to take her leave.

Wang Furen rose and stopped her. "Your Ladyship, please wait — I heard that your household's Second Mistress has fallen gravely ill and has been sent to the countryside to recuperate? I hope she is well?"

"If her constitution is poor, then we at the Wang family shall come to your household tomorrow to present the betrothal gifts. And let us set the wedding date for the twenty-sixth of the third month. Your Ladyship, does that suit?"

Choosing a wedding date ought to involve consulting an astrologer for an auspicious day. Wang Furen's casual arbitrariness gave Fifth Young Miss not even a scrap of face.

The Old Matriarch coughed rapidly and raised her head, fixing Wang Furen with sunken, grim eyes. "Surely we should at least consult an auspicious day?"

Wang Furen spread her hands helplessly. "Is it not because the time is so pressing?"

The Old Matriarch's body swayed.

Pressing indeed — but only because your Wang family spent all this time dragging your feet.

And the implication of her words was plain: she was saying Tong Shi was going to die, so the wedding should happen sooner — and yet "sooner" meant the twenty-sixth of the third month, which was the very same day as Sixth Young Miss's wedding!

Was this sooner?

This was pure deliberate provocation!

"Lady Wang, our Qin family's propriety is strict: elder before younger — Fifth Young Miss must absolutely be married before Sixth Young Miss!" The Old Matriarch's composure finally cracked. Her voice came out shrill and broken.

Wang Furen barely suppressed a laugh.

She wondered whether this old woman felt any shame saying that.

The Qin family had always been run by her own biological son, the Second Master — what sort of proper "elder before younger" order was that?

"Ah? I actually think that by the Qin family's own precedents, Fifth Young Miss being married slightly after Sixth Young Miss would also be acceptable."

"You see — Second Master Qin holds higher office than First Master Qin, and rules the household. Sixth Young Miss is marrying Lord Jiang, while Fifth Young Miss is marrying our Mian'er, who is a provincial examination graduate..."

The Old Matriarch was livid!

Where was the dignity of a great lady in any of this?

They say one should never strike a person in the face. And yet here was the wife of the Directorate of Education's Chancellor — behaving worse than a woman from the lowest household.

Was this supposed to be in-laws? This was practically becoming enemies.

Wang Furen paid no mind to her, and said with cheerful blitheness: "Let's not speak of these idle matters. Since your household's Second Mistress is doing all right at the countryside estate, let us settle on a good day — the fourth month. The twelfth day of the fourth month is an auspicious day for weddings!"

"Absolutely not!" The Old Matriarch's eyes nearly popped out of her head. She couldn't make a scene in the Wang family's household — she was terrified that Wang Furen was deliberately provoking her to blow up, and would then use that as pretext to cancel the engagement entirely.

The Qin family could not afford any more scandal.

Fifth Young Miss could not afford to lose the Wang family match.

If this Wang family engagement also fell through, no notable and powerful family in the capital would have any regard left for Fifth Young Miss.

The Old Matriarch closed her eyes, exhaled a long breath, and forced herself to say: "As Lady Wang wishes. The betrothal gifts according to your Wang family's custom, and the wedding date set for the twenty-sixth of the third month — the same day as our Sixth Young Miss."

Since the Wang family wanted to be so shameless, they could not blame her for giving back what she received.

The Old Matriarch returned to the Qin household seething with hatred.

Fifth Young Miss had been waiting in the Old Matriarch's Shouhe Hall for a long while.

Seeing the Old Matriarch being helped in, she smiled sweetly and went to welcome her. "Grandmother, you're back?"

The Old Matriarch had just suffered an extraordinary humiliation at the Wang family and seeing her granddaughter still smiling so brightly, couldn't hold back — she raised her hand and slapped Fifth Young Miss across the face.

She used her full strength. A pampered daughter of the inner chambers had no resistance. The blow sent Fifth Young Miss crashing to the floor.

"Grandmother?" Fifth Young Miss pressed her burning right cheek and looked at the Old Matriarch in disbelief.

This was, after all, her most beloved granddaughter.

"My darling girl — why is your fate so bitter!" The Old Matriarch's fury and resentment eased considerably at the sight of the poor little figure before her, and she grew calmer.

Fortunately, according to Fifth Young Miss's dream visions, the second Wang son would rise to great prominence in the future...

No hurry, no hurry. At that time, Fifth Young Miss would truly soar to the treetops and become a phoenix. She just had to endure the Wang family one more time!

Fifth Young Miss was both stunned by the Old Matriarch's behavior and terrified her face was ruined, and couldn't help but burst into grievous tears.

The Old Matriarch had people help her up, and then there was washing and applying medicine.

After all the commotion, the Old Matriarch told Fifth Young Miss about her experience at the Wang family.

When Fifth Young Miss heard that the Wang family's betrothal sum for her was a mere five hundred taels and the gifts a pitiful ten trays, everything went dark before her eyes and her ears rang — she couldn't take in another word of what the Old Matriarch said after that.

Why?

She thought back to her previous life: even then, when the Wang family had sent betrothal gifts for Sixth Young Miss, it had been eight hundred taels!

Why was she getting so much less?

In what way was she inferior to Sixth Young Miss?

"Fifth Young Miss? Fifth Young Miss!" The Old Matriarch was frightened by Fifth Young Miss's glazed stare and called out to her urgently.

"You mustn't think such things! When you first took a liking to that Wang Second Son, you already knew his family's resources were limited — you couldn't have been hoping for a large betrothal sum and gifts from them..."

Fifth Young Miss finally came to herself.

"But... but Grandmother, that doesn't mean it can be this little! The Wang family treating me like this is giving our Qin family no face at all!" Fifth Young Miss's face was streaked with tears, her hands clenched white.

How could the Old Matriarch not understand this — but helplessly said: "Everything just piled on at once, and your father and eldest brother have both been reprimanded by the Emperor, and your mother is in such a state..."

"It has always been easy to add flowers to silk brocade, but rare to send charcoal in the snow. The Wang family has their own calculations — that's only to be expected. Since you've set your heart on that Wang Second Son, regardless of all else, just get smoothly through the wedding and be the Wang family's Second Young Mistress — that is what matters!"

"He hasn't made his name yet. Support him as he rises. His family's resources are limited — support him financially. In the future, even if his feelings should wander, he won't dare openly mistreat you as his principal wife..."

Even with her head cleared somewhat, the Old Matriarch managed to comfort her granddaughter sensibly.

She was, after all, the matriarch who had dominated the Qin household's inner court for decades — her wisdom for surviving the back courtyard was not to be underestimated.

Fifth Young Miss gradually calmed down.

A cold, calculating gleam flashed through her beautiful eyes.

Blame Sixth Young Miss for all of it!

If not for her — Jiang Jiuxiao getting involved and even interfering in Seventh Young Miss's affair, leading to her mother being sent to the countryside — how could she have suffered such a great humiliation from the Wang family?

True to her word, Wang Furen the very next day sent people to present the betrothal gifts to Fifth Young Miss.

Qin Mingjie, hearing from the Old Matriarch about the Wang family's attitude, was also full of furious indignation — but he valued his reputation too much to think of standing up for his daughter.

He also understood that given his current situation, Master Wang might not even be willing to see him. They had to avoid the appearance of any connection.

Why subject himself to further humiliation?

He later also persuaded the Old Matriarch, saying the Wang family had always been known for their frugality and simplicity — even the Emperor had praised them for it. The Wang family always followed their own custom when it came to their sons' brides, and apart from the Third Branch's eldest son who was lucky enough to marry a pampered last daughter of a noble house and received a betrothal sum of one thousand taels, there had never been a deviation.

The Wang family had done nothing wrong. Even outsiders would not dare criticize them openly.

If the Qin family went to quarrel with the Wang family over it and word got out, only the Qin family would look foolish.

The Old Matriarch eventually saw the logic too — it was exactly this reasoning that gave Wang Furen the nerve to do it in the first place.

But the bitterness in her heart was immense, and the hatred ran deep.

The Wang family presented Fifth Young Miss's betrothal gifts hastily, with little fanfare. The Qin family was also ashamed and didn't make a big occasion of it — just inviting close family from a few branches for a few tables of food, and calling it done.

This was worlds apart from Fifth Young Miss's long-dreamed-of grand celebration.

She was the most beautiful and most accomplished girl of all the Qin cousins of her generation — her embroidery, household management were all top-notch.

Yet the betrothal her husband's family sent her was the most paltry among all her sisters and cousins. Setting aside any comparison with Sixth Young Miss's betrothal at all.

Even having lived two lives, she could not absorb this blow.

After Fifth Young Miss received the Wang family's betrothal, the Qin family women began sending trousseau gifts one by one.

Fifth Young Miss had assumed that with her eldest sister-in-law Sun Shi's lavish dowry, Sun Shi would send her trousseau gifts worth at least three thousand taels.

To her shock, Sun Shi had only sent over a complete set of solid-gold jewelry, one jade bracelet of decent quality, and five hundred taels in cash — altogether worth barely over a thousand taels.

After receiving Sun Shi's trousseau gift, her face went dark.

With Sun Shi's enormous financial resources, she was this stingy toward her own legitimate sister-in-law!

Her senior maid Xixin leaned close to her ear and whispered: "The person we planted in the Third Young Mistress's courtyard has reported that the Third Young Mistress has found out about the matter of the little mistress Huazhi whom the Third Young Master keeps outside..."

Fifth Young Miss's eyes rolled slowly. She furrowed her delicate brows. "How could that be? Eldest Brother always acts with meticulous care. How could a matter like this get to Sun Shi's ears?"

Xixin thought for a moment, then said: "It's said that the Third Young Mistress went to the West Courtyard a few days ago, said to visit the Seventh Young Miss... but actually first went to see Sixth Young Miss."

"What are you implying?" Fifth Young Miss stared at Xixin. "Say it clearly!"

Xixin had no real basis, but her mother and the mother of Sixth Young Miss's maid Purple Perilla were bitter enemies. She had long despised Purple Perilla for growing more and more prominent alongside Sixth Young Miss.

She gritted her teeth and said quietly: "Young Miss, this servant has heard that Purple Perilla has been going out of the household frequently on errands for Sixth Young Miss ever since Sixth Young Miss got engaged to the Jiang family..."

"Is it possible that that Purple Perilla accidentally spotted our Third Young Master going to meet Huazhi at Yuanbao Lane?"

Fifth Young Miss knew Purple Perilla often went out.

Hearing Xixin say this, her beautiful eyes immediately turned vicious. "You mean... Eldest Brother keeping a mistress coming to light... is Sixth Young Miss's doing? She's the one who told Sun Shi?"

chapters.title: A Plot to Destroy Lord Jiang and Ruyin
chapters.content:
"So that's what Sixth Young Miss has been up to! I thought I had misjudged her — turns out she has such methods after all. Very well. Very, very well indeed." Fifth Young Miss gave a cold smile.

She had not originally planned to move against her so soon. But Sixth Young Miss had trampled her boundaries again and again.

Her gaze grew sinister, and her smile became more devastatingly beautiful.

Xixin found her young miss like this utterly chilling — nothing like the young miss she once knew. She felt a prick of fear.

Quickly she said: "This servant also doesn't know if it was Sixth Young Miss who did it... should we send more people to investigate?"

"No need!" Fifth Young Miss gave a cold laugh. "Whether it was her or not hardly matters now."

The reason she had not moved against Sixth Young Miss until now was that she had intended to keep her for the Jiang family for a while longer — she would need Jiang Sijiu to step in one and a half years from now and solve a great problem for the Qin family.

In her previous life, that was exactly what Jiang Sijiu had done.

But now, Sixth Young Miss was being recklessly bold beyond all sense.

Then she had only herself to blame for bringing a little blood on Fifth Young Miss's hands... It would not be Fifth Young Miss's sin but Sixth Young Miss's own doing.

First, she would deal with the source of that great future problem. That way, the problem itself wouldn't arise a year and a half from now.

In that case, the Jiang family would be of much less value to the Qin family.

She would then find a way to reconnect with Prince An, and use him to bring down Jiang Sijiu and the Jiang family behind him early.

She wanted to watch Sixth Young Miss on her knees begging her.

"Xixin, come here..." Fifth Young Miss beckoned Xixin close and gave her detailed instructions that went on for some time.

Xixin grew more and more alarmed as she listened.

"Young Miss... that person is the Second Master's beloved, and she's already with child..."

"Go quickly!" Fifth Young Miss's dark gaze pinned her.

Xixin dared not defy her, but her whole body was trembling.

Seeing Xixin so unable to handle it, Fifth Young Miss was privately contemptuous.

But her mother was at the countryside estate now, and she had few reliable people of her own.

Otherwise, having her mother personally deal with that seductive woman her father had been keeping for two months would have been the best solution.

Taking a deep breath, she spoke in a soft, gentle tone: "Actually, your young miss has had another frightening dream."

"A year and a half from now, Father will be impeached by a censor over the matter of that Rui Niang. In that incident, your eldest brother will suffer a caning that leaves him a cripple..."

Xixin let out a sharp cry.

Fifth Young Miss spoke in a soothing voice: "You are my person. Even if your young miss has blood on her hands, for your sake I don't want your eldest brother's life ruined."

After the initial terror, Xixin's eyes filled with gratitude, and she gradually grew resolute.

Just as Xixin was about to leave, another little maid came looking for her — one of her informants.

She was a household-born servant. Her mother was the Second Mistress's trusted confidante, and her father and two brothers were the Second Master's trusted men. Her family had deep roots in the Qin household's inner court.

When Xixin heard what her informant had come to tell her, she immediately turned back and reported it to Fifth Young Miss.

A cold light flickered in Fifth Young Miss's eyes.

"Xixin, continue doing what I asked." After Xixin left, Fifth Young Miss instructed Red Sleeve: "Attend to me while I change. I want to see Grandmother."

Within the next five years, the Jiangdong Sun family was critical to the Second Branch of the Qin family — and especially to her eldest brother as the future head of the family.

Sun Shi was proud and aloof. Even after bearing two sons for her eldest brother, she had never once looked at him with genuine respect.

Of course, when her eldest brother first pursued Sun Shi, it was partly for her looks, but more importantly for the centuries-old illustrious clan standing behind her.

Yet what did any of that matter?

Once her eldest brother inherited the Qin family headship, Sun Shi's value would diminish sharply.

Especially since Sun Shi raised both her nephews with their hearts turned entirely toward her as their mother and toward the Sun family — until her eldest brother was utterly disappointed in both his legitimate sons.

He used a scheme to strip the eldest, Zheng'er, of his position.

The second, Shen'er, was somewhat cleverer and escaped the same fate — but what did that matter? He simply hid behind the Jiangdong Sun family and never returned to the capital for the rest of his life. He permanently lost any foothold in the capital.

In the end, the Second Branch of the Qin family was inherited by Huazhi's eldest son.

For the Second Branch, Sun Shi was expendable — and so were the two legitimate sons she had borne.

It was just that right now, Sun Shi absolutely could not be allowed to pull away from her eldest brother.

In her previous life, the Huazhi affair had not been exposed this early.

Later, when Huazhi became pregnant and her eldest brother had a doctor examine her and found she was carrying twin boys — for the sake of those twin boys, her eldest brother told their mother.

Their mother told their grandmother. It was the grandmother who stepped in to give Huazhi an excellent property in a prime location in the capital, set her up comfortably, and helped her safely deliver.

Not until those twins were four years old did Sun Shi learn of the matter.

But by then, her eldest brother had already risen to power, and Sun Shi's playboy second brother had been cast off by Prince An. So Sun Shi had swallowed the existence of Huazhi and her children without letting her natal family interfere.

Now, Sun Shi had found out about Huazhi early — but Fifth Young Miss as a sister-in-law had no ability to intervene.

Naturally, she also didn't want to be drawn too soon into the conflict between her brother and Sun Shi.

So there was only one option: ask the Old Matriarch to go persuade Sun Shi. After all, in her previous life, it had been the Old Matriarch who handled the Huazhi matter.

...

In Shouhe Hall.

The Old Matriarch listened intently as Fifth Young Miss told her she had dreamed that morning again — a dream about the Second Branch's future. The Old Matriarch sat up straight to hear her out.

When she heard that the Second Branch would ultimately rise under the eldest grandson Rongzong's stewardship to become one of the capital's first-tier clans, the Old Matriarch couldn't help but let her aged eyes narrow into slits of satisfaction.

"Good! Wonderful..."

She had barely finished her applause when Fifth Young Miss cut her off.

As Fifth Young Miss spoke about Sun Shi's and her two sons' eventual fates, the Old Matriarch fell into a long, heavy silence.

"Sun Shi is indeed becoming more and more impossible. No matter how distinguished her birth, she married into our Qin family — she must take her husband as her heaven!"

"With her maintaining those airs every day, no wonder your eldest brother has gone looking elsewhere... it's only natural." The Old Matriarch saw nothing particularly wrong with her eldest grandson keeping a mistress outside.

On the contrary, she blamed Sun Shi for being unvirtuous — for being proud of giving the Qin family two grandsons, and still refusing to even arrange a bedchamber servant for her eldest grandson.

"I'm old, and I can't manage what the grandchildren get up to. But as long as I'm alive, I won't watch Sun Shi make trouble in the Qin household for the sake of one woman and give your eldest brother no peace!"

To her mind, it didn't matter which grandson inherited the Qin household she had schemed her whole life to build. Legitimate or concubine-born — whoever had the fortune and the ability to inherit, she didn't care.

Fifth Young Miss lowered her gaze and spoke gently: "Grandmother, but given Eldest Sister-in-law's temperament, I fear she won't easily yield. Her natal family is powerful, and Father and Eldest Brother are in such difficulty right now..."

She left the rest unsaid.

"Never mind — I'll go speak to her myself." The Old Matriarch didn't find it a particularly formidable task.

Fifth Young Miss said sweetly: "Grandmother, your granddaughter will accompany you. It's drizzling outside — let me take your arm."

The Old Matriarch was initially about to decline, but thought about it and nodded. "Very well. You're about to be married out soon too. These inner-court matters — you should start learning to handle them."

Fifth Young Miss obediently agreed.

"Yes, your eldest brother's situation is important, and your own situation is equally important." The Old Matriarch was very pleased that she had promptly come to report her dreams — and her recent dreams had already proven accurate.

The Old Matriarch valued this most beloved granddaughter more and more, and intended to add more to her dowry — it would also serve as a slap to the Wang family's face.

She patted Fifth Young Miss on the shoulder warmly. "I was actually just going to send someone to call you over, to talk with you more about your dowry. Let's settle that first, and then go see Sun Shi."

Fifth Young Miss paused briefly, then added a few more drops of suggestion: she hinted to the Old Matriarch that it might have been Qin Ruyin who told Sun Shi about the Huazhi affair.

After hearing this, the Old Matriarch's aged eyes seemed to hold poison. "That girl Sixth Young Miss — she's becoming more and more impossible to fathom. I don't know how she comes to know such things."

"Grandmother — she is now the wealthiest young miss in the Qin family. Money can make the devil turn the millstone." Fifth Young Miss smiled bitterly.

The Old Matriarch gave a cold snort. "Then let's see whether she has the fortune to keep paying the devil... If not for the Jiang family, your father would have dealt with her long ago!"

"Grandmother, in truth your granddaughter also dreamed of Sixth Young Miss — only the dream was quite unclear..." Fifth Young Miss looked at the Old Matriarch. "Let your granddaughter think on it more carefully. I will find a way to deal with her. But if the methods need to be rather forceful, can Grandmother forgive your granddaughter?"

The Old Matriarch's aged eyes flickered. "You have a way to deal with her — a way that won't trace back to our Qin Second Branch if it involves the Jiang family?"

Fifth Young Miss lowered her head, and the corners of her lips curved with cold menace.

The Jiang family?

The Jiang family had humiliated her so badly on account of Sixth Young Miss, making her the laughingstock of the capital's noble young ladies — did she have any intention of letting the Jiang family be well?

The only price was her father's mistress and the unborn child in her womb.

"Grandmother — although it's not certain that Father and Eldest Brother's trouble was caused by Jiang Jiuxiao, it is certain that Jiang Jiuxiao did nothing to help the Second Branch in that affair."

"On the contrary, he protected that troublesome Concubine Lu of the Third Branch."

"Is this truly the kind of ally our Second Branch needs?"

The Old Matriarch fell silent.

How could she not understand that?

"As things look now," Fifth Young Miss continued, "Jiang Jiuxiao and the Jiang family behind him do not recognize our Second Branch as in-laws — they only recognize the First Branch. In that case, Jiang Jiuxiao and the Jiang family have become our Second Branch's enemies."

The Old Matriarch's aged eyes shifted. "With your father and brother in such a state, our Second Branch is also not well-positioned to act..."

"Grandmother, don't worry." Fifth Young Miss reached out and clasped the Old Matriarch's dry hands. "Your granddaughter will find a way to help our Second Branch remove... the Jiang family as an obstacle."

"How certain are you?" The Old Matriarch's aged face grew grave. Since the Jiang family could not become the Second Branch's ally but had become an enemy, it was better to remove them early.

"Eighty percent." Fifth Young Miss said seriously.

Eighty percent was not a small figure.

The Old Matriarch considered.

"This matter can only wait until after your granddaughter is married off..." Fifth Young Miss met the Old Matriarch's gaze gently. "Only, it may require spending no small amount of money."

The Old Matriarch thought: if Fifth Young Miss waited until after her marriage to act, whatever the outcome, it would have nothing to do with the Second Branch — after all, she would be a married daughter by then.

That was very convenient indeed.

Money was certainly needed for great undertakings.

Fine — she'd add another three thousand taels to Fifth Young Miss's cash dowry.

"Don't act alone rashly. Before truly making any move, come back home to consult with your father and brother." The Old Matriarch cautioned her.

Fifth Young Miss softly agreed.

chapters.title: Remembering a Past Life; Ruyin Intends to Win Over Sun Shi
chapters.content:
In the First Branch of the Qin household.

Qin Ruyin sat cross-legged on the large daybed by the window.

Before her sat a small oblong huali-wood table. She was using a brush to sketch on a smooth, thin piece of paulownia wood, roughly half a chi square.

"Young Miss, I have important news to report!" Purple Perilla lifted the curtain and entered, her expression grave.

Purple Perilla had been out for several days running, primarily finding quality furs and the best carpenters for her young miss.

She had completed both tasks superbly, and the things her young miss intended to make were almost done.

The close daily attending duties had been passed to the four of Banjia's group, while she helped their young miss keep watch inside the household.

Outside the household, her brother kept watch.

She had just barely found a moment to breathe when she noticed something on the Fifth Young Miss's side.

"Young Miss, Fifth Young Miss's maid Xixin slipped out through the back gate. My brother's people have been quietly tailing her..."

Qin Ruyin paused her brush and looked up at Purple Perilla. "No one tipped them off?"

"Young Miss, rest easy — my brother's people are all very sharp." Purple Perilla replied earnestly.

"Keep watching. Be careful and discreet. Our Fifth Young Miss is no ordinary person — don't underestimate her. Report to me when there is news."

Purple Perilla agreed.

Then she reported further: "Young Miss, there is news from the Third Mistress's courtyard as well. It's said the Third Mistress has stopped speaking to Third Young Master again — he has already slept in the study two nights running..."

"Perfect timing — I'll go to Third Sister-in-law's courtyard later." Qin Ruyin said, and continued moving her brush. These were the last few strokes of this batch — the last of the illustrated literacy flashcards combining picture and calligraphy. A final few strokes, done in one flowing motion.

Purple Perilla responded while leaning over to look.

"Young Miss's calligraphy grows more refined every day!" Purple Perilla exclaimed.

The original Qin Ruyin had written a beautiful delicate-flower script. The style Qin Ruyin herself excelled at was regular script — upright and round.

Throughout the court and civil society of the Great Ying Dynasty, there was a great admiration for the increasingly neat and elegant regular script style.

For Qin Ruyin, this was a happy coincidence.

Her eldest brother's two little nieces and her second brother's niece had already begun learning characters and practicing copying. Her second brother's little nephew was still too young.

But Sun Shi's eldest boy was already reading the Analects, and the younger one was reading the Thousand Character Classic. The script they were practicing, however, was the official government hand.

The official government hand was the designated script for writing court memorials in the Great Ying Dynasty.

For children this young to already be practicing it — the father-and-son pair of the Second Branch truly had enormous ambitions.

Fortunately, she had a small aptitude for calligraphy from a young age, and the patience to sit still. Her director-mother at the orphanage, who loved calligraphy, had made her practice many styles.

Regular script she had practiced best. Clerical and seal scripts were also decent.

Later, after she entered university, she had grown fond of classical Chinese painting — especially fish, insects, birds, flowers, and landscapes.

And later still, she had grown fond of simple-line sketches, full of childlike charm.

Her previous life had been brief — only twenty-six years.

She had loved the arts, yet ended up in a large company's sales department.

She made enormous amounts of money and donated most of it to the orphanage that had raised her.

The director-mother fretted over her, telling her to stop donating, to keep more for herself, to stop working so hard and take better care of herself...

But she couldn't stop anymore, even when she was exhausted to the point of collapse.

Because she had very little time left.

She hoped, in the final moments of her life, to earn more money — to let the children in the orphanage live better, and to let the director-mother and all the beloved elders of the orphanage have access to better modern conveniences, so they would not have to work so hard...

In this life, thanks to the original Ruyin's body, she had so many loving blood relations. She loved them too.

And this body of hers was so much healthier than the one she'd had before.

This life, she would certainly live safely, smoothly, and happily — so that the director-mother and the orphanage elders who loved her could feel at ease.

Of course, her young death in that other world had involved no dramatic misfortune.

She simply carried a severe hereditary illness. She had known from a very young age that her life was on a countdown.

When the time ran out, she arrived in this world.

For her, any skill she had genuinely learned — even something for pure amusement — would eventually prove useful.

Like right now.

She had long planned to make simple-line illustrated flashcards for the small children in her eldest and second brothers' families.

Thin paulownia boards with simple sketched illustrations and neat regular-script characters — or catchy poems, idioms, and so on written on them.

Essentially the kind of illustrated literacy flashcards used for early childhood education in her original world.

As for the quality furs she'd had Purple Perilla buy — she was going to sketch out children's clothing designs, and have the most skilled seamstresses in the First Branch sew them into adorable, warm little garments.

These weren't only for Sun Shi's children — of course her brothers' and sisters' children would all get some too.

Making the literacy flashcards was something she did by hand. Sketching the clothing designs was also her own work. But actually sewing the clothes — she genuinely wasn't good at that.

After all, when she was small, the caregivers at the orphanage taught her simple needlework: sewing buttons, stitching a split seam — she could poke a finger and draw blood from all of it.

Conveniently, the original Ruyin's needlework was equally terrible — liberating her entirely from expectation.

And things done well always came quickly. In under three days she had drawn over a hundred simple illustrations — some with large characters in the white space, others with famous poems or idioms.

A few strokes: plum blossoms by a wall in spring, a lotus pond in summer, a crescent moon on an autumn night, winter's cold plum...

The farmer and the snake, Cao Chong weighing the elephant, Chang'e flying to the moon, Kuafu chasing the sun, Jingwei filling the sea...

The first batch had already been distributed to her legitimate nieces, and a portion sent to her second sister's home — the children all adored them.

Her eldest and second sisters-in-law were both grateful and had pressed many fine things upon her in return.

Her second sister had also sent a maid over with a complete set of solid-gold jewelry.

As for her eldest sister — she had no children yet — so Qin Ruyin had sent her three hand-copied reproductions of Master Li Youru's court-lady paintings, which had delighted her greatly.

In return, she sent over three volumes of the Miscellaneous Notes on Local Customs that the original Ruyin had never read.

The small garments made from quality white fox fur combined with other fabrics, crafted by the skilled seamstresses of the First Branch, came out adorable and fashionable.

In all of the capital, they were certainly one-of-a-kind.

When the finished pieces were brought out, the maids and matrons crowded around in a ring, exclaiming in amazement.

Eldest and second sisters-in-law were overjoyed. The little chubby-cheeked nieces and nephews nearly "came to blows" over the clothes.

In the end, it was Mother Li Shi who presided over distributing them.

The children, though young, were sensible — Grandmother personally divided them, so they all accepted.

The small garments for Sun Shi's two sons were also ready.

What Qin Ruyin was doing now was finishing these literacy-and-poetry flashcards. Once the ink dried, she would have them sorted and placed in a fine huali-wood box.

On this day, a light drizzle was falling. The air carried a damp chill.

Sun Shi's two sons were coughing again. Sun Shi had let them skip their lesson with the tutor and was personally accompanying them in practicing their calligraphy.

Qin Ruyin brought Banjia and Xinyi, along with several small maids carrying trays, and went over.

Sun Shi was quite surprised to see her.

After all, it was rare for Sixth Young Miss to step foot alone into the Second Branch's East Courtyard.

Sun Shi took her two little boys and came out to meet her.

The two little ones were still bundled in thick winter clothing, both quite thin.

After Qin Ruyin and Sun Shi had exchanged greetings, Sun Shi had the two children call out to Qin Ruyin.

"Sixth Auntie, good day!"

"Sixth Auntie, good day!"

The two little ones had been very well taught by Sun Shi. Even though they were not feeling their best, they still smiled brightly at Qin Ruyin.

Qin Ruyin's heart softened.

"Zheng'er, Shen'er — good day to you both. Sixth Auntie has brought you some small gifts. Let's see if you like them." She gave a small nod to Xinyi and Banjia.

The two of them gestured for the little maids to bring forward the trays.

Sun Shi's face had already filled with a smile.

But she said politely: "Sixth Sister coming to visit is already a great joy to me and the two boys — there was no need to bring anything."

Qin Ruyin smiled back: "It has been drizzling these past few days without clearing. I thought to make some small things for the little ones' early learning."

"I'd heard recently from my eldest and second sisters-in-law that Zheng'er and Shen'er have been coughing. I thought to have some warm, light, durable little garments made for them as well."

Sun Shi, who was always composed, felt her eyes well up at this.

She didn't speak — she personally walked over to look at what Qin Ruyin had prepared for her two boys.

When she saw the thin, smooth paulownia boards, each with a sketched scene and neat, rounded regular-script writing, Sun Shi's eyes lit up at once.

"The plum tree by the wall, a few branches bold, braving the cold, they bloom alone..." (quoting classical verse)

A few spare strokes had conjured the image of several red plum branches by a wall, dotted with blossoms opening defiantly in the snow.

The scene was vivid and full of feeling.

If even she as an adult who had read every classical text found herself unable to look away, how much more so would children.

"These... were all drawn and written by Sixth Sister herself?"

Qin Ruyin nodded with a smile. "My painting and calligraphy don't compare to Third Sister-in-law's. If Third Sister-in-law finds them of use, let the children play with them. If they're not of use..."

"Of use!" Sun Shi immediately grabbed both of Qin Ruyin's hands and looked at her earnestly. "Your painting technique I can't judge, but the artfulness and liveliness are evident... the calligraphy is beautiful too! Very much of use!"

"Only — what a trouble for you. With your own wedding so close, you still made time to do all this for the little monkeys..."

Qin Ruyin cut her off with a smile. "Third Sister-in-law mustn't say that. I have fewer than ten days left at home. To be able to do something for the children gives me joy."

Sun Shi could sense the sincerity with which she spoke of the children.

She had barely said the words when two excited cries rang out from the little ones.

"Big Brother, mine is 'a shepherd boy rides a yellow ox'!"

"Little Brother, mine is 'Hou Yi shoots the sun'!"

Both women turned to look at the two little boys.

"Sixth Sister — look how much your two nephews love them!" As she said it, tears streamed down her face. "I as their mother... haven't seen the two children this happy in so long..."

Qin Ruyin quickly produced a handkerchief to wipe her tears, and said softly: "Third Sister-in-law — the children are happy, so you should be happy too."

A mother crying in front of her children would frighten them.

Sun Shi came back to herself, gave Qin Ruyin a grateful glance, and used the handkerchief Qin Ruyin had handed her to quickly dry her tears.

She said with embarrassment: "What a thing to let Sixth Sister see."

Qin Ruyin shook her head. "Not at all. Third Sister-in-law's love for her children is sincere — I know that."

While they were speaking, the eldest boy Zheng'er started coughing.

Sun Shi hurried over to pat his back herself.

Qin Ruyin furrowed her brow. "Zheng'er's cough sounds quite serious. Has a physician been called?"

Mentioning this was hardly helpful.

At the question, Sun Shi's eyes went sharp with sudden fury.

Qin Rongzong was simply not human. He didn't deserve to be those children's father.

chapters.title: Ruyin's Gifts; Third Sister-in-Law Prepares to Strike Back
chapters.content:
He was keeping a mistress outside behind her back, and still had the nerve to be resentful when she confronted him. The boys' coughs were this severe, yet he wouldn't allow a physician to be called.

He was doing it deliberately — deliberately using the children to force her to yield to him, to preserve his male pride, to facilitate his shameless desire to have women on both sides.

A complete piece of trash.

Zheng'er, wanting to look at the other flashcards, wriggled free from his mother's arms.

Sun Shi came back to herself and handed the children over to their wet nurses and maids.

She pulled Qin Ruyin's hand and led her to sit on the large daybed by the window. "Sixth Sister, come sit and talk."

Qin Ruyin smiled and nodded.

Sun Shi's attitude toward her was considerably warmer than before.

The senior maids personally brought fragrant tea and pastries, eagerly encouraging her: "Sixth Young Miss, please have more — our Third Mistress is very glad you came!"

Qin Ruyin smiled and nodded.

Sun Shi personally lifted the teacup and handed it to Qin Ruyin. Then, her eyes reddening, she gave a soft, quiet sigh.

"Old trouble. Every time the weather turns cold, Zheng'er starts coughing. It's a minor complaint — just that the nighttime coughing keeps him from sleeping well."

"Zheng'er was not like this before his third birthday?" Qin Ruyin asked, then added: "I remember Zheng'er had quite a healthy constitution before the age of three."

Sun Shi nodded. "Yes, Zheng'er was indeed very healthy before the age of three."

She glanced at Shen'er. "Shen'er was also quite robust before three. Now... same as his eldest brother — when the cold sets in, the coughing starts, and on bad nights neither can sleep."

Sun Shi had no intention of concealing things in front of Qin Ruyin and directly let the hatred show in her eyes.

"Yet their father says little boys shouldn't be so pampered — calling for a physician every time there's a cough, afraid people will say the two boys have weak constitutions. This time... he simply won't allow it."

Qin Ruyin inwardly spat hard at Qin Rongzong.

All excuses!

She was certain it was purely because he found Sun Shi's cold war insufferable to his male ego, and was using the children to force Sun Shi to capitulate.

Trash!

Even if Qin Rongzong had no such ulterior motive — letting children of three and five years old tough out a cold cough without a physician was just cruel.

In her view, children of three to five years old needed sleep above all else.

Rising earlier than all the adult members of the household, suffering chronic sleep deprivation — how could their immune systems stay strong?

And with a weakened immune system, unable to tolerate either heat or cold — of course they'd fall ill.

Especially in winter, or in early spring with its treacherous reversals of temperature — that was the most wearing time of all.

Qin Rongzong wanted face, was self-righteous, and for his own selfish ends was willing to be this cruel to his own biological sons.

Looking at Sun Shi's expression, this had also thoroughly crossed the line for her as a mother.

"Third Sister-in-law — speaking of cold weather, I had the little garments I designed drawn for my nephews and nieces made up into actual clothing. I also brought several sets for Zheng'er and Shen'er each."

Sun Shi smiled again in thanks, thinking of how pitiable her two boys were.

The Sixth Auntie from the other branch thought so much of her own legitimate nephews and nieces, never forgetting them.

Even though Sixth Young Miss couldn't sew herself, she still went to the effort of sketching clothing designs and having them made.

And her two boys' legitimate Fifth Auntie? Her embroidery was superb — yet the purses she sent her two sons were sewn by the maids around her.

Given that, she as a sister-in-law had given that fifth young miss only a surface-level gift at trousseau time.

"Xinyi, go fetch a few of the small garments and let the two boys see if they like them. If they don't, I'll draw different designs when I have time and have those made too." Qin Ruyin instructed Xinyi.

Xinyi smiled and agreed.

Inwardly she thought: made according to the designs their young miss drew — even we grown-ups can't stop admiring them, so how much more so the two little boys.

"Sixth Sister, whatever you've brought them, they'll love it." Sun Shi said. "This is already wonderful!"

She could see the garments used excellent fur — this girl had truly put care into them.

She accepted Sixth Young Miss's goodwill.

"Ah! Is that a little bear ear?"

"This one... is this a little tiger head?"

Xinyi and two little maids shook out the small garments to show the children, and they heard the children's delighted cries of amazement.

Sun Shi looked over.

This style... complete with fluffy little bear-ear hoods and little tiger-head hoods.

She had never seen children's clothes made in such an endearing style...
