She didn't know that this design had been sketched by Qin Ruyin based on the children's vest style from her original world.

"The hoods are made of fur, the outer fabric is Shu brocade, and the inside is also warm fur — wrapped in the brocade." Qin Ruyin said smilingly. "So it's very warm indeed."

"The boys are still so young. In cold weather, wearing bulky padded coats makes it hard even to run around, let alone practice their calligraphy — that would be even more of a struggle."

Sun Shi nodded repeatedly. The delight in her bright, limpid eyes grew stronger.

She had initially not been particularly moved by mention of the small garments — but now they had truly reached her heart!

Qin Ruyin finished, then shifted her tone.

"But since both of them have been coughing every time the cold sets in since they turned three, I imagine it's no coincidence. I'm afraid just keeping warm isn't enough to solve the problem — they also need to sleep well."

Qin Ruyin added: "Sleep enough hours!"

"Third Sister-in-law has probably heard that I love reading miscellaneous books. I truly do read very widely. Once I came across a fragmentary volume called The Children's Classic. It said that children around three to five years of age need five to six hours of sleep to do well."

"Children between six and twelve also need at least four and a half hours."

Sun Shi's jaw tightened, and her expression darkened.

Whether or not what Sixth Young Miss said was entirely accurate, her two boys truly weren't getting enough sleep each day.

Let alone five or six hours — they couldn't even manage four and a half.

She had complained often before, but Qin Rongzong just said that he and his brothers had all grown up this way and each of them was healthy and strong.

The implication being that she was spoiling the children — their weak constitutions were her fault for coddling them.

At that point the two little boys came trotting over and asked their mother and Sixth Auntie whether they could put on the clothes she'd brought right away.

"Of course! Thank your Sixth Auntie for her thoughtfulness!" Sun Shi immediately beamed and nodded to the two boys' wet nurses.

The two little ones cheered, then with perfect manners turned to thank Qin Ruyin.

After trying them on, they refused to take them off.

Maids, matrons, and wet nurses all crowded around the two adorably dressed little boys and praised them — every word coming from the heart.

Zheng'er and Shen'er, though young, were children with the purest and most sensitive instincts.

They could tell whether a compliment was sincere or false, or whether the person truly pitied them.

Most of the praise they had received from those around them and their mother's attendants had, in truth, been laced with pity.

But this time was different.

They were genuinely happy — truly, genuinely happy.

The children's lifted spirits made them much livelier — and afterward, their coughing was heard far less.

Sun Shi said nothing, but her heart was deeply grateful to Qin Ruyin.

The servants in Sun Shi's courtyard, in fact, showed more respect to Sixth Young Miss of the First Branch than they showed to their own Fifth Young Miss of the Second Branch.

After the children returned to playing with their flashcards, Qin Ruyin spoke to Sun Shi again: "Third Sister-in-law, for children to be truly well, their eating also needs to be well managed."

Sun Shi nodded and said warmly: "They eat very well — all the finest ingredients from my own estate: vegetables, poultry, livestock, even game..."

"That's excellent, but I don't mean that." Qin Ruyin shook her head.

"I know our household has an unspoken custom — whoever is ill is to be starved. Something about the stomach being too weak when ill to digest properly... but one has to look at the situation."

"A child crying from hunger — and you still don't feed him — where will he find the strength to fight off illness?"

Sun Shi froze.

Actually, the Sun family had this same custom. She herself as a child had experienced crying from hunger while ill, and those around her not daring to let her eat.

Qin Ruyin continued: "My mother always said — when any of the six of us fell ill as children, as long as we cried out that we were hungry, she would feed us."

"If it was hard to digest, she'd cook the congee very soft, then add fresh river shrimp, shredded chicken, or minced fish or pork or lamb into a clay pot and simmer it with the congee... as long as they could eat, within a few days they'd be jumping and bouncing again."

Sun Shi understood that she was genuinely thinking of the children. Just having that intention was already deeply appreciated.

She also thought of how the First Branch's master and mistress, in such difficult circumstances, had raised all six children — and raised them so well.

The mistress of the First Branch, having raised six of her own so well — her experience could not be lacking.

For the sake of her two sons, she would try it!

Sun Shi clasped Qin Ruyin's hands and thanked her sincerely. Then she bit her lip and looked at Qin Ruyin, her expression complicated with guilt. "Sixth Sister is kind-hearted. I was wrong before — I never thought Sixth Sister would care for Zheng'er and Shen'er so much..."

Qin Ruyin shook her head. "Regardless of what goes on between the adults — Third Sister-in-law has done nothing wrong. And children are innocent. Besides, Zheng'er and Shen'er are my nephews."

Of course, Qin Ruyin said as much — but whether children were truly innocent depended on what their parents had done.

The reason she could treat Sun Shi and Sun Shi's children differently was, most importantly, that Sun Shi had never done anything excessive to the First Branch.

"However..." Qin Ruyin's tone turned firm. "When the children are unwell, a physician should be called! Third Brother is being foolish — Third Sister-in-law, don't listen to him. He hasn't endured pregnancy and childbirth himself. The pain isn't in his body, so he speaks lightly."

Sun Shi tightened her grip on Qin Ruyin's hand and lowered her voice: "Yes, I haven't listened to him. My second and third brothers on my mother's side both have settled households in the capital — I've already sent word to my second and third sisters-in-law..."

Although these words seemed unremarkable on the surface, Qin Ruyin understood perfectly: Sun Shi had no intention of continuing to endure Qin Rongzong.

There was no need for her to endure any longer.

Sun Shi also understood that Qin Rongzong's affair with his outside mistress was likely unknown to the First Branch side — and Sixth Young Miss definitely didn't know.

She had no wish to tell Sixth Young Miss — after all, she was still an unmarried young miss.

Qin Ruyin thought: the "seeds" she had intended to plant had already been sown. It was time to take her leave.

Then she heard the maid outside crying in near-panic.

"Grand Matriarch — Fifth Young Miss — please let this servant go in and announce to the Third Young Mistress..."

The curtain of Sun Shi's warm chamber was lifted without courtesy by the Old Matriarch's people.

chapters.title: Wishing Fifth Sister Barren Yet Surrounded by Grandchildren
chapters.content:
Sun Shi and Qin Ruyin exchanged a glance, and both women rose to welcome the guests.

"Have I come at a bad time, interrupting you two having your little secret conversation?" The Old Matriarch's aged eyes swept over Qin Ruyin's face, then over Sun Shi's — probing and opaque.

Earlier, when Fifth Young Miss said the Huazhi affair had been revealed to Sun Shi by Sixth Young Miss, she had still had some doubts.

But seeing these two sisters-in-law sitting together so warmly, she no longer had anything to doubt.

Sun Shi's eyes dimmed. She immediately rose and stepped in front of Qin Ruyin, and she was the first to bow in greeting to the Old Matriarch.

Qin Ruyin conveniently followed her lead and gave a perfunctory curtsy — inwardly rolling her eyes enormously.

"Grandmother was joking. Sixth Sister brought some lovely early-education things she made for her own sister-in-laws' children, and didn't forget Zheng'er and Shen'er either. Daughter-in-law was just thanking her."

These words put the empty-handed grandmother and granddaughter in rather an awkward position.

Having said this, Sun Shi paid no mind to either of their expressions and simply invited the Old Matriarch to take the seat of honor.

She didn't address Fifth Young Miss at all, leaving her own senior maid to attend to her.

Once the Old Matriarch was seated, Sun Shi smiled at Qin Ruyin. "Sixth Sister's regular script is truly superb, and your paintings are beautiful. Would you do your sister-in-law the favor of going to the side chamber to keep Zheng'er and Shen'er company for a while?"

Qin Ruyin understood the kindness behind the offer.

By not letting her leave right away, Sun Shi was giving her face — showing she valued Qin Ruyin, and demonstrating that Sun Shi trusted her. That way, no one in the household would spread word that Qin Ruyin had come to curry favor with the Second Branch's Third Mistress.

Seeing Sun Shi's arrangement, both the Old Matriarch and Fifth Young Miss were deeply displeased.

Watching Qin Ruyin take the two little boys by the hand and lead them to the adjacent sitting room, the Old Matriarch gave Sun Shi a pointed look:

"Child, you're already a mother of two — haven't you seen through those things of love and sentiment yet?"

"How can you let a trivial little plaything from outside cause such trouble between you and Rongzong? This old woman has heard that Rongzong has already slept in the study two nights... can't you bring yourself to give a little ground?"

The Old Matriarch was pulling rank as an elder in Sun Shi's presence.

"Or are you determined to push your own husband into the arms of those other warblers and swallows, and only come to regret it then?"

Sun Shi looked at the Old Matriarch and gave a calm, quiet smile.

"Grandmother was joking. Your daughter-in-law has nothing to regret."

The Old Matriarch's salt-and-pepper brows drew tightly together.

She took a deep breath, disapproval plain in her gaze. "You're still showing off in front of your grandmother? A wife yielding to her husband is not humiliating — on the contrary, it is charm, something that invites a man's fond indulgence..."

"Grandmother, whoever wants that kind of charm is welcome to it. Your daughter-in-law is not interested." Sun Shi remained gentle and soft-spoken, but the words were firm.

The Old Matriarch found her impenetrable — needle couldn't prick through, water couldn't splash in. She grew impatient.

She shifted topics.

"This old woman understands. It was wrong of Rongzong to secretly keep that woman Huazhi in Yuanbao Lane behind your back. He wronged you. But since it has already happened, you can..."

Sun Shi smiled and interrupted her for the first time. "You worry too much, Grandmother. Your daughter-in-law is not wronged, and has nothing to be wronged about."

Fifth Young Miss, seeing Sun Shi constantly deflecting with the Old Matriarch, speaking with veiled sarcasm, while the Old Matriarch — as grandmother-in-law — had things she couldn't say too harshly, grew anxious.

"Third Sister-in-law — honestly, men taking multiple wives and concubines is simply nothing! You truly don't need to make such a scene with Third Brother. The one who suffers is you, and those two nephews of mine are the ones who are hurt."

"Women endure all the pain of pregnancy and childbirth to bring children into the world. Since ancient times, who has not prized many children and many blessings? Just think of those women as vessels to bear more children for Third Brother. In any case, all children born will be recorded under your name and call you mother. When it comes time to be filial, they'll be filial to you, their official mother — what is wrong with that?"

Fifth Young Miss had lived two lives, totaling nearly a hundred years — and even with all her efforts at concealment, in unguarded moments her words carried the stiff cadence of age beyond her years.

Sun Shi stared at this legitimate sister-in-law with cold, appraising eyes.

When Fifth Young Miss's voice grew louder, Qin Ruyin had the wet nurses carry Zheng'er and Shen'er back to their own rooms.

She truly could not listen any further.

She rose, smoothed her clothes, and walked over.

"Hearing what Fifth Sister just said to Third Sister-in-law — should I wish Fifth Sister barren and yet surrounded by grandchildren? Is that the meaning?"

"Then after Fifth Sister marries Fifth Brother-in-law, pile on the concubines and bed-warmers as fast as possible, and let Fifth Brother-in-law be surrounded by sons and grandchildren early. When Fifth Brother-in-law has grandchildren, Fifth Sister has grandchildren too!"

The moment she spoke, everyone looked at her — even the composed and restrained Sun Shi couldn't hold it together anymore. Her upturned lips refused to be pressed down.

She had to admit: ever since she learned that Qin Rongzong had secretly kept a mistress outside, she had been wondering whether the people of his household knew — and what their reaction was.

Now she knew.

And her heart went even colder.

Then she heard the Sixth Young Miss from the other branch stand up and fiercely counter Fifth Young Miss's words on her behalf — and her heart slowly began to warm again.

"Sixth Young Miss!" The Old Matriarch was extremely sensitive to the phrase "barren yet surrounded by grandchildren," and seeing Qin Ruyin make things this blunt and ugly, her gaze cut at Qin Ruyin like a blade. "How can you curse your Fifth Sister?"

Fifth Young Miss came to her senses and trembled all over. In her previous life she had married twice — and the second time had indeed been barren yet surrounded by grandchildren...

That wretched Sixth Young Miss!

Seeing the Old Matriarch accuse her of cursing Fifth Young Miss, Qin Ruyin immediately said with great seriousness: "Grand Matriarch cannot use your seniority to smear me with false accusations! I cursed no one whatsoever!"

"What I said was: Fifth Sister herself just advised Third Sister-in-law to help Third Brother take in more concubines, to fill his household with children — which would spare Third Sister-in-law from further child-bearing hardship."

"By the same logic — Fifth Sister thinks so highly of this for Third Sister-in-law, surely she must wish the same for herself. Otherwise, how could she have the face to advise it?"

The Old Matriarch's head rang and went blank. Several moments passed before her vision cleared.

"I have said my piece." Qin Ruyin had already given her wide sleeve a shake, and made a half-bow toward Sun Shi. "Third Sister-in-law, I've overstayed my welcome. I should take my leave."

Sun Shi immediately rose and returned the half-bow with genuine warmth. "Thank you, Sixth Sister, for thinking of Zheng'er and Shen'er — Third Sister-in-law will always hold that in her heart!"

She then instructed her wet nurse: "Mama, please see Sixth Sister back to the West Courtyard."

Seeing Sun Shi give Qin Ruyin such regard, both the Old Matriarch and Fifth Young Miss were burning with resentment.

Both were privately furious at Sun Shi for siding with outsiders over family.

But for now, the grandmother and granddaughter needed Sun Shi to willingly bring Qin Rongzong's outside mistress back into the household — so they had to keep pressing Sun Shi.

Sun Shi continued to sidestep every attempt. The Old Matriarch grew impatient.

She made a hard threat: "If you won't handle this troublesome matter yourself, this old woman will have no choice but to handle it for you. Only — don't come crying to me later if word gets out."

Sun Shi still answered lightly: "No regrets. Grandmother, do as you see fit."

The Old Matriarch had made herself ridiculous and left in barely-suppressed irritation.

Fifth Young Miss had to swallow her own deep resentment and rise to support the Old Matriarch as she left.

But Sun Shi called out to stop her. "Fifth Sister, wait a moment."

Fifth Young Miss turned back, her face no longer holding any trace of soft vulnerability. She said flatly: "Third Sister-in-law, what else is there?"

"When did Fifth Sister come to know about your Third Brother keeping a mistress outside?" Sun Shi asked.

chapters.title: By a Twist of Fate, a Trafficked Girl Is Saved
chapters.content:
Fifth Young Miss's heart lurched.

She hadn't expected Sun Shi to be this sharp-eyed — to have noticed this.

But she couldn't possibly admit that she had known for a long time about her eldest brother keeping an outside mistress.

Sun Shi didn't miss the expression on her face. She laughed coldly inwardly.

The Old Matriarch also realized something, and quickly turned back. Her sunken, heavy gaze fixed on Sun Shi: "It was this old woman who told Fifth Young Miss. Are you blaming me?"

"Daughter-in-law would not dare." Sun Shi lowered her head, concealing the sharp, cold light in her eyes.

Even Fifth Young Miss — her little sister-in-law — knew about Qin Rongzong keeping an outside mistress. Only she, the wife, had been kept in the dark.

Treated as a fool to be played with.

Since the Qin Second Branch wanted to be the aggressor, they couldn't blame her for retaliating.

After the grandmother and granddaughter left, Sun Shi's wet nurse immediately looked at her with heartbreak.

"Young Miss, the Grand Matriarch knows about that matter, and even the Fifth Young Miss — a girl of the inner chambers — knows too. But they all hid it from you. This is truly bullying beyond endurance..."

Sun Shi turned and patted the wet nurse. She said quietly: "Never mind. Mama, attend to me with pen and ink — I will write three letters myself. Two to Jiangdong, one to Second Brother. For the letter to Second Brother, Mama, please deliver it personally."

The wet nurse understood why her young miss was writing to the Sun family home in Jiangdong.

Her heart ached more seeing Sun Shi.

"Young Miss, have you truly made up your mind?"

Sun Shi nodded firmly. "Yes, I have made up my mind."

Then she gave a self-mocking shake of her head. "I should have listened to my eldest brother long ago. If I had decided then, it would have been far better than receiving this kind of humiliation today."

"If not for Sixth Young Miss being here today to stand up for me, I would truly have become a laughingstock."

The wet nurse was also filled with great fury.

The Qin family broke their word, and then banded together to deceive her young miss.

At least there was still Sixth Young Miss among the Qin family, standing on her young miss's side, warming her heart.

Her young miss was so precious — raised on books since girlhood. If she had not been a woman and had joined the men in taking the examinations, she could have passed the jinshi degree at minimum.

Yet the final outcome was marriage into the Qin Second Branch... the Qin Second Branch was at the very least rooted three generations forward!

But they didn't cherish it.

Then let it be their Second Branch's own misfortune.

Only...

The wet nurse thought for a moment, and softly asked: "Young Miss — the two young masters..."

"I'm taking them with me!" Sun Shi didn't even hesitate. The words came out at once.

"I'm afraid the Qin family won't let the children go..." The wet nurse's heart ached — her young miss had borne the Qin family two legitimate sons. Daughters would have been easier to take.

Sun Shi gave a cold snort. "They have no say in the matter."

Could the Qin Second Branch in its current condition afford a fight to the death with her?

The wet nurse knew more than most. Seeing her young miss so resolute, she understood that her young miss had already prepared for the worst.

She said: "Young Miss, no matter what decision you make, this old servant will follow you, and stay by your side! Only — since it must be done, take advantage of this moment when the Qin Second Branch is in the thick of their troubles... and move quickly!"

Sun Shi shook her head. "The original plan was naturally to move as fast as possible. But with this business coming to a head, it would reflect badly on the Qin family's young ladies. The others I don't care about — but I cannot leave Sixth Young Miss without consideration..."

She patted the wet nurse's hand reassuringly and continued: "The Qin Second Branch shows the signs of a family in decline. But Zheng'er and Shen'er are, after all, descendants of the Qin family. As their mother, if I can't give them a good father, I must at least find them a good paternal clan to rely on... I trust Sixth Young Miss."

The wet nurse said in puzzlement: "Young Miss, didn't you say you want to take the two young masters away?"

Sun Shi nodded. "Taking them to raise them myself is one thing. Finding them a reliable paternal clan is another. Mama doesn't need to worry about this — I've thought it all through."

Sun Shi finished writing her letters and quickly had them sent.

Qin Ruyin, having returned to her own courtyard a short while before, received from Sun Shi a gift sent by Sun Shi's senior maid Hongdou: eight bolts of fine Shu brocade and a complete set of ruby jewelry.

That set of rubies — perfectly colored, flawlessly clear — was worth at minimum a thousand taels.

When Qin Ruyin was about to decline it, Sun Shi's senior maid Hongdou said with great gravity: "Sixth Young Miss, my young miss asked this servant to tell you — this is a thank-you gift. It is to thank you for the sincere words you spoke to her."

Qin Ruyin noticed acutely that Sun Shi's maid Hongdou referred to her mistress as "my young miss" — not "the Third Young Mistress," as the servants around Sun Shi had always said before.

Since it was so, Qin Ruyin no longer declined and accepted Sun Shi's precious "thank-you gift."

She thought for a moment, then drawing on memories from her past life, she wrote down three dietary remedy recipes for strengthening young children's digestive health — in the current parlance, they would be called "medicinal food prescriptions."

All three were foods she had eaten often in the orphanage in her past life and that had genuinely strengthened her constitution.

After handing the three prescriptions to Hongdou, she especially noted: "Tell Third Sister-in-law from me — before using any of these three medicinal food prescriptions on Zheng'er and Shen'er, she must have them reviewed by a physician she trusts."

She had no wish to owe Sun Shi a debt of gratitude — but she also didn't want these three prescriptions to cause any trouble.

Food as therapy, medicinal food — it was something people ate. Having Sun Shi take it to her own trusted physician first would put Sun Shi's mind at ease as well.

Hongdou took her leave with many thanks.

She was one of Sun Shi's most capable people and understood perfectly: to her young miss, this "return gift" was worth far more than the precious jewelry and fabrics she had brought.

Inwardly she praised the girl: Sixth Young Miss is exactly as her young miss said — someone who treats others with sincerity. She understands the value of true heart given for true heart.

"Young Miss! How could you just hand over all the medicinal prescriptions to the Third Young Mistress!" Purple Perilla saw Hongdou leave and immediately panicked.

Qin Ruyin stared at her in surprise.

Purple Perilla had been growing more and more bold lately. She had already been quietly displeased when her young miss personally labored to make pictures and calligraphy cards and send garments to the Second Branch's children.

Now her young miss had on impulse given away three dietary prescriptions as a return gift, and this maid was up in arms again?

"Young Miss! Medicinal food prescriptions are tremendous things! If I had known you had them, you should have written them down and put them in the dowry list... they could be passed to our own future young misses as dowry one day!"

"And now you just gave them away so generously to someone else..." Purple Perilla's eyes were nearly red with distress.

She only knew that her young miss loved reading miscellaneous books — she had no idea her young miss had actually found truly valuable things in those books!

If she had known earlier, she would have told her young miss long ago to keep them hidden and not give them to others.

Was this what it had come to?

Qin Ruyin found the whole thing a little funny and a little touching.

Those dietary remedies — she had dozens more stored in her head.

In her past life, growing up in an orphanage, the vast majority of the children there had physical disabilities or various illnesses. The orphanage had limited funds — not every little angel had the opportunity for hospital treatment, and many difficult conditions even major hospitals could do nothing about.

To care for these children with various impairments, the director-mother and the elders of the orphanage had spent untold energy finding and spreading word of all manner of remedies...

She had also helped care for the younger children in her spare time from studying, and through sheer accumulated experience had become something like a half-doctor.

Only — treating illness was a very serious matter. She only had broad knowledge; she was not a physician.

Leave professional matters to professionals.

Qin Ruyin gave Purple Perilla a smiling glance.

"You little creature — worrying needlessly. They're just trivial little trifles."

Someone of Sun Shi's birth and state of mind — you could send her all the gold and silver in the world and it wouldn't move her. She might even look down on you for it.

But put care into her children — even the most worthless trinket, as long as her children loved it, she would look on you more highly.

Qin Ruyin wasn't trying to flatter Sun Shi. What she wanted was to temporarily bring Sun Shi over to her side.

She wanted Sun Shi and the Sun family behind her to make a decisive break with the Second Branch.

Ideally, this could be done before she was married out.

That would eliminate a great deal of unnecessary trouble.

And also remove an enormous threat from the First Branch.

Beyond that — she simply felt indignant on Sun Shi's behalf.

A woman as accomplished as Sun Shi — why should she be exploited by the Qin family as their support pillar?

Her two sons growing weaker and weaker, in an era with high child mortality, her two well-mannered, well-raised little boys — what fault was it of theirs?

"Don't worry. When Purple Perilla gets married one day, your young miss will also give three of these prescriptions... no! Six, ten — all fine!" she added, teasing her.

"Young Miss..." Purple Perilla went red to the tips of her ears. Banjia and the others nearby covered their mouths to stifle their laughter.

"You still laugh? I'll have the young miss marry all of you off, and just keep me with her always..."

"That won't do!"

"Exactly! If anyone's being married off first, it's Purple Perilla..."

The girls laughed and teased in a cheerful tangle.

Until a little maid outside urgently came looking for Purple Perilla, saying there was something important to report.

...

"Young Miss, my brother sent word!" Purple Perilla was back in less than a quarter of an hour, the shock and gossip in her eyes completely undisguised.

"Fifth Young Miss sent her maid Xixin to a place called Heishanmen. That Heishanmen takes in many down-and-out wanderers from the jianghu..."

Qin Ruyin understood — that Heishanmen was likely a place in the capital that dealt in grey or black dealings.

"So our Fifth Young Miss has finally made her move?" Qin Ruyin's brow furrowed slightly. "Is she buying assassins to come after me?"

"No, no!" Purple Perilla also looked puzzled. "My brother said — it's targeting a young woman. The young woman is a trafficked beauty from the south... but she has already been saved by my brother's people."

"Your brother and his people — are they all right?" Qin Ruyin immediately asked.

"Young Miss, don't worry — my brother and his people are fine. Only two people were wounded with knife injuries."

Qin Ruyin told Purple Perilla to send extra silver — both as a reward and to help the injured men recover.

Purple Perilla hesitated briefly, then told her young miss: "Young Miss — my brother also said that today he and his people nearly lost their lives. Someone saved them."

Qin Ruyin gave a soft gasp of alarm. "That dangerous?"

So the people Fifth Young Miss had sent through Xixin were assassins!

What had that young woman done to provoke Fifth Young Miss — for her to send hired killers? A double death, no less?

"Young Miss, don't worry. The two people who saved my brother said his group ran into those killers purely by accident. They've handled it — my brother and his people will be safe."

Purple Perilla had been frightened herself, but hearing that those who saved her brother were people from the side of Li Yi, Lord Jiang's personal attendant — she was no longer worried.

"We should properly thank the people who saved your brother." Qin Ruyin's words barely landed before Purple Perilla smilingly replied: "They are people of Li Yi, Lord Jiang's personal attendant."

"Oh..." Qin Ruyin was stunned. "How could it be his people? What a coincidence."

"Young Miss, this servant thinks — is this not the connection between you and Lord Jiang? His people happened to save your people — is that not fate if not what is?"

"In this servant's view, when you want to give thanks, you should wait until you've married into the Jiang family and then personally thank Lord Jiang yourself!"

Qin Ruyin: ...

Was she being teased by her own senior maid?

"Hmph hmph... if you want to thank someone, go thank Lord Jiang yourself on your brother's behalf."

Purple Perilla immediately put on a sad face. "Lord Jiang wouldn't care about this servant's thanks. This servant knows Lord Jiang would certainly be very pleased if the young miss went to thank him herself..."

"Away with you!" Qin Ruyin felt her maid was becoming more and more outrageous.

"That young woman who was saved... well, that unfortunate girl — where is she being kept now? Has her background been investigated?"

This was the first time the reborn Fifth Young Miss had moved this ruthlessly. For it to be this drastic — that young woman must be no ordinary person.

"Ah, Young Miss — I nearly forgot about that." Purple Perilla quickly turned serious. "Those two people who saved my brother have helped settle the woman in an inn."

She handed Qin Ruyin a slip of paper with the inn's name.

Qin Ruyin glanced at it, then folded it shut.

The woman had been saved, but Qin Ruyin was not in a position to personally go out to see her.

She was still thinking over what to do when she heard another little maid outside urgently looking for Purple Perilla.

Purple Perilla hurried out, and was back in less than a moment — the shock and gossip in her eyes completely unmasked.

"Young Miss, this is truly beyond belief! The trafficked young woman you accidentally saved turns out to be..."

chapters.title: Allow Me to Congratulate You on Being a Grandmother Again
chapters.content:
After Qin Ruyin heard Purple Perilla's full account, she was both astonished and speechless — disgusted for quite a long while afterward.

"Young Miss, this was all arranged by Lord Jiang's Li Yi personally, and he specifically had my brother come deliver the message to you himself... saying that the matter of this young woman is not something convenient for you to get involved in, and that it would be better to have an elder of the household step in."

Li Yi had handled things with exceptional thoroughness. The young woman's most loyal little maidservant had also been rescued, and a matron had been arranged to look after the two terrified mistress and servant.

After the matron had comforted the pair, the young woman Rui Niang, once she calmed down, asked about the matron's background.

The matron taking care of them said she was a retired palace servant woman, once having served in the inner palace.

She had come to look after Rui Niang's household specifically because she had been paid to do a job on someone's behalf.

The incident Rui Niang had suffered — she was not the least bit surprised by it. It was merely inner-court trickery.

Rui Niang was sharp-witted. She understood that someone had deliberately saved her life.

Whatever the rescuer's motives, she no longer dared to keep up her dignity and stay outside. She wanted to use the child in her womb to enter the Qin household.

She had heard from the master that the Qin family's Grand Matriarch was an old woman who cared deeply about descendants and heirs...

To protect herself and her unborn child, she knelt and asked the matron to show her a way forward.

The matron at first was reluctant to speak. Rui Niang wept and begged.

Unable to hold out, the matron gave her a thorough analysis.

In the end, she told her: if she wished to protect the child's life, going to plead with the Qin family's Grand Matriarch might work. But if she wished to protect both herself and the child, she should go to the First Branch master and mistress of the Qin family.

Rui Niang thought carefully over what the matron had analyzed, found it all very reasonable, and took the matron's advice.

Having learned of Li Yi's arrangements through Purple Perilla, Qin Ruyin privately applauded — he was truly worthy of being one of Jiang Jiuxiao's men. The handling of this matter was perfectly to her taste.

Not only did it completely remove her from the Rui Niang affair, it required not even a scrap of brainwork from her. She only had to follow the plan that had been laid out.

"After thinking it through, she told Purple Perilla: "Take two hundred taels of silver. Give fifty taels each to the two people who saved your brother — to thank them for saving his life. Give the remaining hundred taels to Li Yi as thanks for his help in arranging everything."

Purple Perilla acknowledged and made to leave — but did not do so immediately.

Qin Ruyin looked at her in puzzlement. "Is there something else?"

"Young Miss, Li Yi also told my brother one more thing — said that these past few days Lord Jiang has been very busy, and has barely been eating his meals... this servant was wondering: why would he tell my brother that?"

Qin Ruyin didn't think much of it and said with a smile: "Li Yi is loyal to his master and feels for him, probably."

"Young Miss, since Li Yi mentioned this, he must certainly be hoping Young Miss knows about it." Purple Perilla looked at her young miss with hopeful eyes, clearly wanting her to do something.

"Hmm..." Qin Ruyin considered for a moment, then instructed Purple Perilla: "Send someone to Mother's small kitchen and see what pastries were made there today. Take a little of each kind and have your brother bring it to Li Yi."

Purple Perilla's eyes lit up. She immediately felt her young miss had woken up to things — wonderful, truly wonderful!

She admired Li Yi enormously — worthy of being Lord Jiang's capable right hand.

After Purple Perilla left, Qin Ruyin took Banjia and Juhong and went to her mother Li Shi's courtyard.

She told Li Shi the full story of Rui Niang, the young woman from the south.

Li Shi was wide-eyed and speechless. "What is this rare and extraordinary spectacle?"

"The two of them, father and son, are really..." Li Shi couldn't find words adequate to express her inner contempt.

"Mother — in any case, the two of them are quite shameless." Qin Ruyin tugged her mother's hand. "I'm told that Rui Niang is already with child..."

"Already with child?" Li Shi grew agitated. "This is such a spectacle! Thank goodness your Second Aunt is at the countryside estate — otherwise, who knows how furious she'd be!"

She also felt somewhat conflicted.

On the one hand, as a woman, her sympathies were with Rui Niang. On the other hand, she had despised Tong Shi as her sworn enemy for so long that she was quite happy to see Tong Shi suffer.

Steadying herself, Li Shi quickly worked it out. "By rights, your Second Uncle — that sly, calculating man — if he wanted a woman, why not simply take her in openly as a concubine? Tong Shi would be a willing sacrifice for appearances' sake, even if it made her teeth hurt. She'd bring the woman in rather than let her lurk outside..."

"Mother, what you should be asking is: why did Fifth Sister move to kill her so ruthlessly? That was taking two lives in one blow!"

Li Shi thought seriously for a moment, and still couldn't form a coherent theory.

"That Rui Niang chose to be someone's outside mistress — you can't say she's likable. But thinking about it — a beautiful little girl who went through misery to learn all kinds of accomplishments, and then caught the eye of a powerful official like your Second Uncle. What could she do?"

"Ah, she has no other recourse but to survive... your Fifth Sister should not have tried to kill her for it."

"And your Fifth Sister is a girl of the inner chambers — she shouldn't be so ruthless and cold-blooded in her methods."

Qin Ruyin nodded. "It's funny, isn't it — Fifth Sister went to the Old Matriarch's today and encouraged Third Sister-in-law to help Third Brother take concubines. Then turned around and tried to have her own father's outside mistress murdered... the double standard really is breathtaking."

"But I think — Fifth Sister wanting someone killed means she already knew something about the Second Branch that is extremely damaging, either to them or to herself. Mother, if that's the case, then we truly must keep Rui Niang alive."

Hearing her youngest daughter's analysis, Li Shi was immediately enlightened and nodded repeatedly.

"Ruyin, this is not something an unmarried young girl like you should be involved in. Hand this to your mother — you can pretend you know nothing about it." Li Shi's expression turned grave.

She paused and added: "Tell Purple Perilla's brother to keep his head down these few days — don't let your Second Uncle's people catch him out."

Qin Ruyin agreed.

"Purple Perilla has already made arrangements. That Rui Niang's most loyal little maidservant is already waiting at the back gate..."

Li Shi nodded. "Good. Your mother will see to things now. You go find your eldest and second sisters-in-law to chat."

Qin Ruyin knew her mother intended to keep any sharp eyes from finding a hold against her, and to fully extract her — an unmarried girl — from the Rui Niang affair.

She obediently went to her eldest sister-in-law's courtyard.

At dinner time.

Shouhe Hall blazed with candlelight.

The cause: Li Shi had suddenly brought a young woman of eighteen or nineteen — of extraordinary beauty — to Shouhe Hall.

The young woman was delicate and willowy, dressed in soft, elegant simplicity, wearing only a single high bun pinned with one jade hairpin of deep translucent green.

She walked with her head bowed the whole way, carefully cradling her abdomen with both hands — drawing the sidelong glances of every maid and matron serving in Shouhe Hall.

Of course, quite a few experienced matrons had already guessed something.

These women served the Old Matriarch. In their hearts, they were all waiting for Li Shi to make herself a fool.

Li Shi brought the young woman to pay respects to the Old Matriarch. The Old Matriarch looked at this young woman and privately sighed — what a southern beauty.

Then she saw the woman cradling her abdomen whether standing or bowing, and immediately understood.

She also had some interest in watching Li Shi humiliate herself.

She had the young woman sit, then asked Li Shi, unable to keep the smile from her voice: "Eldest daughter-in-law — this young woman appears to be with child. How far along? Are you about to become a stepmother — or are you going to be a grandmother again?"

Li Shi paused.

This old woman, at first glance, already assumed the child in Rui Niang's belly was First Branch stock?

Li Shi's startled reaction successfully deepened the Old Matriarch's misapprehension.

For years she had always resented that Qin Mingfeng — that step son — and Li Shi as a couple had worked in such perfect harmony, that she had spent decades trying to plant people in the First Branch's back court without success.

The two of them had walked side by side from youth to now, still deeply in love, not so much as a bedchamber servant in the inner court.

Being also a woman — why did Li Shi get to have such a fortunate life?

Right now, looking at this — no matter how loving a couple, wasn't Li Shi now here with a woman carrying someone's child, registering it officially?

If it were a grandchild, she wouldn't be bringing just Li Shi — she'd have brought at least one of her daughters-in-law too.

"Grand Matriarch, I don't have such fortune as to be this child's stepmother or grandmother." Li Shi covered her lips and gave a sudden laugh.

She reached back and gently pulled Rui Niang — still standing behind her, cradling her abdomen — forward. "Rui Niang, you're carrying a child — stop standing. Sit down."

Then her brow sharpened and she turned to the Old Matriarch's senior maid: "Miss Lüliu — won't you fetch a cushioned stool for Rui Niang?"

"Eldest daughter-in-law," the Old Matriarch said, cold-eyed, "in this old woman's courtyard, it is not yet your turn to give orders to this old woman's people."

Li Shi immediately replied: "Oh, Grand Matriarch — I'm only thinking of you."

"Oh goodness, how silly of me! I haven't congratulated you yet — it seems you are about to welcome another grandson. May I offer my congratulations on your becoming a grandmother again, and your Second Sister-in-law on becoming a stepmother..."

"Thump."

The Old Matriarch's teacup hit the floor. Tea and leaves splattered in all directions, causing the servants to shriek and leap about.

"What... what did you say?" The Old Matriarch's gaze shifted with great difficulty from Li Shi's face to the pale, frightened little face of Rui Niang, and then down to her abdomen.

"This... this young woman's child is..."

The Old Matriarch's vision went dark.

What sin was this?

How many more "surprises" did this father-and-son pair of Second Branch have in store for her?

Li Shi nodded openly. "Yes, exactly what Your Ladyship is thinking — Rui Niang's child is the Second Master's!"

She added with a laugh: "Grand Matriarch, what were you thinking? Our First Master's family, every man and boy is clean-living — not so much as a bedchamber servant between any of them. How could they have an outside mistress and even get a child by her?"

The Old Matriarch had gone completely limp, not even able to open her mouth.

Li Shi then turned to Rui Niang, who was still standing, and said gently: "Rui Niang, I speak no ill of you. A young girl like you — what can you do?"

"Poor child. The Second Master had you redeemed from your contract, kept you at home safe and well to carry the child, and then this calamity falls from the sky — you nearly lost your life and the child's too..."

The Old Matriarch, hearing Li Shi's words, flinched visibly. "She... she and the child nearly came to harm?"

Li Shi immediately nodded: "Is that not so? After the girl was saved by some kind souls, she was driven to seek shelter — and found her way to me. I couldn't bear to turn her away, so I brought her here to the Grand Matriarch."

Almost before the words were out, someone outside announced: "Grand Matriarch — the Second Master is here. The Fifth Young Miss... the Fifth Young Miss has also come."

Father and daughter had arrived together?

A light of pure entertainment rose in Li Shi's eyes.

chapters.title: Li Shi Watches the Show; Fifth Young Miss Gets Struck
chapters.content:
"Rui Niang! Rui Niang..." Qin Mingjie burst in with such urgency that the jade bead curtain of the Old Matriarch's chamber clattered violently.

Fifth Young Miss came running behind him calling "Father," but he didn't even spare her a glance.

Fifth Young Miss's heart hammered with dread, her face drained of color.

"Rui Niang — are you all right?" Qin Mingjie charged in like a young man in the grip of first passion, and when he saw Rui Niang standing unharmed behind Li Shi, finally exhaled.

As it happened, after court today he had gone directly to the large house he'd bought for Rui Niang.

His personal attendant knocked for a long time with no one answering the door.

He had an immediate premonition of disaster.

When he had people break the door down, his attendant reported that those he'd arranged to look after Rui Niang were mostly dead, and the others were nowhere to be found.

Qin Mingjie was instantly seized with both panic and fury, trembling, growling orders to have it investigated — alive or dead, he wanted Rui Niang.

Forcing himself to swallow his anguish and return to the Qin household, his people then told him that Rui Niang had escaped assassination that day and taken shelter in the Qin household.

Only Rui Niang had gone to the wrong person — she had found her way to the First Branch's Li Shi.

The moment Rui Niang saw Qin Mingjie, her eyes went immediately red as a rabbit's. In a weak, delicate voice she cried: "My lord..."

Qin Mingjie longed to rush over and take the beauty in his arms to comfort her.

He was about to move when the Old Matriarch, seated in the grand chair, gave a hard, pointed cough that stopped him cold.

Li Shi was still here.

The little ones were still here.

This Second Son had truly lost his mind.

The Old Matriarch also felt she was losing her mind.

"My lord — Rui Niang and... the child in this womb nearly didn't get to see you again..." Rui Niang wept softly, with admirable restraint, causing her shoulders to tremble with delicate vulnerability.

Qin Mingjie's heart shattered.

Li Shi stared with wide eyes, feeling every tooth in her mouth go sour. Goodness!

She could barely believe that this man — who had completely lost his head over a pretty face — was the respectable third-rank court official who had oppressed her husband for decades.

At this moment, she truly felt wronged on her husband's behalf.

Qin Mingjie used enormous effort to finally pull himself together.

His hands clasped behind his back, his eyes still practically glued to Rui Niang's face. "Rui Niang, rest easy. I have already sent people to investigate. I will find whoever dared harm you and the child, and have them cut to pieces!"

At this declaration, everyone present was more or less composed — everyone except Fifth Young Miss. Her legs went soft and she nearly crumpled to the ground.

Her heart pounded wildly, like a drumbeat.

Li Shi curved her lips and looked at Fifth Young Miss, asking in feigned surprise: "Fifth Young Miss, why does your complexion look so terrible? Are you ill?"

Li Shi's question successfully redirected all eyes to Fifth Young Miss.

Rui Niang's gaze drifted over with quiet intensity. This young woman — so strikingly beautiful, not much older than herself — must be the Qin family Second Branch's youngest legitimate daughter.

By rights, a girl of the inner chambers should not be this alarmed over an incident involving her father's mistress...

Could it be?

Rui Niang clenched her slender hands tightly, and drew her gaze back. Looking pitiable and helpless, she leaned entirely on Qin Mingjie.

When the Old Matriarch looked at Fifth Young Miss, her heart gave a frightened lurch.

Could it be that the hired assassination was Fifth Young Miss's doing?

That seed of suspicion planted itself in her heart, and the Old Matriarch no longer knew how to face this granddaughter she had cherished since infancy.

But she saw the gleam of pure entertainment on Li Shi's face, and moved quickly to send her away.

"Eldest daughter-in-law — this old woman thanks you for bringing Rui Niang here. What follows is our Second Branch's private affair. Eldest daughter-in-law, please go back and have your evening meal."

Li Shi gave a cold snort. "Truly... these eyes of mine are going. You needn't drive me off, Grand Matriarch — I was leaving anyway."

The Old Matriarch gritted her teeth. Qin Mingjie's face also darkened.

This shameful affair had been witnessed by Li Shi. To keep it from spreading, both mother and son had no choice but to swallow their anger in her presence.

Li Shi rose and lightly brushed nonexistent dust off her clothing. Her eyes turned to Rui Niang.

"Rui Niang — you are young with no one to rely on. Take good care of yourself."

Rui Niang's face went pale. Instinctively her hands pressed more tightly over her abdomen.

Li Shi led her maids and matrons away with sweeping confidence.

Qin Mingjie was about to comfort Rui Niang when someone outside announced that his personal attendant Song Dacheng — also the father of Xixin — had an urgent matter to report.

The color drained from Xixin's face.

Fifth Young Miss didn't look much better.

At that moment, Qin Mingjie was reeking of murder. He barked: "Let him in to speak."

The Old Matriarch tried to stop him but couldn't in time, and could only say: "Old Second — have Song Dacheng report from behind the bead curtain. This is a room full of women save for yourself — it really isn't proper to have a male servant enter!"

Qin Mingjie gave a cold grunt. He was truly beside himself with fury.

Song Dacheng, his heart trembling, reported on what he had found regarding the assassination attempt on Rui Niang.

It had in fact been very easy to trace.

Simple hired assassination.

And the person who had done the hiring was someone no one could have predicted.

It was none other than the gracious, accomplished, most shining jewel of the Qin family — Fifth Young Miss.

Song Dacheng at that moment wished he could give that brainless Fifth Young Miss several hard slaps across the face.

If you want to hire killers, hire killers — but then sending your own intimate personal maidservant to do the hiring in person...

Was it not obvious with one look?

If that maid had been some stranger, the matter might have been managed. But it was his own flesh-and-blood daughter.

His Song family was finished...

"Fifth Young Miss... Fifth Young Miss, we have no old grudge or new enmity between us. This concubine doesn't even know you. Why did you have to be so cruel to me?"

"Even if... even if this concubine is a woman the master keeps outside, not a respectable woman, that is still no crime worthy of death!"

Rui Niang wept as though her heart and guts were being torn apart.

"My lord... my lord, I had originally thought the master's enemies were trying to kill me and the child to take revenge on you. But I never in the world imagined it was your Fifth Young Miss who wanted me dead... how... how am I supposed to go on living?"

Rui Niang embraced her abdomen and crumpled softly to the floor. In a panic, Qin Mingjie bent down and caught her in his arms — then stood up, lifting her into a bridal carry.

He strode with murder in his eyes to Fifth Young Miss, raised his foot, and kicked her squarely in the kneecap.

Fifth Young Miss let out a sharp cry and crashed to the ground, tears instantly flooding her face.

"Father... Father, let your daughter explain..."

"My lord, my lord... Rui Niang has some pain in the belly." Rui Niang shot a dark sideways glance at Fifth Young Miss collapsed on the ground, then immediately looked up at Qin Mingjie in frightened appeal.

The Old Matriarch stared at the chaos with her temples throbbing incessantly.

"Mingjie — go settle Rui Niang first. Call the household physician in to examine her." She issued the command. "But you shouldn't have... not even let Fifth Young Miss explain, and then..."

"Mother! After all these years, I have finally found a woman I truly care for. I cannot stand by and watch her suffer! And this unfilial girl even conspired to kill both Rui Niang and the child."

"Just this one kick is already showing her mercy as her father!" Qin Mingjie glared coldly at the crumpled Fifth Young Miss. "And this is only because you are about to be married out. Otherwise, go accompany your mother in the countryside!"

Madness.

True madness.

The Old Matriarch stared at her most prideful son with shocked, disbelieving eyes.

But what could she do?

Her son had spent forty-odd years before he met a woman he truly loved.

She could only clean up after him.

...

The Old Matriarch ordered the maids and matrons to carry Fifth Young Miss onto the small couch in the outer chamber of her own room.

"Fifth Young Miss, don't resent your father — he lost his head for a moment." The Old Matriarch tried to comfort her granddaughter. "And you — even if you had every reason in the world, you absolutely cannot go hiring killers on a whim like that!"

Even if it had to be done, it required thorough planning.

She was still young — too young. Not careful enough in her methods. The Old Matriarch sighed inwardly.

"At least this matter hasn't spread outside. Only family knows. This old woman has already disciplined those below. The people on Li Shi's side have also been warned. You can set your mind at ease."

Fifth Young Miss's face was chalk-white, and tears would not stop falling.

No matter how she turned it over, she couldn't understand: she had the advantage of being reborn, and had acted to rid the family of the future threat that Rui Niang represented — and yet somehow everything had become her fault?

Her father hadn't just kicked her once — he had developed hatred for her!

Thinking of the look in her father's eyes as he carried Rui Niang away and glanced back at her — she went cold to her bones...

With her father hating her now, the only one she could rely on was her grandmother!

Once she calmed down, Fifth Young Miss immediately threw herself into the Old Matriarch's arms.

"Grandmother... your granddaughter is not that cold-blooded, to kill without reason. Granddaughter acted out of necessity, for the family's sake — it was the only way."

The Old Matriarch was startled. She said at once: "Fifth Young Miss, what do you mean by that?"

chapters.title: The First Branch Wants to Split the Clan; Lord Jiang Sends a Gift
chapters.content:
Qin Ruyue made up another excuse about a dream, telling Old Madam Qin—half-truthfully—her reasons for wanting Ruiniang killed.

Old Madam Qin thus learned that years later, Ruiniang, the kept mistress whom Qin Mingjie had been hiding outside for years, would catch the eye of an imperial prince. In the struggle to possess Ruiniang, the prince would use underhanded means that nearly brought about the ruin of the entire Qin family...

"At the time, no one dared speak up for our Qin family. After all, Father had kept a mistress in secret for years, hiding it from my mother—that practically amounted to favoring a concubine over a wife. In the end, it was your granddaughter who went to Jiang Jiuxiao for help..."

Old Madam Qin drew a slow, deep breath. Her aged eyes settled heavily on Ruyue. "Since in your dream it was the Fourth of the Jiang family who ultimately saved the day, why not again let your Sixth Sister..."

Qin Ruyue met her grandmother's gaze with an expression of helpless resignation.

"Grandmother, if we could rely on Sixth Sister this time, why would I take such a reckless risk?"

Old Madam Qin fell silent.

Indeed. That girl, the Sixth, was no longer so easy to manage.

"Your granddaughter was clearly thinking of the family's interests... yet now not only has she earned Father's contempt, but if word gets out that she hired someone to commit murder, your granddaughter will have no way to go on living! The Wang family will certainly come to call off the engagement."

"Grandmother, what is your granddaughter to do?"

"Why didn't you tell your grandmother sooner? Even if you had told your father, he might not have been unwilling to believe you." Old Madam Qin ground her teeth, staring at this most cherished of her granddaughters.

Acting without consulting her elders, going off on her own—and bringing down such terrible trouble—that was entirely her fault.

What a headache.

Qin Ruyue spoke at once: "If your granddaughter had told you, you would have believed me—but you are getting on in years, and I couldn't bear to worry you over something that wouldn't happen for several years yet. As for Father—as you saw today, he is so devoted to that Ruiniang; how would he ever believe your granddaughter?"

"It was precisely because I foresaw that Father not only wouldn't believe me, but would grow estranged from me, that I thought to plan in secret—to quietly neutralize the great crisis years before it arrived." Qin Ruyue clasped Old Madam Qin's two hands.

She pleaded through her tears: "Grandmother, now I can only beg your protection..."

Old Madam Qin pulled her hands free from Qin Ruyue's grasp and let out a long sigh. "The matter of Ruiniang ends here! You were genuinely in the wrong—you didn't think it through!"

"If you had only told your elders, we could have brought Ruiniang into the Qin household, let her live quietly in the inner quarters, bear children in peace. Wouldn't that have been enough to prevent the future calamity?"

"Why did you have to be so ruthless?" Old Madam Qin shook her head. She did not accept Qin Ruyue's reasoning.

She also found her far too cruel and vicious.

To be so heartless toward her father's woman—a woman who was with child, no less.

Whatever else one might say, the child in Ruiniang's belly was her own flesh-and-blood grandchild.

And that child was born of the woman her son loved most. That made it altogether different.

Qin Ruyue had not expected that even after learning the truth of what she had done, Old Madam Qin would still hold this attitude.

Her mind went blank.

How could this be?

Hadn't Grandmother always cared most about the rise and fall of the family? She had acted for the sake of the Qin family—surely Grandmother would stand on her side after learning the truth!

Yet Grandmother's attitude now was plainly one of standing with Father...

"Fifth Girl, there are not many days left before you are wed. In the days remaining, you must stay quietly in the inner court and not cause any more trouble." Old Madam Qin's patience was exhausted, and she had no desire to face her weeping granddaughter.

Even if this granddaughter was the one she loved most.

Even so, in her heart, the ones she loved best had always been those who had crawled out from her own womb.

Moreover, Old Madam Qin strongly suspected that this granddaughter had not told her the full truth of what she had dreamed.

Hiring someone to kill Ruiniang—there must also have been a personal grievance behind it.

After all, Ruiniang was so strikingly beautiful that even this old woman, upon seeing her, felt a fondness. How much more so a man.

A mother knows her own son.

She believed that her second son's heart was buried with Ruiniang.

And if Qin Mingjie was wholly devoted to Ruiniang, he would surely dote on Ruiniang's children by extension.

That would inevitably harm the interests of Madam Tong and the children she had borne him.

Fifth Girl had an even stronger motive—to rid herself of Ruiniang for the sake of her own mother and her brothers and sisters.

* * *

Not long after, the head matron of Qin Mingjie's courtyard came in person to report to Old Madam Qin.

Ruiniang had been settled into his courtyard by Qin Mingjie, who had formally granted her the status of "concubine."

Old Madam Qin was privately startled.

To dote so intensely on a woman—that was not what she, his mother, wished to see.

Yet from her son's perspective, that Ruiniang was a treasure he had lost and found again. For now, all she could do was take things one step at a time.

The head matron of Qin Mingjie's courtyard also reported to Old Madam Qin that Fifth Young Miss's dowry would remain exactly as it was—not a single item would be added—and that Old Madam Qin need not add anything either.

Qin Ruyue was lying on the small daybed outside Old Madam Qin's inner room and heard every word clearly. Her heart ached and throbbed; fresh tears rolled down her cheeks.

After Qin Mingjie's head matron departed, Old Madam Qin went to look in on Qin Ruyue, and sighed inwardly.

"You heard all of that, didn't you? In the future, before you do anything, you must first inform your elders, and think three times before acting!"

"Just recently, your father had been feeling that the Wang family had been stingy with the betrothal gifts and wanted to add another thirty percent to your dowry to put the Wang family to shame... and now look."

* * *

The news that the Second Master of the Qin family had taken a strikingly beautiful new concubine spread quickly through the inner quarters of the Qin household.

Thanks to Old Madam Qin's swift and iron-handed measures, word of Qin Ruyue having hired assassins to kill her father's concubine did not leak out.

Even so, Qin Ruyue was placed under house arrest by the Second Master of the Qin family, confined until her wedding day.

This caused no small amount of speculation behind closed doors.

Whether the Second Master's taking a concubine and the Fifth Young Miss's confinement were somehow connected.

The secret behind it all was clear to every adult member of the First Branch, and though they had no intention of spreading it, they held the Second Branch in even deeper contempt.

The First Master of the Qin family formally revealed to his family his plan to split away from the Second Branch.

Every adult member of the First Branch—including young Master Jin—gave their approval.

"Strangely enough, the things the Second Branch has been doing lately seem as if they've been put under some kind of curse—one stupid thing after another, practically without end." The First Master of the Qin family was genuinely puzzled.

Madam Li said: "Isn't that a good thing? It means Heaven itself can't stand how the Second Branch conducts itself! As the old saying goes: 'Those whom Heaven wishes to destroy, it first drives mad.'"

The First Master looked at his wife and smiled warmly: "My wife is right—you've been getting more and more eloquent lately. Beautifully said!"

At those words, Madam Li's face flushed red.

She gave Qin's First Master a push. "What are you saying? The children are right here!"

The First Master just smiled and said nothing more.

Qin Rujin and the others were either bowing their heads or covering their mouths to stifle laughter.

"My lord, never mind the laughter—since we are going to split from the clan, we must plan ahead."

Madam Li put on a serious face. "Hurry and have someone make a record of all the foolish things the Second Branch has done of late... there will be a fight to be had at the clan hall when the time comes!"

The First Master's expression also sobered. "My dear, rest easy—I have already been attending to that."

Only then did Madam Li set her mind at ease.

Qin Rujin had barely returned to her own courtyard when Zisu came rushing over, her face lit with delight. "Miss, Lord Jiang sent Li Yi in person to bring you osmanthus-and-water-chestnut cake made by that Master Li chef from the Xilai Grand Restaurant!"

Qin Rujin was also pleasantly surprised.

Ever since eating it that one time, she had truly been craving it.

Unfortunately, the osmanthus-and-water-chestnut cake handmade by Master Li of the Xilai Grand Restaurant was not something that could simply be bought with silver.

Word had it that just a few days ago, Old Madam Qin had sent someone with First Master Qin Mingfeng's visiting card and still hadn't been able to purchase any.

"Bring it quickly! I'll go to Mother's courtyard—Father is there too—and we can all enjoy a treat." Qin Rujin said brightly.

"Oh, Miss, there's no need to bring it—Lord Jiang already prepared some for everyone! Three portions were sent in all!"

Zisu was absolutely beaming, grinning from ear to ear.

"Miss, Lord Jiang also sent you another gift!"

"Oh?" Qin Rujin was surprised. She had assumed the osmanthus-and-water-chestnut cake was Jiang Jiuxiao's way of returning the gesture for the pastries she had sent back with Li Yi yesterday.

"He has another gift for me?"

chapters.title: Ruyin Personally Makes a Soup
chapters.content:
Zisu nodded emphatically.

With the air of presenting a treasure, she lifted a beautifully carved red sandalwood box from the table behind her.

"This is what Lord Jiang sent separately for you—he said you are to open it yourself..."

Qin Rujin smiled.

Lord Jiang... well, Uncle Jiang was actually quite a decent sort!

She lightly opened the lid of the box.

A magnolia hairpin, carved from top-quality white mutton-fat jade, lay quietly against the red silk inside. Beside it rested...

A letter?

Qin Rujin raised an eyebrow.

So Uncle had finally come to his senses and written her a love letter?

That was impossible.

She had to admit—she truly adored this magnolia hairpin, so warm and lustrous, so exquisitely beautiful.

After admiring and running her fingers over it for a moment, she reluctantly set it back inside the elegant little red sandalwood box.

Then she unfolded the letter.

Oh?

This magnolia jade hairpin was a belated gift for her coming-of-age ceremony at fifteen last year?

Uncle Jiang was truly thoughtful.

Qin Rujin thought with a pleased smile.

This uncle—though he was cool and cold, and genuinely seemed to have no interest in women—at least he knew how to treat people right.

No wonder he'd risen to such high office!

But as she read on, her beautiful eyes narrowed.

Oh ho!

The rest of the letter was telling her that her involvement in the affair with Ruiniang had been far too dangerous.

And then it told her not to worry—he had already sent people to scrub away every trace of her involvement.

Mm-hmm!

Courtesy before force, as it were?

She knew what was good for her!

She also took good advice.

Reading further, Uncle Jiang had not lectured her at all. Instead, he had sent her four people with capable fighting skills...

One to serve as her household stewardess, one to serve as a personal maid, and two men to handle affairs outside the household.

How thoughtful of him to consider all this!

At the end, he wrote the following: "The wedding day draws near. I trust you will exercise restraint, refrain from mischief, and come to me as my new bride whole and unscathed."

Qin Rujin: "......"

She buried her face in her hands!

Uncle Jiang really did treat her like a little child...

(TL: I keep using the word uncle, but uncle is not really it. The actual term is used to refer to older men. I wanted to say old man Jiang, but it really did not fit. This is the best I can come up with.)

"Zisu, Banxia." Not knowing the answer, she simply asked: "Am I really that mischievous?"

Banxia, who had no idea what was going on, shook her head at once: "Of course not! Our Miss is the most sensible and well-behaved girl there is..."

Zisu, however, covered her lips to hide a smile. She suddenly remembered there was something else she still hadn't reported to her mistress—she quickly organized her thoughts.

It turned out that when Li Yi had come in person to deliver the gifts that day, he had quietly whispered something to Zisu on the side: that his master had lately not only been too busy to eat properly, but had also been diagnosed by a physician with a weak and ailing stomach...

Qin Rujin listened to what Zisu murmured to her and couldn't help but be exasperated.

"Zisu—if you have something to say, just say it all at once next time, will you?"

It wasn't as if it were something awkward to say.

Zisu smiled and apologized to her mistress, then added: "Miss, it's not that your maidservant was dragging things out—I felt that Li Yi was being a bit unreasonable about it."

Qin Rujin found that odd.

"How so? Did he suggest something? Or does Lord Jiang have some opinion about it?"

If Uncle Jiang had anything to say, he would have written it clearly in his letter. There would be no need to have Li Yi pass on a message.

Qin Rujin thought.

Zisu said: "That Li Yi asked your maidservant whether it would be possible to request that you personally make some pastries—he felt his master would be more willing to eat them if they came from your own hands..."

Qin Rujin said: "That Li Yi really is quite a busybody—worrying about even this sort of thing. He must have had a hard time working up to it."

But in the very next moment, she caught on: "Which means the pastries I sent back with Li Yi for Lord Jiang—he didn't eat them? Or barely touched them?"

What an opportunity.

What a wonderful opportunity!

Li Yi really was doing her a great favor!

Hearing her mistress ask, Zisu quickly shook her head: "This maidservant doesn't know for certain, but from the way Li Yi was grinning from ear to ear, it seems Lord Jiang must have been very pleased to receive the pastries the Miss sent over."

Whether he ate them and whether he liked them were two different things.

Banxia chimed in from the side, urging: "Miss, then just go make a trip to the First Mistress's small kitchen—whether you know how to cook or not doesn't really matter. Even watching the cook do it counts as you being present. We just need the claim that you made it with your own hands..."

Hey!

Qin Rujin did not care for that suggestion.

She could make pastries.

And make them quite well, in fact!

She could cook proper meals too!

Not to boast, but her culinary skills were actually quite good.

"Making pastries is such a big deal now?"

Qin Rujin rolled up her sleeves. "Come—your Miss has read so many miscellaneous books; they weren't read for nothing. I'll show you what I'm made of right now!"

She also told Zisu: "Go quickly and let someone tell Li Yi he may go about his business for now. When my gift is ready, have your brother deliver it to him."

Then, with a glance at Juhong: "Juhong, have the people keep a few pieces of the osmanthus-and-water-chestnut cake Lord Jiang sent over, and send the rest to Mother's courtyard for her to divide among everyone."

Zisu and Juhong acknowledged the orders.

"Banxia, find me a set of old, dark-colored clothes and help me change. We're going to Mother's small kitchen."

"Miss, are you really going to cook yourself?" Banxia looked at her mistress with concern.

But Qin Rujin thought: after she married into the Jiang household, if she couldn't stomach the food made by the Jiang family's main kitchen, she would certainly need to have her own small kitchen in her courtyard for cooking separately.

She also thought about how the original Rujin had never voluntarily cooked before. If she suddenly revealed cooking skills after arriving at the Jiang household, that sharp-minded Uncle Jiang would likely grow suspicious.

Better to take advantage of this opportunity to quietly show off a little at home first—make something, and have Li Yi bring it to Uncle Jiang.

This way, she was using Li Yi's hand to establish the fact openly before Uncle Jiang.

And since the original Rujin loved reading miscellaneous books, anything could be attributed to having read it somewhere—she would just say she followed a recipe. No one could say anything about that.

"Of course it's real. Do I look like I'm joking?"

Banxia looked at her mistress's confident and lovely face, and for some reason—instinctively—believed that her mistress really could do it...

Qin Rujin swept into Madam Li's small kitchen with Banxia at her heels.

The cooks and kitchen helpers who had been lounging about chatting gave a start.

When they understood that the Sixth Young Miss had come to cook, one by one they stared wide-eyed with their mouths hanging open.

Banxia quickly said: "Don't just stand there staring—our Miss has read so many books; it's not like she read them for nothing! Is making a dish really such a big affair?"

The cooks and helpers dared not speak, but their expressions were plainly disapproving.

Qin Rujin ignored them, only asking them for the ingredients she needed.

She had already decided on the way over: she would simmer a pot of Chinese yam and pork rib soup, and make jujube paste and hawthorn cake.

The jujube paste could be made by soaking dried jujubes until soft, pitting them, and mashing them.

The hawthorn—she would simply use ready-made preserved hawthorn fruit, finely chopped.

Both were very easy to obtain.

She would start with just these two dishes to test the waters.

For the pork rib soup, the most critical step was deodorizing the ribs. She knew the little tricks for removing that gamey smell by heart—as long as the deodorizing was done well, the soup couldn't go wrong.

* * *

The Chinese yam and pork rib soup was rich, fragrant, and creamy white. The jujube paste and hawthorn cake was soft, glutinous, sweet, and appetite-stimulating.

Qin Rujin had not let the fretting cooks lay a hand on anything—aside from tending the fire, she had done everything herself from start to finish.

Banxia held her head high with pride as she packed the soup her mistress had personally simmered and the cake her mistress had personally made into an elegant food hamper.

Under the admiring gazes of the cooks and kitchen helpers, she picked up the hamper and accompanied her mistress out of the small kitchen.

Qin Rujin kept a good portion back, and instructed the small kitchen to send the remaining large portion of the yam-and-rib soup and the jujube yam cake to her mother's courtyard.

After handing the hamper to Zisu, Qin Rujin took Banxia along and went to her mother's courtyard to freeload on the noon meal.

Madam Li and the First Master were there with their grandchildren, eating the cake and drinking the soup she had personally simmered.

When she arrived, Madam Li smiled with narrowed eyes in delight. "I never imagined—my child can simmer such a delicious soup, make such good cake!"

Qin Rujin glanced at the osmanthus-and-water-chestnut cake in her mother's hand and smiled warmly: "No matter how good what your daughter makes, it still can't be as delicious as Master Li's."

Madam Li laughed it off dismissively.

The First Master nodded with a smile: "No—what Yinniang made is the better dish. What Father and your mother taste is Yinniang's filial devotion!"

The children were all holding pieces of jujube paste yam cake; they all loved the tart-sweet flavor.

The osmanthus-and-water-chestnut cake had too strong an osmanthus fragrance; none of them much cared for it.

"Little Auntie's cake is so delicious, and the meat broth is so good to drink! We never knew..."

The eldest girl from the big brother's family gazed at Qin Rujin with sparkling eyes. "Little Auntie, will you make some for us again next time?"

"Of course!" Qin Rujin nodded, wearing her most doting smile.

She then said to Madam Li: "Why are you drinking the soup already? Isn't the noon meal later?"

"Your soup smelled so delicious, the little monkeys caught a whiff and demanded some immediately—what could I do? Besides, there are no such strict rules here at Mother's. Eat what you want, drink what you want—just don't stuff yourselves."

Qin Rujin nodded. That was her mother's style.

Madam Li finished the cake in her hand and looked at Qin Rujin again.

"Mother has already heard from the small kitchen that not a single person helped—you made everything with your own hands. I never imagined my little daughter had been hiding such a talent!"

The First Master also nodded, and said to Madam Li: "Before, my dear, you were always worried about Yinniang reading miscellaneous books—you even gave her brothers a beating over it. But look now—reading miscellaneous books isn't such a bad thing after all!"

Madam Li said proudly: "Hmph—my daughter can learn good things from any book she reads; other people can't necessarily say the same!"

The First Master nodded with a smile: "Absolutely right! Whatever my wife says is right!"

Madam Li then asked Qin Rujin: "I heard from people in the main kitchen that you kept some and had it sent out. Who did you send it to?"

The First Master also looked over.

Qin Rujin had no choice but to tell her parents about Jiang Jiuxiao's belated coming-of-age gift to her, and about the people he had sent along.

Madam Li was immediately overjoyed.

The First Master, perceptive as ever, noticed something: all the people Jiang, the future son-in-law, had sent to Yinniang were skilled in martial arts. That meant the Jiang family truly cared about his Yinniang—afraid the child might do something without weighing the consequences, and come to some misfortune...

Ptoo, ptoo, ptoo!

The First Master furiously spat at himself mentally, and the smile on his face deepened.

He said to Qin Rujin: "Yinniang, you did exactly right. Lord Jiang occupies a high position, handles affairs with thoroughness, and has a long-sighted view. Since he thinks of you so carefully, you must not simply receive his kindness without returning it in kind..."

Madam Li said: "Our Yinniang has a head on her shoulders! And what return gesture is more sincere than preparing a meal with one's own hands? Our future Jiang son-in-law is a lucky man—to have found such a warm and considerate girl as our Yinniang!"

* * *

At noon, Jiang Jiuxiao's colleagues all filed off to the official canteen for the midday meal.

In the great Ying dynasty, the court provided officials with two "work meals" per day—morning and noon—and the place where officials went to eat was called the official canteen.

Lord Huang, the Right Vice Minister of the Ministry of Personnel, stepped forward to invite him: "Lord Jiang, shall we go together to the official canteen for lunch?"

Jiang Jiuxiao shook his head. "Lord Huang, please go ahead. This official is not hungry yet—I'll take a turn around the covered corridor first and clear my head."

Lord Huang had no choice but to go on without him.

Just at this moment, Li Yi came hurrying over with a food hamper.

"My lord!"

Jiang Jiuxiao furrowed his brow, looking at him: "Has my mother had you bring a meal again?"

Li Yi shook his head with a mysterious expression: "Master, this time it was not the Old Madam who had your servant bring food. Care to guess who sent it?"

"My elder sister-in-law?" Jiang Jiuxiao said, then shook his head himself. His elder sister-in-law had sent food twice before, and after he declined, she had never again made that thankless gesture.

"Not the First Mistress either! It was the Sixth Young Miss!" Li Yi's eyes were bright with excitement; he emphasized each word: "And she made it herself in the kitchen!"

"Her?" Jiang Jiuxiao's brow and eyes relaxed without him realizing it. "She made it herself?"

He found it somewhat amusing.

That delicate little young miss—she actually went and cooked herself?

He had no idea what it would turn out like.

He wondered if it would even be edible.

chapters.title: Lord Jiang Is Willing to Owe a Favor for Ruyin's Sake
chapters.content:
"Why did the Sixth Young Miss... cook herself?" Jiang Jiuxiao suddenly sensed something was off. His eyes settled heavily on Li Yi.

Li Yi's legs went weak on the spot. On this pleasant spring day of warm sun and gentle breeze, he broke out in a cold sweat all over.

"In...in reply to my lord—it was your servant... your servant who spoke out of turn, and let slip a word to Zisu, the Sixth Young Miss's maid, that you had lately been diagnosed by a physician with a weak stomach..."

"Yes, you did speak out of turn." Jiang Jiuxiao nodded, and showed no sign of anger.

Li Yi exhaled in relief.

My lord had not told him not to speak out of turn next time... which meant he dared to do it again next time!

Recalling what Zisu had said, he scrambled to urge his master:
