"My lord, upon hearing that you've been unwell in the stomach lately, the Sixth Young Miss specially simmered a pot of Chinese yam and pork bone soup and made jujube paste and hawthorn cake... which strengthen and warm the stomach and spleen."

"Would you... eat a little of it?" Li Yi finished speaking and watched his master anxiously.

"Give it here."

"Ah?" Li Yi was slow to react.

Jiang Jiuxiao frowned with displeasure: "The hamper."

Li Yi quickly offered it with both hands.

Jiang Jiuxiao, one hand clasped behind his back, carried the hamper in the other and strolled leisurely in the direction of the official canteen.

The little young miss had made it with her own hands—he ought at least to give her that much face.

And if he was going to eat, he should go to the official canteen.

The official canteen was already lively with conversation.

The elderly Lord Cheng, the Minister of the Ministry of Personnel, sat eating alone. The long table he occupied could have seated at least six people.

But Lord Cheng had an aloof and proud temperament, and had never liked sharing a meal with others. The Ministry of Personnel was also a position that drew too much attention—it was a matter of avoiding appearance of impropriety.

No one nearby dared sit close to him; they all kept their distance.

After all, officially, other officials were also supposed to avoid even the appearance of closeness with the Ministry of Personnel.

Yet Lord Cheng was exceptionally fond of his own deputy, the Left Vice Minister Jiang Jiuxiao.

Seeing him arrive alone, carrying a food hamper, Lord Cheng was rather surprised, and waved him over.

Jiang Jiuxiao smiled faintly, gave a slight nod, and walked toward Lord Cheng.

As he passed through the room, the assembled officials paused their chopsticks to greet him.

He returned each greeting with a nod, but did not pause for anyone.

"Has family sent a meal again today?" Lord Cheng looked at him with some concern. "This can't go on—you really should have the physician take another look."

Jiang Jiuxiao wasn't quite sure where to begin.

"What's this—still don't feel like eating? You should at least eat something. Whatever family sends is always better than what the official canteen prepares."

After a pause, Lord Cheng frowned: "But you can't eat only what's sent from home every day—if those old fussbuckets from the Censorate see it, they'll have something to nag about again."

"Old Cheng is stepping down next month, and I don't know how many eyes are on the Ministry of Personnel. You must take care of your health—there's a hard battle ahead!"

Jiang Jiuxiao smiled and agreed.

He said gently: "This time it was not sent from home—it was brought by the new bride who will soon be entering my household. She pressed her sincerity upon me so warmly I had no choice but to bring it to the canteen to eat."

Lord Cheng broke into a smile.

"Oh? Then old Cheng must congratulate Jiuxiao on gaining such a warm and attentive wife!"

Jiang Jiuxiao smiled and offered his thanks, then opened the food hamper.

Lord Cheng looked in as well.

A pot of Chinese yam and meat soup, and a portion of elegantly presented cake.

The fragrance of sweet jujubes and tart hawthorn wafted out.

"Looks quite good. The Sixth Young Miss of the Qin family's First Branch is a thoughtful one." Lord Cheng chuckled, stroking his beard.

He was getting on in years and had already eaten a fair amount; he was nearly full.

Jiang Jiuxiao glanced at Lord Cheng with a smile. "My lord, you know of the Qin family's First Branch?"

"How could I not? The Qin family is no great name among the capital's noble families, yet who doesn't know of Qin Mingjie's domineering ways?"

"Old Cheng, though unwilling to meddle in others' affairs, still hears things. Your marriage to the Sixth Young Miss of the Qin First Branch was rather rushed—had old Cheng not been bedridden at the time, he had half a mind to try to dissuade you."

After a pause, Lord Cheng stroked his salt-and-pepper beard: "Looking at it now, however, old Cheng is at ease."

Jiang Jiuxiao was not Lord Cheng's own student, but he was the most prized student of Lord Cheng's elder brother-in-law.

Because of that connection, Lord Cheng had regarded this young man—so precocious and brilliantly gifted—as something of a nephew over the years.

Talented people appear in every age, and the court of the great Ying dynasty ought to be entrusted to upright young men like this—men who feared no authority, whose hearts were fixed entirely on the people and on governance.

"You may set your mind at ease." Jiang Jiuxiao had already taken a sip of the soup. Whatever concern he had held for that girl vanished the instant the soup touched his lips.

He said nothing more, and quickly finished the pot of soup—the ribs had been deboned, leaving only the meat.

He ate that too.

Tender, soft, rich, and fragrant.

The Chinese yam was soft and starchy, gently sweet, with a lingering aftertaste.

He ate with half-closed eyes, without the least resistance.

Lord Cheng watched him empty the whole pot clean, and couldn't help being mildly astonished.

"Jiuxiao... This old man seems to recall that you do not eat Chinese yam."

"Oh?" Jiang Jiuxiao surfaced from his enjoyment. "Do I?"

"Old Cheng's memory is not what it once was, but it's still reliable—I absolutely could not be mistaken. You simply do not eat Chinese yam..."

"My lord, please try some of the cake." Jiang Jiuxiao knew Lord Cheng could be rather stubborn, and had no desire to argue with him. So he invited the old lord to try the cake.

He added: "This was made by the hand of the new bride soon to enter my household."

Lord Cheng's expression turned serious at once. A young girl had made it herself, after all.

No wonder!

After his brief gravity, the old lord smiled again: "No wonder Jiuxiao is no longer a picky eater."

"Mm!" After eating a piece of the jujube hawthorn cake, Lord Cheng nodded at Jiang Jiuxiao. "The flavor is quite good. Chinese yam, red jujube, hawthorn—the young lady was thoughtful."

Jiang Jiuxiao curved the corner of his mouth and said: "My lord, Jiuxiao would ask a favor."

Lord Cheng looked at him. "Is it for the Sixth Young Miss of the Qin family?"

Jiang Jiuxiao smiled without answering.

Lord Cheng sighed softly and lowered his voice.

"You have never once come to old Cheng with a private matter—this is the first time... This old man is a man on the verge of retirement; of course he cannot bear to disappoint you. Tell me."

The official canteen was crowded with eyes and ears. Those who lingered after eating chatted noisily, and people generally did not discuss private matters here.

But with no one daring to sit near the two of them—one old, one young—and with their voices kept low, there was no fear of being overheard.

Lord Cheng listened to what Jiang Jiuxiao had to say, stroked his beard, and nodded.

"Your father-in-law has spent all these years on a sinecure, yet he has never once slackened off. His official rank has not moved... and yes, someone has been deliberately holding him down."

"Old Cheng will find someone to smooth the way. You may set your mind at ease."

Jiang Jiuxiao offered his thanks.

Lord Cheng laughed. "No need to thank me—the Sixth Young Miss of the Qin family has already thanked old Cheng with her cake."

He added: "These days, there are precious few young ladies in the inner quarters with the Sixth Young Miss's culinary skill. And even among those few, even fewer have her degree of sincerity."

Then he changed the subject abruptly: "Only, Jiuxiao—what is past is past. Don't let old ghosts take hold of your heart. You would wrong yourself, and you would wrong the one who comes after."

At the Qin residence.

Old Madam Qin had already learned that Qin Rujin had personally cooked a soup and pastries and had Zisu send them out.

After the noon meal, she sent someone to summon Qin Rujin and Madam Li, mother and daughter, to the Hall of Longevity and Peace.

Without even asking them to sit, she turned on Qin Rujin at once.

She said that for an unmarried young lady of good family, it was entirely improper to personally cook a dish and have her personal maid send it to an outsider.

Even if it was sent to her betrothed Jiang Jiuxiao, it still brought disgrace to all the young ladies of the Qin family.

Qin Rujin had no patience for wrangling with this old woman.

She said directly: "Those words of the Old Madam are truly laughable!"

"When Fifth Sister secretly met privately with Second Young Master Wang to exchange poetry, you said she was perfectly chaste—merely the impulsive expression of two young hearts. But now that it is my turn—I, who am already formally engaged to Lord Jiang—and I hear that Lord Jiang's stomach is unwell and prepare a soup and pastries to send to him, suddenly this brings disgrace upon all the Qin young ladies?"

"I would like to ask the Old Madam—what was it, then, when Fifth Sister, not yet engaged to anyone, met privately with a man from outside and exchanged poems with him?"

Of course, she had no personal objection to Fifth Young Miss's secret meeting with a man outside—if it was born of genuine feeling and remained within the bounds of propriety, that was fine.

She herself had met privately with Jiang Jiuxiao.

But this old woman's blatant double standard was something she could not tolerate.

Old Madam Qin was speechless at Qin Rujin's counter-question. She lost quite a lot of face.

Just as she was wracking her brain for a reply, a servant came to report to Madam Li: "First Mistress, the Second Young Mistress and Third Young Mistress of the Jiangdong Sun family have come to pay their respects."

Madam Li froze. "Are you certain it is the Second Young Mistress and Third Young Mistress of the Jiangdong Sun family who wish to see me?"

Old Madam Qin forgot entirely about attacking Qin Rujin, and immediately demanded in a sharp voice: "You there—speak clearly! Have the Second Young Mistress and Third Young Mistress of the Jiangdong Sun family truly come to the Qin household?"

If so, since they were the sisters-in-law of Madam Sun from her own family, they ought first to call upon her—this very Grand Matron—before anyone else.

But after listening to the servant woman from the First Branch swear up and down that it was so, Old Madam Qin could produce no further sound.

How could those two have gone straight past her to seek out Madam Li? What did they take her for, this Grand Matron?

For the women of the Jiangdong Sun family to show such disrespect—outrageous!

Old Madam Qin was dizzy with fury, her vision going dark. Then a little maid from her own courtyard came to report that the Second Young Master and Third Young Master of the Jiangdong Sun family had arrived to pay their respects to the Grand Matron.

So they had split up to call on different people.

Old Madam Qin's heart lifted considerably.

Madam Li had already taken Qin Rujin and made her farewells. Old Madam Qin raised her voice sharply: "Halt!"

"Eldest daughter-in-law, the Jiangdong Sun family is still the kinfolk of the Second Branch. With your Second Sister-in-law currently away from the household, the two young Sun mistresses have naturally come to call upon you, the First Mistress of the Qin household... but mind your words! If you say anything wrong, this old one will not let it pass!"

Madam Li gave a cold sniff, turned back, and looked Old Madam Qin up and down with a mocking appraisal. "Old Madam need not worry—a clear conscience fears no ghosts at the door!"

And with that, she swept out, pulling Qin Rujin along with her.

She couldn't wait.

The Jiangdong Sun family was as formidable as expected—they had come knocking this fast!

The moment Madam Li said those words, Old Madam Qin felt a jolt in her heart.

Terrible! Could it be that that foolish woman had told her own family about Rong Gong's kept mistress?

Otherwise, why would the Sun family's young masters and young mistresses—who came to the capital no more than twice a year—show up all at once at this particular moment?

That insufferable madwoman!

And she was supposed to be the most prized daughter of the Jiangdong Sun family...

Realizing how bad things had become, Old Madam Qin called out in rapid succession for someone to come.

"Quickly, quickly, quickly! Send someone to the court gate at once—the moment the Second Master and Third Young Master come down from office, have them return home immediately!"

chapters.title: Amicable Separation or Divorce—Pick One
chapters.content:
Madam Li swept Qin Rujin back to the Western Courtyard.

Though Qin Rujin was not yet a married woman, the two Sun sister-in-laws had come to the First Branch as guests, so she too should go to receive them.

"So this must be the Sixth Young Miss—truly a poised and graceful young lady!" The Second Young Mistress of the Sun family praised her as she looked her over, and as she did, she slipped a jade bangle from her own wrist and onto Qin Rujin's.

Qin Rujin looked to her mother Madam Li. The Third Young Mistress of Sun pulled her to her side.

She took a red-gold bangle with ruby inlay from her own hair, and pressed it into the bun at Qin Rujin's temple.

Smiling, she said: "You must keep it—my Second Sister-in-law and I took one look at the Sixth Young Miss and loved her. You will be married soon, and we two sisters-in-law are giving you a little trousseau addition—nothing valuable, just a token."

Her words were modest, but in truth, whether it was the Second Young Mistress's jade bangle or her own red-gold ruby phoenix hairpin, both were of considerable value.

Madam Li nodded to Qin Rujin: "Quickly—thank the two young Sun mistresses for their kindness!"

Qin Rujin thanked the two Sun mistresses, and a little maid came in from outside to report.

Madam Sun had come over with her two young sons.

After the ladies had exchanged greetings, Madam Sun's two sons stepped forward to bow to their elders.

The Second and Third Young Mistresses of Sun drew both boys into their arms and praised them warmly.

The two boys then went over to find Qin Rujin.

"Sixth Auntie, those wooden card games you made are so useful—Didi and I have memorized them all! Do you have any more?" Zheng'er looked at Qin Rujin with eager eyes.

Madam Sun moved to stop him, but Qin Rujin gave Madam Sun a small shake of her head.

She smiled: "Third Sister-in-law, while you adults talk, I'll take Zheng'er and Shen'er to play with their cousins."

Madam Sun smiled and nodded her assent.

Qin Rujin crouched down to speak to Zheng'er and Shen'er: "Your cousins have different wooden cards—you can look at theirs first and trade. Once Sixth Auntie has some free time, I'll make you more. Agreed?"

Both boys were wonderfully well-behaved. They nodded obediently and smiled with pure, sweet sincerity.

The Second and Third Young Mistresses of Sun exchanged a satisfied glance.

"The Sixth Young Miss is so patient—and see how the children adore her!" The Second Young Mistress of Sun said approvingly.

Madam Sun nodded in agreement: "Second Sister-in-law, Third Sister-in-law, you don't know—I owe so much to the Sixth Young Miss! These past few days, I've stopped making the children wake early to read and practice their brushwork—I've let them sleep until they wake on their own. I've also been following the medicinal diet recipes the Sixth Young Miss gave me. From this morning until now, neither child has coughed once..."

Madam Sun was genuinely and deeply grateful to Qin Rujin.

The Second and Third Young Mistresses were also delighted to hear it, and both poured even more enthusiastic praise upon Qin Rujin to Madam Li.

Madam Li was full of pride, though she kept a modest tongue: "My Sixth Girl has loved reading miscellaneous books since she was small. When she was little, I scolded her for reading them in secret—I was afraid she'd pick up bad habits... Who would have imagined she'd actually manage to learn tricks that other people don't know? Take those medicinal diet recipes she gave Wenlan—I was still worried they weren't trustworthy. But Wenlan used them on the children and they worked—truly, thank heaven and earth!"

Madam Sun then went on to tell her two sisters-in-law, in detail, everything Qin Rujin had said to her heart-to-heart that day.

After hearing it, the Second and Third Young Mistresses of the Sun family were deeply moved inwardly.

They were both mothers themselves, and they understood perfectly: if that Sixth Young Miss had not been a kind-hearted person who truly cared for her nephew-in-laws, she could never have taken such pains.

To advise an unrelated sister-in-law to let the children sleep enough, and then to provide medicinal diet recipes on top of that—neither of those things was easy to do without risking being misunderstood. Someone ungrateful might even think the Sixth Young Miss was deliberately keeping the children from their studies, or intentionally trying to harm them with dubious recipes...

No wonder their little sister-in-law, clearly belonging to the Second Branch of the Qin family, had still chosen the First Branch.

The Sixth Young Miss truly deserved it!

The two sisters-in-law exchanged another glance and silently resolved to add several more layers to the trousseau they would give the Sixth Young Miss.

At the Hall of Longevity and Peace.

Old Madam Qin personally entertained the Sun family's Second and Third Young Masters.

After sitting down, host and guests, Old Madam Qin chatted with the two Sun young masters.

Though the Second Young Master of the Sun family was the elder, Old Madam Qin preferred talking with the Third.

After all, the Second Young Master of the Sun family had been notorious for his dissolute ways in his youth, and at over thirty, was still merely muddling along in the Imperial Guards with a fifth-rank military post.

The Third Young Master of the Sun family was entirely different. At only twenty-seven or twenty-eight, he was a fourth-rank chief superintendent in the Maritime Trade Division of the Ministry of Revenue.

A position of real authority.

Only, the Third Young Master of the Sun family was upright and steady by nature, and given that the Qin family had mistreated his sister, he had absolutely no desire to respond to Old Madam Qin's deliberate attempts to ingratiate herself.

It was the Second Young Master who was worldly and smooth—however furious he might be inwardly, his face remained smiling.

Old Madam Qin settled considerably.

Thinking that both brothers seemed to be in a reasonable temper—especially the Second Young Master of the Sun family, who seemed to show her the proper respect due an elder.

She concluded that the Sun family must have realized that a married daughter is water spilled from a bucket—once poured, the Sun family could not truly cause trouble for her Qin family.

Not only couldn't they cause trouble—perhaps this visit by the two young couples was actually to mediate peace between Madam Sun and her eldest grandson Rong Gong.

Thinking along these lines, the old woman once again began leaning on her age and seniority.

Yet she failed to reflect on why the Second and Third Young Mistresses of the Sun family had not come together to pay their respects to her, and had instead gone to the First Branch.

The Third Young Master of the Sun family was already barely containing himself, held back only by a glance from the Second Young Master.

Old Madam Qin, though ostensibly praising Madam Sun to the Sun brothers, kept implying between her words that Madam Sun was no longer young, had only two children, and ought to be magnanimous and broad-minded enough to allow her husband to take concubines and multiply his descendants.

At those words, the Second Young Master of the Sun family smiled even more broadly, bowed with a cupped-fist salute, and said: "Grand Matron, there is something in what you say. However, this would violate the oath that was made between Qin Rong Gong and our Sun family at the outset."

Old Madam Qin frowned, though she kept the smile on her old face.

She stared fixedly at the Second Young Master of the Sun family and replied: "Second Young Master, as they say, times change. For families like ours, the most important thing is the proliferation of the clan and a household full of descendants."

"Our Rong Gong, all these years, has given your sister every last corner of his heart—there has never even been a bed-warming maid in his inner chambers!"

"By that measure, even among all the young gentlemen in this capital city, how many could match our Rong Gong?" Old Madam Qin smiled thinly.

"Second Young Master, Third Young Master—could even you achieve this? You couldn't, could you?"

Anger had already appeared on the Third Young Master of the Sun family's face. The Second Young Master was still smiling, but the warmth had entirely left his voice.

"Grand Matron is right. Only, we young men of our family had no need to deceive our wives' families or make solemn oaths to lure young ladies into marriage, and then break those oaths afterward."

Old Madam Qin nearly choked with rage.

The Third Young Master of the Sun family was finished letting his brother dance around this old woman.

He stood up abruptly and fixed Old Madam Qin with a direct stare: "Grand Matron, whether Qin Rong Gong wants three wives and four concubines or wants to surround himself with women is his own business—but why did he have to act like a petty man, keeping a secret mistress hidden outside, and hide it only from our sister? To torment her?"

"The elders of your entire Qin family knew about it. Even your Fifth Young Miss, who isn't yet married, knew about it. And yet only our sister was kept in the dark—just to make her miserable?"

Old Madam Qin's face went dark.

So the matter of Rong Gong's kept mistress had come out after all...

"Third Young Master... this matter isn't as serious as all that. It's not that Rong Gong deliberately hid it from Wenlan—it was..."

The Third Young Master of the Sun family laughed coldly: "Then he still did this shameless and despicable thing while hiding it from Wenlan!"

Seeing his attitude, Old Madam Qin knew this matter couldn't be resolved with pleasant words.

She was blunt: "So let Third Young Master and Second Young Master say it plainly—what must our Second Branch of the Qin family do for Wenlan to stop being angry?"

Seeing her respond like a common street brawler, the Third Young Master of the Sun family flung his sleeve in fury.

The smile vanished entirely from the Second Young Master's face. He said coldly: "Our Wenlan has no need to be angry at Qin Rong Gong. Since this matter has come to light, it is your Second Branch of the Qin family—it is Qin Rong Gong—who has broken his word. Wenlan has no wish to spend her life with such a faithless man. Let the Sun and Qin families part on good terms—let the two of them separate amicably, and from now on, each goes their own way."

"Amicable separation? The Sun family wants an amicable separation?" Old Madam Qin shot to her feet, her eyes wide with shock.

She had absolutely not expected the Sun family to be this uncompromising—no mediation, no negotiation, just a direct demand for separation!

Amicable separations in the great Ying dynasty were not unheard of, but they were certainly not easy to arrange.

Madam Sun Wenlan had already borne two legitimate grandsons for her Qin family—and now she wanted to separate?

Absolutely impossible!

Old Madam Qin gathered herself and slowly sat back down.

She gave a cold laugh and, with raised eyebrows, fixed the Sun brothers with her gaze.

"Two Young Masters, forgive this old one for speaking plainly—an amicable separation is utterly out of the question! Our Qin family does not agree."

The Sun brothers only laughed coldly.

Old Madam Qin drew a slow, deep breath and tried to persuade:

"Wenlan has already borne two legitimate grandsons for our Qin family—our family values her deeply. It is merely that our Rong Gong committed the small transgression that all men are prone to committing. Surely this can be discussed calmly—why must it go all the way to separation?"

"Whatever demands your Sun family has, feel free to raise them. This old one can promise: if it is within our Qin family's power to do it, we will certainly do it!"

The Second Young Master of the Sun family frowned: "Grand Matron, our brothers came today specifically to discuss this with you in good faith. Must you force the Sun family to resort to harsher measures?"

Old Madam Qin's eyes narrowed, and she kept up a brittle show of confidence: "What's this? Are the two of you threatening to use the Sun family's power to coerce our Qin family?"

The Second Young Master of the Sun family set aside all pleasantries and said, cold and flat: "What if the Sun family does use its power to coerce your Qin family—what would you do about it?"

The Third Young Master laughed coldly: "Either an amicable separation, or she casts him off. Your Qin family—pick one."

chapters.title: A Deal with the First Branch of the Qin Family
chapters.content:
"Ca...cas...cast him off?" Old Madam Qin's vision went dark. Her perfectly fine eldest grandson—that dragon among men—was going to be cast off by Madam Sun Wenlan?

Was there no justice left in the world?

"How... how dare the Sun family even suggest casting him off?" Old Madam Qin could no longer maintain the poise and dignity of a matriarch long accustomed to authority. She shot to her feet, pointing at the two Sun brothers.

"The Sun family is truly going too far—do you take our Qin family for people with no one to speak for them?"

The Second Young Master of the Sun family rose, and said to the Third: "We have delivered the message. Let us take our leave of the Grand Matron and go pay our respects to the First Master of the Qin family."

The two brothers still bowed a proper and courteous farewell to Old Madam Qin. She couldn't even stay sitting—her whole body went limp.

She called out after the Sun brothers: "Why... why do you need to see our First Master? This is a matter for our Second Branch..."

The Sun brothers paid not the slightest attention, as if they hadn't heard a word.

At the same moment, in the Western Courtyard.

After listening to what the two Sun young mistresses had to say, Madam Li was silent for a long while.

She looked at Madam Sun with the sympathetic eyes of a woman: "Wenlan, do you truly wish to go to this extreme?"

Madam Sun smiled softly and nodded with resolve.

"First Aunt by marriage, I cannot go on living with Qin Rong Gong."

"When I first married him, it was not my own wish—his mother used despicable means to ruin my reputation... Later, I thought: as long as Qin Rong Gong truly kept his oath to my Sun family and wasn't like his mother... well, he might not be such a bad person to entrust one's life to. Unfortunately..."

Madam Sun shook her head with a bitter smile: "Wenlan was too naive. I made things too simple, and thought too well of him."

Then Madam Sun's gaze became resolute and clear. She said firmly: "In our Sun family, our ancestors left behind one rule handed down through the generations: once faithless, forever unforgivable."

"If he hadn't sworn that oath before my Sun family's elders and then broken it—if I were the one clinging to this and refusing to let go, I could acknowledge that I was not being magnanimous enough..."

Madam Sun tucked a strand of hair behind her ear. "But it was Qin Rong Gong who was faithless. So there is nothing more to be said."

Madam Li could only nod and didn't know what to say to comfort Madam Sun.

She could only say with concern: "If you want to separate from Rong Gong, then your family back home..."

The Second Young Mistress of Sun immediately said: "First Mistress, be at ease. Our entire Sun family, from the eldest to the youngest, supports Wenlan in seeking separation!"

Madam Li felt relief on Madam Sun's behalf, and a touch of envy as well.

It was because she had such a family behind her—one that truly loved their daughter—that Madam Sun had the courage to be absolutely unyielding and insist on separation.

"That is all for the best. Wenlan has noble character, and she's just past the age of blossoming—her appearance goes without saying. Leaving a man who was never worthy of her in the first place can only lead to a better life!" Madam Li said from the heart.

Madam Sun gave her a grateful smile.

Madam Li asked again: "Seeking an amicable separation won't be easy—setting aside everything else, there are the children..."

The Third Young Mistress of Sun immediately said: "First Mistress, our Sun family will be taking both boys!"

Madam Li was taken aback.

"Wenlan wants to take both boys with her?"

Madam Sun nodded.

The Second Young Mistress of Sun also said: "Of course we must take both boys. The roots of the Qin Second Branch are crooked at the core—how could our Sun family bear to leave those two fine boys behind in the Qin household?"

Madam Li absolutely agreed with those words.

"If the Sun family raises both boys, that is naturally the best possible outcome. But..." Madam Li gave Madam Sun a worried look: "Wenlan, I fear it will be very difficult."

Madam Sun pressed her lips together.

The Second Young Mistress of Sun thought for a moment, then spoke.

"First Mistress, you have been genuinely kind to us—our Sun family is truly grateful, both to you and to the Sixth Young Miss."

"The Qin Second Branch will agree. They cannot bear the consequences of refusing..."

Madam Li's expression grew alert.

Knowing that the Second Young Mistress of Sun dared to say such a thing, the Sun family must certainly have a decisive card to play.

So she said no more.

In the First Master of the Qin family's study.

He had not expected the two elder brothers of Madam Sun to come to call on him after seeing Old Madam Qin.

The Sun brothers showed him far more genuine respect than they had shown Old Madam Qin.

A respect that came from the heart.

Not entirely because Madam Sun had written in her letters that Qin Rujin, the Sixth Young Miss, had done things for her two nephews.

The Sun family's roots in the court ran far deeper than the Qin Second Branch. They had long known that the First Master of the Qin family, Qin Mingfeng, was a capable man who could actually get things done.

Only in years past, for the sake of their sister, the Sun family had stood by and watched while the Second Master of the Qin family quietly worked to suppress the First Master.

After all, their sister was the daughter-in-law of the Qin Second Branch.

They had not wanted to see the Qin First Branch rise to prominence and then retaliate against the Second Branch.

Even if the First Branch of the Qin family were straightforward and upright people who would not target the Second Branch women who had married in, there was still the matter of the impact on their nephews' interests.

The Sun family had never been ashamed of their own self-interest.

Now, for the sake of their sister's and nephews' interests, they were equally willing to join forces with the Qin First Branch—even at great cost.

The First Master of the Qin family listened to what the two Sun brothers had to say, and was not surprised.

The Sun family was not to be trifled with.

He knew it, and Qin Mingjie knew it too.

Unfortunately, Qin Mingjie had grown accustomed to smooth sailing over the years. He had assumed that since Rong Gong had married a Sun daughter, Madam Sun would be unable to stir up any real trouble within the Qin inner quarters.

Qin Mingjie had not forgotten how fearsome the Jiangdong Sun family was—he had simply looked down on Madam Sun, believing that as a married-out daughter, she was entirely subject to the Qin family's will.

But what was the result?

People must always pay a heavy price for their own arrogance.

"Two Young Sirs—have you already discussed this matter with your family's elders?" The First Master of the Qin family asked nothing else—just this one question.

Both the Second and Third Young Masters of the Sun family nodded.

The Second Young Master added: "Within three days, my elder brother will arrive. He will represent our Sun family in formally proposing the separation to Second Master Qin."

"If the Second Branch of the Qin family refuses to agree, our Sun family will proceed with casting him off," the Third Young Master added.

The First Master of the Qin family nodded: "This matter—your Sun family and the young mistress have made your decision; proceed with it then. Our Qin First Branch has always been at odds with the Second Branch, as I'm sure both Young Sirs know. It is not easy for me to intervene."

The Second Young Master of the Sun family said earnestly: "You are right—our Sun family understands that well. Writing two different characters for 'Qin' is still impossible; our sister's separation from Qin Rong Gong, once made public, will inevitably affect the later generations of the Qin First Branch somewhat..."

The First Master of the Qin family raised his hand to cut off the Second Young Master's words.

"That is of no concern. To be frank with both Young Sirs—our First Branch has lived under the Second Branch's suppression for all these years, until the First Branch no longer resembles a First Branch, and the Second Branch no longer resembles a Second Branch. The family clan has always sided with the Second Branch. I have been thinking of splitting the clan."

The First Master of the Qin family was not afraid to speak of this matter with the two Sun family brothers.

This event would be yet another severe blow to the Qin Second Branch.

The First Master of the Qin family was pleased to see it.

Since the two Sun brothers had come to see him—and had revealed the Sun family's plans so openly—they clearly wanted something from the First Branch.

They were suggesting cooperation.

The Sun brothers were startled to hear this.

"Qin Uncle, is that truly so?" The Second Young Master immediately changed his form of address—if this were true, it was an unexpected windfall.

Originally, the Sun family had been concerned that the First Branch of the Qin family might step in to try to keep the two young nephews with the Qin family.

If the First Branch had intervened, the Sun family would have had difficulty pressing too hard.

The First Branch of the Qin family might not appear prominent at the moment, but they were the main line of descent of this branch of the Qin family—its rightful and legitimate trunk.

Many things worked differently when the First Branch intervened versus when the Second Branch acted on its own.

Not to mention: the First Branch's maternal family, the Xianyang Wei family, was not to be trifled with.

The Sun family had already made inquiries—ever since the Sixth Young Miss of the Qin First Branch had become engaged to the Jiang family, the Xianyang Wei family had begun to make moves.

These things, women in the inner quarters wouldn't know about and wouldn't pay attention to. But those of them who moved in the world outside understood it perfectly.

The Qin Second Branch could no longer hold the First Branch down.

Of course, the marriage alliance between the Qin First Branch and the Jiang family was merely the icing on the cake.

In truth, this First Master of the Qin family had been enduring patiently for so many years—sooner or later, he would have broken free from the Qin Second Branch's shackles on his own.

"That is the intention—though it is not easy to achieve." The First Master of the Qin family raised his teacup and took a sip, then smiled: "It will come in time."

The Second Young Master of the Sun family immediately said: "If in the future Qin Uncle has need of the Sun family, please do not hesitate to ask!"

"Thank you!" The First Master of the Qin family set down his teacup and returned the cupped-fist salute.

The Second Young Master of the Sun family rose at once to return the bow, then said: "Qin Uncle, the Sun family has one further imposition to ask of you!"

"Please speak, Young Sir." The First Master of the Qin family nodded.

After hearing the Second Young Master's request, the First Master of the Qin family stroked his beard.

Under the anxious gaze of the two Sun brothers, he said gently: "Just have Zheng'er and Shen'er entered in the name of my eldest son, Rong Cheng, for now. If your Sun family has other plans later on, we can speak of them then."

The Sun brothers were astonished.

Their Sun family had only sought to have the two young nephews registered under the name of the Second Young Master Qin Rong Lie of the Qin First Branch.

They had not wished to do anything ungrateful or ungracious—they had no desire to take the name of the legitimate eldest grandson from the head of the Senior Line.

The First Master of the Qin family smiled: "There is no need for that. Zheng'er and Shen'er are by rights the legitimate eldest and second grandsons of this branch of the Qin family. Besides, as the Young Sirs have said—you merely wish the two boys to have a proper belonging; they are sons of the Qin family."

"For that alone, I should be the one thanking your Sun family for your great righteousness!"

The First Master of the Qin family's words moved the Sun brothers deeply.

They also understood, at last, why their sister had insisted on choosing the First Branch of the Qin family.

Truly, the people of the Qin First Branch were upright, tolerant, and magnanimous.

That Qin Mingjie and his son weren't fit to carry the First Branch's shoes!

The First Master of the Qin family had thought it through very clearly.

His eldest son had not yet produced a son; his second son's boy was only two years old—younger than Madam Sun's two children.

The legitimate eldest grandson of this branch of the Qin family was, by right, Madam Sun's Zheng'er. What was there to argue about?

Even after the First Branch split from the clan, there was no problem. The two boys Madam Sun had borne would be raised by Madam Sun and the Sun family, taken back to Jiangdong.

He also knew that Madam Sun had, in her youth, almost been betrothed to the Third Young Master of the Jiangdong Lu family... In the future, whether Madam Sun might take the two boys and remarry was hard to say.

His gesture of goodwill would earn the gratitude of the Jiangdong Sun family. It was a worthwhile exchange.

The two Sun brothers tried to press further, but the First Master of the Qin family smiled: "My second son already has a son. It's my eldest who has only two daughters—giving those two girls two brothers is a fine thing."

The two Sun brothers exchanged a glance.

Then both brothers expressed their gratitude once more.

"Well then—our Zheng'er and Shen'er having two elder sisters could not be better! That means our Sun family has gained two nieces as well!"

That promise carried real weight.

It meant the Jiangdong Sun family was formally acknowledging their kinship with Qin Rong Cheng of the Qin First Branch.

The First Master of the Qin family did not decline, and accepted with a smile.

The Sun brothers also expressed that the separation would be timed to avoid disrupting the joyous occasion of the Sixth Young Miss's upcoming wedding, so as not to cause any trouble for her.

The First Master of the Qin family also expressed his gratitude for that.

Whether one called it a negotiation or a deal—an agreement had been reached.

Both sides were satisfied.

As evening fell, the sky blazed with a magnificent crimson that was beautiful beyond words.

Yet the faces of the Second Master of the Qin family Qin Mingjie and his eldest son Qin Rong Gong were dark enough to drip water.

Father and son, heedless of their colleagues' greetings, boarded their carriage and rushed home at speed.

Before the carriage had even come to a full stop, both jumped out, and hurried straight toward Old Madam Qin's Hall of Longevity and Peace.

chapters.title: Malicious Siblings
chapters.content:
Old Madam Qin was both furious and anxious, and had taken to her bed.

When the father and son went to see her, they found her lying there, a cloth band tied around her forehead, moaning and crying out.

It gave them quite a fright.

When the father and son learned from Old Madam Qin's own lips that the Sun brothers had brought their wives to call—and that it was to discuss a formal separation from the Qin family—both stared with wide, bulging eyes in fury.

"Separation?" Qin Rong Gong, young and unable to contain himself, gave a harsh, cold laugh: "They want to separate just because the Sun family wants to? What does that make our Qin family? What does that make me, Qin Rong Gong?"

"Who told you to swear that oath to the Sun family back then? Now they're holding your broken word against you—what can you do about it? Aiyo..." Old Madam Qin was so furious she collapsed heavily back onto the bed.

The Second Master of the Qin family Qin Mingjie also gave this son he had always prided himself on a kick in the gut.

"Look at what you've done! Your mother's family is so formidable—and you still dared to keep a secret mistress behind Madam Sun's back!"

The kick had truly hurt, and Qin Rong Gong gave up all pretense of filial propriety: "And what are you scolding me for, Father? Didn't Father also keep that Ruiniang outside as a mistress?"

"Besides—when Mother used those methods to force the Sun family into agreeing to give Madam Sun to us, it still didn't work. I had no other choice but to swear that oath. Who could have known they'd take it so seriously?"

"And besides, it was I who came up with the idea of taking an oath—Father and Grandmother both agreed to it at the time!"

Qin Mingjie swayed on his feet, his old face turning crimson, and then he erupted, roaring and cursing at his eldest son.

"Oh, what a disaster! What a disaster!" Old Madam Qin beat the bed's edge with her fists. "You're at each other's throats when the house is on fire..."

Just then, a servant woman came to report at the door: that the Sun family's young masters and their wives had left the Western Courtyard and were heading toward the Hall of Longevity and Peace.

So they had been waiting until Qin Mingjie and his son returned from court. Both couples had stayed—naturally, a separation was something to be discussed fully with the person directly involved and the head of the household.

"They still haven't left?" Qin Rong Gong was furious.

He and his father had rushed home only wanting to consult with his grandmother on how to deal with this situation.

He had no desire to face the Sun family directly just yet.

Old Madam Qin's eyes were vacant. "They were waiting for you two, of course! Can't you see? This matter... cannot be resolved peaceably now."

* * *

In the Qiwu Garden.

Qin Ruyue, confined to quarters on orders from the Second Master, heard that the Sun family had sent people to the Qin household demanding a separation, and immediately forgot all about being under house arrest.

She swept out with her senior maid Hongsui and headed for Old Madam Qin's Hall of Longevity and Peace.

Her other senior maid, Xixin, had already taken the blame for her and been punished by Old Madam Qin.

She had been hauled onto a cart by the rough-handed servant women and married off that very day to the youngest son of a farm steward on one of Old Madam Qin's own estates.

A senior maid of a legitimate young miss—one who could, at the very least, have become a household manager's wife if she followed her mistress into marriage.

And now, she was ruined.

The next day, Qin Ruyue did give Xixin a parting trousseau, but it was worth only seventy or eighty taels of silver.

Xixin's father and brothers had also been implicated—they had been punished by the Second Master of the Qin family and reduced to servants doing rough labor at the Qin household.

Hongsui, loyal as she was to her mistress Qin Ruyue, felt a chill in her heart looking at all of this.

She couldn't help thinking: the Sixth Young Miss was better than this.

There had been a time during a tea-flower gathering when she'd followed the Fifth Young Miss and nearly tripped and fallen. Xixin had happened to be nearby and caught her. Later, through Zisu, the Sixth Young Miss had quietly sent her ten taels of silver as a trousseau gift—telling her to find a way to quietly pass it on to Xixin, now married off to that estate.

Whether the Sixth Young Miss had done it to win loyalty or not—at least she was willing to repay a debt of gratitude.

Following a mistress like that was following someone with a real future.

"Grandmother! Father! Elder Brother!" Qin Ruyue didn't even wait for anyone in the Hall of Longevity and Peace to announce her. She swept aside the bead curtain and barged into Old Madam Qin's inner room.

Old Madam Qin, seeing the house-arrested Qin Ruyue charge in, began coughing in agitation.

Qin Mingjie was furious.

Ruiniang had not ceased to blow in his ear, and his former overwhelming doting on this legitimate youngest daughter had turned to an equal and opposite disgust.

Qin Ruyue didn't even wait for him to erupt—she went straight to the point.

"Elder Brother must not separate from Elder Sister-in-law!"

"In the future, Elder Brother is the one who will help our Qin family rise to become a first-tier noble house of the capital! And for that, we cannot do without Elder Sister-in-law's family, the Sun family, behind us..."

She had only told Old Madam Qin this before.

Old Madam Qin, harried by one piece of bad news after another these past few days, had never had the opportunity to tell her son and grandson.

"Father! Elder Brother! You must believe me!" Qin Ruyue's gaze blazed with conviction, her whole body radiating a powerful certainty.

And that actually stopped Qin Mingjie and his son cold.

Even Old Madam Qin, lying in bed, grew calm.

That's right.

There was still the Fifth Girl.

Every dream of the Fifth Girl's had come true without exception.

"Fifth Girl—though what you say is so, the two elder brothers of your sister-in-law have made it clear that the Sun family intends to separate... If our Qin family refuses to agree, they will have Madam Sun cast him off!"

"Ca...cast him off? She wants to cast him off?" Qin Rong Gong shot to his feet in fury. "What utter insolence—she's a woman; what gives her the right to cast me off?"

Qin Ruyue quickly moved to calm her elder brother.

Qin Mingjie, after hearing Qin Ruyue's words, was already turning several thoughts over in his mind.

But after hearing Old Madam Qin say that if they refused a separation, the Sun family would have Madam Sun cast Rong Gong off, his face darkened again.

"Rong Gong, no matter what—you cannot agree to separate from Madam Sun!"

"You heard what Fifth Girl said too—she dreamed that you will lead our Qin family to rise to the heights of the capital's first-tier noble houses someday. Don't let yourself be confused over a woman!"

Qin Mingjie finally pulled himself free, temporarily, from the grip of Ruiniang's whispered influence.

Family comes first.

The Fifth Girl could not be abandoned—her predictive dreams were always right.

He had secretly asked an eminent monk about it—yes, there were people who, by some gift of heaven and earth, could dream in this prophetic way...

"Rong Gong—if necessary, that woman you've been keeping outside..." Qin Mingjie made a gesture of cutting a head off.

Qin Rong Gong's eyes filled with anguish, and his resolve wavered.

Huazhi was the woman he truly loved.

Apart from her low birth, she was perfect in every way—every part of her drew him in deeply.

He couldn't bear to give up Huazhi.

Yet the glory of the family name was more important still...

His heart felt as if it had been cleaved in two—on one side, Huazhi; on the other, the family's glory.

Just as Qin Rong Gong was in agonized deliberation, Old Madam Qin was also reminded of what Qin Ruyue had said about Huazhi and her child.

She frowned at Qin Ruyue again: "But Fifth Girl—you also said in that dream that Huazhi would bear your elder brother a pair of auspicious twin sons, and that our Qin family's legacy would depend on those twin sons in the future."

Qin Rong Gong's whole being shuddered.

"Father—Huazhi cannot be given up!"

He stared sharply at Qin Ruyue: "Then tell me—in my rise to lead the Qin family to the top of the capital's first-tier noble houses, who plays the decisive role?"

Qin Mingjie also fixed Qin Ruyue with a burning look.

In his heart, he was greatly impressed by his eldest son—young men really did have sharper wits. He had gone straight to the critical point.

Qin Ruyue frowned.

In truth, she was very reluctant to lay all her cards out before her father and brother.

But now she had already lost her father's affection...

"My dreams only show me the broad strokes—the fine details are not visible."

Qin Ruyue bit her lip. When she caught the cold, dark look her father Qin Mingjie was casting at her, her heart gave a violent shudder.

She was, after all, only a weak woman who depended on her mother for support.

Her thoughts moved swiftly, and she had no choice but to say: "It seems... in the dream, Prince An appeared."

"Prince An?" Qin Mingjie murmured to himself, and then a smile rose on his face.

He looked at Qin Rong Gong and said: "That aligns well—you are Prince An's study companion. His Highness naturally treats you differently from the others."

"Though you were distanced from His Highness somewhat over the affair with the Seventh Girl—that was unavoidable. But His Highness ultimately extended a helping hand, which shows he still thinks highly of you."

"After all, those stubborn old critics and young critics at the Censorate are all watching."

After a pause, Qin Mingjie continued: "This is exactly the time, Rong Gong, when you must hold steady—on no account neglect your private visits to His Highness Prince An."

Qin Mingjie's thoughts were going in the same direction, and a smile broke across his face.

Ha!

The Jiangdong Sun family.

They actually dared to be so overbearing—so utterly contemptuous of him, Qin Rong Gong.

Did they really think he couldn't manage without them?

Madam Sun wanted a separation and was threatening that if he refused she'd cast him off?

He would make her regret it!

If he couldn't touch the Jiangdong Sun family at large, couldn't he at least humiliate her—this daughter the Sun family had "spilled out of the bucket"?

"But Father—Elder Brother's second cousin gets along extremely well with Prince An..." Qin Ruyue, seeing her father and brother so carelessly overconfident, felt a chill in her heart.

She couldn't help but speak up to warn them.

But in the very next moment, Qin Mingjie dismissed her with contempt: "What does a girl in the inner quarters know?"

"Do you really think what leads your elder brother to lift the Qin family to greater heights is Madam Sun and the Sun family behind her?"

"Well, there is that..." Old Madam Qin said slowly, "The Jiangdong Sun family, however formidable, cannot stand against His Highness Prince An—a true son of Heaven's bloodline."

Prince An's birth mother, though the late Emperor had decreed she accompany him in death, had had all her dowry assets bestowed by imperial decree upon Prince An.

Prince An's mother's family had been keeping an extremely low profile over the years, never sticking their heads out.

No further word had been heard of Prince An's mother's family in the capital's noble circles.

But the quieter they were, the more it showed that Prince An was a man of great depth and vision—and that his mother's family was formidable as well.

Otherwise, considering Prince An had been living under the current Emperor's thumb all these years, it would have been miracle enough just to keep his life, let alone maintain his titles and fortune.

Qin Ruyue, seeing her father, brother, and grandmother all taking this attitude, felt even colder inwardly.

She closed her eyes, drew a slow breath, and then opened them again.

"Grandmother, Father, Elder Brother..." Qin Ruyue looked at each of them in turn.

"The Sun family still has its uses for us—why not first keep them placated? Elder Brother could quietly move Huazhi out of the capital, settle her elsewhere... As for Elder Sister-in-law—who can say but that one day her health might simply... give out?"

Old Madam Qin's heart gave a start. It was a good plan, she admitted.

But this girl's intentions...still so young, and already this vicious.

Qin Rong Gong's eyes lit up.

He knew perfectly well what his sister's words concealed.

Thinking it through carefully—this was indeed the best solution he could think of for now.

He would grit his teeth and endure the Sun family a little longer, endure Madam Sun a little longer.

"But what if the Sun family and Madam Sun still insist on having their way?" Qin Mingjie, at heart, was also in favor of this plan.

Yet he was worried.

He knew the Sun family's power all too well.

If they truly insisted, it would be very hard to manage.

Qin Ruyue said softly: "Father, Elder Sister-in-law is a mother. She cannot bear to leave Zheng'er and Shen'er."

"Good! That is what we'll do!" Qin Rong Gong laughed coldly and made up his mind.

At that moment, a servant woman outside the Hall of Longevity and Peace came to report: Madam Sun and her two elder brothers and their wives had arrived.

chapters.title: Not Even a Single Feather Left for the Qin Family
chapters.content:
"Fifth Girl—you must not be present at a time like this. Go to the back room and wait. No matter what happens, do not come out!" Old Madam Qin immediately turned to Qin Ruyue and issued an incontestable order.

Qin Ruyue also knew she should not be there at this moment. She acknowledged the order and retreated with Hongsui into the back room off Old Madam Qin's main chamber.

Qin Mingjie and Qin Rong Gong gave their robes a quick shake and straightened themselves up briefly.

Madam Sun entered with her two elder brothers and their wives.

Old Madam Qin put on a sickly, listless appearance.

When Madam Sun and the Sun brothers and their wives bowed in greeting, the old woman listlessly waved them off, saying she was unwell and they needn't stand on ceremony.

But neither Madam Sun nor her brothers and sisters-in-law played along.

After a moment, Madam Sun said coolly: "Since the Grand Matron is not feeling well, do please rest. My brothers only need to speak with Second Master Qin and Qin Rong Gong..."

Neither Old Madam Qin nor Qin Rong Gong's father and son looked pleased.

Madam Sun was no longer calling her mother-in-law or father-in-law, and referred to her husband by his full name, with no trace of deference.

Outrageous!

She was still the Qin family's daughter-in-law!

Old Madam Qin naturally had no wish to miss the great negotiation between the two families, so she sat back up, affecting her ailment.

She said: "Never mind—I am not too badly off. As long as our two families can resolve this matter satisfactorily, this old one would gladly lay down her life..."

Madam Sun and her brothers and sisters-in-law paid no attention to the old woman's hollow performance.

The Third Young Master of the Sun family came straight to the point and laid out the Sun family's intentions to the Qin father and son.

"Separation?" Qin Mingjie shook his teacup and shook his head. "Young Sir Sun, you are all still too young. A trifling matter between husband and wife—how can one simply talk about separation over a little thing like this?"

"If the world's married couples all separated over every little dispute, where would that lead?"

"Let's not be ruled by emotion. Rong Gong was in the wrong—our Qin family admits it! Whatever way Wenlan wishes to punish Rong Gong, I can promise: the elders of our Qin family will absolutely not interfere in the private affairs of husband and wife!"

The implication was clear: don't let your Sun family interfere in the private affairs of husband and wife either.

His high-minded speech was met only by cold laughter from the Sun brothers and sisters-in-law.

Qin Mingjie felt the Sun family youngsters were insulting him, and a flame of fury rose within him.

But remembering what the Fifth Girl had said, he forced his face into an even deeper smile.

"As for that young woman surnamed Hua—my intention is to quietly send her far, far away, so that she never again appears before Wenlan for the rest of her days. How would that be?"

Even Madam Sun herself couldn't suppress a cold laugh at that.

So—young or old, the Qin family still hadn't thought of giving up Huazhi.

She was actually a little curious now.

What in the world was so remarkable about that Huazhi? Mere beauty alone couldn't be enough to make all the Qin family—young and old—willing to risk offending her Sun family just to protect this woman...

Seeing Madam Sun and her brothers and sisters-in-law still utterly immovable, Qin Rong Gong's face darkened to the point where he nearly bit through his own tongue.

But unwilling to truly give up the Sun family's support, he forced a smile, walked to Madam Sun's side, and reached for her hand.

"Wenlan..."

Madam Sun put both hands behind her back and stepped two paces away. She looked at him, cold and remote.

Qin Rong Gong's hands found nothing. His handsome face stiffened.

The Second and Third Young Masters of the Sun family immediately stepped forward, shielding Madam Sun behind them.

"Wenlan, why this?" Qin Rong Gong pressed through gritted teeth.

"We have been husband and wife for so many years, and we have two sons together—do you truly want to separate from me?" he said, keeping his temper under tight control.

"You are not fit to mention my two sons!" Madam Sun said, cold as ice.

If he had his way in raising the boys, their health would only grow weaker and weaker—she would not be surprised if they ended up cripples.

Even for the sake of her two sons, she had to keep her resolve firm this time and see the separation through!

Qin Rong Gong's face fell, and he said with furious irritation: "I am their father—what do you mean, not fit to mention them?"

"Wenlan, Zheng'er and Shen'er are the sons of my Qin family! For you to act so impulsively—to make such a scene over a trifling matter and talk about separation—have you ever thought of the two boys?"

Madam Sun replied coldly: "It is precisely because I am thinking of them that I want to separate from you!"

Qin Rong Gong clenched his jaw, his chest aching as if his lungs were on fire.

"If we truly separate, those two boys will have no mother. Can you really bring yourself to do it? Can you truly bear to leave them?"

Madam Sun answered without a moment's hesitation: "You are wrong. They will not lose their mother. It is only you—their father—they will lose. Without you, they will only be better off."

That completely enraged both Old Madam Qin and Qin Mingjie.

Old Madam Qin struck first: "Wenlan, what do you mean by this? You want to separate, and you want to take my two precious great-grandchildren with you?"

Qin Mingjie brought his fist down hard on the tea table: "Impossible! Out of the question! Madam Sun—you've lost your mind!"

He could no longer contain himself.

Look around this whole capital—find a woman who, after separating, takes the husband's children with her? Even if it were just two granddaughters, he would not let them be taken. How much less his two eldest grandsons!

"Separation out of the question—taking my grandsons even more out of the question!" Qin Mingjie gritted his teeth and stared at the Sun brothers: "If the Qin family woman can truly not go on living with our Qin family's son, then she may request to be let go of her own accord!"

Being "let go of one's own accord" meant being cast down from one's position. In the great Ying dynasty, a woman who had been "let go" was one who had committed a serious fault and been dismissed by her husband's family—or one who had been demoted from wife to concubine by her husband's family.

Qin Mingjie was using this threat to force the Sun family and Madam Sun to back down from the separation, to make them think twice.

The Qin family could only accept Madam Sun being "let go"—but the Sun family would have to seriously consider that, wouldn't they?

Besides, if Madam Sun actually agreed to be "let go," it wouldn't be a bad outcome for the Qin family either.

Even without the Sun family as in-laws, being "let go" meant she could not take her dowry with her.

And Madam Sun's dowry—even the Second Master Qin Mingjie had always looked upon it with covetous eyes... If it could all stay for his sons and grandsons to use, the Qin family would not have lost at all.

Madam Sun and her brothers and sisters-in-law all looked displeased.

A dark smile of triumph rose on Qin Rong Gong's handsome face.

In his heart, he inwardly praised his father—old ginger really was spicier than young. Well played.

With this move, let's see if Madam Sun still dared to talk of separation!

"Qin Rong Gong—have you truly forgotten, clean as a whistle, how you and the Tong mother and son plotted and schemed to ruin Wenlan's reputation all those years ago?"

Into the long silence, the Second Young Mistress of the Sun family suddenly shot to her feet, staring at Qin Rong Gong with ferocious eyes.

Thinking of what had happened back then still made her heart ache with hatred.

Back then, their little sister-in-law Wenlan was not yet engaged.

Because the Second and Third families had both settled in the capital, and both her husband and the third family's younger brother-in-law held offices in the capital, Wenlan was always going back and forth between Jiangdong and the capital.

The girl got along not only well with her brothers but extremely well with all the sisters-in-law, too.

She and the other sisters-in-law regarded Wenlan as their own little sister.

That year, her own family had been hosting a peony banquet, and she had brought Wenlan back to help with preparations.

Unexpectedly, something happened on the day of the banquet.

Wenlan had been knocked over at the party by a little maid carrying a basin of water, and was soaked through.

Wenlan's own senior maid had somehow gotten a bad stomach, and was led away by the senior maid of the host's elder sister-in-law to go change her clothes.

No sooner had the host's sister-in-law's maid taken up post outside the door than she fainted. Qin Rong Gong—drunk and shouting—stumbled through the door of the very room where Wenlan was changing.

Qin Rong Gong's drunken shouting drew a few noble young ladies who had been enjoying the garden—and with Wenlan hiding inside the room unwilling to come out, she had no way to explain herself.

At the time, the Qin Second Madam had been notified and came rushing over, and on the spot gave Qin Rong Gong several resounding slaps. Some people praised her for being upright and not shielding her own...

Afterward, Madam Tong brought lavish gifts and led Qin Rong Gong in person to the Jiangdong Sun family to apologize.

She had also brought the official matchmaker.

In the end, Wenlan, for the sake of her family and the family's later generations, and seeing Qin Rong Gong's earnest oath, agreed to marry into the Qin family despite her lesser inclination.

The in-laws, elder and younger alike, had comforted her—said it wasn't her fault, told her not to dwell on it... but the matter had stayed buried in her heart, and had never truly passed.

Afterward, she still could not reconcile herself to the fact that her wonderful Wenlan—the Wenlan who had been on the verge of a betrothal with the Third Young Master of the Lu family of Jiangdong—had been taken by that pile of dung that was the Qin family.

So without telling her in-laws, she had consulted with her husband and, with the support and help of their family, spent two full years quietly investigating what had truly happened.

Sadly, Madam Tong had acted with considerable ruthlessness—by the time they got to the truth, the witnesses were either dead or had disappeared without a trace...

For a long time, this matter had been kept from the Sun family's elders and from Wenlan.

Only because by the time she had uncovered the truth, Wenlan had already given birth to Zheng'er.

And Qin Rong Gong had indeed been keeping his oath in those years.

But vile people, no matter how long they dissemble, will always, one day, reveal their true and ugly nature.

Qin Rong Gong was jolted to his core, but swiftly steadied himself.

He looked coldly at the Second Young Mistress of the Sun family: "What are you implying, Second Sister-in-law? To insult my mother like this is truly going too far!"

Qin Mingjie also frowned.

Old Madam Qin, who had been half-lying on the bed, was so startled by the Second Young Mistress of the Sun family's words that she reared up like a cornered old fox, staring at the Second Young Mistress with fierce, fixed eyes.

The Second Young Mistress of the Sun family met Qin Rong Gong's gaze coldly.

"What happened that day occurred at my own family's home—I have never stopped investigating! Do you really think that just because you got rid of the witnesses, no one would ever know the truth?"

Qin Rong Gong's eyelids flickered twice.

But thinking that his mother had personally seen to it at the time, leaving no one alive, he felt steady again.

"To frame a man—there's no shortage of ways to go about it. I truly don't know what Second Sister-in-law is talking about." So long as there were no witnesses, what could the Sun family do, even knowing the truth?

Qin Rong Gong's brazen shamelessness made the Second Young Mistress of the Sun family shake with rage.

The Second Young Master of the Sun family, seeing this, narrowed his eyes to sharp, hard slits.

"Qin Rong Gong—the Sun family has never publicized that shameful incident. You assumed that was because the Sun family dared not make it public, did you?"

"If the world worked the way my second sister-in-law has just described—where a person can be convicted on a word alone—the prisons would be full long since," Qin Rong Gong scoffed, looking at the Second Young Master of the Sun family. "Isn't that so, Second Elder Brother?"

The Second Young Master of the Sun family held Qin Rong Gong's gaze and said, unhurried: "Then—what if I were to raise this matter with His Highness Prince An?"

Qin Rong Gong swayed on his feet. His face turned instantly white as snow.

It was Qin Mingjie who held steady. He said with a cold laugh: "In that case, Young Sir of the Sun family, please by all means go speak to His Highness. This old one trusts that His Highness is a wise and discerning judge who would not listen to an unsubstantiated claim."

"And further—this old one will memorialize the Emperor and have the Censorate investigate the Sun family for slandering and blackening the name of our Qin family!"

Madam Sun immediately said: "Then please, Second Master Qin, by all means write your memorial to the Emperor. If our Sun family has indeed made groundless accusations, the Emperor is clearly wise—he will certainly send men to investigate and get to the truth of this matter."

Qin Mingjie went rigid.

Madam Sun looked at her two brothers: "Second Brother, Third Brother—since the Qin family refuses to accept an amicable separation, there is nothing more to discuss. When Elder Brother arrives in the capital, let Elder Brother memorialize the Emperor to seek justice on my behalf."

The meaning was clear: she intended to take this all the way.

She wanted the current Emperor himself to order a full investigation into what had happened years ago.

It sounded absurd—yet if the Jiangdong Sun family truly pushed for a full investigation, the Emperor would indeed grant the Sun family that much face, and order one.

Of that, Qin Mingjie had not the slightest doubt.

He ground his teeth in hatred so fierce he nearly shattered them.

This Madam Sun was truly formidable—she had gone straight for the jugular...

"Madam Sun—are you truly this heartless?" Qin Mingjie's face was shadowed with darkness. "Is there truly no alternative to separation?"

"I want only the amicable separation—and to take Zheng'er and Shen'er with me." Madam Sun said, steady and clear: "And, of course, every last piece of my dowry."

She would not leave the Qin family so much as a single feather!

chapters.title: Disgrace
chapters.content:
What a Disgrace!

Qin Mingjie fell silent. Qin Rong Gong unclenched his back molars.

Old Madam Qin nearly wept with rage.

"Why is the Second Master not speaking?" The Third Young Master of the Sun family looked over with cold eyes.

"Just as my sister says—if your Qin family still insists on refusing the conditions my sister has put forward, then the Sun and Qin families will settle the matter before the Emperor."

With that, he didn't bother looking at the Qin family again. He said to his own brothers and sisters-in-law: "Second Brother, Second Sister-in-law—let's take Sister and the two nephews and go back to the Sun residence."

The Second Young Master of the Sun family agreed.

"Hold on!" Qin Mingjie called out, his old face gone dark: "If a separation is to happen, it can. Madam Sun may take her dowry—but Zheng'er and Shen'er must stay!"

He could not afford to lose face like this!

For his eldest son and daughter-in-law to separate was already going to be a laughingstock all over the capital. If he couldn't even hold onto his grandsons—the Qin family would have no more standing in the capital's social circles.

When they heard him say this, both Qin Rong Gong and Old Madam Qin understood his plan.

Qin Rong Gong was entirely unwilling, and let out a sharp, wretched cry: "Father!"

Old Madam Qin's face also flushed red as she looked at her son: "Mingjie, this cannot be..."

Qin Mingjie waved them both off. The Sun family genuinely dared to take this disaster all the way to the Emperor.

If the Emperor truly intervened and dispatched the Imperial Guard to investigate that old affair... what event could withstand a full investigation by those brutes?

If things truly went that far, the Qin family would be permanently finished in the entire world of capital society.

If all of Madam Tong's past deeds were dug up... it would also implicate the Heshui Tong family, and the Tong family would never forgive him for it.

"Second Master Qin—Zheng'er and Shen'er are coming with me. There is no room for negotiation." Madam Sun held her ground, not yielding a single inch.

The Second Young Master of the Sun family also said: "Second Master Qin, our Jiangdong Sun family has prepared for every contingency, and we will stand behind our sister to the very end!"

"And if the Jiangdong Sun family alone cannot make the Qin family agree—what if the Jiangdong Lu family is added to the balance?" The Third Young Master added coldly.

Qin Mingjie, Qin Rong Gong, and Old Madam Qin were all struck with shock.

The Jiangdong Lu family was also going to get involved?

Qin Rong Gong suddenly recalled a rumor.

Madam Sun, in her youth, had grown up alongside Lu the Third of the Lu family...

Could it be—?

He stared at Madam Sun with vicious, furious eyes. But she didn't so much as cast a glance in his direction.

Complete indifference.

"Wenlan—let's go back to the capital Sun residence." The Second and Third Young Mistresses of the Sun family each took Madam Sun by an arm.

Madam Sun's and the children's trunks had already been packed.

Going to the capital's Sun residence today, Madam Sun would take with her all the gold, silver, and the deeds to properties that she could carry.

As for the larger pieces of furniture and belongings, those would be moved after the separation documents were obtained.

The separation documents could not be obtained today.

They would have to wait until Madam Sun's eldest brother arrived in the capital, and then arrange for the Qin family clan leader and elders to serve as witnesses.

Once the separation documents were signed, they would need to be submitted to the county office for the official record.

The Second Young Master of the Sun family gave Qin Mingjie a cupped-fist bow: "My elder brother will be in the capital by first light tomorrow. May I ask Second Master Qin and Young Master Qin to take leave from their superiors tomorrow? Otherwise, I can only approach your superiors directly to request the leave, and come find the two of you at the yamen. When it comes to that, I trust both gentlemen will find it hard to keep their dignity."

* * *

Madam Sun and her children left with the Sun brothers and sisters-in-law for the capital Sun residence.

Qin Rujin was finishing up her evening meal at her mother's courtyard when head matron Wang came to report the news. She looked to her father.

"Father, do you think... will Second Uncle and Third Cousin give in so easily and agree to let Third Sister-in-law separate and take the children?"

The First Master of the Qin family stroked his beard, then gave a measured nod.

"Those two—father and son—are the most calculating pair, and the most astute at reading the wind. The Sun family has shown they are in earnest and presented their forces openly. The Second Branch's father and son would not dare hold out against them hard."

"But if the Sun family or your Third Sister-in-law show the slightest hesitation—any suggestion that there is still room to negotiate—the Second Branch's father and son will press twice as hard..."

Qin Rujin nodded softly.

She thought: Third Sister-in-law is truly remarkable—she struck the Second Branch's father and son right at their most vulnerable point.

Madam Li, listening to the father and daughter chat, couldn't help sighing beside them.

"What a thing this is! And just when our Yinniang's wedding day is drawing closer and closer—the Second Branch has one thing after another."

She looked at the First Master of the Qin family: "It's just like you said before, my lord—could the Second Branch have been hexed?"

The First Master of the Qin family just smiled and said nothing.

Wasn't this a good thing?

Madam Li said: "Whatever happens—I don't care. Just let the Second Branch keep from causing trouble on the day our Yinniang is married off. If they dare make a scene, I won't care about losing face—I'll go to the clan hall and curse them out myself!"

The First Master of the Qin family gave his wife a reassuring smile: "My dear, rest easy. On the day Yinniang is wed—and that's also the day Fifth Girl is wed—the Second Branch will not be causing any scenes."

Madam Sun had also chosen her moment shrewdly.

Before the Fifth Young Miss left the family in marriage, she had acted decisively and put the separation on the table. As for the Sun family's rapid response—that had surely been well within Madam Sun's expectations.

Indeed, from the day Madam Sun married into the Second Branch of the Qin family, the Jiangdong Sun family had always been prepared for the possibility that one day she would leave and come home.

The First Master of the Qin family glanced at his own well-behaved little daughter.

Runjin had married down; Ruxiu was marrying at an equal level; only Rujin was marrying up.

As the old saying goes, it is rare for a woman who marries into a higher family to live contentedly.

Madam Sun of the Jiangdong Sun family had married down and still lived so wretchedly, ending in separation.

But at least Madam Sun had her family and the backing they provided—the exit of separation, prepared for her all along.

He, as a father, also wanted to give his three daughters the confidence that they could leave if they had to...

"Yinniang—your Third Uncle will be back in the household tomorrow. Once he returns, he will attend to the matter of formally adopting the Seventh and Eighth Girls from the Third Branch."

"After that, your Third Uncle and I will be doing several things. When you hear about them after you've married into the Jiang family, don't be alarmed..."

"Father!" Qin Rujin startled. The First Master of the Qin family waved a hand at her: "It will be all right—don't worry. Everything has been arranged in advance; nothing will go wrong."

Madam Li patted her little daughter's hand: "Trust your father. He has always been careful and deliberate in everything he does. In all these years, whenever he has set his mind on something, there has never been a time it didn't succeed..."

Madam Li's eyes took on a look of pride and ferocious determination.

The First Branch had endured enough!

They were just waiting for Rujin to marry out, and then it would be time to fight the Second Branch to the finish and split the clan!

Qin Rujin grew even more worried.

The First Master smiled and said to his wife: "My dear—rein it in, rein it in. Look, you're frightening the child."

Madam Li couldn't help but feel a little embarrassed.

"Yinniang, rest easy. This time your father is not fighting alone—there is also the Xianyang Wei family from your great-grandfather's side to stand behind him. Even if something doesn't go quite right, we won't come out too badly."

Qin Rujin saw that neither parent would tell her the specifics, and she had no other option.

Besides trusting them, all she could do was think of more strategies to serve as a safety net for her family back home.

Fortunately, there was good news on Uncle Ning's side.

One day, people from the imperial merchant Xu family had suddenly approached Uncle Ning, asking if the Ning family was interested in maritime trade.

If they were acting alone, the Ning family certainly wouldn't dare.

The risks were too great.

But with the imperial merchant Xu family leading the way, that was entirely different!

In just two short years, the Xu family had raked in enormous profits from maritime trade alone, and had remitted a huge sum of taxes to the imperial treasury.

They had not only earned the Emperor's praise but had become the favored darlings of the Ministry of Revenue.

To encourage the Xu family to continue developing maritime trade vigorously, the notoriously stingy Minister of Revenue—called the "iron rooster" of the six ministries—had actually taken the initiative to memorialize the Emperor, requesting that the Xu family's maritime trade be exempted from one-tenth of its taxes.

Now the remaining six of the eight imperial merchant families that had not yet entered maritime trade were scrambling anxiously to get a foothold in it.

To that end, the Ministry of Works' Bureau of Shipbuilding had already been given orders for over a hundred massive ocean-going merchant vessels this year.

They said that not just the Bureau of Shipbuilding—the entire Ministry of Works was in such high spirits, more jubilant than at the new year celebrations.

Of course, those six imperial merchant families that wanted in now had already missed their first-mover advantage.

They were all now begging the Xu family to bring them along.

But the Xu family had been squeezed out in the land trade by the other seven imperial merchants—for these latecomers to now come begging the Xu family to help them get rich at sea was not going to happen without bleeding a little.

The negotiations were ongoing.

For private merchants and great wealthy magnates, gaining access to the Xu family's network was even harder.

The Ning family patriarch had envied it from afar, but never dared to dream of it.

Uncle Ning naturally knew what the Ning family patriarch had in mind.

He promptly reported to the First Master of the Qin family.

The First Master of the Qin family felt this was an exceptionally rare opportunity for the Ning family, and told Uncle Ning to quickly write a letter summoning the Ning family patriarch to the capital.
