Uncle Ning then introduced the Ning family patriarch, who came to the capital and paid a visit to the eldest young master of the Xu family.

It was only then that the Ning family patriarch understood: the Xu family's eldest young master had sought out the Ning family because of Lord Jiang Jiuxiao, the Left Vice Minister of the Ministry of Personnel.

And Lord Jiang Jiuxiao was soon to take the Sixth Young Miss of the Qin First Branch as his wife...

The Ning family patriarch immediately told Uncle Ning that he intended to prepare a substantial trousseau for the Sixth Young Miss of the Qin family.

At the same time, he came to regard Uncle Ning—a son of the Ning family who had grown up outside—with considerably more importance.

When the eldest young master of the Xu family heard that the Ning family patriarch was being so cooperative and sensible, he was quite pleased.

Now the Ning family's goods had already been loaded onto the Xu family's massive ocean-going merchant ships and were sailing south.

And Uncle Ning, acting nimbly, had had the Ning family convert the handsome trousseau they had prepared for Qin Rujin directly into silver.

Then he took that sum—thirty-eight thousand and eight hundred taels—and used it, following the goods list Qin Rujin had once written for him, to purchase cargo, which was also loaded together onto the Xu family's maritime trade vessels...

Of course, these subsequent events were still unknown to Qin Rujin.

Uncle Ning had kept it from her.

He feared that if she found out the Ning family had prepared nearly forty thousand taels' worth of trousseau for her, she would feel the weight of it too heavily.

All Qin Rujin knew was that the Ning family had decided to enter the maritime trade business, and that they had found a way in.

She had also already planned ahead: she took the silver she had received as gifts and divided it into several portions. Eight thousand taels went in a bank note to Uncle Ning to arrange as he saw fit.

Uncle Ning intended to wait until the first batch of maritime goods sold successfully, and then use Qin Rujin's eight thousand taels to buy another cargo to ship south.

* * *

Just as Qin Rujin was about to return to her own courtyard, someone from the Qin family clan suddenly came in urgent summons for the First Master of the Qin family.

Qin Rujin did not leave right away, but helped her mother find a change of clothes for her father.

After the First Master of the Qin family had gone, Madam Li gave a cold laugh.

"It must be your Second Uncle going to the clan to tell them about your Third Cousin's separation. The clan certainly won't want to lose so much face—so they're urgently calling your father to come discuss."

Madam Li had guessed correctly.

Because the Sun family had said that Madam Sun's eldest brother would arrive in the capital first thing the next morning, and the separation would happen tomorrow, Qin Mingjie and his son had no choice but to rush to the clan that very night, and tell the clan leaders about Madam Sun's intention to separate from Qin Rong Gong.

The Qin family clan leader and the elders were all deeply shocked, and shook their heads repeatedly in refusal.

When they heard Qin Mingjie say that not only did Madam Sun want a separation, but she also intended to take the Qin family's children with her, the old clan leader forgot all about Qin Mingjie's official rank.

He launched into a tirade right at Qin Mingjie.

"What a disgrace! What a disgrace..."

"That demand is this outrageous and you agreed to it... can this old one continue to live with his face? Even in death, how could this old one face the ancestors of the Qin family in the underworld!"

But then after Qin Mingjie helplessly explained the Sun family's threats, the old clan leader was also left without recourse.

One of the elders recovered his wits quickly and sent someone to go fetch Qin Mingfeng—the First Master of the Qin family—to come and discuss.

Qin Mingjie was unhappy about it, and said a few words. The old clan leader glared at him.

"When all is said and done, Mingfeng is still the eldest legitimate son of the First Branch's Senior Line—the legitimate eldest son at that. It is only right to hear his view on Rong Gong's separation! What if he can think of something?"

Whatever rank one held as an official, one was still a son of the Qin family.

The old clan leader was in his fifth generation, with great-great-grandchildren—within the Qin family, he was absolute authority, and even Qin Mingjie dared not defy him too openly.

He had no choice but to agree, with a dark face, to invite the First Master of the Qin family.

Once the First Master of the Qin family arrived, the old clan leader told him of the Sun family's threats and asked him to think of something.

If the separation could not be prevented, at least the two children should be kept with the Qin family.

If the Qin family couldn't even hold on to the children after a separation—how would they show their faces in the capital's social circles?

The First Master of the Qin family bowed to the clan leader and elders, and said: "Please, Clan Leader and elders, do not upset yourselves—do take care of your health."

His words successfully calmed the several white-haired old men.

The First Master of the Qin family, under the dark glares of Qin Mingjie and his son, sat down with unruffled composure.

Then he looked up at Qin Mingjie and his son.

"Do Second Brother and Rong Gong not yet know? The Sun family and Madam Sun want to take the children—but they have never said anything about stripping the boys of their Qin family status."

The old clan leader gave a start, then immediately urged him: "Mingfeng—what is this? Tell us quickly!"

chapters.title: Madam Sun Successfully Obtains the Separation
chapters.content:
Qin Mingjie and his son's eyes contracted sharply.

Could the Sun family have secretly reached some kind of deal with the First Branch that would be to their disadvantage?

Unable to think of anything else, and unable to contain himself, Qin Mingjie stared at the First Master of the Qin family with fierce eyes and growled:

"Elder Brother! This is a private matter of our Qin Second Branch. You have no business making private arrangements with the Sun family behind our backs—our Second Branch will not recognize any of it!"

The First Master of the Qin family only smiled and looked helplessly toward the old clan leader.

The old clan leader brought his walking staff down with a sharp thud, and roared at Qin Mingjie: "You—hold your tongue! Wait until your elder brother has finished speaking before you open your mouth!"

Qin Mingjie was so thoroughly humiliated by the old clan leader that he nearly spat blood.

Sitting in the rightful place of the eldest legitimate son of the Senior Line—that position brought nothing but advantage.

His official rank was higher, and within this entire generation of the Qin family's children he was the most accomplished one.

Even in the matter of giving "tribute" to these old fossils, he had never skimped—these old fossils had accepted no end of favors from the Second Branch of the Qin family...

Yet the moment things came before the clan, these old fossils still sided with Qin Mingfeng, that useless man!

On what grounds?

The First Master of the Qin family had already explained the Sun family's plan to the old clan leader and the elders.

The elders looked at one another.

The old clan leader stroked his snow-white beard.

"Mingfeng—you agreed?" The old clan leader said neither yes nor no, but simply asked the First Master of the Qin family this one thing.

The First Master of the Qin family smiled with mild resignation: "If the clan leader and Second Brother and his son all agree, then of course I agree."

"No! I refuse!"

"Absolutely not!" Qin Rong Gong suddenly exploded, his red-rimmed eyes fixing the First Master of the Qin family in a hard stare.

"On what grounds does First Uncle agree? Those are my Second Branch's children!"

"You couldn't possibly be doing this just because your eldest son Qin Rong Cheng still hasn't produced a son—and so you want to claim another man's sons and another man's grandsons for yourself, could you?"

The First Master of the Qin family swept him a cold glance.

And didn't deign to respond.

The old clan leader picked up the teacup beside him and flung it at Qin Rong Gong's feet, bellowing: "You—shut your mouth! What good did reading all those books do you!"

"Your First Uncle thinks of you, thinks of your Second Branch, and never speaks ill of you in public—and what more do you want from him?"

"Clan Leader, please don't upset yourself—it's not good for your health." The First Master of the Qin family only looked at the old clan leader, and said gently: "Old Clan Leader, speaking plainly—the Sun family was actually being very sincere and courteous in what they suggested... They originally proposed entering Zheng'er and Shen'er under the name of my second son Rong Lie, with no wish to take the name of the legitimate eldest grandson of the Qin family Senior Line."

The First Master spoke with increasing clarity and precision, not caring in the least about the expressions around him: "But I thought—Zheng'er is by right the legitimate eldest grandson of this branch of the Qin family. It only makes sense to have him entered under Rong Cheng's name."

At those words, the way the old clan leader and the elders looked at the First Master of the Qin family changed entirely.

Now there was a grandson of the Qin family who truly had the family's interests at heart!

He could even give up the name of the legitimate eldest grandson...

After a pause, the First Master continued: "If our Qin family agrees, then Zheng'er and Shen'er remain sons of our Qin family. But if our Qin family refuses and provokes the Sun family's wrath—they are genuinely capable of having Zheng'er and Shen'er change their surname altogether."

"And can we afford for the bloodline of the Qin and Sun families combined to be severed entirely from our Qin family?"

The old clan leader was so agitated he forgot to stroke his beard and just kept nodding rapidly.

The difference was plain as day!

Absolutely plain as day!

Even two of the elders who had always sided with Qin Mingjie and his son couldn't help but be moved—they had to admire the First Master Qin Mingfeng's tolerance, magnanimity, and the big-picture perspective he brought to the family's interests.

"Mingjie, Rong Gong—have you two heard clearly enough? Have you anything more to say?" The old clan leader snorted coldly, shooting the father and son an irritated look.

Qin Mingjie's face was dark: "Elder Brother is merely seizing the moment to play the good man—just doing this to win the praise of the clan leader and elders."

The old clan leader slapped the table: "Since you say so—then go you and tell the Sun family to leave the children with our Qin family!"

"This..." Qin Mingjie's old face flushed red. He was forced to say: "The two brothers of the Sun family made it plain—if we refused to let the Sun family take the children, they would go before the Emperor to have it adjudicated."

"Then what in the blazes are you even arguing about!" The usually refined and cultivated old clan leader could not restrain himself and let out a crude outburst.

"You two can't do a single thing, you've had your weakness grabbed and exploited by the Sun family, and they've got you completely cornered—and you still have the nerve to criticize your elder brother?"

"If not for Mingfeng's magnanimity, the Sun family wouldn't even leave the children the Qin surname—and our Qin family would lose face utterly and completely, and the Jiangdong Sun family would then be at our throats until one side was destroyed!"

"Agreeing to let the children be entered under the Senior Line—at least the Sun family is still willing, for the children's sake, not to deal with our Qin family in the most extreme way possible."

The First Master of the Qin family pressed home the point: "The clan leader is absolutely right—the Sun brothers also said clearly: for the sake of the children, certain things they could do, they will choose not to."

"There—you see? Didn't old Cheng say as much?" The old clan leader sighed heavily: "That's exactly it! Old Cheng can now see that the Sun family was already going easy on you..."

"Enough of this... since things have come to this pass, don't drag it out any further. Go ahead with the separation as quietly as possible, and get it over with quickly. Ripping the bandage off in one go is better."

"The wedding of the Sixth Young Miss and the Fifth Young Miss is still coming up—there absolutely cannot be any more trouble at this critical moment!" The old clan leader's worried face grew even more deeply lined with wrinkles.

"Our old Qin family has truly had enough..."

The First Master of the Qin family bowed in acknowledgment.

The old clan leader looked at the unwilling, surly Qin Mingjie and his son, and said irritably: "Have you two anything more to say?"

Father and son had no choice but to affirm, with dark faces, that they had nothing more.

The First Master of the Qin family rose with a bow: "Clan Leader, elders—there is one more matter Mingfeng must inform you of."

The old clan leader and the elders all nodded, telling him to proceed.

The First Master of the Qin family then spoke of the matter of adopting the two young ladies from the Third Branch.

The old clan leader silently heaved a long sigh in his heart.

That the Second Branch of the Qin family had pressured the Third Branch daughters into becoming concubines—an affair of that magnitude—even if the Second Branch had concealed it as tightly as they could, he, as clan leader, still knew.

Only these few elders did not know.

It was a disgrace for the Qin family—the old clan leader had not told even his own eldest son, let alone spoken of it with these elders.

The elders were all surprised, and began whispering among themselves.

Qin Mingjie's expression went pitch-black.

This Qin Mingfeng—what a masterful hand!

He had assumed the First Branch was only using the matter of adopting the two Third Branch girls as a pretext—a deliberate ploy to humiliate the Second Branch.

He hadn't expected the man was serious about it!

One of the elders who sided with Qin Mingjie, seeing Qin Mingjie's face turn ugly, looked at the First Master and asked: "Mingjie, why is it that out of nowhere you want to adopt the Seventh and Eighth Girls from the Third Branch?"

The First Master of the Qin family glanced at Qin Mingjie, then told the questioning elder: "You might want to ask Second Brother first."

The elder was startled, looked again at Qin Mingjie, saw his expression turn even more distasteful, and realized inwardly that the Second Branch must have stirred up something again. He dared say no more.

The old clan leader also had no wish for the family's shame to be known far and wide, and directly nodded his approval to the First Master of the Qin family.

"It speaks well of your compassionate heart, Mingfeng. As long as your Third Brother agrees, have the Seventh and Eighth Girls adopted into your household. As they are daughters of a concubine, there is no need for a grand ceremony—just a family feast to mark the occasion is fine."

After a pause, he added: "It will also be those two girls' good fortune!"

The other elders all nodded in agreement, praising the First Master and Madam Li for their kindness and generosity.

The Seventh Young Miss and Eighth Young Miss were daughters born of a concubine of the Third Branch—but once adopted into the First Branch, they would become legitimate daughters of the First Branch's Senior Line.

What else could that be called if not good fortune?

Admittedly, the First Branch, by adopting two daughters, would have to exert effort to arrange their marriages and send them off, and would also need to prepare dowries befitting legitimate daughters.

Strictly calculated, the First Branch was the one at a disadvantage.

But then again, if the First Branch could arrange good marriages for the two girls, that too could prove beneficial to the First Branch.

The old clan leader understood in his heart that Qin Mingfeng was not that kind of calculating man.

He was concerned that the Second Branch, not above any means, would ruin the two Third Branch girls in order to pave the way for their own sons.

Truly worthy of the eldest legitimate son of the Qin Senior Line!

Thinking of the Second Branch's father and son, the old clan leader and several of the elders felt increasingly disappointed in them.

A son born of a concubine was in the end not what a son born of the legitimate wife of the Xianyang Wei family was!

Qin Mingjie and his son plainly felt the disappointment of the old clan leader and the elders, and father and son were both furious and resentful.

All those years of silver spent—and it was all for nothing, as if feeding it to dogs...

The old clan leader, having finished the business at hand, drove Qin Mingjie and his son away in irritation, keeping only the First Master of the Qin family for a private word.

* * *

At first light the next morning, the eldest young master of the Sun family arrived in the capital, dusty from the road.

Without even sitting down for breakfast, he brought the Second and Third Young Masters of the Sun family and Madam Sun and the others to the Qin household.

After the old clan leader had kept the First Master of the Qin family for a long talk the previous night, he had brought several elders early the next morning and taken up position at the Qin household in readiness.

Not to haggle and wrangle with the Sun family—but because, in his long conversation with the First Master, he had learned much information vital to the Qin family's reputation. He had no choice but to bring them all in force and stand watch personally over Qin Mingjie and his son.

To prevent them from stirring up more trouble and provoking the Sun family into a fight to the bitter end, which would destroy both sides...

In truth, once the Qin family had conceded, the Sun family was also quite accommodating.

Before the Qin family clan leader and elders, Zheng'er and Shen'er were formally entered under the name of the Senior Line's eldest son, Qin Rong Cheng.

This matter had also been discussed in advance by the First Master of the Qin family with his son Qin Rong Cheng and his wife Lin Xi.

The young couple had been quite willing to accept this arrangement.

After all, neither of them knew if the next child would be a son, and what if fate had decreed that they would have no sons? If Zheng'er and Shen'er—with the powerful Jiangdong Sun family as their maternal family—became their brothers, it would be a blessing for their own girls.

The young couple thought it a fine thing indeed and felt no resentment.

The Second Branch of the Qin family, covetous as they were of Madam Sun's dowry, had no recourse—they could only watch, helplessly, as dozens of strong servants from the capital's Sun residence carried out every last piece of Madam Sun's dowry, large and small, until not a single item remained.

The separation documents were prepared in four copies.

Madam Sun and Qin Rong Gong each received one copy.

One copy was taken by the old clan leader to be kept in the clan archives. One was submitted to the county office for the official record.

The sons Madam Sun had borne—Zheng'er and Shen'er—were entered under the Senior Line's Qin Rong Cheng, and remained sons of the Qin family.

But the children would be taken back to the Jiangdong Sun household by Madam Sun to be raised there.

The Sun family pledged that the boys' schooling, training, and future marriages upon reaching adulthood would all be handled by the Sun family—not a single coin need come from the Qin family.

With this, the old clan leader and the elders were very satisfied—they felt the Jiangdong Sun family, being a noble clan of several centuries' standing, truly knew how to conduct themselves with decency.

In the end, everyone was quite satisfied with how things concluded—everyone, that is, except the Qin Second Branch, who looked as if they were in mourning at a funeral.

Before the Sun family departed, the eldest Sun sister-in-law brought Madam Sun and the two younger sisters-in-law to pay a final call on Madam Li.

The eldest Sun sister-in-law especially requested to meet the Sixth Young Miss, Qin Rujin.

chapters.title: Rujin—Destined for Joy and Fortune
chapters.content:
The eldest Sun sister-in-law was already thirty-seven. Her face was oval, her brows like crescent moons; she was well-preserved, and one could see at a glance that she had been a great beauty in her youth.

She was the principal wife who managed the Jiangdong Sun family's inner household, and had been doing so for twenty years. She looked soft and pliable, as if she had no temper at all.

But those black, bright eyes of hers gleamed with keen intelligence—one look at them told you she was no one to be trifled with.

When Qin Rujin arrived, the eldest Sun sister-in-law took her hands and praised her effusively.

She praised her until Qin Rujin's face flushed red with embarrassment, and only then relented. She then drew a small, exquisite sachet from the wide sleeve of her robe and pressed it into Qin Rujin's hand.

"Sixth Young Miss, don't take offense—this old-fashioned thing of mine: please accept this sachet as your sister-in-law's gift to you upon first meeting, and as a small addition to your trousseau."

She had brought a sachet, and then called it both a first-meeting gift and a trousseau gift. Madam Li immediately surmised that the eldest Sun sister-in-law had given a bank note.

Qin Rujin looked at the exquisite sachet and happily accepted it at once. But when she felt the sachet—plump and full, with the texture of paper inside—Qin Rujin froze.

The eldest Sun sister-in-law, seeing that little expression on her face, laughed frankly and openly.

The Second and Third Sisters-in-law of Sun also laughed.

Madam Sun also smiled at Qin Rujin, pressing her lips together.

They had all seen it—this silly girl, when she first reached out to take the sachet, had truly liked the sachet for its beauty.

It was only when she felt there were papers inside that she didn't know what to do with herself.

"Sixth Young Sister, just keep it! My eldest sister-in-law is a goddess of fortune—she loves nothing more than showering gifts of money on pretty young girls like you. And especially so, since this is her first time meeting you." Madam Sun smiled as she explained.

Madam Li knew that if the eldest Sun sister-in-law had put her hand to it, the silver would not be small. She also couldn't help saying: "But this—how can we accept?"

The eldest Sun sister-in-law laughed broadly at Madam Li: "Aunt, you mustn't stand on ceremony with our Sun family. Because of Zheng'er and Shen'er, we are as close kin as can be now!"

And then, as if in jest, she added: "Besides—this is the first time I've met the Sixth Young Miss. How could I let myself be outdone by my Second and Third Sisters-in-law?"

The Second and Third Sun sisters-in-law pretended to take offense, shaking their heads: "Eldest Sister-in-law loves to tease us."

The eldest Sun sister-in-law laughed even more openly.

The Second Sun sister-in-law looked at Qin Rujin, and her eyes grew red.

"Honestly—I am grateful to the Sixth Young Miss from the very bottom of my heart! If not for the Sixth Young Miss truly caring for our two eldest nephews' wellbeing... awakening our Wenlan... I fear our Wenlan would have spent a lifetime mired in that rotten pit that is the Qin Second Branch."

She had never stopped carrying a thorn in her heart over what had happened to Madam Sun at her own family home that year.

And later, after she uncovered the truth that Madam Sun had been schemed against by Qin Rong Gong and his mother—one can imagine the pain that caused her.

Now that Madam Sun had formally separated from Qin Rong Gong, that long-buried thorn was finally pulled free.

The eldest Sun sister-in-law also grew serious, and looked at Qin Rujin: "What my Second Sister-in-law says is right. Our Sun family always felt that Qin Rong Gong was never worthy of our Wenlan! But Wenlan's heart was heavy—she thought too much of the family and of those who would come after in the family. Ignoring our urging and our family elders' counsel, she went ahead and married into that household... Now, at last, a weight on our hearts and on our family elders' hearts has been lifted! Wenlan told us herself—the one thing that truly made up her mind was what the Sixth Young Miss did for our two eldest nephews..."

Madam Sun also spoke to Qin Rujin with a face full of gratitude: "Perhaps the Sixth Young Miss herself feels it was merely a small matter—but it made me, as a mother, understand one thing clearly."

"Even you could think so carefully of Zheng'er's and Shen'er's wellbeing, and put yourself in their place—so what have I, their own mother, done for them all these years?"

"Nothing! Beyond giving them life, I did nothing but watch, helplessly, as their father and grandfather wore them down, making them weaker and weaker... and I never even truly fought with their father on their behalf..." Madam Sun spoke, and tears began to stream down her face.

Madam Li quickly rose, went to her, and wiped her tears with a handkerchief, sharing the feeling deeply as a woman who had also been a daughter-in-law of the Qin family.

"Don't blame yourself! It wasn't your fault!" Madam Li patted Madam Sun's shoulder.

She spoke from her own experience to comfort Madam Sun.

"I won't let others say I speak out of turn—I'll just say it directly: your former mother-in-law and that former grand-mother-in-law of yours were truly too formidable."

"If you had fought with Qin Rong Gong for the sake of the children—well, right or wrong aside, what came next would have been no end of unpleasantness, and they might have deliberately tormented the children to get back at you."

"These things—I've been through them too. Only in my case, I wasn't fighting with the children's father over the children—I was fighting with those two women, mother-in-law and grand-mother-in-law."

The three Sun sisters-in-law also comforted her, and Qin Rujin also soothed her. At last, Madam Sun stopped crying.

"Before, I had gone down a dead-end road in my thinking—always telling myself that even for the children's sake, for the family's face, if I could endure it I would endure it... But the truth is, I was only moving myself with my own endurance. The children were small, with no power to resist those who claimed to act in their interest but were in fact harming them. If they could have resisted, they would have."

"My whole family has been worrying about me and suffering on my behalf all these years, all because I had married so unhappily..." Madam Sun raised her eyes and looked at the Second Sun sister-in-law, her face full of remorse.

"Especially Second Sister-in-law—you have worked so hard on my account all these years. The fault was mine, Sister!"

The Second Sun sister-in-law quickly shook her head and stepped forward to take both of Madam Sun's hands: "All of that is behind us now! Nothing but good days ahead!"

Qin Rujin was also deeply moved.

Moved by Madam Sun's awakening. Moved by how every member of the Sun family, from the very first, had stood firmly on Madam Sun's side—not treating her as a daughter spilled from a bucket, never to be retrieved.

And moved by how all of Madam Sun Wenlan's sisters-in-law had stood on the side of women, thinking for their little sister-in-law's sake.

This was a kindred spirit, a mutual support between women.

In this ancient feudal society, it was something rare and precious.

Father had said that the Jiangdong Sun family was the most pragmatic and calculating—siding always with whoever was winning. Yet the Jiangdong Sun family also had within it these wonderfully endearing women.

"Aunt—this is a half-trousseau that our Jiangdong Sun family has specially prepared for the Sixth Young Miss. Here is the trousseau list—please keep it safe!"

The eldest Sun sister-in-law rose to her feet and, with both hands, passed a thick sheaf of papers to Madam Li.

Madam Li was so startled her mind went blank for a moment.

In the great Ying dynasty, a complete trousseau consisted of sixty-four carrying cases. Any family with some means would prepare sixty-four for their daughter—a full trousseau.

Those with more modest circumstances would make do with thirty-two cases—half a trousseau.

But sixty-four cases from a noble family and sixty-four cases from a family of moderate means were two entirely different things.

The Jiangdong Sun family calling this a half-trousseau—its value was something Madam Li didn't dare calculate.

Madam Li also didn't dare accept it.

This was an entirely different matter from a first-meeting gift or even a trousseau addition.

The eldest Sun sister-in-law, seeing she wouldn't take it, immediately became serious: "Aunt—you aren't still standing on ceremony with our Sun family, are you? Even just for the sake of Zheng'er and Shen'er—you ought to accept it on the Sixth Young Miss's behalf!"

The Second and Third Sun sisters-in-law also urged her.

"Aunt, my eldest sister-in-law made a solemn pledge before our family elders—if Yinniang doesn't accept, our Sun family will have no face left."

Madam Sun simply took the half-trousseau list from her eldest sister-in-law's hands and pressed it directly into Madam Li's.

"Aunt, if you don't accept—then I can't very well let Zheng'er and Shen'er be registered under my eldest brother's and sister-in-law's name."

Once Madam Sun had said that, Madam Li had no choice but to accept it on Qin Rujin's behalf.

Qin Rujin was also in a daze.

The eldest Sun sister-in-law, seeing that everything had been settled, and that the afternoon was growing late, rose to make her farewells.

She also told Madam Li cheerfully: "When the day of our Sixth Young Miss's wedding arrives, I shall bring my sisters-in-law and Wenlan early to the household to help!"

Only one's own family would come early to help on the day of a great family occasion.

Madam Li happily agreed.

Zheng'er and Shen'er, though young, had still been told by Madam Sun about the separation from their father.

She also told them that their father was still their father—after coming to the capital from Jiangdong to visit their grandparents in the First Branch, they were also welcome to visit the Second Branch's grandparents and great-grandmother, as well as their father.

Children are the most perceptive of all—from the time they had been old enough to read the expressions on adults' faces, they had known something wasn't right between their mother and father.

Now that Mother had told them she and Father had separated, the children were, in a way, calmer than before—more at ease than they had been when they were still anxious and uncertain.

Mother was not going to stop them from seeing their family in the capital, and that was good.

As for why Mother had separated from Father—clearly Father had done something wrong, something Mother couldn't forgive.

Children still needed their mothers the most at this age, and naturally, they were willing to go with her.

When it came time to say goodbye to the children, Qin Rujin gave them the set of over fifty wooden cards she had hastily finished making.

Even the girls from Qin Rong Cheng's and Qin Rong Lie's families each took the half of their own share and gave it to Zheng'er and Shen'er.

The children even made a promise—when Zheng'er and Shen'er came back to the capital next time, they would come back to visit. And when they did, they would see who had learned the most characters, who could recite the most poems...

After seeing Madam Sun Wenlan and her sisters-in-law off, Madam Li let out a long breath of relief.

When she unrolled the half-trousseau list the Sun family had given and read it through, Madam Li nearly cried out loud.

This half-trousseau from the Sun family was worth at least as much as everything the entire First Branch had jointly prepared for Yinniang's dowry!

Madam Li's hands were shaking as she held the half-trousseau list.

"Quick—look at what the eldest Sun sister-in-law gave you in the sachet." Madam Li said to her youngest daughter, her voice trembling.

Qin Rujin handed the sachet to her mother, and asked: "Mother, is the trousseau Wenlan's family prepared for me too generous?"

Madam Li nodded emphatically, then gave the trousseau list to her daughter and took the sachet.

She opened the sachet and saw bank notes, neatly folded in squares.

She spread them open: six of them.

Each bank note was for five hundred taels.

Madam Li drew in another sharp breath.

What an open-handed woman the eldest Sun sister-in-law was!

And that was not counting the separate trousseau gifts the Second and Third Sun sisters-in-law had added for Qin Rujin today.

Each one would be worth at least several hundred taels.

Adding those to the first-meeting gifts they had given previously, together it was quite a lavish sum...

"The Sun family is truly generous!" Madam Li couldn't help exclaiming.

Just at that moment, the First Master of the Qin family, having finished seeing off the eldest Sun brother and his party, came back into the room and heard his wife's exclamation.

He quickly asked what had happened.

Madam Li handed the half-trousseau list the Sun family had given to Qin Rujin over to the First Master: "Have a look yourself, my lord—it's what the Sun family gave Yinniang."

The First Master took it and read it, then was silent.

After a long moment, the First Master couldn't help but exclaim: "This much! At the very least, over twenty thousand taels of silver!"

"Can't it be?" Madam Li looked at him: "Should we really just accept all this on Yinniang's behalf? My lord, I wonder if we ought to think this over."

After a pause, she added: "And that's not all, actually. Adding the trousseau gifts the Sun family gave Yinniang on top of that—I'd say at least this many!" Madam Li held up four fingers.

After all, the jewelry Madam Sun had given Qin Rujin on several occasions was likely worth several thousand taels.

The First Master also felt the heat of it.

He stroked his beard and deliberated for a moment, then gave a small nod: "It has already been accepted—there is nothing more to be said. In future, we will simply treat Zheng'er and Shen'er as our own grandchildren."

Madam Li breathed a quiet sigh of relief.

"That's the way. We'll treat Zheng'er the same as our very own legitimate eldest grandson, and Shen'er the same as our own grandson."

This meant that in the future, when these two boys grew up, the First Branch of the Qin family would also have to give them a generous betrothal gift—just as they would for their own legitimate eldest grandson.

The First Master of the Qin family nodded, then passed the trousseau list back to Qin Rujin with a smile: "This trousseau list—keep it yourself. Rest easy: this was given to you by the Sun family, and so it belongs to you."

Madam Li took over the thread: "As for the matters of returning courtesy and gifts—your father and mother are here for that. You need not worry in the least!"

The First Master then said: "At the root of it, this is also a deal that the Jiangdong Sun family struck with our First Branch of the Qin family. The reason it was given to you in particular, Yinniang, is first: because your great wedding is coming up and the timing fell to you. Second: your Madam Sun sister and the Sun family all privately believe it was you who helped Madam Sun make up her mind to seek the separation. And third: it is, in an indirect way, giving our First Branch and the Jiang family some face..."

Qin Rujin knew her father was right.

Madam Li knew it too.

She then smiled: "Whatever the case—the Jiangdong Sun family's conduct is truly beyond reproach."

The First Master nodded: "Of course. Otherwise, how could they have remained standing firm for several centuries?"

"As for Wenlan, that child—she may well remarry someday. When that day comes, we should prepare a full trousseau for her as if she were our own daughter."

The First Master nodded: "Yes—so preparations should begin now. From what I've seen, Wenlan is no hidebound traditionalist. She has gone through a separation with such clean resolve and decisiveness—remarrying would be no great difficulty."

"I really..." Qin Rujin was still in a daze: "I never imagined—that with Wenlan separating from her husband, I'm the one who ended up with a small fortune!"

Madam Li laughed heartily, full of pride: "When you were small, Mother took you to your maternal grandmother's family at the Liuyuan Temple to pray and burn incense. The abbess at the Liuyuan Temple took one look at you and said you were fated for joy and fortune."

"Back then Mother didn't believe it—I thought the abbess was just saying nice things to please us. But looking at it now—the abbess wasn't wrong in the slightest!" The First Master said with a laugh.

That evening, the Third Uncle of the Qin family returned to the household......

books.title: After the Mad Tyrant's Rebirth, She Was Taken by Force
books.author: Qiong Yu
books.synopsis:
They say the reigning emperor is cold and ruthless, his moods as unpredictable as the wind, rarely gracing the inner palace with his presence — until the fourth daughter of the Xi family entered the court.

To say that among three thousand women in the harem, he favored only her alone would not be an exaggeration.

He bestowed upon her the character *Chen*, then granted her Chenqian Palace — the grandest and closest to his own quarters. Within a mere half year, she rose from the rank of Zhaoyi to the head of the Four Consorts.

He even had honeydew melons transported from two thousand li away, delivered to her door each and every day — all because she was fond of them.

All of it, just to coax a smile from his beauty.

Yet no one knew that this same Consort Chen, she who reigned supreme in the inner palace, would one day be found huddled in the corner of a resplendent golden hall, tears blurring her vision, watching with frightened eyes as a figure draped in shadow slowly approached.

The faint clinking of iron chains drifted through the air.

The tall man clad in imperial yellow knelt on one knee before Xi Rongyan
